column5: Worthy are the hands who bow, help in prostrating and embrace a brother Sikh;

column2: ਕਿਰਤਿ ਵਿਰਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਧਰਮ ਦੀ; ਹਥਹੁ ਦੇਕੈ ਭਲਾ ਮਨਾਵੈ॥
column5: Who eam livelihood honestly and munificently confer favour on others.

column2: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਪਰਸਿ ਅਪਰਸ ਹੋਇ; ਪਰ ਤਨ ਪਰ ਧਨ ਹਥੁ ਨ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: Worthy of praise are the hands of such a Sikh who by coming in touch with Guru becomes indifferent to worldly materials and lays not his eyes on another's wife or property;

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਪੂਜ ਕੈ; ਭਾਇ ਭਗਤਿ ਭੈ ਭਾਣਾ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: Who loves another Sikh and embraces the love, devotion, and fear of God;

column2: ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ਨ ਆਪ ਗਣਾਵੈ॥੧੨॥
column5: He effaces his ego and does not assert himself.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੩. ਚਰਣ ਸਫਲਤਾ}
column5: The usefulness of feet

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੈਰ ਸਕਾਰਥੇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਚਾਲ ਚਲੰਦੇ॥
column5: Blessed are the feet of the Sikhs who walk in Guru's way;

column2: ਗੁਰੂ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਜਾਨਿ ਚਲਿ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਇ ਬਹੰਦੇ॥
column5: Who go to the Gurudwara and sit their in the holy congregation;

column2: ਧਾਵਨ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਨੋ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾ ਨੋ ਖੋਜਿ ਲਹੰਦੇ॥
column5: Who search out the Guru's Sikhs and hasten to do them favours.

column2: ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਪੰਥਿ ਨ ਧਾਵਨੀ; ਮਾਇਆ ਵਿਚਿ ਉਦਾਸ ਰਹੰਦੇ॥
column5: Worthy are the feet of the Silk's who do not go on the way of duality and possessing wealth remain indifferent to it.

column2: ਬੰਦਿ ਖਲਾਸੀ ਬੰਦਗੀ; ਵਿਰਲੇ ਕੇਈ ਹੁਕਮੀ ਬੰਦੇ॥
column5: Few are the people who abide by the orders of the Supreme Commander, do Him homage and thus escape from their bonds;

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖਾਂ ਪਰਦਖਣਾ; ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਰਹਰਾਸਿ ਕਰੰਦੇ॥
column5: Who adopt the custom of circumambulating the Guru's Sikhs and falling at their feet.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਚੇਲੇ ਪਰਚੈ ਪਰਚੰਦੇ॥੧੩॥
column5: The Guru's Sikhs delight in such enjoyments.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੪. ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਪਰੋਪਕਾਰੀ}
column5: Gurmukh, the altruist

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਮਨਿ ਪਰਗਾਸੁ ਹੈ; ਪਿਰਮ ਪਿਆਲਾ ਅਜਰੁ ਜਰੰਦੇ॥
column5: The enlightened mind of the Sikhs drinks and digests the unbearable cup of the love of the Lord.

column2: ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਰੰਦੇ॥
column5: Armed with the knowledge of the Brahm, they meditate upon the transcendental Brahm.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵਲੀਣ ਹੋਇ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਣੰਦੇ॥
column5: Merging their consciousness in the Word-sabad, they recite the indescribable story of the Word-the Guru.

column2: ਭੂਤ ਭਵਿਖਹੁੰ ਵਰਤਮਾਨ; ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਲਖੰਦੇ॥
column5: They are competent to see incomprehensible pace of the past, present and future.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖਫਲੁ ਅਛਲੁ ਛਲੁ; ਭਗਤਿ ਵਛਲ ਕਰਿ ਅਛਲੁ ਛਲੰਦੇ॥
column5: Never deluding fruit of joy, the gurmukhs get, and with grace of the God, kind to devotees, they rather delude the evil propensities.

column2: ਭਵਜਲ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਬੋਹਿਥੈ; ਇਕਸ ਪਿਛੇ ਲਖ ਤਰੰਦੇ॥
column5: They work as a boat in the world-ocean and ferry across the millions who follow one gurmukh, the Guru-oriented person.

column2: ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰੀ ਮਿਲਨਿ ਹਸੰਦੇ॥੧੪॥
column5: The altruist Sikhs always come up smiling.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੫. ਘਰਬਾਰੀ ਸਿੱਖ ਦੀ ਰਹਿਣੀ}
column5: The conduct of the house holder Sikh

column2: ਬਾਵਨ ਚੰਦਨ ਆਖੀਐ; ਬਾਹਲੇ ਬਿਸੀਅਰੁ ਤਿਸੁ ਲਪਟਾਹੀ॥
column5: The snakes are said to be coiled around the sandal tree (but the tree is not influenced by their poison).

column2: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਪਥਰਾਂ; ਪਥਰ ਪਾਰਸ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਜਾਹੀ॥
column5: The philosopher's stone exists among stones but does not turn out to be an ordinary stone.

column2: ਮਣੀ ਜਿਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਸਪਾਂ ਸਿਰੀਂ; ਓਇ ਭਿ ਸਪਾਂ ਵਿਚਿ ਫਿਰਾਹੀ॥
column5: The jewel-holding snake also roams about among the ordinary snakes.

column2: ਲਹਰੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਹੰਸੁਲੇ; ਮਾਣਕ ਮੋਤੀ ਚੁਗਿ ਚੁਗਿ ਖਾਹੀ॥
column5: From the waves of the pond, the swans pick up only pearls and gems to eat.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਜਲ ਕਵਲ ਅਲਿਪਤੁ ਹੈ; ਘਰਿਬਾਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਤਿਵਾਹੀ॥
column5: As the lotus remains unsmeared in water, the same is the position of the householder Sikh.

column2: ਆਸਾ ਵਿਚਿ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਹੋਇ; ਜੀਵਨੁ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਜੀਵਾਹੀ॥
column5: He residing among all the hopes and cravings around, adopts the skill of liberation in life and lives (happily).

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਕਿਤੁ ਮੁਖਿ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ॥੧੫॥
column5: How could one eulogise the holy congregation.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੬. ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਤੇ ਸਿੱਖ ਉਸਤੁਤਿ}
column5: The True Guru and praise

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੁਰਖੁ; ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰਿ ਆਕਾਰੁ ਬਣਾਇਆ॥
column5: The formless Lord has assumed the form of true Guru, the blessed one.

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ; ਸੁਣਿ ਚਰਣਿ ਸਰਣਿ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਜੁ ਆਇਆ॥
column5: Fortunate is the Sikh of the Guru who listening to the teaching of the Guru has sought the shelter of the Guru-feet.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਹੈ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੰਗੁ ਚਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: The way of the gurmukhs is blessed on which one treads through the holy congregation.

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਚਰਣ; ਧੰਨੁ ਮਸਤਕੁ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਣੀ ਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: Blest is feet of the true Guru and that head is also fortunate which reposes on the feet of Guru.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਹੈ; ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਪਰਸਣਿ ਆਇਆ॥
column5: The glimpse of the true Guru is auspicious and the Sikh of the Guru is also blessed one who has come to have sight of the Guru.

column2: ਭਾਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਵਿਚਿ ਹੋਇ; ਦਇਆਲੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਮੁਹਿ ਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: The Guru loves happily the devotional feelings of the Sikh.

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਉ ਮਿਟਾਇਆ॥੧੬॥
column5: The wisdom of the Guru decimates duality.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੭. ਸਫਲ ਸਮੇਂ}
column5: The blessed time

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਪਲੁ ਚਸਾ ਘੜੀ ਪਹਰ ਧੰਨੁ; ਧੰਨੁ ਥਿਤਿ ਸੁ ਵਾਰ ਸਭਾਗੇ॥
column5: Blessed is the moment, the blinking time, the hour, the date, the day (during which you remember the Lord).

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਦਿਹੁ ਰਾਤਿ ਹੈ; ਪਖੁ ਮਾਹ ਰੁਤਿ ਸੰਮਤਿ ਜਾਗੇ॥
column5: Day, night, fortnight, months, season and the year are auspicious wherein mind tries to rise (to divinity).

column2: ਧੰਨ ਅਭੀਚੁ ਨਿਛਤ੍ਰ ਹੈ; ਕਾਮੁ ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਅਹੰਕਾਰੁ ਤਿਆਗੇ॥
column5: Blessed is the abhijit nakstra which inspires to repudiate the lust, the anger and the ego.

column2: ਧੰਨੁ ਧੰਨੁ ਸੰਜੋਗੁ ਹੈ; ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਰਾਜ ਪਿਰਾਗੇ॥
column5: That time is fortunte wherein (through meditation on God) one gets the fruits of the holy dip at the sixty eight pilgrim centers and the Prayagraj.

column2: ਗੁਰੂ ਦੁਆਰੈ ਆਇ ਕੈ; ਚਰਣਕਵਲ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪਾਗੇ॥
column5: Reaching the door of Guru (the Gurudwara) mind gets absorbed in the delight of the lotus feet (of Guru).

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਅਵੇਸੁ ਕਰਿ; ਅਨਭੈ ਪਿਰਮ ਪਿਰੀ ਅਨੁਰਾਗੇ॥
column5: Adopting the teachings of Guru, the state of fearlessness and total absorption in the love (of Lord) is attained.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਅੰਗਿ ਅੰਗਿ ਇਕ ਰੰਗਿ ਸਮਾਗੇ॥
column5: Immersing the consciousness in the sabad (word) through and in the holy congregation, every limb ( of the devotee) reverberates the lustre of the (steadfast) colour of the Lord.

column2: ਰਤਨ ਮਾਲ ਕਰਿ ਕਚੇ ਧਾਗੇ॥੧੭॥
column5: The Sikhs of the Guru have made jewel garland of the fragile thread of breath (and they make full use of the same).

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੮. ਘਰਬਾਰੀ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤ}
column5: A liberated house holder

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਿਠਾ ਬੋਲਣਾ; ਜੋ ਬੋਲੈ ਸੋਈ ਜਪੁ ਜਾਪੈ॥
column5: The polite language of a Sikh brings out what he thinks in his mind and heart.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅਖੀ ਦੇਖਣਾ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਧਿਆਨ ਧਰੈ ਆਪੁ ਆਪੈ॥
column5: A Sikh beholds God everywhere with his own eyes, and that is equal to a yogi's meditation.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਨਣਾ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕਰਿ; ਪੰਚ ਸਬਦੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦਿ ਅਲਾਪੈ॥
column5: When a Sikh listens attentively to, or himself sings, the word of God, that is equal to the five ecstatic sounds in the brain of a yogi.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਕਿਰਤਿ ਕਮਾਵਣੀ; ਨਮਸਕਾਰੁ ਡੰਡਉਤਿ ਸਿਞਾਪੈ॥
column5: Earning livelihood with his hands by a Sikh is equal to the obeisance and prostration (of Hindus).

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਚਲਣਾ; ਪਰਦਖਣਾ ਪੂਰਨ ਪਰਤਾਪੈ॥
column5: When, the gurmukh, walks to behold the Guru, that is equal to an extremely holy circumambulation.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਖਾਣਾ ਪੈਨਣਾ; ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਪਛਾਪੈ॥
column5: When the Guru oriented person eats and clothes himself, that is equal to the performance of Hindu sacrifice and offering.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਵਣੁ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਹੈ; ਆਪੇ ਆਪਿ ਨ ਥਾਪਿ ਉਥਾਪੈ॥
column5: When gurmukh sleeps, that is equal to a yogi's trance and the gunnukh withdraws not his thoughts from the object (God the Guru) of his concentration.

column2: ਘਰਬਾਰੀ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ; ਲਹਿਰ ਨਹੀਂ ਭਵ ਲੋਭ ਬਿਆਪੈ॥
column5: The householder is liberated in life; he is not afraid of the waves of the world's ocean and fear does not enter his heart.

column2: ਪਾਰਿ ਪਏ ਲੰਘਿ ਵਰੈ ਸਰਾਪੈ॥੧੮॥
column5: He goes beyond the region of blessings and curses, and does not utter them.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੯. ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਦੀ ਧਾਰਨਾ}
column5: The realization by Gurmukh

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਤਿ ਸਰੂਪੁ ਹੈ; ਧਿਆਨ ਮੂਲੁ ਗੁਰ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਜਾਣੈ॥
column5: That the true Guru is the truth incarnate and is the basis of meditation is well known (to gurmukh).

column2: ਸਤਿਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾਪੁਰਖੁ ਮੂਲਮੰਤ੍ਰ ਸਿਮਰਣ ਪਰਵਾਣੈ॥
column5: Satnam, Karta Purakh is accepted as the basic formula, the muli mantr, by gurmukh.

column2: ਚਰਣਕਵਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸੁ; ਪੂਜਾ ਮੂਲ ਪਿਰਮ ਰਸ ਮਾਣੈ॥
column5: He accepting the sweet sap of the lotus feet as fundamental, quaffs the joy of love for the supreme.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਗੁਰ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਤੇ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਆਣੈ॥
column5: He enters into the immersion of word-consciousness through the Guru and the holy congregation.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥੁ ਅਗੰਮੁ ਹੈ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਨਿਹਚਲੁ ਚਲਣੁ ਭਾਣੈ॥
column5: The way of the gurmukh is beyond the ken of mind and speech and he in accordance with the wisdom of the Guru and his own steadfast will, treads on it.

column2: ਵੇਦ ਕਤੇਬਹੁ ਬਾਹਰੀ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਕਉਣ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੈ॥
column5: Who can describe the importance of the parable (of gurmukh) because it is beyond the Vedas and the Katebas, (the four holy books of semitic religion).

column2: ਵੀਹ ਇਕੀਹ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਸਿਞਾਣੈ॥੧੯॥
column5: This way can be identified only by crossing the limits and anxieties about the high and low of the world.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੦. ਮਨਮੁਖ ਗਤੀ}
column5: Position of a Manmukh

column2: ਸੀਸੁ ਨਿਵਾਏ ਢੀਂਗੁਲੀ; ਗੱਲ ਬੰਧੈ ਜਲੁ ਉਚਾ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: To get water from a stream or pond, the dhingali (a pole with a bucket one end and a fulcrum in the middle used for drawing water) is lowered by catching hold of its neck, i.e. it is humbled forcibly and does not go down of its own.

column2: ਘੁਘੂ ਸੁਝ ਨ ਸੁਝਈ; ਚਕਈ ਚੰਦ ਨ ਡਿਠਾ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: The owl is not pleased on beholding the sun or chakavi; ruddy sheldrake, the moon.

column2: ਸਿੰਮਲ ਬਿਰਖ ਨ ਸਫਲੁ ਹੋਇ; ਚੰਦਨਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਨ ਵਾਂਸਿ ਸਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: The silk cotton (simbal) tree yields no fruit and the bamboo grows near the sandal but is not perfumed thereby.

column2: ਸਪੈ ਦੁਧੁ ਪੀਆਲੀਐ; ਤੁਮੇ ਦਾ ਕਉੜਤੁ ਨ ਜਾਵੈ॥
column5: Given milk to drink a serpent does not part with its poison and the bitterness of the colocynth also does not depart.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਥਣਿ ਚੰਬੜਿ ਚਿਚੜੀ; ਲੋਹੂ ਪੀਐ ਦੁਧੁ ਨ ਖਾਵੈ॥
column5: The tick clings to the cow's udder but drinks blood instead of milk.

column2: ਸਭ ਅਵਗੁਣ ਮੈ ਤਨਿ ਵਸਨਿ; ਗੁਣ ਕੀਤੇ ਅਵਗੁਣ ਨੋ ਧਾਵੈ॥
column5: All these demerits I have and if any one do me a favour, I return it with undesirable trait.

column2: ਥੋਮ ਨ ਵਾਸੁ ਕਥੂਰੀ ਆਵੈ॥੨੦॥੬॥
column5: Garlick can never have the perfume of musk.

column2: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ॥
column5: One Oankar, the primal energy, realized through the grace of divine preceptor

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧. ਮੰਗਲਾਚਰਣ}
column5: One Oankar, the primal energy, realized through the grace of divine preceptor

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸਚਾ ਪਾਤਿਸਾਹੁ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਚੁਖੰਡੁ ਵਸਾਇਆ॥
column5: The true Guru is true emperor who has founded the abode of truth in the form of the congregation of the saints.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਲੈ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਹੋਇ; ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇ ਨ ਆਪੁ ਗਣਾਇਆ॥
column5: The Sikhs living there being taught by the Guru, lose their ego and never make themselves noticed.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਭੋ ਸਾਧਨਾ; ਸਾਧਿ ਸਧਾਇ ਸਾਧੁ ਸਦਵਾਇਆ॥
column5: The Sikhs of the Guru get themselves called sadhus only after accomplishing all sorts of discipline.

column2: ਚਹੁ ਵਰਣਾ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਦੇ; ਮਾਇਆ ਵਿਚਿ ਉਦਾਸੁ ਰਹਾਇਆ॥
column5: They preach to all the four varnas and themselves remain indifferent in the midst of maya.

column2: ਸਚਹੁ ਓਰੈ ਸਭ ਕਿਹੁ; ਸਚੁ ਨਾਉ ਗੁਰਮੰਤੁ ਦਿੜਾਇਆ॥
column5: They explain clearly that everything is below truth i.e. the truth is highest and only this mantra should be recited with deep integrity.

column2: ਹੁਕਮੈ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਭ ਕੋ; ਮੰਨੈ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਸੁ ਸਚਿ ਸਮਾਇਆ॥
column5: Everything is subsumed in the divine order and whosoever bows his head before His order, merges in the truth.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਇਆ॥੧॥
column5: The consciousness attuned to the Word makes man competent to behold the invisible Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨. ਦੋ ਦੀ ਗਿਣਤੀ, ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਮਹਿਮਾਂ}
column5: Twosome,the glory of Gurmukh ,

column2: ਸਿਵ ਸਕਤੀ ਨੋ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਚੰਦੁ ਸੂਰਜ ਦਿਹੁੰ ਰਾਤਿ ਸਧਾਏ॥
column5: Conquering Siva and S'akti (the rajas and tamas qualities), the gurmukhs have disciplined the moon-sun (ira, pingala) and also the time known by days and nights.

column2: ਸੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਸਾਧੇ ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ; ਨਰਕ ਸੁਰਗ ਪੁੰਨ ਪਾਪ ਲੰਘਾਏ॥
column5: Subjugating pleasure and pain, joy and suffering, they have gone beyond hell and heaven, sin and virtue.

column2: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਣ ਜੀਵਨੁ ਮੁਕਤੁ; ਭਲਾ ਬੁਰਾ ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਸਤ੍ਰ ਨਿਵਾਏ॥
column5: They have humbled life, death, liberation in life, right and wrong, enemy and friend.

column2: ਰਾਜ ਜੋਗ ਜਿਣਿ ਵਸਿ ਕਰਿ; ਸਾਧ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਵਿਜੋਗ ਰਹਾਏ॥
column5: Being victors of raj and yoga (temporality and spirituality), they have disciplined alliance as well as separation.

column2: ਵਸਗਤਿ ਕੀਤੀ ਨੀਂਦ ਭੂਖ; ਆਸਾ ਮਨਸਾ ਜਿਣਿ ਘਰਿ ਆਏ॥
column5: Conquering sleep, hunger, hope and desire, they have made their abode in their own true nature.

column2: ਉਸਤੁਤਿ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਹਿੰਦੂ ਮੁਸਲਮਾਣ ਸਬਾਏ॥
column5: Going beyond praise and slander, they have become beloved of the Hindus as well as Muslims.

column2: ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਪਾਖਾਕ ਸਦਾਏ॥੨॥
column5: They bow before all and consider themselves as dust.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੩. ਤਿੰਨ ਦੀ ਗਿਣਤੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Threesome-gurmukh

column2: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨ ਮਹੇਸ ਤ੍ਰੈ ਲੋਕ; ਵੇਦ ਗੁਣ ਗਿਆਨ ਲੰਘਾਏ॥
column5: The gurmukhs have gone ahead of the three worlds, three gunas (rajas, sattva and tamas) and Brahma Visnu Mahesa.

column2: ਭੂਤ ਭਵਿਖਹੁ ਵਰਤਮਾਨੁ; ਆਦਿ ਮਧਿ ਜਿਣਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਸਿਧਾਏ॥
column5: They know the mystery of the beginning, the middle, the end, of past, present and future.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕਰਮ ਇਕੱਤ੍ਰ ਕਰਿ; ਜੰਮਣ ਮਰਣ ਜੀਵਣ ਜਿਣਿਆਏ॥
column5: They keep together in one line their mind, speech and action and conquer birth, life and death.

column2: ਆਧਿ ਬਿਆਧਿ ਉਪਾਧਿ; ਸਾਧਿ ਸੁਰਗ ਮਿਰਤ ਪਾਤਾਲ ਨਿਵਾਏ॥
column5: Subjugating all the maladies, they have humbled this world, heaven and the nether world.

column2: ਉਤਮ ਮਧਮ ਨੀਚ ਸਾਧਿ; ਬਾਲਕ ਜੋਬਨ ਬਿਰਧਿ ਜਿਣਾਏ॥
column5: Winning the top, middle and the lowest positions they have conquered the childhood, youth and the old age.

column2: ਇੜਾ ਪਿੰਗਲਾ ਸੁਖਮਨਾ; ਤ੍ਰਿਕੁਟੀ ਲੰਘਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਬੇਣੀ ਨਾਏ॥
column5: Crossing trikuti, the conjunction of three naris – ira, pingala, susumna in between the eyebrows, they have bathed in the triveni, the pilgrimage centre at the confluence of Ganges, Yamuna and Sarasvati.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਇਕੁ ਮਨਿ ਇਕ ਧਿਆਏ॥੩॥
column5: With concentrated mind, gurmukhs adore only one Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੪. ਚੌਕੜੀ ਦਾ ਵਰਣਨ, ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Four some-gurmukh ,

column2: ਅੰਡਜ ਜੇਰਜ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਸੇਤਜ ਉਤਭੁਜ ਖਾਣੀ ਬਾਣੀ॥
column5: The gurmukhs subdue the four life-mines (egg, foetus, sweat, vegetation) and the four speeches (para, posyanti, madhyama, vaikhari~.

column2: ਚਾਰੇ ਕੁੰਡਾ ਚਾਰਿ ਜੁਗ; ਚਾਰ ਵਰਨ ਚਾਰਿ ਵੇਦ ਵਖਾਣੀ॥
column5: Four are directions, four the yugas (ages), four varnas and four are the Vedas.

column2: ਧਰਮ ਅਰਥੁ ਕਾਮੁ ਮੋਖੁ ਜਿਣਿ; ਰਜ ਤਮ ਸਤ ਗੁਣ ਤੁਰੀਆ ਰਾਣੀ॥
column5: Conquering dharma, artha, kama, moksa and crossing three stagges of rajas, sattva and tamas they enter into the fourth stage turiya, the stage of supreme bliss.

column2: ਸਨਕਾਦਿਕ ਆਸ੍ਰਮ ਉਲੰਘਿ; ਚਾਰਿ ਵੀਰ ਵਸਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਆਣੀ॥
column5: They control Sanak, Sanandan Sanatan, Sanatkumar, the four ashramas and the four warriors (in the field of charity, dharma, compassion and warfare).

column2: ਚਉਪੜਿ ਜਿਉ ਚਉਸਾਰ ਮਾਰਿ; ਜੋੜਾ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਕੋਇ ਰਞਾਣੀ॥
column5: As in chaupar (a game like blackgamman played with an oblong dice) one is victorious by winning all the four sides, and a twosome is not killed,

column2: ਰੰਗ ਬਿਰੰਗ ਤੰਬੋਲ ਰਸ; ਬਹੁ ਰੰਗੀ ਇਕੁ ਰੰਗੁ ਨੀਸਾਣੀ॥
column5: As in chaupar (a game like blackgamman played with an oblong dice) one is victorious by winning all the four sides, and a twosome is not killed,

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤ ਨਿਰਬਾਣੀ॥੪॥
column5: So the gurmukh also makes pair with the One Lord and becomes undefeatable.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੫. ਪੰਚ ਹਰ ਸੰਖੵਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Fivesome-gurmukh

column2: ਪਉਣ ਪਾਣੀ ਬੈਸੰਤਰੋ ਧਰਤਿ ਅਕਾਸੁ; ਉਲੰਘਿ ਪਇਆਣਾ॥
column5: Gurmukh goes beyond air, water, fire, earth and sky.

column2: ਕਾਮ ਕਰੋਧੁ ਵਿਰੋਧੁ ਲੰਘਿ; ਲੋਭੁ ਮੋਹੁ ਅਹੰਕਾਰ ਵਿਹਾਣਾ॥
column5: Resisting lust and anger he crosses the greed, infatuation and ego.

column2: ਸਤਿ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਦਇਆ ਧਰਮੁ ਅਰਥੁ; ਸੁਗ੍ਰੰਥ ਪੰਚ ਪਰਵਾਣਾ॥
column5: He espouses truth, contentment, compassion, dharma and fortitude.

column2: ਖੇਚਰ ਭੂਚਰ ਚਾਚਰੀ ਉਨਮਨ ਲੰਘਿ ਅਗੋਚਰ ਬਾਣਾ॥
column5: Getting above of the khechar bhuchar chachar, unman and agochar (all yogic postures) mudras he concentrates upon the One Lord.

column2: ਪੰਚਾਇਣ ਪਰਮੇਸਰੋ; ਪੰਚ ਸਬਦ ਘਨਘੋਰ ਨੀਸਾਣਾ॥
column5: He beholds God in five (select persons) and the five sounds of five words become his special marks.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਚ ਭੂ ਆਤਮਾ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਧ ਸੁਹਾਣਾ॥
column5: Antahkaran, the basis of all five external elements is cultivated and cultured by gurmukh in the holy congregation.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਨ ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣਾ॥੫॥
column5: This way immersing in undisturbed trance he gets liberated from the cycle of transmigration.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੬. ਛੇ ਦੀ ਗਿਣਤੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Sixsome-gurmukh

column2: ਛਿਅ ਰੁਤੀ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਧਨਾਂ; ਛਿਅ ਦਰਸਨ ਸਾਧੈ ਗੁਰਮਤੀ॥
column5: Attaining spiritual discipline through the six seasons, gurmukh assimilates even the six philosophies.

column2: ਛਿਅ ਰਸ ਰਸਨਾ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਰਾਗ ਰਾਗਣੀ ਭਾਇ ਭਗਤੀ॥
column5: He conquers the six tastes (sour, sweet, astringent, bitter, tart and saltish) of the tongue and alongwith six musical measures and their consorts surrenders with full devotions.

column2: ਛਿਅ ਚਿਰਜੀਵੀ. ਛਿਅ ਜਤੀ ਚੱਕ੍ਰਵਰਤਿ; ਛਿਅ ਸਾਥਿ ਜੁਗਤੀ॥
column5: He understands and accomplishes the ways of life of six immortal ones, six yatis (ascetics) and six yogic chakras.

column2: ਛਿਅ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਛਿਅ ਕਰਮ ਜਿਣਿ; ਛਿਆ ਗੁਰਾਂ ਗੁਰ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨਿਰਤੀ॥
column5: Conquering the six codes of conduct and the six philosophies, he cultivates friendship with the six gurus (teachers of these philosophies).

column2: ਛਿਅ ਵਰਤਾਰ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਛਿਅ ਛਕ ਛਤੀ ਪਵਣ ਪਰਤੀ॥
column5: He turns his face from the five external organs plus one internal organ, the mind, and their attendant thirty six kinds of hypocrisies.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤੀ॥੬॥
column5: Reaching the holy congregation the consciousness of a gurmukh gets absorbed in the Word of Guru.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੭. ਸਪਤ ਸੰਖੵਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Sevensome-gurmukh

column2: ਸਤ ਸਮੁੰਦ ਉਲੰਘਿਆ; ਦੀਪ ਸਤ. ਇਕ ਦੀਪਕ ਬਲਿਆ॥
column5: Getting above of the seven oceans and seven Continents, the gurmukh lights the lamp of knowledge.

column2: ਸਤ ਸੂਤ ਇਕ ਸੂਤਿ ਕਰਿ; ਸਤੇ ਪੁਰੀਆ ਲੰਘਿ ਉਛਲਿਆ॥
column5: He binds the seven threads (five organs, mind and wisdom) of body into one thread (of high consciousness) and goes across the seven (mythological) habitats (puris).

column2: ਜਤ ਸਤੀ ਜਿਣਿ ਸਪਤ ਰਿਖਿ; ਸਤਿ ਸੁਰਾ ਜਿਣਿ ਅਟਲੁ ਨ ਟਲਿਆ॥
column5: Understanding the intrinsic meaning of seven satis, seven rishis and seven musical notes, he remains steadfast in his resolves.

column2: ਸਤੇ ਸੀਵਾ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਸਤੀ ਸੀਵੀ ਸੁਫਲਿਓ ਫਲਿਆ॥
column5: Crossing the seven stages of knowledge, gurmukh gets the fruit of the knowledge of Brahm, the basis of all the stages.

column2: ਸਤ ਅਕਾਸ ਪਤਾਲ; ਸਤ ਵਸਿਗਤਿ ਕਰ. ਉਪਰੇਰੈ ਚਲਿਆ॥
column5: Controlling the seven nether worlds and seven skies he goes beyond them.

column2: ਸਤੇ ਧਾਰੀ ਲੰਘਿਕੈ; ਭੈਰਉ ਖੇਤ੍ਰਪਾਲ ਦਲ ਮਲਿਆ॥
column5: Getting across the seven streams, he decimates the armies of Bhairav and other protectors of the worlds.

column2: ਸਤੇ ਰੋਹਣਿ ਸਤਿ ਵਾਰ; ਸਤਿ ਸੁਹਾਗਣਿ ਸਾਧ ਨ ਢਲਿਆ॥
column5: The seven rohinis seven days and the seven married women and their ritualistic activities cannot upset him.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਵਿਚ ਖਲਿਆ॥੭॥
column5: Gurmukh always remains stabilized in the true congregation.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੮. ਅਸ਼ਟ ਸੰਖੵਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Eightsome-gurmukh

column2: ਅਠੈ ਸਿਧੀ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ; ਸਾਧਿਕ ਸਿਧ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਫਲਾਈ॥
column5: Accomplishing eight siddhis (powers) the gurmukh has attained the fruit of adept trance (siddh samadhi).

column2: ਅਸਟ ਕੁਲੀ ਬਿਖੁ ਸਾਧਨਾ; ਸਿਮਰਣਿ ਸ਼ੇਖ ਨ ਕੀਮਤਿ ਪਾਈ॥
column5: The practices by the eight ancestoral family houses of Sesanag could not understand His mystery.

column2: ਮਣੁ ਹੁਇ ਅਠ ਪੈਸੇਰੀਆ; ਪੰਜੂ ਅਠੇ ਚਾਲੀਹ ਭਾਈ॥
column5: One maund (old Indian weighing unit) consists of eight panseris (about five kilograms), and five multiplied by eight is equal to forty.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਚਰਖਾ ਅਠ ਖੰਭੀਆ; ਇਕਤੁ ਸੂਤ ਰਹੈ ਲਿਵਲਾਈ॥
column5: The spinning wheel having eight spokes keeps its consciousness concentrated in a single thread.

column2: ਅੱਠ ਪਹਿਰ ਅਸਟਾਂਗੁ ਜੋਗੁ; ਚਾਵਲ ਰਤੀ ਮਾਸਾ ਰਾਈ॥
column5: Eight watches, eight limbed yoga, chaval (rice), ratti, rais, masa (all old Indian measuring units of time and weight) have among themselves the relationship of eight i.e. eight rais = one chaval, eight chavals = one ratti and eight rattis = one masa.

column2: ਅਠ ਕਾਠਾ ਮਨੁ ਵਸ ਕਰਿ; ਅਸਟ ਧਾਤੁ ਇਕੁ ਧਾਤ ਕਰਾਈ॥
column5: Controlling the mind comprising eight inclinations, the gurmukh has made it homogenous as the eight metals after mixing become one metal.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਵਡੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ॥੮॥
column5: Great is the glory of the holy congregation.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੯. ਨਵ ਸੰਖੵਾ-ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Ninesome-gurmukh -

column2: ਨਥਿ ਚਲਾਏ ਨਵੈ ਨਾਥਿ ਨਾਥਾ ਨਾਥ ਅਨਾਥ ਸਹਾਈ॥
column5: Though, the gurmukh subdues the nine naths (ascetic yogis) yet he considers himself as without any father i.e. most humble, and God as the father of the fatherless ones.

column2: ਨਉ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਫੁਰਮਾਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰਭਾਈ॥
column5: Nine treasures are in his command and the great ocean of knowledge goes with him like his brother.

column2: ਨਉ ਭਗਤੀ ਨਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਲਿਵਲਾਈ॥
column5: Neo devotees practise nine types of ritualistic devotion but gurmukh remains immersed in the loving devotion.

column2: ਨਉ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਸਾਧ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤ ਵਿਚਿ ਪੂਰੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ॥
column5: With the blessings of the Guru and living the household life, he controls all the nine planets.

column2: ਨਉਖੰਡ ਸਾਧ ਅਖੰਡ ਹੋਇ ਨਉ ਦੁਆਰਿ ਲੰਘਿ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਈ॥
column5: Even conquering the nine divisions of earth, he never gets broken up and, going above the illusions of nine doors of body, hecomes to reside in his own self.

column2: ਨਉ ਅੰਗ ਨੀਲ ਅਨੀਲ ਹੋਇ ਨਉ ਕੁਲ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਈ॥
column5: From nine numbers have been counted infinite numbers, and controlling the nine pleasures (ras) in the body, gurmukh stays in the equipoise.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਫਲੁ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਈ॥੯॥
column5: Only gurmukhs receive the unattainable fruit of the supreme delight.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੦. ਦਸ ਸੰਖੵਾ-ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Tensome-gurmukh -

column2: ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਦਸ ਨਾਵ ਧਰਿ ਸਚ ਨਾਵ ਵਿਣੁ ਨਾਵ ਗਣਾਇਆ॥
column5: Sannyasis, giving ten nomenclatures to their sects, but in fact being devoid of the true Name have (egotistically) got their own names counted.

column2: ਦਸ ਅਵਤਾਰ ਅਕਾਰੁ ਕਰਿ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਨ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਇਆ॥
column5: Even the ten incarnations when they came in (human) form did not see that invisible Oankar.

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਪੁਰਬ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਵਿਚਿ ਦਸ ਪੁਰਬੀਂ ਗੁਰਪੁਰਬਿ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ॥
column5: Celebrations of the ten auspicious days (no-moon, full moon days etc.) at pilgrimage centres could not know the real importance of Gurpurb, the anniversaries of the Gurus.

column2: ਇਕ ਮਨ ਇਕ ਨ ਚੇਤਿਓ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਵਿਣੁ ਦਹਦਿਸਿ ਧਾਇਆ॥
column5: The individual did not ponder upon the Lord with his concentrated mind and bereft of the holy congregation he is running in all the ten directions.

column2: ਦਸ ਦਹਿਆਂ ਦਸ ਅਸ੍ਵਮੇਧ ਖਾਇ ਅਮੇਧ ਨਿਖੇਧੁ ਕਰਾਇਆ॥
column5: Ten days of Muslim Muharram and ten horse sacrifices (asvamedh) are prohibited in Gurmat (Sikhism).

column2: ਇੰਦਰੀਆ ਦਸ ਵਸਿ ਕਰਿ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਜਾਂਦਾ ਵਰਜਿ ਰਹਾਇਆ॥
column5: Gurmukh, controlling the ten organs stops the mind racing in ten directions.

column2: ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਜਗੁ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਇਆ॥੧੦॥
column5: He humbly bows at the Guru's feet and the whole world falls at his feet.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੧. ਏਕਾਦਸ਼ ਸੰਖੵਾ-ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Elevensome-gurmukh -

column2: ਇਕ ਮਨਿ ਹੋਇ ਇਕਾਦਸੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਰਤੁ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਿ ਭਾਇਆ॥
column5: Like a faithful wife, gurmukh likes the fast of ekadasi in the form of concentration of mind (Hindus generally observe fast on the eleventh day of lunar month).

column2: ਗਿਆਰਹ ਰੁਦ੍ਰ੍ਰ ਸਮੁਦ੍ਰ ਵਿਚਿ ਪਲ ਦਾ ਪਾਰਾਵਾਰ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ॥
column5: Eleven Rudras (different forms of Siva) could not understand the mystery of this world - ocean.

column2: ਗਿਆਰਹ ਕਸ ਗਿਆਰਹ ਕਸੇ ਕਸਿ ਕਸਵੱਟੀ ਕਸ ਕਸਾਇਆ॥
column5: The gurmukh has controlled all the eleven (ten organs and the mind). Their eleven objects also he has controlled and he has purified the mind-gold by rubbing it on the touchstone of devotion.

column2: ਗਿਆਰਹ ਗੁਣ ਫੈਲਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਕਚ ਪਕਾਈ ਅਘੜ ਘੜਾਇਆ॥
column5: Cultivating eleven virtues he has chiselled and stabilized the tardy mind.

column2: ਗਿਆਰਹ ਦਾਉ ਚੜ੍ਹਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਦੂਜਾ ਭਾਉ ਕੁਦਾਉ ਰਹਾਇਆ॥
column5: Assuming eleven virtues (truth, contentment, compassion, dharma, control, devotion etc.) he has erased duality and dubiousness.

column2: ਗਿਆਰਹ ਗੇੜਾ ਸਿਖੁ ਸੁਣਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖੁ ਲੈ ਗੁਰਸਿਖੁ ਸਦਾਇਆ॥
column5: Listening to the mantra eleven times, the gurmukh adopting the teaching of the Guru, is called Gursikh.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਸਬਦੁ ਵਸਾਇਆ॥੧੧॥
column5: In the holy congregation only the Word-Guru resides in one's heart.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੨. ਦ੍ਵਾਦਸ਼ ਸੰਖੵਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Twelvesome-gurmukh

column2: ਬਾਰਹ ਪੰਥ ਸਧਾਇਕੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਗਾਡੀ ਰਾਹ ਚਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: Winning over the twelve sects of yogis, the gurmukhs started a simple and straight way (for liberation).

column2: ਸੂਰਜ ਬਾਰਹ ਮਾਹ ਵਿਚਿ ਸਸੀਅਰੁ ਇਕਤੁ ਮਾਹਿ ਫਿਰਾਇਆ॥
column5: It looks as if the sun circumambulates earth in twelve months and the moon in one month but the fact is that the work completed by the person having tamas and rajas qualities in twelve months is done in one month by the person having sattva quality.

column2: ਬਾਰਹ ਸੋਲਹ ਮੇਲਿ ਕਰਿ ਸਸੀਅਰ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸੂਰ ਸਮਾਇਆ॥
column5: Combining twelve (months) and sixteen (phases of moon) the sun merges into the moon i.e. rajas and tamas get absobed into the sattva.

column2: ਬਾਰਹ ਤਿਲਕ ਮਿਟਾਇਕੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਤਿਲਕੁ ਨੀਸਾਣੁ ਚੜਾਇਆ॥
column5: Gurmukh repudiating the twelve types of marks on forehead only keeps on his head the mark of the love of Lord.

column2: ਬਾਰਹ ਰਾਸੀ ਸਾਧਿ ਕੈ ਸਚਿ ਰਾਸਿ ਰਹਰਾਸਿ ਲੁਭਾਇਆ॥
column5: Conquering the twelve zodiac signs, gurmukh remains absorbed in the capital of truthful conduct.

column2: ਬਾਰਹ ਵੰਨੀ ਹੋਇ ਕੈ ਬਾਰਹ ਮਾਸੇ ਤੋਲਿ ਤੁਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: Becoming pure gold of twelve masas (twenty four carrots) they come true to their worth in the world market.

column2: ਪਾਰਸ ਪਾਰਸਿ ਪਰਸਿ ਕਰਾਇਆ॥੧੨॥
column5: Touching the philosopher's stone in the form of Guru, the gunnukhs also become philosopher's stone.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੩. ਤ੍ਰ੍ਰੵੋਦਸ਼ ਸੰਖੵਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ}
column5: Thirteensome-gurmukh

column2: ਤੇਰਹ ਤਾਲ ਅਊਰਿਆ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਤਪ ਤਾਲ ਪੁਰਾਇਆ॥
column5: Thirteen beats of music are incomplete but gurmukh with his accomplishment of the rythm (of household life) attains delight.

column2: ਤੇਰਹ ਰਤਨ ਅਕਾਰਥੇ ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਰਤਨੁ ਧਨੁ ਪਾਇਆ॥
column5: Thirteen jewels are also futile for the Gurmukh who gets the jewel of teaching of the Guru.

column2: ਤੇਰਹ ਪਦ ਕਰਿ ਜਗ ਵਿਚਿ ਪਿਤਰ ਕਰਮ ਕਰਿ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: The ritualistic people have overawed the people in their thirteen types of rituals.

column2: ਲਖ ਲਖ ਜਗ ਨ ਪੁਜਨੀ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਚਰਣੋਦਕ ਪੀਆਇਆ॥
column5: Myriad burnt offerings (yajna) cannot be equated with nectar of the feet of gurmukh.

column2: ਜਗ ਭੋਗ ਨਈਵੇਦ ਲਖ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮੁਖਿ ਇਕੁ ਦਾਣਾ ਪਾਇਆ॥
column5: Even one grain of gurmukh;s is equal to millions of yajnas, offerings and edibles.

column2: ਗੁਰਭਾਈ ਸੰਤੁਸਟੁ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲੁ ਪਿਰਮ ਚਖਾਇਆ॥
column5: And by making their fellow disciples of the Guru content, the Gurmukhs remain happy.

column2: ਭਗਤਿ ਵਛਲੁ ਹੋਇ ਅਛਲ ਛਲਾਇਆ॥੧੩॥
column5: God is undeceivable but He is dodged by the devotees.
Fourteen,Fifteen and Sixteensome-gurmukh14 -15 -16 -

column2: ਚਉਦਹ ਵਿਦਿਆ ਸਾਧਿਕੈ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਕਥ ਕਹਾਣੀ॥
column5: Accomplishing the fourteen skills, gurmukhs adopt the indescribable skill of the wisdom of Guru (Gurmat).

column2: ਚਉਦਹ ਭਵਣ ਉਲੰਘਿਕੈ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਨੇਹੁ ਨਿਰਬਾਣੀ॥
column5: Going across the fourteen worlds they reside in their own self and remain immersed in the state of nirvana.

column2: ਪੰਦ੍ਰਹ ਥਿਤੀ ਪਖੁ ਇਕੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਨ ਸੁਕਲ ਦੁਇ ਪੱਖ ਨੀਸਾਣੀ॥
column5: One fortnight consists of fifteen days; one is the dark (krsna) fortnight and the second is moonlit light (sukla)fortnight.

column2: ਸੋਲਹ ਸਾਰ ਸੰਘਾਰੁ ਕਰਿ ਜੋੜਾ ਜੁੜਿਆ ਨਿਰਭਉ ਜਾਣੀ॥
column5: Like the game of dice, ousting the sixteen counters and making the pair only, one attains fearlessness.

column2: ਸੋਲਹ ਕਲਾ ਸੰਪੂਰਣੋ ਸਸਿ ਘਰਿ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਵਿਰਤੀ ਹਾਣੀ॥
column5: When moon, the master of sixteen phases (full of sattvic quality) enters into the sun (full of rajas and tamas), it gets faded.

column2: ਨਾਰਿ ਸੋਲਹ ਸੀਂਗਾਰ ਕਰਿ ਸੇਜ ਭਤਾਰ ਪਿਰਮ ਰਸੁ ਮਾਣੀ॥
column5: Woman also using sixteen types of adornments goes to the bed of her husband and enjoys the extreme delight.

column2: ਸਿਵ ਤੈ ਸਕਤਿ ਸਤਾਰਹ ਵਾਣੀ॥੧੪॥
column5: The power (sakti) of Siva i.e. maya keeps with her seventeen speeches or variations of its powers.
Eighteen to thirty-four18 34

column2: ਗੋਤ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਸੋਧਿਕੈ ਪੜੈ ਪੁਰਾਣ ਅਠਾਰਹ ਭਾਈ॥
column5: Thoroughly understanding the eighteen gotras, sub castes, the gurmukhs go through the eighteen puranas.

column2: ਉਨੀ ਵੀਹ ਇਕੀਹ ਲੰਘਿ ਬਾਈ ਉਮਰੇ ਸਾਧਿ ਨਿਵਾਈ॥
column5: Jumping over nineteen, twenty and twenty-one.

column2: ਸੰਖ ਅਸੰਖ ਲੁਟਾਇਕੈ ਤੇਈ ਚੌਵੀ ਪੰਜੀਹ ਪਾਈ॥
column5: They make the number of twenty-three, twenty-four and twenty-five meaningful.

column2: ਛਬੀ ਜੋੜਿ ਸਤਾਈਹਾ ਆਇ ਅਠਾਈਹ ਮੇਲ ਮਿਲਾਈ॥
column5: In the name of twenty-six, twenty-seven, twenty-eight they meet the Lord.

column2: ਉਲੰਘਿ ਉਣਤੀਹ ਤੀਹ ਸਾਧਿ ਲੰਘਿ ਇਕਤੀਹ ਵਜੀ ਵਧਾਈ॥
column5: Crossing twenty-nine, thirty and reaching thirty-one, in their heart they feel blest and delighted.

column2: ਸਾਧ ਸੁਲਖਣ ਬਤੀਹੇ ਤੇਤੀਹ ਧ੍ਰੂ ਚਉਫੇਰਿ ਫਿਰਾਈ॥
column5: Accomplishing the thirty-two saintly characteristics, like Dhru they make thirty-three crore gods and goddesses shake and revolve around (them).

column2: ਚਉਤੀਹ ਲੇਖ ਅਲੇਖ ਲਖਾਈ॥੧੫॥
column5: Touching thirty-four they realize the Invisible Lord i.e. the gurmukhs going above all the numbers get exhilerated in the love of Lord who is beyond all the counts.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੬. ਈਸ਼੍ਵਰੋਪਮਾ}
column5: Praises of the lord

column2: ਵੇਦ ਕਤੇਬਹੁ ਬਾਹਰਾ ਲੇਖ ਅਲੇਖ ਨ ਲਖਿਆ ਜਾਈ॥
column5: God is beyond Vedas and katebas (holy books of Semitic religions) and He cannot be visualized.

column2: ਰੂਪ ਅਨੂਪੁ ਅਚਰਜੁ ਹੈ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਅਗੋਚਰ ਭਾਈ॥
column5: His form is grand and awe-inspiring. He is beyond the reach of body organs.

column2: ਇਕੁ ਕਵਾਉ ਪਸਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਤੋਲਨ ਤੁਲਾਧਾਰ ਨ ਸਮਾਈ॥
column5: He created this cosmos by His one big bang which cannot be weighed on any scale.

column2: ਕਥਨੀ ਬਦਨੀ ਬਾਹਰਾ ਥਕੈ ਸਬਦੁ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ॥
column5: He is indescribable and many a man in order to reach Him has got tired by putting their consciousness into the Word.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕਰਮ ਅਗੋਚਰਾ ਮਤਿ ਬੁਧਿ ਸਾਧਿ ਸੋਝੀ ਥਕਿ ਪਾਈ॥
column5: Being beyond the ken of mind, speech, and action, the wisdom, intellect and all practices have also left hope of catching hold of Him.

column2: ਅਛਲ ਅਛੇਦ ਅਭੇਦ ਹੈ ਭਗਤਿ ਵਛਲੁ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਛਾਈ॥
column5: Undeceivable, beyond time and non dual, the Lord is kind to devotees and pervades through the holy congregation.

column2: ਵਡਾ ਆਪ ਵਡੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ॥੧੬॥
column5: He is great and His grandeur is also great

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੭. ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ}
column5: Gurmukh and fruits of delight

column2: ਵਣ ਵਣ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਣਾਸਪਤਿ ਰਹੈ ਉਜਾੜਿ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਅਵਸਾਰੀ॥
column5: The vegetation in the desolate places in the forest remains unknown.

column2: ਚੁਣਿ ਚੁਣਿ ਆਂਜਨਿ ਬੂਟੀਆ ਪਤਿਸਾਹੀ ਬਾਗ ਲਾਇ ਸਵਾਰੀ॥
column5: The gardeners choose and pick up some plants and plant them in the garden of the kings.

column2: ਸਿੰਜਿ ਸਿੰਜਿ ਬਿਰਖ ਵਡੀਰੀਅਨਿ ਸਾਰਿ ਸਮ੍ਹਾਲਿ ਕਰਨ ਵੀਚਾਰੀ॥
column5: They are grown by irrigation, and the thoughtful persons take care of them.

column2: ਹੋਇ ਸਫਲ ਰੁਤਿ ਆਈਐ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਫਲ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸ ਭਾਰੀ॥
column5: In the season they fructify and offer juicy fruits.

column2: ਬਿਰਖਹੁ ਸਾਉ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਫਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਸਾਉ ਸੁਗੰਧਿ ਸੰਜਾਰੀ॥
column5: There is no taste in the tree but in fruit resides taste as well as flavour.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਜਗਤ੍ਰ੍ਰ ਵਿਚਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ॥
column5: In the world, the perfect Brahm resides in holy congregation of the gurmukhs.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅਪਰ ਅਪਾਰੀ॥੧੭॥
column5: In fact, the gurmukhs themselves are the infinite pleasure-giving fruit in the world.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੮. ਅੰਬਰ ਵਰਣਨ}
column5: The sky

column2: ਅੰਬਰ ਨਦਰੀ ਆਂਵਦਾ ਕੇਵਡੁ ਵਡਾ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ॥
column5: The sky is seen but none knows its extent.

column2: ਉਚਾ ਕੇਵਡੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਸੁੰਨ ਸਰੂਪ ਨ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੈ॥
column5: How much high it is in the form of vaccum is not known to anybody.

column2: ਲਖ ਫਰਾਸ਼ ਲਖ ਸਾਰਵਾਨ ਮੀਰਾਂ ਖੋਰ ਸਈਸ ਵਹੀਰਾ॥
column5: Millions of flowers, camel drivers, syces, and grooms are there.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਲਖ ਜਲੇਬਦਾਰ ਗਾਡੀਵਾਨ ਚਲਾਇ ਗਡੀਰਾ॥
column5: Millions are maintenance officers and drivers of the royal carriages.

column2: ਛੜੀਦਾਰ ਦਰਵਾਨ ਖਲੀਰਾ॥੧੯॥
column5: Many stick-holding gatekeepers stand and wait.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੦. ਗਿਣਤੀ}
column5: Count

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਲਖ ਨਗਾਰਚੀ ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਢੋਲੀ ਸਹਨਾਈ॥
column5: Many are kettledrum and drum-beaters and many play on clarinets.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਹੀ ਤਾਇਫੇ ਢਾਢੀ ਬਚੇ ਕਲਾਵਤ ਗਾਈ॥
column5: Many are prostitutes, bards and singers of qauwali, a particular type of song sung usually in-group in particular modes mostly by Muslims.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਬਹੁਰੂਪੀਏ ਬਾਜੀਗਰ ਲਖ ਭੰਡ ਅਤਾਈ॥
column5: Many are mimics, acrobats and million are jesters.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਲਖ ਮਸਾਲਚੀ ਸਮਾ ਚਰਾਗ ਕਰਨਿ ਰੁਸਨਾਈ॥
column5: Many are torchbearers who light the torches.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਹੀ ਕੋਰਚੀ ਆਮਲ ਪੋਸ਼ ਸਿਲਹ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ॥
column5: Many are the keepers of army store and many are officers who wear comfortable suit of armor.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਹੀ ਆਬਦਾਰ ਕਿਤੜੇ ਬਾਵਰਚੀ ਨਾਨਵਾਈ॥
column5: Many are water carriers and cooks who cook nans, a kind of round, flat bread.

column2: ਤੰਬੋਲੀ ਤੋਸਕਚੀ ਸੁਹਾਈ॥੨੦॥
column5: Betel sellers and incharge of store room for precious articles of their own glory.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੧. ਗਿਣਤੀ}
column5: Count

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਖੁਸਬੋਇਦਾਰ ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਰੰਗਰੇਜ ਰੰਗੋਲੀ॥
column5: Many are perfume sellers and many dyers who use colors to make many designs (rangolis).

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਮੇਵੇਦਾਰ ਹਨਿ ਹੁਡਕ ਹੁਡਕੀਏ ਲੋਲਣਿ ਲੋਲੀ॥
column5: Many are servants working on contract and many are frolicsome prostitutes.

column2: ਖਿਜਮਤਿਗਾਰ ਖਵਾਸ ਲਖ ਗੋਲੰਦਾਜ ਤੋਪਕੀ ਤੋਲੀ॥
column5: Many are personal maidservants, bomb-throwers, cannoners and many are carriers of war material.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਤਹਵੀਲਦਾਰ ਮੁਸਰਫਦਾਰ ਦਰੋਗੇ ਓਲੀ॥
column5: Many are revenue officers, superintending officers, policemen and estimators.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਕਿਰਸਾਣ ਹੋਇ ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਸਾਣੀ ਅਤੁਲੁ ਅਤੋਲੀ॥
column5: Many are farmers who weigh and take care of the agricultural crop and its allied works.

column2: ਮੁਸਤੌਫੀ ਬੂਤਾਤ ਲਖ ਮੀਰਸਾਮੇ ਬਖਸੀ ਕੋਲ ਕੋਲੀ॥
column5: Millions are accountants, home secretaries, oath officers, finance ministers and tribal people who prepare bows and arrows.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਦੀਵਾਨ ਹੋਇ ਕਰਨਿ ਕਰੋੜੀ ਮੁਲਕ ਢੰਢੋਲੀ॥
column5: Many becoming custodians of property administer the country.

column2: ਰਤਨ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਮੋਲ ਅਮੋਲੀ॥੨੧॥
column5: Many are there who have the accounts of invaluable jewels etc. and deposit them properly.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੨. ਗਿਣਤੀ}
column5: Count

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਹੀ ਜਉਹਰੀ ਲਖ ਸਰਾਫ ਬਜਾਜ ਵਪਾਰੀ॥
column5: Many are jewelers, goldsmiths and cloth merchants.

column2: ਸਉਦਾਗਰ ਸਉਦਾਗਰੀ ਗਾਂਧੀ ਕਾਸੇਰੇ ਪਾਸਾਰੀ॥
column5: Then there are itinerant traders, perfumers, coppersmiths and sellers of provision.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਪਰਚੂਨੀਏ ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਦਲਾਲ ਬਜਾਰੀ॥
column5: Many are retailers and many are brokers in the market.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਸਿਕਲੀਗਰਾਂ ਕਿਤੜੇ ਲਖ ਕਮਗਰ ਕਾਰੀ॥
column5: Many are arms manufacturers and many are working on alchemical materials.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਕੁਮ੍ਹਿਆਰ ਲਖ ਕਾਗਦ ਕੁਟ ਘਣੇ ਲੂਣਾਰੀ॥
column5: Many are potters, paper pounders and producers of salt.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਦਰਜੀ ਧੋਬੀਆਂ ਕਿਤੜੇ ਜਰ ਲੋਹੇ ਸਿਰ ਹਾਰੀ॥
column5: Many are tailors, washermen, and goldplaters.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਭੜਭੂੰਜੇ ਭਠਿਆਰੀ॥੨੨॥
column5: Many are grain parchers who make fire in hearths specially designed for parching grain.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੩. ਗਿਣਤੀ}
column5: Count

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਕਾਰੂੰਜੜੇ ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਦਬਗਰ ਕਾਸਾਈ॥
column5: Many are green grocers, many are makers of kuppas, large vessels made from raw hide usually for holding and carrying oil, and may more are butchers.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਮੁਨਿਆਰ ਲਖ ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਚਮਿਆਰ ਅਰਾਈ॥
column5: Many are toy and bangle sellers and many are leather workers and vegetable growers-cum-sellers.

column2: ਭੰਗਹੇਰੇ ਹੋਇ ਕੇਤੜੇ ਬਗਨੀਗਰਾ ਕਲਾਲ ਹਵਾਈ॥
column5: Many are toy and bangle sellers and many are leather workers and vegetable growers-cum-sellers.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਭੰਗੀ ਪੋਸਤੀ ਅਮਲੀ ਸੋਫੀ ਘਣੀ ਲੁਕਾਈ॥
column5: Millions drink hemp and many are brewers of wine from rice and barley, and confectioners are also many there.

column2: ਕੇਤੜਿਆ ਕਹਾਰ ਲਖ ਗੁਜਰ ਲਖ ਅਹੀਰ ਗਣਾਈ॥
column5: Millions of cattle breeders, palanquin bearers and milk-men may be presently counted.

column2: ਕਿਤੜੇ ਹੀ ਲਖ ਚੂਹੜੇ ਜਾਤਿ ਅਜਾਤਿ ਸਨਾਤ ਅਲਾਈ॥
column5: Millions of scavengers and outcaste pariahs (chandal) are there.

column2: ਨਾਵ ਥਾਵ ਲਖ ਕੀਮ ਨ ਪਾਈ॥੨੩॥
column5: Thus myriads are names and places which cannot be counted.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੪. ਸਭ ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਬਣੋਂ}
column5: All should become gurmukh

column2: ਉਤਮ ਮਧਮ ਨੀਚ ਲਖ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨੀਚਹੁ ਨੀਚ ਸਦਾਏ॥
column5: Millions are low, medium and high but gurmukh calls himself low of the lowly.

column2: ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਪਾ ਖਾਕ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਗੁਰਸਿਖੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਏ॥
column5: He becoming dust of the feet and guru’s disciple erases his ego.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਭਉ ਭਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਸੇਵਕ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਏ॥
column5: Going with love and respect to the holy congregation, he serves there.

column2: ਮਿਠਾ ਬੋਲਣ ਨਿਵ ਚਲਣੁ ਹਥਹੁ ਦੇ ਕੈ ਭਲਾ ਮਨਾਏ॥
column5: He speaks mildly, behaves humbly and even by giving something to somebody wishes good of others.

column2: ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵਲੀਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਦਰਗਹ ਮਾਣ ਨਿਮਾਣਾ ਪਾਏ॥
column5: Absorbing consciousness into the Word that humble person receives honour in the court of the Lord.

column2: ਚਲਣੁ ਜਾਣਿ ਅਜਾਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਆਸਾ ਵਿਚਿ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਵਲਾਏ॥
column5: Considering death as the last truth and becoming unknown to cunningness he remains indifferent to hopes and desires.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲੁ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਏ॥੨੪॥੮॥
column5: The imperceptible fruit of delight is seen and received only by Gurmukh.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧. ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ, ਗੁਰੂ, ਸ਼ਬਦ, ਸਤਿਸੰਗ}
column5: Wondrous Lord, Guru, Word, Holy congregation

column2: ਗੁਰ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ॥
column5: Guru is replica of the perfect Braham who is unmanifest and indestructible.

column2: ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦੁ ਹੈ ਸਤਸੰਗਿ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ॥
column5: Word of Guru (and not his body) s transcendent Brahm who resides in the holy congregation.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਚੁ ਖੰਡੁ ਹੈ ਭਾਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਅਭਿਆਸੀ॥
column5: The company of the sadhus is the abode of truth where opportunity for loving devotion is created.

column2: ਚਹੁ ਵਰਨਾ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਪਰਗਾਸੀ॥
column5: Here all the four varnas are preached to and the wisdom of the Guru (Gurmat) brought before the people.

column2: ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਪਾਖਾਕ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਹਿਰਾਸੀ॥
column5: Only here touching the feet and by becoming dust of the feet, gurmukhs become followers of the way of discipline.

column2: ਮਾਇਆ ਵਿਚਿ ਉਦਾਸੁ ਗਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਆਸ ਨਿਰਾਸੀ॥੯॥
column5: Becoming neutral amidst hopes, the individuals through the holy congregation go beyond maya.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨. ਗੁਰ ਸਿੱਖੀ}
column5: Discipleship of the Guru

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖੀ ਬਾਰੀਕ ਹੈ ਸਿਲ ਚਟਣ ਫਿਕੀ॥
column5: To be disciple of the Guru is very subtle activity and it is like licking of the tasteless stone.

column2: ਤਿਖੀ ਖੰਡੇ ਧਾਰ ਹੈ ਉਹ ਵਾਲਹੁਂ ਨਿਕੀ॥
column5: It is thinner than the hair and sharper than the edge of the sword.

column2: ਭੂਹ ਭਵਿਖ ਨ ਵਰਤਮਾਨ ਸਰਿ ਮਿਕਣਿ ਮਿਕੀ॥
column5: Nothing is equal to it in present, past and future.

column2: ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ ਏਤੁ ਘਰਿ ਹੋਇ ਇਕਾ ਇਕੀ॥
column5: In the house of Sikhism, the duality gets erased and one becomes one with that One.

column2: ਦੂਆ ਤੀਆ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਸਣ ਕਕਾ ਕਿਕੀ॥
column5: Man forgets the idea of second, third, when and why.

column2: ਸਭੈ ਸਿਕਾਂ ਪਰਹਰੈ ਸੁਖੁ ਇਕਤੁ ਸਿਕੀ॥੨॥
column5: Repudiating all the desires, the individual gets delight in the hope of one Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੩. ਗੁਰਮੁਖਤਾਈ}
column5: The way of Gurmukh

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਆਖੀਐ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਹਿਤਕਾਰੀ॥
column5: The way leading to the adoption of beneficent wisdom of the Guru (Gurmat) is known as the Gurmukh-way.

column2: ਹੁਕਮਿ ਰਜਾਈ ਚਲਣਾ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦ ਵੀਚਾਰੀ॥
column5: In it one is taught to live in the will of the Lord and to ponder upon the Word of Guru.

column2: ਭਾਣਾ ਭਾਵੈ ਖਸਮ ਕਾ ਨਿਹਚਉ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ॥
column5: The will of the Master comes to be loved and in all thoughts permeates the formless Lord.

column2: ਇਸਕ ਮੁਸਕ ਮਹਕਾਰ ਹੈ ਹੁਇ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰੀ॥
column5: As love and fragrance do not remain hidden, the Gurmukh also does not remain concealed and gets himself busy in altruistic activities.

column2: ਸਿਦਕ ਸਬੂਰੀ ਸਾਬਤੇ ਮਸਤੀ ਹੁਸੀਆਰੀ॥
column5: He imbibes in him the faith, contentment, ecstasy, and the qualities of being skilful.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ ਜਿਣਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰੀ॥੩॥
column5: Gurmukh decimates ego and conquers it.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੪. ਸਿੱਖ ਦੀ ਕਰਨੀ}
column5: Conduct of a Sikh

column2: ਭਾਇ ਭਗਤਿ ਭੈ ਚਲਣਾ ਹੋਇ ਪਾਹੁਣਿਚਾਰੀ॥
column5: Considering himself a guest, the sikh spends his life in loving devotion.

column2: ਚਲਣੁ ਜਾਣਿ ਅਜਾਣ ਹੋਇ ਗਹੁ ਗਰਬੁ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ॥
column5: They (Sikhs) remain unknown to deceit and pull out ego from their minds.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਨਿਤ ਪਰਾਹੁਣੇ ਇਹ ਕਰਣੀ ਸਾਰੀ॥
column5: Their true conduct is to treat themselves as guests in this world.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੇਵ ਕਮਾਵਣੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਪਿਆਰੀ॥
column5: The object of the Gurmukh is service and only such action is loved by the Lord.

column2: ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵਲੀਣ ਹੋਇ ਪਰਵਾਰ ਸੁਧਾਰੀ॥
column5: Merging consciousness in the Word they reform the whole family (in the form of world).

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਜਾਇ ਸਹਜ ਘਰਿ ਨਿਰਮਲਿ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ॥੪॥
column5: Through holy congregation they become pure and formless and get established in the final stage of equipoise.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੫. ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਦੀ ਆਤਮ ਖੇਡ}
column5: The spiritual sport of the gursikh

column2: ਪਰਮ ਜੋਤਿ ਪਰਗਾਸੁ ਕਰਿ ਉਨਮਨਿ ਲਿਵਲਾਈ॥
column5: Kindling the supreme light in his mind a gurmukh remains absorbed in the state of supreme trance.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਤਤੁ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ਕਰਿ ਅਨਹਦਿ ਧੁਨਿ ਵਾਈ॥
column5: When he adopts the supreme reality (Lord) in his mind, the unstruck melody starts ringing.

column2: ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਪਰਬੋਧ ਕਰਿ ਪਰਮਾਤਮ ਹਾਈ॥
column5: Becoming conscious for altruism now resides in his heart the sense of God’s omnipresence.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਅਵੇਸੁ ਕਰਿ ਅਨਭਉ ਪਦ ਪਾਈ॥
column5: Inspired by the teachings of the Guru, gurmukh attains the state of fearlessness.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਧਨਾ ਇਕ ਮਨਿ ਇਕੁ ਧਿਆਈ॥
column5: Disciplining himself in the company in the holy ones i.e. losing his ego, he remembers Lord with single-minded devotion.

column2: ਵੀਹ ਇਕੀਹ ਚੜ੍ਹਾਉ ਚੜ੍ਹਿ ਇਉਂ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਈ॥੫॥
column5: This way, entering from this world into the spiritual world, he finally establishes himself in his real nature.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੬. ਨਿਜ ਸਰੂਪਕ ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਵੵਾਪਕਤਾ ਦਾ ਅਨਭੈ}
column5: Realization of the all-pervasiveness of the wondrous Guru

column2: ਦਰਪਣਿ ਵਾਂਗਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਰਿ ਆਪੁ ਆਪ ਨਿਹਾਲੈ॥
column5: As is the reflection in the mirror. He sees in the world His ownself.

column2: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਹੈ ਚੰਦੁ ਜਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਭਾਲੈ॥
column5: That perfect Lord is there in all the selves; the ignorant individual searches Him outside as the moon sees its own reflection in the water and feels it is there.

column2: ਗੋਰਸੁ ਗਾਈ ਵੇਖਦਾ ਘਿਉ ਦੁਧ ਵਿਚਾਲੈ॥
column5: Lord Himself is there in the milk, cow and ghee.

column2: ਫੁਲਾਂ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵਾਸੁ ਲੈ ਫਲੁ ਸਾਉ ਸਮ੍ਹਾਲੈ॥
column5: Taking fragrance from the flowers He Himself is the flavour in them.

column2: ਕਾਸਟਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਚਲਿਤੁ ਵੇਖਿ ਜਲ ਧਰਤਿ ਹਿਆਲੈ॥
column5: His own phenomenon is there in wood, fire, water, earth and snow.

column2: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਪੂਰਨੁ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵੇਖਾਲੈ॥੬॥
column5: The perfect Lord resides in all the selves and is visualised by a rare gurmukh.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੭. ਐਸੇ ਸਿੱਖ ਦੀ ਰਹਿਣੀ}
column5: Conduct of a Sikh of the Guru

column2: ਦਿਬ ਦਿਸਟਿ ਗੁਰ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਰਿ ਸਿਖ ਵਿਰਲਾ ਕੋਈ॥
column5: Rare is the gurmukh who concentrates upon the Guru and attains the divine sight.

column2: ਰਤਨ ਪਾਰਖੂ ਹੋਇ ਕੈ ਰਤਨਾ ਅਵਲੋਈ॥
column5: He is the jeweller having the capacity of testing as well as keeping the jewels in the from of virtues.

column2: ਮਨੁ ਮਾਣਕੁ ਨਿਰਮੋਲਕਾ ਸਤਿਸੰਗਿ ਪਰੋਈ॥
column5: His mind becomes pure like ruby and he remains absorbed in the holy congregation.

column2: ਰਤਨਮਾਲ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਜਗਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਣ ਗੋਈ॥
column5: His mind becomes pure like ruby and he remains absorbed in the holy congregation.

column2: ਜੀਵਦਿਆਂ ਮਰਿ ਅਮਰੁ ਹੋਇ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮੋਈ॥
column5: He is dead while being alive i.e. he turns his face from evil propensities.

column2: ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਜਾਣੈ ਜਾਣੋਈ॥੭॥
column5: Totally merging himself in the supreme light he understands his self as well as the Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੮. ਐਸਾ ਸਿੱਖ ਗੁਰੂ ਨਾਲ ਅਭੇਦ}
column5: Indenticalness of the Sikh with the Guru

column2: ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਣ ਗਹਿਰ ਗੰਭੀਰਾ॥
column5: Elated in the music and sound (of the word), the disciple of the Guru becomes full of serene qualities.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵਲੀਣ ਹੋਇ ਅਨਹਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਧੀਰਾ॥
column5: His consciousness gets merged into the Word and his mind stabilizes in the unstruck melody.

column2: ਜੰਤ੍ਰ੍ਰੀ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ੍ਰ ਵਜਾਇਦਾ ਮਨਿ ਉਨਮਨਿ ਚੀਰਾ॥
column5: The Guru plays upon the instrument of sermon, listening to which the mind dones clothes of the highest state of equipoise (to dance before the Lord).

column2: ਵਜਿ ਵਜਾਇ ਸਮਾਇ ਲੈ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦ ਵਜੀਰਾ॥
column5: The Sikh of the Guru, getting attuned to the instrument of teaching ultimately turns out himself to be a player of the Guru Word.

column2: ਅੰਤਰਿਜਾਮੀ ਜਾਣੀਐ ਅੰਤਰਿਗਤਿ ਪੀਰਾ॥
column5: Now the omniscient Lord understands his pangs of separation.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਚੇਲਾ ਚੇਲਾ ਗੁਰੂ ਬੇਧਿ ਹੀਰੈ ਹੀਰਾ॥੮॥
column5: The disciple transforms into Guru and the guru into disciple in the same manner, as the diamond cutter in fact is also a diamond.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੯. ਤਥਾਚ}
column5: Indenticalness of the Sikh with the Guru

column2: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਹੋਇਆ ਪਾਰਸਹੁਂ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਡਿਆਈ॥
column5: The greatness of the gurmukh is that he being the philosopher’s stone makes every one a philosopher’s stone.

column2: ਹੀਰੈ ਹੀਰਾ ਬੇਧਿਆ ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਈ॥
column5: As the diamond is cut by the diamond, the light of the gurmukh merges in Supreme Light.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ੍ਰੀ ਵਾਈ॥
column5: His consciousness is attuned to the Word as the mind of the player absorbs in the instrument.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਚੇਲਾ ਚੇਲਾ ਗੁਰੂ ਪਰਚਾ ਪਰਚਾਈ॥
column5: Now the disciple and the Guru become identical. They become one and merge in one another.

column2: ਪੁਰਖਹੁੰ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ਪੁਰਖੋਤਮ ਹਾਈ॥
column5: From man was born man (from Guru Nanak to Guru Angad) and he became the superior man.

column2: ਵੀਹ ਇਕੀਹ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਕੈ ਹੋਇ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਈ॥੮॥
column5: Crossing the world with one jump he merged in the innate knowledge.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੦. ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਤ ਸਚਖੰਡ}
column5: Holy congregation, abode the truth -

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦੇਖਦੋ ਪਰਮਾਤਮੁ ਦੇਖੈ॥
column5: He who beholds the true Guru has beholden the Lord.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵਲੀਣ ਹੋਇ ਅੰਤਰਿਗਤਿ ਲੇਖੈ॥
column5: Putting his consciousness into the Word he concentrates upon his self.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਕਵਲ ਦੀ ਵਾਸਨਾ ਹੋਇ ਚੰਦਨ ਭੇਖੈ॥
column5: Enjoying the fragrance of the lotus feet of the Guru he transforms himself into sandal.

column2: ਚਰਣੋਦਕ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਵਿਸੇਖੈ॥
column5: Tasting the nectar of the lotus feet he goes into a special wondrous state (of super consciousness).

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਚਿਤੁ ਕਰਿ ਵਿਚਿ ਰੂਪ ਨ ਰੇਖੈ॥
column5: Now in consonance with the Gurmat, the wisdom of the Guru, he stabilizing the mind goes beyond the boundaries of forms and figures.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਚਖੰਡਿ ਜਾਇ ਹੋਇ ਅਲਖ ਅਲੇਖੈ॥੧੦॥
column5: Reaching the holy congregation, the abode of truth, he himself becomes like that imperceptible and ineffable Lord

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੧. ਸਾਖੀ ਅਵਸਥਾ}
column5: Witness state

column2: ਅਖੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਦੇਖਦਾ ਦਰਸਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਦਿਸੈ॥
column5: He who sees from inside the eyes, is in fact beholden outside also.

column2: ਸਬਦੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ਸੁਰਤੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਰਿਸੈ॥
column5: He is described through words and He is illumined in the consciousness.

column2: ਚਰਣ ਕਵਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਾਸਨਾ ਮਨੁ ਭਵਰੁ ਸਲਿਸੈ॥
column5: For the fragrance of the lotus feet of the Guru, the mind, becoming the black bee, enjoys the pleasure.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੰਜੋਗੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਵਿਜੋਗਿ ਨ ਕਿਸੈ॥
column5: Whatever is attained in the holy congregation, he gets not away from it.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਚਿਤੁ ਹੈ ਚਿਤੁ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਜਿਸੈ॥
column5: By putting the mind into the teachings of the Guru, the mind itself changes according to the wisdom of the Guru.

column2: ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਪੂਰਣ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੂ ਹੈ ਤਿਸੈ॥੧੧॥
column5: The true Guru is the form of that transcendental Brahm who is beyond all the qualities.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੨. ਈਸ਼੍ਵਰੀਯ ਗੁਣ}
column5: Godly Virtues

column2: ਅਖੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਦਿਸਟਿ ਹੋਇ ਨਕਿ ਸਾਹੁ ਸੰਜੋਈ॥
column5: He is sight in the eyes and breath in the nostril.

column2: ਕੰਨਾਂ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੋਇ ਜੀਭ ਸਾਦੁ ਸਮੋਈ॥
column5: He is consciousness in the ears and taste in the tongue.

column2: ਹਥੀ ਕਿਰਤਿ ਕਮਾਵਣੀ ਪੈਰ ਪੰਥੁ ਸਥੋਈ॥
column5: With hands He works and becomes fellow traveller on the path.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਮਤਿ ਸਬਦਿ ਵਿਲੋਈ॥
column5: The gurmukh has attained the fruit of delight after churning the Word with consciousness.

column2: ਪਰਕਿਰਤੀ ਹੂੰ ਬਾਹਰਾ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਵਿਰਲੋਈ॥
column5: Any rare gurmukh remains away from the effects of maya.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਚੰਨਣ ਬਿਰਖੁ ਮਿਲਿ ਚੰਨਣ ਹੋਈ॥੧੨॥
column5: The holy congregation is a sandal tree to which whosoever becomes sandal

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੩. ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਮੰਤ੍ਰ}
column5: Wahiguru mantar

column2: ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਦੀ ਕਿਉ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਏ॥
column5: How is the dynamism of the Unmanifest known?

column2: ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਹੈ ਅਕਥ ਦੀ ਕਿਉ ਆਖਿ ਸੁਣਾਏ॥
column5: How can the story of that ineffable Lord be told?

column2: ਅਚਰਜ ਨੋ ਆਚਰਜ ਹੈ ਹੈਰਾਣ ਕਰਾਏ॥
column5: He is wonderful for the wonder itself.

column2: ਵਿਸਮਾਦੇ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਹੈ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਸਮਾਏ॥
column5: The absorbents in the wondrous realization get themselves elated.

column2: ਵੇਦੁ ਨ ਜਾਣੈ ਭੇਦੁ ਕਿਹੁ ਸੇਸਨਾਗੁ ਨ ਪਾਏ॥
column5: The Vedas also do not understand this mystery and even the Sesanag (mythological snake having thousand hoods) cannot know its limits.

column2: ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਸਾਲਾਹਣਾ ਗੁਰੁਸਬਦ ਅਲਾਏ॥੧੩॥
column5: Vahiguru, God, is eulogised through recitation of the Word of the Guru, Gurbani.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੪. ਗੁਰਮੁਖ ਗੁਣ}
column5: Virtues in a Gurmukh

column2: ਲੀਹਾ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਚਲੀਐ ਜਿਉ ਗਾਡੀ ਰਾਹੁ॥
column5: As, a coach on the highway goes through the beaten tracks,

column2: ਹੁਕਮਿ ਰਜਾਈ ਚਲਣਾ ਸਾਧਸੰਗੁ ਨਿਬਾਹੁ॥
column5: In the holy congregation one goes on abiding with the divine ordinance (hukam) and will of the Lord.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਧਨ ਸੋਘਾ ਰਖਦਾ ਘਰਿ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਾਹੁ॥
column5: As, the wise person keeps money intact at home

column2: ਜਿਉ ਮਿਰਜਾਦ ਨ ਛਡਈ ਸਾਇਰੁ ਅਸਗਾਹੁ॥
column5: And the deep ocean leaves not its general nature;

column2: ਲਤਾ ਹੇਠਿ ਲਤਾੜੀਐ ਅਜਰਾਵਰ ਘਾਹੁ॥
column5: As the grass is trampled under the feet,

column2: ਧਰਮਸਾਲ ਹੈ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਹੰਸ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਵਾਹੁ॥
column5: Like this (earth) inn is the Manasarovar and the disciples of the Guru are swans

column2: ਰਤਨ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦੁ ਕਰਿ ਕੀਰਤਨੁ ਖਾਹੁ॥੧੪॥
column5: Who in the form of kirtan, singing of the holy hymns, eat the pearls of the Word of the Guru.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੫. ਚੰਨਣਾਦਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਸ਼ਟਾਂਤ}
column5: Example of sandal

column2: ਚੰਨਣੁ ਜਿਉ ਵਣਖੰਡ ਵਿਚਿ ਓਹੁ ਆਪੁ ਲੁਕਾਏ॥
column5: As the sandal tree tries to conceal itself in the forest (but cannot remain hidden),

column2: ਪਾਰਸੁ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਪਰਬਤਾ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਪਤ ਵਲਾਏ॥
column5: The philosopher’s stone being identical with ordinary stones in the mountains spends its time in hiding.

column2: ਸਤ ਸਮੁੰਦੀ ਮਾਨਸਰੁ ਨਹਿ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਏ॥
column5: The seven seas are manifest but the Manasarovar remains invisible to common eyes.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਪਰਛਿੰਨਾ ਪਾਰਜਾਤ ਨਹਿ ਪਰਗਟੀ ਆਏ॥
column5: As parijat, wish fulfilling tree, also keeps itself unseen;

column2: ਜਿਉ ਜਗਿ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਕਾਮਧੇਨੁ ਨਹਿ ਆਪ ਜਣਾਏ॥
column5: Kamaddhenu, wish fulfilling cow, also lives in this world but never makes itself noticed.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਾ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਲੈ ਕਿਉ ਆਪੁ ਗਣਾਏ॥
column5: Likewise why should they who have adopted the teachings of the true Guru, include themselves in any count.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੬. ਗੁਰ ਚੇਲਾ, ਚੇਲਾ ਗੁਰੂ}
column5: Guru disciple, disciple Guru ,

column2: ਦੁਇ ਦੁਇ ਅਖੀ ਆਖੀਅਨਿ ਇਕ ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦਿਸੈ॥
column5: The eyes are two but they behold the one (Lord).

column2: ਦੁਇ ਦੁਇ ਕੰਨ ਵਖਾਣੀਅਨਿ ਇਕ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਲਿਸੈ॥
column5: The ears are two but they bring out the one consciousness.

column2: ਦੁਇ ਦੁਇ ਨਦੀ ਕਿਨਾਰਿਆਂ ਪਾਰਾਵਾਰੁ ਨ ਤਿਸੈ॥
column5: The river has two banks but they are one through the connection of water and are not separate.

column2: ਇਕ ਜੋਤਿ ਦੁਇ ਮੂਰਤੀ ਇਕ ਸਬਦ ਸਰਿਸੈ॥
column5: The Guru and the disciple are two identities but one shabad, Word permeates through both of them.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਚੇਲਾ ਚੇਲਾ ਗੁਰੂ ਸਮਝਾਏ ਕਿਸੈ॥੧੬॥
column5: When the Guru is disciple and the disciple Guru, who can make the other understand.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੭. ਗੁਰੂ ਚੇਲੇ ਦਾ ਕੰਮ}
column5: Activity of the disciple Guru

column2: ਪਹਿਲੇ ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਦੇ ਸਿਖ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਏ॥
column5: First the Guru making the disciple sit near his feet preaches to him.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਧਰਮਸਾਲ ਸਿਖ ਸੇਵਾ ਲਾਏ॥
column5: Telling him about the distinction of the holy congregation and the abode of dharma, he is put into the service (of the mankind).

column2: ਭਾਇ ਭਗਤਿ ਭੈ ਸੇਵਦੇ ਗੁਰਪੁਰਬ ਕਰਾਏ॥
column5: Serving through loving devotion, the servants of the Lord celebrate the anniversaries.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਕੀਰਤਨੁ ਸਚਿ ਮੇਲਿ ਮਿਲਾਏ॥
column5: Attuning the consciousness with the Word, through the singing of hymns, one meets the truth.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗੁ ਸਚ ਦਾ ਸਚੁ ਪਾਰ ਲੰਘਾਏ॥
column5: The Gurmukh walks the path of Truth; practicing Truth he crosses the Worldly ocean.

column2: ਸਚਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਸਚਿਆਰ ਨੋ ਮਿਲਿ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਏ॥੧੭॥
column5: Thus the truthful one obtains the truth and getting it, the ego is erased.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੮. ਚਰਨਾਂ ਤੋਂ ਨਿੰਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਦਾ ਉਪਦੇਸ਼}
column5: Learning humility from feet

column2: ਸਿਰ ਉਚਾ ਨੀਵੇ ਚਰਣ ਸਿਰਿ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: The head is high and the feet are at a low level but still the head bows upon the feet.

column2: ਮੁਹੁ ਅਖੀ ਨਕ ਕੰਨ ਹਥ ਦੇਹ ਭਾਰ ਉਚਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: The feet carry the burden of mouth, eyes, nose, ears, hands and the whole body.

column2: ਸਭ ਚਿਹਨ ਛਡਿ ਪੂਜੀਅਨਿ ਕਉਣੁ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: Then, leaving aside all the body organs, only they (feet) are worshipped.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਰਣੀ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤੀ ਨਿਤ ਚਲਿ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: They daily go to the holy congregation in the shelter of the Guru.

column2: ਵਤਨਿ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਨੋ ਕਰਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਵਸਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: Then they run for the altruistic works and accomplish the work to the maximum possible.

column2: ਮੇਰੀ ਖਲਹੁੰ ਮੌਜੜੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਹੰਢਾਂਦੇ॥
column5: Alas! Were it so that shoes made of my skin were used by the Sikhs of the Guru.

column2: ਮਸਤਕ ਲਗੇ ਸਾਧ ਰੇਣੁ ਵਡਭਾਗ ਜਿਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਦੇ॥੧੮॥
column5: Whosoever gets the dust of the feet of such people (with above virtues) he is fortunate and a blessed one.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੯. ਧਰਤੀ ਤੋਂ ਉਪਦੇਸ਼}
column5: Learning from the earth

column2: ਜਿਉ ਧਰਤੀ ਧੀਰਜ ਧਰਮਸਾਲ ਮਸਕੀਨੀ ਮੂੜੀ॥
column5: As the earth is embodiment of continence, dharma and humility,

column2: ਸਭਦੂੰ ਨੀਵੀਂ ਹੋਇ ਰਹੀ ਤਿਸ ਮਣੀ ਨ ਕੂੜੀ॥
column5: It remains under the feet and this humility is true and not false.

column2: ਕੋਈ ਹਰਿਮੰਦਰੁ ਕਰੈ ਕੋ ਕਰੈ ਅਰੂੜੀ॥
column5: Somebody constructs temple of god on it and some gather rubbish heaps on it.

column2: ਜੇਹਾ ਬੀਜੈ ਸੋ ਲੁਣੈ ਫਲ ਅੰਬ ਲਸੂੜੀ॥
column5: Whatever is sowed is got accordingly whether is be a mango or lasuri, a glutinous fruit.

column2: ਜੀਵਦਿਆਂ ਮਰਿ ਜੀਵਣਾ ਜੁੜਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜੂੜੀ॥
column5: While being dead in life i.e. deleting ego from the self, the gurmukhs join gurmukhs in the holy congregation.

column2: ਲਤਾਂ ਹੇਠਿ ਲਤਾੜੀਐ ਗਤਿ ਸਾਧਾਂ ਧੂੜੀ॥੧੯॥
column5: They become the dust of the feet of the holy men, which is trampled under the feet.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੦. ਜਲ ਤੋਂ ਉਪਦੇਸ਼}
column5: Learning from water

column2: ਜਿਉ ਪਾਣੀ ਨਿਵਿ ਚਲਦਾ ਨੀਵਾਣਿ ਚਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: As water flows downwards and takes with it whosoever meets it (and makes it also humble),

column2: ਸਭਨਾ ਰੰਗਾਂ ਨੋ ਮਿਲੈ ਰਲਿ ਜਾਇ ਰਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: All the dyes mix up in water and it becomes one with every colour;

column2: ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਕਮਾਵਦਾ ਉਨਿ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ॥
column5: Erasing ego it does altruistic deeds;

column2: ਕਾਠੁ ਨ ਡੋਬੈ ਪਾਲਿ ਕੈ ਸੰਗਿ ਲੋਹੁ ਤਰਾਇਆ॥
column5: It does not sink the wood, it rather makes the iron swim with it;

column2: ਵੁਠੇ ਮੀਹ ਸੁਕਾਲੁ ਹੋਇ ਰਸ ਕਸ ਉਪਜਾਇਆ॥
column5: It makes for prosperity when it rains in rainy season.

column2: ਜੀਵਦਿਆ ਮਰਿ ਸਾਧ ਹੋਇ ਸਫਲਿਓ ਜਗਿ ਆਇਆ॥੨੦॥
column5: Likewise, the holy saints getting dead in life i.e. removing their ego, making their coming to the world fruitful.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੧. ਬ੍ਰਿੱਛ ਤੋਂ ਉਪਦੇਸ਼}
column5: Learning from tree

column2: ਸਿਰ ਤਲਵਾਇਆ ਜੰਮਿਆ ਹੋਇ ਅਚਲੁ ਨ ਚਲਿਆ॥
column5: With feet upward and head down, the tree gets rooted and stands unmoved.

column2: ਪਾਣੀ ਪਾਲਾ ਧੁਪ ਸਹਿ ਉਹ ਤਪਹੁ ਨ ਟਲਿਆ॥
column5: It bears with water, cold and sunshine but does not turn its face from self-mortification.

column2: ਸਫਲਿਓ ਬਿਰਖ ਸੁਹਾਵੜਾ ਫਲ ਸੁਫਲੁ ਸੁਫਲਿਆ॥
column5: Such a tree is blessed one and becomes full of fruit.

column2: ਫਲ ਦੇਇ ਵੱਟ ਵਗਾਇਐ ਕਰਵਤਿ ਨ ਹਲਿਆ॥
column5: On stoning, it gives fruit and does not stir even under the sawing machine.

column2: ਬੁਰੇ ਕਰਨਿ ਬੁਰਿਆਈਆ ਭਲਿਆਈ ਭਲਿਆ॥
column5: The wicked go on doing evil deeds whereas the gentle remain busy in good activities.

column2: ਅਵਗੁਣ ਕੀਤੇ ਗੁਣ ਕਰਨਿ ਜਗਿ ਸਾਧ ਵਿਰਲਿਆ॥
column5: Rare are the people in world who with their saintly heart do good to the evil.

column2: ਅਉਸਰਿ ਆਪ ਛਲਾਇਂਦਾ ਤਿਨਾ ਅਉਸਰੁ ਛਲਿਆ॥੨੧॥
column5: The commoners are duped by time i.e. they change according to the time, but the holy men succeed in deluding the time i.e. they remain free from the influence of time.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨੨. ਮੁਰੀਦ ਲੱਛਣ}
column5: Virtues of a servant

column2: ਮੁਰਦਾ ਹੋਇ ਮੁਰੀਦੁ ਸੋ ਗੁਰ ਗੋਰਿ ਸਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: The disciple who remains dead (among hopes and desires) will ultimately enter into the grave of the Guru i.e. he will transform himself into Guru.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਓਹੁ ਆਪੁ ਗਵਾਵੈ॥
column5: He merges his consciousness in the Word and loses his ego.

column2: ਤਨ ਧਰਤੀ ਕਰਿ ਧਰਮਸਾਲ ਮਨੁ ਦਭੁ ਵਿਛਾਵੈ॥
column5: Accepting body in the form of earth as the resting place, he spreads mat of mind over it.

column2: ਲਤਾਂ ਹੇਠਿ ਲਤਾੜੀਐ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦੁ ਕਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: Even if he gets trampled under the feet, he conducts himself according to the teachings of the Guru.

column2: ਭਾਇ ਭਗਤਿ ਨੀਵਾਣੁ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਠਹਰਾਵੈ॥
column5: Getting imbued with the loving devotion, he becomes humble and stabilises his mind.

column2: ਵਰਸੈ ਨਿਝਰ ਧਾਰ ਹੋਇ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਚਲਿ ਆਵੈ॥੨੨॥੯॥
column5: He himself moves towards the holy congregation and the grace of the Lord showers upon him.

column2: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ॥
column5: One Oankar, the primal energy, realized through the grace of the divine preceptor

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧. ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਧ੍ਰੂ ਭਗਤ ਜੀ}
column5: One Oankar, the primal energy, realized through the grace of the divine preceptor

column2: ਧ੍ਰੂ ਹਸਦਾ ਘਰਿ ਆਇਆ; ਕਰਿ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਪਿਉ ਕੁਛੜਿ ਲੀਤਾ॥
column5: Boy Dhru came smiling to his house (palace) and his father full of love put him into his lap.

column2: ਬਾਹਹੁ ਪਕੜਿ ਉਠਾਲਿਆ; ਮਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਰੋਸੁ ਮਤ੍ਰੇਈ ਕੀਤਾ॥
column5: Seeing this, the stepmother got angry and catching hold of his arm pushed him out of the lap of the father (the king).

column2: ਡੁਡਹੁਲਿਕਾ ਮਾਂ ਪੁਛੈ; ਤੂੰ ਸਾਵਾਣੀ ਹੈ ਕਿ ਸੁਰੀਤਾ॥
column5: Tearful with fear he asked his mother whether she was a queen or a maidservant?

column2: ਸਾਵਾਣੀ ਹਾਂ ਜਨਮ ਦੀ; ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਭਗਤੀ ਕਰਮਿ ਦ੍ਰਿੜ੍ਹੀਤਾ॥
column5: O son! (said she) I was born queen but I did not remember God and did not undertake acts of devotion (and this is the reason of yours and mine plight).

column2: ਕਿਸੁ ਉਦਮ ਤੇ ਰਾਜੁ ਮਿਲੈ; ਸਤ੍ਰੂ ਤੇ ਸਭ ਹੋਵਨਿ ਮੀਤਾ॥
column5: With that effort can the kingdom be had (asked Dhru) and how can enemies turn friends?

column2: ਪਰਮੇਸਰੁ ਆਰਾਧੀਐ; ਜਿਦੂ ਹੋਈਐ ਪਤਿਤ ਪੁਨੀਤਾ॥
column5: The Lord should be worshipped and thus the sinners also become sacred ones (said the mother).

column2: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਚਲਿਆ ਕਰਣਿ ਤਪੁ; ਮਨ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਹੋਇ ਅਤੀਤਾ॥
column5: Listening to this and getting totally detached in his mind Dhru went out (to the jungle) to undertake rigorous discipline.

column2: ਨਾਰਦ ਮੁਨਿ ਉਪਦੇਸਿਆ; ਨਾਮ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਅਮਿਓ ਰਸੁ ਪੀਤਾ॥
column5: On the way, sage Narad taught him the technique of devotion and Dhru quaffed the nectar from the ocean of the Name of the Lord.

column2: ਪਿਛਹੁ ਰਾਜੇ ਸਦਿਆ; ਅਬਿਚਲ ਰਾਜ ਕਰਹੁ ਨਿਤ ਨੀਤਾ॥
column5: (After some time) King (Uttanpad) called him back and asked him (Dhru) to rule forever.

column2: ਹਾਰਿ ਚਲੇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਗ ਜੀਤਾ॥੧॥
column5: The gurmukhs who seem to be losing i.e. who turn their faces from the evil propensities, conquer the world.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੨. ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਹਲਾਦ ਭਗਤ}
column5: Prahlad

column2: ਘਰਿ ਹਰਣਾਖਸ ਦੈਤ ਦੇ; ਕਲਰਿ ਕਵਲੁ ਭਗਤੁ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦੁ॥
column5: Prahlad, the saint, was born in the house of demon (king) Haranakhas like a lotus is born in the alkaline (barren) land.

column2: ਪੜ੍ਹਨ ਪਠਾਇਆ ਚਾਟਸਾਲ; ਪਾਂਧੈ ਚਿਤਿ ਹੋਆ ਅਹਿਲਾਦੁ॥
column5: When he was sent to seminary, the brahmin purohit became elated (because the king’s son was now his disciple).

column2: ਸਿਮਰੈ ਮਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ; ਗਾਵੈ ਸਬਦੁ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਨਾਦੁ॥
column5: Prahlad would remember the name of Ram in his heart and outwardly also he would eulogise the Lord.

column2: ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਨਿ ਸਭ ਚਾਟੜੈ; ਪਾਂਧੇ ਹੋਇ ਰਹੇ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ॥
column5: Now all the disciples became devotees of Lord, which was an awful and embarrassing situation for all the teachers.

column2: ਰਾਜੇ ਪਾਸਿ ਰੂਆਇਆ; ਦੋਖੀ ਦੈਤਿ ਵਧਾਇਆ ਵਾਦੁ॥
column5: The priest (teacher) reported or complained to the king (that O king your son has become devotee of God).

column2: ਜਲ ਅਗਨੀ ਵਿਚਿ ਘਤਿਆ; ਜਲੈ ਨ ਡੁਬੈ ਗੁਰਪਰਸਾਦਿ॥
column5: The malevolent demon picked up the quarrel. Prahlad was thrown into fire and water but with the grace of Guru (the Lord) neither he was burnt nor drowned.

column2: ਕਢਿ ਖੜਗੁ ਸਦਿ ਪੁਛਿਆ; ਕਉਣੁ ਸੁ ਤੇਰਾ ਹੈ ਉਸਤਾਦੁ॥
column5: Angered as he was, Hiranyaksyapu took out his double-edged sword and asked Prahlad who his Guru (Lord) was.

column2: ਥੰਮ੍ਹੁ ਪਾੜਿ ਪਰਗਟਿਆ; ਨਰਸਿੰਘ ਰੂਪ ਅਨੂਪ ਅਨਾਦਿ॥
column5: At the same moment Lord God in the form of man-lion came out of pillar. His form was grand and majestic.

column2: ਬੇਮੁਖ ਪਕੜਿ ਪਛਾੜਿਅਨੁ; ਸੰਤ ਸਹਾਈ ਆਦਿ ਜੁਗਾਦਿ॥
column5: That wicked demon was thrown down and killed and thus it was proved that the Lord is kind to devotees since the time immemorial.

column2: ਜੈ ਜੈਕਾਰ ਕਰਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿ॥੨॥
column5: Seeing this Brahma and other gods started eulogising the Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੩. ਰਾਜਾ ਬਲਿ}
column5: Bali, the king

column2: ਬਲਿ ਰਾਜਾ ਘਰਿ ਆਪਣੈ; ਅੰਦਰਿ ਬੈਠਾ ਜਗੁ ਕਰਾਵੈ॥
column5: Bali, the king, was busy in performing a yajna in his palace.

column2: ਬਾਵਨ ਰੂਪੀ ਆਇਆ; ਚਾਰਿ ਵੇਦ ਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਠ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ॥
column5: A low stature dwarf in the form of brahmin came there reciting all the four Vedas.

column2: ਰਾਜੇ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਦਿਆ; ਮੰਗ ਸੁਆਮੀ ਜੋ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: The king after calling him in asked him to demand anything he liked.

column2: ਅਛਲੁ ਛਲਣਿ ਤੁਧੁ ਆਇਆ; ਸੁਕ੍ਰ ਪਰੋਹਿਤੁ ਕਹਿ ਸਮਝਾਵੈ॥
column5: Immediately priest Sukracharya made the king (Bali) understand that he (the beggar) is undeceivable God and He had came to delude him.

column2: ਕਰਉ ਅਢਾਈ ਧਰਤਿ ਮੰਗਿ; ਪਿਛਹੁ ਦੇ ਤ੍ਰਿਹੁ ਲੋਅ ਨ ਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: The dwarf demanded two and half steps length of earth (which was granted by the king).

column2: ਦੁਇ ਕਰਵਾਂ ਕਰਿ ਤਿੰਨ ਲੋਅ; ਬਲਿ ਰਾਜਾ ਲੈ ਮਗਰੁ ਮਿਣਾਵੈ॥
column5: Then the dwarf expanded his body so much that now the three worlds were insufficient for him.

column2: ਬਲਿ ਛਲਿ ਆਪੁ ਛਲਾਇਅਨੁ; ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਗਲਿ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: Even knowing this deception Bali allowed himself to be deceived so, and seeing this Vishnu embraced him.

column2: ਦਿਤਾ ਰਾਜੁ ਪਤਾਲ ਦਾ; ਹੋਇ ਅਧੀਨੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਵੈ॥
column5: When he covered the three worlds in two steps, for third half-step king Bali offered his own back.

column2: ਹੋਇ ਦਰਵਾਨ ਮਹਾਂ ਸੁਖੁ ਪਾਵੈ॥੩॥
column5: Bali was given the kingdom of the netherworld where surrendering to God he engaged himself in the loving devotion of the Lord. Vishnu was delighted to be the doorkeeper of Bali.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੪. ਅੰਬਰੀਕ ਭਗਤੀ}
column5: Ambaris

column2: ਅੰਬਰੀਕ ਮੁਹਿ ਵਰਤੁ ਹੈ; ਰਾਤਿ ਪਈ ਦੁਰਬਾਸਾ ਆਇਆ॥
column5: One evening while king Ambaris was fasting he was visited by sage Durvasa

column2: ਭੀੜਾ ਓਸੁ ਉਪਾਰਣਾ; ਓਹੁ ਉਠਿ ਨ੍ਹਾਵਣਿ ਨਦੀ ਸਿਧਾਇਆ॥
column5: The king was to break his fast while serving Durvasa but the rishi went to the riverbank to take a bath.

column2: ਚਰਣੋਦਕੁ ਲੈ ਪੋਖਿਆ; ਓਹੁ ਸਰਾਪੁ ਦੇਵਣ ਨੋ ਧਾਇਆ॥
column5: Fearing the change of date (which would deem his fast fruitless), the king broke his fast by drinking the water which he had poured on the feet of the rishi. When the rishi realised that the king had not served him first, he ran to curse the king.

column2: ਚਕ੍ਰ ਸੁਦਰਸਨੁ ਕਾਲ ਰੂਪ ਹੋਇ; ਭੀਹਾਵਲੁ ਗਰਬੁ ਗਵਾਇਆ॥
column5: On this, Vishnu ordered his death like disc to move towards Durvasa and thus the ego of Durvasa was removed.

column2: ਬਾਮ੍ਹਣੁ ਭੰਨਾ ਜੀਉ ਲੈ; ਰਖਿ ਨ ਹੰਘਨਿ ਦੇਵ ਸਬਾਇਆ॥
column5: Now Brahmin Durvasa ran for his life. Even the gods and deities could not afford him shelter.

column2: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਲੋਕੁ ਸਿਵ ਲੋਕੁ ਤਜਿ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਲੋਕੁ ਬੈਕੁੰਠ ਤਜਾਇਆ॥
column5: He was avoided in the abodes of Indra, Siva, Brahma and the heavens.

column2: ਦੇਵਤਿਆਂ ਭਗਵਾਨੁ ਸਣੁ ਸਿਖਿ ਦੇਇ ਸਭਨਾਂ ਸਮਝਾਇਆ॥
column5: Gods and God made him understand (that none except Ambaris could save him).

column2: ਆਇ ਪਇਆ ਸਰਣਾਗਤੀ; ਮਾਰੀਦਾ. ਅੰਬਰੀਕ ਛੁਡਾਇਆ॥
column5: Then he surrendered before Ambaris and Ambaris saved the dying sage.

column2: ਭਗਤਿ ਵਛਲੁ ਜਗਿ ਬਿਰਦ ਸਦਾਇਆ॥੪॥
column5: The Lord God came to be known in the world as benevolent to devotees.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੫. ਰਾਜਾ ਜਨਕ}
column5: King Janak

column2: ਭਗਤੁ ਵਡਾ ਰਾਜਾ ਜਨਕੁ ਹੈ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਵਿਚਿ ਉਦਾਸੀ॥
column5: King Janak was a great saint who amidst maya remained indifferent to it.

column2: ਦੇਵ ਲੋਕ ਨੋ ਚਲਿਆ; ਗਣ ਗੰਧਰਬ ਸਭਾ ਸੁਖਵਾਸੀ॥
column5: Along with gans and gandharvs (calestial musicians) he went to the abode of the gods.

column2: ਜਮਪੁਰਿ ਗਇਆ ਪੁਕਾਰ ਸੁਣਿ; ਵਿਲਲਾਵਨਿ ਜੀਅ ਨਰਕ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ॥
column5: From there, he, hearing the cries of inhabitants of hell, went to them.

column2: ਧਰਮਰਾਇ ਨੋ ਆਖਿਓਨੁ; ਸਭਨਾ ਦੀ ਕਰਿ ਬੰਦ ਖਲਾਸੀ॥
column5: He asked the god of death, Dharamrai, to relieve all their suffering.

column2: ਕਰੇ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਧਰਮਰਾਇ; ਹਉ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਠਾਕੁਰੁ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ॥
column5: Hearing this, the god of death told him he was a mere servant of the eternal Lord (and without His orders he could not liberate them).

column2: ਗਹਿਣੇ ਧਰਿਅਨੁ ਏਕ ਨਾਉ; ਪਾਪਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਕਰੈ ਨਿਰਜਾਸੀ॥
column5: Janak offered a part of his devotion and remembrance of the name of the Lord.

column2: ਪਾਸੰਗਿ ਪਾਪੁ ਨ ਪੁਜਨੀ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਉ ਅਤੁਲ ਨ ਤੁਲਾਸੀ॥
column5: All the sins of hell were found not equal even to the counterweight of balance.

column2: ਨਰਕਹੁ ਛੁਟੇ ਜੀਅ ਜੰਤ; ਕਟੀ ਗਲਹੁੰ ਸਿਲਕ ਜਮ ਫਾਸੀ॥
column5: In fact no balance can weigh the fruits of recitation and remembrance of the Lords name by the gurmukh.

column2: ਮੁਕਤਿ ਜੁਗਤਿ; ਨਾਵੈ ਦੀ ਦਾਸੀ॥੫॥
column5: All the creatures got liberated from hell and the noose of death was cut. Liberation and the technique of attaining it are the servants of the name of the Lord.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੬. ਹਰੀ ਚੰਦ ਤੇ ਤਾਰਾ ਰਾਣੀ}
column5: Harischandar and queen Taramati

column2: ਸੁਖੁ ਰਾਜੇ ਹਰੀ ਚੰਦ ਘਰਿ; ਨਾਰਿ ਸੁ ਤਾਰਾ ਲੋਚਨ ਰਾਣੀ॥
column5: King HariChand had a queen with beautiful eyes, Tara, who had made his home the abode of comforts.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਗਾਵਦੇ; ਰਾਤੀ ਜਾਇ ਸੁਣੈ ਗੁਰਬਾਣੀ॥
column5: At night she would go to the place where in the form of holy congregation, would recite the holy hymns.

column2: ਪਿਛੈ ਰਾਜਾ ਜਾਗਿਆ; ਅਧੀ ਰਾਤਿ ਨਿਖੰਡਿ ਵਿਹਾਣੀ॥
column5: After she had left, the King awoke in the middle of the night and realised she had gone.

column2: ਰਾਣੀ ਦਿਸਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ; ਮਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਵਰਤ ਗਈ ਹੈਰਾਣੀ॥
column5: He could not find the Queen anywhere and his heart filled with astonishment

column2: ਹੋਰਤੁ ਰਾਤੀ ਉਠਿ ਕੈ; ਚਲਿਆ ਪਿਛੈ ਤਰਲ ਜੁਆਣੀ॥
column5: The following night he followed the young queen.

column2: ਰਾਣੀ ਪਹੁਤੀ ਸੰਗਤੀ'; ਰਾਜੇ ਖੜੀ ਖੜਾਉ ਨੀਸਾਣੀ॥
column5: The queen reached the holy congregation and the King lifted one of her sandals from there (so that he could prove the infidelity of the queen).

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਆਰਾਧਿਆ; ਜੋੜੀ ਜੁੜੀ ਖੜਾਉ ਪੁਰਾਣੀ॥
column5: When about to go, the queen concentrated upon the holy congregation and the one sandal became a pair.

column2: ਰਾਜੇ ਡਿਠਾ ਚਲਿਤੁ ਇਹੁ; ਏਹ ਖੜਾਵ ਹੈ ਚੋਜ ਵਿਡਾਣੀ॥
column5: The king upheld this feat and realized that there her matching sandal was a miracle.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਵਿਟਹੁ ਕੁਰਬਾਣੀ॥੬॥
column5: I am sacrifice unto the holy congregation.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੭. ਬਿਦਰ ਅਤੇ ਦੁਰਜੋਧਨ}
column5: Vidur and Duryodhan

column2: ਆਇਆ ਸੁਣਿਆ ਬਿਦਰ ਦੇ; ਬੋਲੈ ਦੁਰਜੋਧਨੁ ਹੋਇ ਰੁਖਾ॥
column5: Hearing that Lord Krishan was served and stayed over at humble Bidar's home, Duryodhan remarked sarcastically.

column2: ਘਰਿ ਅਸਾਡੇ ਛਡਿ ਕੈ; ਗੋਲੇ ਦੇ ਘਰਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਕਿ ਸੁਖਾ॥
column5: Leaving our grand palaces, how much happiness and comfort did you attain in the home of a servant?

column2: ਭੀਖਮੁ ਦੋਣਾ ਕਰਣ ਤਜਿ; ਸਭਾ ਸੀਗਾਰ ਵਡੇ ਮਾਨੁਖਾ॥
column5: You gave up even Bhikhaum, Dohna and Karan who are recognised as great men who are adorned in all courts.

column2: ਝੁੰਗੀ ਜਾਇ ਵਲਾਇਓਨੁ; ਸਭਨਾ ਦੇ ਜੀਅ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਧੁਖਾ॥
column5: We have all been anguished to find that you have lived in a hut”.

column2: ਹਸਿ ਬੋਲੇ ਭਗਵਾਨ ਜੀ; ਸੁਣਿਹੋ ਰਾਜਾ ਹੋਇ ਸਨਮੁਖਾ॥
column5: Then smilingly, Lord Krishan asked the King to come forward and to listen carefully.

column2: ਤੇਰੇ ਭਾਉ ਨ ਦਿਸਈ; ਮੇਰੇ ਨਾਹੀ ਅਪਦਾ ਦੁਖਾ॥
column5: I see no love and devotion in you (and hence I have not come to you).

column2: ਭਾਉ ਜਿਵੇਹਾ ਬਿਦਰ ਦੇ; ਹੋਰੀ ਦੇ ਚਿਤਿ ਚਾਉ ਨ ਚੁਖਾ॥
column5: No heart I see has even a fraction of the love that Bidar bears in his heart.

column2: ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਭਾਉ ਭਗਤਿ ਦਾ ਭੁਖਾ॥੭॥
column5: The Lord needs loving devotion and nothing else.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੮. ਦ੍ਰੋਪਤੀ}
column5: Draupadi

column2: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਸਭਾ ਦੁਸਾਸਣੈ; ਮਥੇਵਾਲ ਦ੍ਰੋਪਤੀ ਆਂਦੀ॥
column5: Dragging Daropati by the hair, Dusasanai brought her into the assembly.

column2: ਦੂਤਾ ਨੋ ਫੁਰਮਾਇਆ; ਨੰਗੀ ਕਰਹੁ ਪੰਚਾਲੀ ਬਾਂਦੀ॥
column5: He commanded his men to strip the maidservant Dropati stark naked.

column2: ਪੰਜੇ ਪਾਂਡੋ ਵੇਖਦੇ; ਅਉਘਟਿ ਰੁਧੀ ਨਾਰਿ ਜਿਨਾ ਦੀ॥
column5: All the five Pandavs who she was the wife of, beheld this.

column2: ਅਖੀ ਮੀਟਿ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਰਿ; ਹਾਹਾ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਨ ਕਰੈ ਬਿਲਲਾਂਦੀ॥
column5: Crying, totally dejected and helpless, she closed her eyes. Single-mindedly she invoked Krishna for help.

column2: ਕਪੜ ਕੋਟੁ ਉਸਾਰਿਓਨੁ; ਥਕੇ ਦੂਤ ਨ ਪਾਰਿ ਵਸਾਂਦੀ॥
column5: The servants were taking off the clothes from her body but more layers of clothes formed a fort around her; the servants got tired but the layers of clothes were never ending.

column2: ਹਥ ਮਰੋੜਨਿ ਸਿਰੁ ਧੁਣਨਿ; ਪਛੋਤਾਨਿ ਕਰਨਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਜਾਂਦੀ॥
column5: The servants were now writhing and frustrated on their abortive attempt and felt that they themselves were ashamed.

column2: ਘਰਿ ਆਈ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਮਿਲੈ; ਪੈਜ ਰਹੀ ਬੋਲੇ ਸ਼ਰਮਾਂਦੀ॥
column5: On reaching home, Dropati was asked by Lord Krishna whether she was saved in the assembly.

column2: ਨਾਥ ਅਨਾਥਾਂ ਬਾਣਿ ਧੁਰਾਂਦੀ॥੮॥
column5: She shyly replied, “Since perennial times you are living up to your reputation of being father of the fatherless ones.”

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੯. ਸੁਦਾਮਾ ਭਗਤ}
column5: Sudama

column2: ਬਿਪ ਸੁਦਾਮਾ ਦਾਲਿਦੀ; ਬਾਲ ਸਖਾਈ ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਸਦਾਏ॥
column5: Sudama, a poor brahman, was known to be a friend of Krishna from childhood.

column2: ਲਾਗੂ ਹੋਈ ਬਾਮ੍ਹਣੀ; ਮਿਲਿ ਜਗਦੀਸ ਦਲਿਦ੍ਰ ਗਵਾਏ॥
column5: His brahmin wife always pestered him as to why he did not go to Lord Krishna to alleviate his poverty.

column2: ਚਲਿਆ ਗਣਦਾ ਗਟੀਆਂ; ਕਿਉ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਈਐ ਕਉਣੁ ਮਿਲਾਏ॥
column5: He was perplexed and pondered over how he could get re-introduced to Krishna, who could help him meet the Lord.

column2: ਪਹੁਤਾ ਨਗਰਿ ਦੁਆਰਕਾ; ਸਿੰਘ ਦੁਆਰਿ ਖਲੋਤਾ ਜਾਏ॥
column5: He reached the town of Duaraka and stood before the main gate (of the palace of Krishna).

column2: ਦੂਰਹੁੰ ਦੇਖਿ ਡੰਡਉਤ ਕਰਿ; ਛਡਿ ਸਿੰਘਾਸਣੁ ਹਰਿ ਜੀ ਆਏ॥
column5: Seeing him from a distance, Krishna, the Lord, bowed and leaving his throne came to Sudama.

column2: ਪਹਿਲੇ ਦੇ ਪਰਦਖਣਾ; ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈਕੇ ਲੈ ਗਲਿ ਲਾਏ॥
column5: First he circumambulated around Sudama and then touching his feet he embraced him.

column2: ਚਰਣੋਦਕੁ ਲੈ ਪੈਰ ਧੋਇ; ਸਿੰਘਾਸਣੁ ਉਤੇ ਬੈਠਾਏ॥
column5: Washing his feet he took that water and made Sudama sit on the throne.

column2: ਪੁਛੇ ਕੁਸਲੁ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਕਰਿ; ਗੁਰ ਸੇਵਾ ਦੀ ਕਥਾ ਸੁਣਾਏ॥
column5: Then Krishna lovingly enquired about his welfare and talked about the time when they were together in the service of the guru (Sandipani).

column2: ਲੈਕੇ ਤੰਦੁਲ ਚਬਿਓਨੁ; ਵਿਦਾ ਕਰੇ ਅਗੈ ਪਹੁਚਾਏ॥
column5: Krishna asked for the rice sent by Sudama’s wife and after eating, came out to see off his friend Sudama.

column2: ਚਾਰਿ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਸਕੁਚਿ ਪਠਾਏ॥੯॥
column5: Though all the four boons (righteousness,wealth,fulfillment of desire and libereation) were given to Sudama by Krishna, Krishna's humbleness still made him feel totally helpless.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੦. ਜੈਦੇਉ ਭਗਤ}
column5: Jaidev

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਜੈਦੇਉ; ਕਰਿ ਗੀਤ ਗੋਵਿੰਦ ਸਹਜ ਧੁਨਿ ਗਾਵੈ॥
column5: Getting immersed in the loving devotion, the devotee Jaidev would sing the songs of the Lord (Govind).

column2: ਲੀਲ੍ਹਾ ਚਲਿਤ ਵਖਾਣਦਾ; ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: He would describe the glorious feats accomplished by God and was greatly loved by him.

column2: ਅਖਰ ਇਕੁ ਨ ਆਵੜੈ; ਪੁਸਤਕੁ ਬੰਨ੍ਹਿ ਸੰਧਿਆ ਕਰਿ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: He (Jaidev) knew no would and hence binding his book would return home in the evening.

column2: ਗੁਣ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਘਰਿ ਆਇਕੈ; ਭਗਤ ਰੂਪਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਲੇਖੁ ਬਣਾਵੈ॥
column5: God, the repository of all virtues in the form of the devotee Himself wrote all the songs for him.

column2: ਅਖਰ ਪੜ੍ਹਿ ਪਰਤੀਤਿ ਕਰਿ; ਹੋਇ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਨ ਅੰਗਿ ਸਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: Jaidev would get elated seeing and reading those words.

column2: ਵੇਖੈ ਜਾਇ ਉਜਾੜਿ ਵਿਚਿ; ਬਿਰਖੁ ਇਕੁ ਆਚਰਜ ਸੁਹਾਵੈ॥
column5: Jaidev saw a wonderful tree in the deep forest.

column2: ਗੀਤ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਸਪੂਰਣੋ; ਪਤਿ ਪਤਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ॥
column5: Each and every leaf had the songs of the Lord Govind written on it. He could not understand this mystery.

column2: ਭਗਤਿ ਹੇਤਿ ਪਰਗਾਸੁ ਕਰਿ; ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਮਿਲੈ ਗਲਿ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: Due to the love for the devotee, God embraced him in person.

column2: ਸੰਤ ਅਨੰਤ ਨ ਭੇਦ ਗਣਾਵੈ॥੧੦॥
column5: God and saint have no veil in between.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੧. ਨਾਮਦੇਵ}
column5: Namdev

column2: ਕੰਮ ਕਿਤੈ ਪਿਉ ਚਲਿਆ; ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਨੋ ਆਖਿ ਸਿਧਾਇਆ॥
column5: Namdev's father was called to do some work so he called Naamdev.

column2: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਦੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੀ; ਦੁਧੁ ਪੀਆਵਣੁ ਕਹਿ ਸਮਝਾਇਆ॥
column5: He told Namdev to serve Thakur, the Lord, with milk.

column2: ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਇਸਨਾਨੁ ਕਰਿ; ਕਪਲ ਗਾਇ ਦੁਹਿਕੈ ਲੈ ਆਇਆ॥
column5: After taking bath Namdev brought the milk of black-teat cow.

column2: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਨੋ ਨ੍ਹਾਵਾਲਿਕੈ; ਚਰਣੋਦਕੁ ਲੈ ਤਿਲਕੁ ਚੜ੍ਹਾਇਆ॥
column5: Having bathed the Thakur, he put the water used to wash the Thakur, on his own head.

column2: ਹਥਿ ਜੋੜਿ ਬਿਨਤੀ ਕਰੈ; ਦੁਧੁ ਪੀਅਹੁ ਜੀ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਰਾਇਆ॥
column5: Now with folded hands he requested the Lord to have milk.

column2: ਨਿਹਚਉ ਕਰਿ ਆਰਾਧਿਆ; ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਦਰਸੁ ਦਿਖਲਾਇਆ॥
column5: Becoming steadfast in his thoughts when he prayed, the Lord appeared before him in person.

column2: ਭਰੀ ਕਟੋਰੀ ਨਾਮਦੇਵਿ; ਲੈ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਨੋ ਦੂਧ ਪੀਆਇਆ॥
column5: Namdev made Lord drink the full bowl of milk.

column2: ਗਾਇ ਮੁਈ ਜੀਵਾਲੀਓਨੁ; ਨਾਮਦੇਵ ਦਾ ਛਪਰੁ ਛਾਇਆ॥
column5: On another occasion God brought a dead cow to life and also thatched the hut of Namdev.

column2: ਫੇਰਿ ਦੇਹੁਰਾ ਰਖਿਓਨੁ; ਚਾਰਿ ਵਰਨ ਲੈ ਪੈਰੀ ਪਾਇਆ॥
column5: On yet another occasion, God rotated the temple (after Naamdev was not allowed entrance) and made all the four castes (varnas) bow at the feet of Namdev.

column2: ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾ ਦਾ ਕਰੇ ਕਰਾਇਆ॥੧੧॥
column5: The Lord accomplishes whatever is done and desired by saints.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੨. ਨਾਮਦੇਵ ਅਰ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਚਨ}
column5: Namdev and Trilochan

column2: ਦਰਸਨੁ ਵੇਖਣ ਨਾਮਦੇਵ; ਭਲਕੇ ਉਠਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਚਨ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: Trilochan awoke early daily just to have sight of Namdev,

column2: ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਨਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਦੁਇ ਜਣੇ; ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਹਰਿ ਚਲਿਤੁ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ॥
column5: Together they would concentrate on the Lord and Namdev would tell him the grand stories of God.

column2: ਮੇਰੀ ਭੀ ਕਰਿ ਬੇਨਤੀ; ਦਰਸਨੁ ਦੇਖਾਂ. ਜੇ ਤਿਸੁ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: (Trilochan asked Namdev) “kindly pray for me so that if the Lord accepts, I may also have a glimpse of His blessed vision.”

column2: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਜੀ ਨੋ ਪੁਛਿਓਸੁ; ਦਰਸਨੁ ਕਿਵੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਚਨੁ ਪਾਵੈ॥
column5: Namdev asked Thakur, the Lord, as to how Trilochan could have sight of the Lord ?

column2: ਹਸਿਕੈ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਬੋਲਿਆ; ਨਾਮਦੇਉ ਨੋ ਕਹਿ ਸਮਝਾਵੈ॥
column5: The Lord God smiled and explained to Naamdev;

column2: ਹਥਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਭੇਟੁ ਸੋ; ਤੁਸਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਚਨ ਮੈ ਮੁਹਿ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: “No offerings are needed by me. Out of my delight only, I would make Trilochan to have sight of me.”

column2: ਹਉ ਅਧੀਨੁ ਹਾਂ ਭਗਤ ਦੇ; ਪਹੁੰਚਿ ਨ ਹੰਘਾਂ ਭਗਤੀ ਦਾਵੈ॥
column5: I am under the total control of the devotees and their loving claims I can never reject; rather I myself also cannot understand them.

column2: ਹੋਇ ਵਿਚੋਲਾ ਆਣਿ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ॥੧੨॥
column5: Their loving devotion, in fact, becomes mediator and makes them meet me.”

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੩. ਧੰਨਾ ਅਤੇ ਬ੍ਰਾਹਮਨ}
column5: Dhanna and brahmin

column2: ਬਾਮ੍ਹਣੁ ਪੂਜੈ ਦੇਵਤੇ; ਧੰਨਾ ਗਊ ਚਰਾਵਣ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: A brahman would worship gods (in the form of stone idols) where Dhanna used to graze his cow.

column2: ਧੰਨੈ ਡਿਠਾ ਚਲਿਤੁ ਏਹੁ; ਪੁਛੈ ਬਾਮ੍ਹਣੁ ਆਖਿ ਸੁਣਾਵੈ॥
column5: On seeing his worship, Dhanna asked the brahman what he was doing.

column2: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਦੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੈ; ਜੋ ਇਛੈ ਸੋਈ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਵੈ॥
column5: “Service to the Thakur (God) gives the desired fruit,” replied the brahman.

column2: ਧੰਨਾ ਕਰਦਾ ਜੋਦੜੀ; ਮੈ ਭਿ ਦੇਹ ਇਕ ਜੇ ਤੁਧੁ ਭਾਵੈ॥
column5: Dhanna requested, “O brahman, if you agree kindly give one to me.”

column2: ਪਥਰੁ ਇਕ ਲਪੇਟਿ ਕਰਿ; ਦੇ ਧੰਨੈ ਨੋ ਗੈਲ ਛੁਡਾਵੈ॥
column5: The brahman rolled a stone, gave it to Dhanna and thus got rid of him.

column2: ਠਾਕੁਰ ਨੋ ਨ੍ਹਾਵਾਲਿ ਕੈ; ਛਾਹਿ ਰੋਟੀ ਲੈ ਭੋਗੁ ਚੜ੍ਹਾਵੈ॥
column5: Dhanna bathed the Thakur and offered him bread and buttermilk.

column2: ਹਥਿ ਜੋੜਿ ਮਿੰਨਤ ਕਰੈ; ਪੈਰੀ ਪੈ ਪੈ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਮਨਾਵੈ॥
column5: With folded hands and falling at the feet of the stone he begged for his service to be accepted.

column2: ਹਉ ਭੀ ਮੁਹੁ ਨ ਜੁਠਾਲਸਾਂ; ਤੂ ਰੁਠਾ ਮੈ ਕਿਹੁ ਨ ਸੁਖਾਵੈ॥
column5: Dhanna said, “I will also not eat because how can I be happy if you are annoyed.”

column2: ਗੋਸਾਈ ਪਰਤਖਿ ਹੋਇ; ਰੋਟੀ ਖਾਇ ਛਾਹਿ ਮੁਹਿ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: (Seeing his true and loving devotion) God was forced to appear and eat his bread and buttermilk.

column2: ਭੋਲਾ ਭਾਉ ਗੋਬਿੰਦੁ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ॥੧੩॥
column5: In fact, innocence like that of Dhanna makes the sight of the Lord available.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੪. ਬੇਣੀ ਭਗਤ}
column5: Beni

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੇਣੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ; ਜਾਇ ਇਕਾਂਤੁ ਬਹੈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: Saint Beni, a gurmukh, used to sit in solitude and would enter a meditative trance.

column2: ਕਰਮ ਕਰੈ ਅਧਿਆਤਮੀ; ਹੋਰਸੁ ਕਿਸੈ ਨ ਅਲਖੁ ਲਖਾਵੈ॥
column5: He would perform spiritual activities and in humbleness would never tell anyone.

column2: ਘਰਿ ਆਇਆ ਜਾ ਪੁਛੀਐ; ਰਾਜ ਦੁਆਰਿ ਗਇਆ ਆਲਾਵੈ॥
column5: Reaching back home when asked, he would tell people that he had gone to the door of his king (the Supreme Lord).

column2: ਘਰਿ ਸਭ ਵਥੂ ਮੰਗੀਅਨਿ; ਵਲ ਛਲੁ ਕਰਿਕੈ ਝਤੁ ਲੰਘਾਵੈ॥
column5: When his wife asked for some household material he would avoid her and thus spend his time performing spiritual activities.

column2: ਵਡਾ ਸਾਂਗੁ ਵਰਤਦਾ; ਓਹ ਇਕ ਮਨਿ ਪਰਮੇਸਰੁ ਧਿਆਵੈ॥
column5: One day while concentrating on the Lord with single-minded devotion, a strange miracle happened.

column2: ਪੈਜ ਸਵਾਰੈ ਭਗਤ ਦੀ; ਰਾਜਾ ਹੋਇਕੈ ਘਰਿ ਚਲਿ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: To keep the glory of the devotee, God Himself in the form of King went to his house.

column2: ਦੇਇ ਦਿਲਾਸਾ ਤੁਸਿਕੈ; ਅਣਗਣਤੀ ਖਰਚੀ ਪਹੁਚਾਵੈ॥
column5: In great joy, He consoled everyone and made available profuse money for expenditure.

column2: ਓਥਹੁ ਆਇਆ ਭਗਤ ਪਾਸਿ; ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਹੇਤੁ ਉਪਜਾਵੈ॥
column5: From there He came to His devotee Beni and compassionately loved him.

column2: ਭਗਤ ਜਨਾਂ ਜੈਕਾਰੁ ਕਰਾਵੈ॥੧੪॥
column5: This way He arranges applause for His devotees.

column2: {ਪਉੜੀ ੧੫. ਕਬੀਰ ਅਤੇ ਰਾਮਾਨੰਦ}
column5: Kabir and Ramanand

column2: ਹੋਇ ਬਿਰਕਤੁ ਬਨਾਰਸੀ; ਰਹਿੰਦਾ ਰਾਮਾਨੰਦੁ ਗੁਸਾਈਂ॥
column5: Being detached from the world, Brahmin Ramanand lived in Varanasi (Kasi).

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤੁ ਵੇਲੇ ਉਠਿਕੈ; ਜਾਂਦਾ ਗੰਗਾ ਨ੍ਹਾਵਣ ਤਾਈਂ॥
column5: He would rise early in the morning and go to the Ganges to bathe.

column2: ਅਗੋ ਹੀ ਦੇ ਜਾਇਕੈ; ਲੰਮਾ ਪਿਆ ਕਬੀਰ ਤਿਥਾਈਂ॥
column5: Once even before Ramanand, Kabir went there and lay in the way.

column2: ਪੈਰੀ ਟੁੰਬਿ ਉਠਾਲਿਆ; ‘ਬੋਲਹੁ ਰਾਮ’ ਸਿਖ ਸਮਝਾਈ॥
column5: Touching with his feet Ramanand awakened Kabir and told him to speak ‘Ram’, the true spiritual teaching.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਲੋਹਾ ਪਾਰਸੁ ਛੁਹੇ; ਚੰਦਨ ਵਾਸੁ ਨਿੰਮੁ ਮਹਿਕਾਈ॥
column5: As the iron touched by philosopher’s stone becomes gold and the margosa tree (Azadirachta indica) is made fragrant by sandal.

column2: ਪਸੂ ਪਰੇਤਹੁ ਦੇਵ ਕਰਿ; ਪੂਰੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੀ ਵਡਿਆਈ॥
column5: The wondrous Guru turns even animals and ghosts into angels.

column2: ਅਚਰਜ ਨੋ ਅਚਰਜੁ ਮਿਲੈ; ਵਿਸਮਾਦੈ ਵਿਸਮਾਦੁ ਮਿਲਾਈ॥
column5: Meeting the wonderous Guru the disciple wonderfully merges into the great wonderous Lord.

column2: ਝਰਣਾ ਝਰਦਾ ਨਿਝਰਹੁ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬਾਣੀ ਅਘੜ ਘੜਾਈ॥
column5: Then from the Self springs a fountain and the words of the gurmukhs shape a beautiful form

column2: ਰਾਮ ਕਬੀਰੈ ਭੇਦੁ ਨ ਭਾਈ॥੧੫॥
