column5: My Supplication to Ad(i) Purakh (Primordial Lord), salutation the holy feet of the True Guru (who is the embodiment of the Lord)

column2: ਘਟ ਘਟ ਕਾ ਪਰਵੇਸ; ਏਕ ਅਨੇਕ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਸਸਿ॥੧॥
column5: Like the moon, who though one, resides everywhere and in everyone and yet remains one.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਓਨਮ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ; ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਆਦੇਸ॥
column5: Salutation in the holy feet of Satguru, the embodiment of glorious Waheguru who is the Primeval Lord.

column2: ਏਕ ਅਨੇਕ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਸਸਿ; ਘਟ ਘਟ ਕਾ ਪਰਵੇਸ॥੨॥
column5: He is like the moon, Who though one is present everywhere and yet remains one.

column2: ਛੰਦ॥ ਘਟ ਘਟ ਕਾ ਪਰਵੇਸ; ਸੇਸ ਪਹਿ ਕਹਤ ਨ ਆਵੈ॥
column5: Waheguru (Lord) who is all-pervading and whose extent cannot be defined even by Sheshnag (a mythological serpent with thousand heads),

column2: ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਕਹਿ ਨੇਤਿ; ਬੇਦ ਬੰਦੀਜਨ ਗਾਵੈ॥
column5: Whose praises Ved, Bhats and aIl others have been singing since eons and yet say-not this, not even this.

column2: ਆਦਿ ਮਧਿ ਅਰੁ ਅੰਤ; ਹੁਤੇ ਹੁਤ ਹੈ ਪੁਨ ਹੋਨਮ॥
column5: Who was there in the beginning, in between era and will remain in future,

column2: ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਆਦੇਸ; ਚਰਨ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਓਨਮ॥੩॥੧॥
column5: My supplication to Him through the holy feet of the True Guru in which He is totally effulgent. (1)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ; ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰ ਅਨੰਤ ਗੁਰ॥
column5: Eternal, imperceptible, fearless, beyond reach, illimitable, infinite and destroyer of the darkness of ignorance

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਨਕਦੇਵ; ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ॥੪॥
column5: Waheguru (Lord) who is transcendental and immanent in the form of Guru Nanak Dev.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰ ਅਨੰਤ ਗੁਰ; ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ॥
column5: The embodiment of formless God, who is imperishable, beyond description, inaccessible, illimitable, infinite and destroyer of darkness of ignorance.

column2: ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਨਕਦੇਵ॥੫॥
column5: Satgur (True Guru) Nanak Dev is the immanent form of God.

column2: ਛੰਤ॥ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਨਕਦੇਵ; ਦੇਵ ਦੇਵੀ ਸਭ ਧਿਆਵਹਿ॥
column5: All gods and goddesses contemplate on the True Guru, Guru Nanak Dev.

column2: ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ; ਰਾਗ ਰਾਗਨਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵਹਿ॥
column5: They along with minstrels of heaven sing his praises to the accompaniment of musical instruments producing ecstatic music.

column2: ਸੁੰਨ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਅਗਾਧਿ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਪਰੰਪਰ॥
column5: Saints and holy men in his company (Guru Nanak) go in deep meditation and state of nothingness,

column2: ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ; ਅਗਮ ਅਗਮਿਤਿ ਅਪਰੰਪਰ॥੬॥੨॥
column5: And get absorbed in the eternal, imperceptible, infinite, fearless, and inaccessible Lord (Satguru). (2)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਰੂਪ; ਪਰਮ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਹਿ॥
column5: The light eternal of Guru Nanak Dev melded into the light of Guru Angad Dev who acquired effulgence like the former.

column2: ਅਦਭੁਤ ਅਤਿਹਿ ਅਨੂਪ; ਪਰਮ ਤਤ ਤਤਹਿ ਮਿਲਿਓ॥੭॥
column5: With the light of Guru Nanak merging with that of Guru Angad Dev Ji, the latter became wondrous in form and beyond words of praise.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਪਰਮ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਹਿ; ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਰੂਪ॥
column5: Light supreme (Guru Nanak Dev Ji) merged in Guru Angad Dev's light who himself became light divine.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਤਤ ਤਤਹਿ ਮਿਲਿਓ; ਅਦਭੁਤ ਅਤਿਹੀ ਅਨੂਪ॥੮॥
column5: Truth of Guru Nanak merged with the essence of Guru Angad transforming him into astonishing form.

column2: ਛੰਦ॥ ਅਦਭੁਤ ਅਤਿਹਿ ਅਨੂਪ; ਰੂਪ ਪਾਰਸ ਕੈ ਪਾਰਸ॥
column5: Guru Angad coming in contact with Guru Nanak the philosopher-stone, became a philosopher-stone himself. His form too became wondrous.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਅੰਗਦ ਮਿਲਿ ਅੰਗ ਸੰਗ; ਮਿਲਿ ਸੰਗ ਉਧਾਰਸ॥
column5: Becoming inseparable from Guru Nanak, Lehna Ji became Guru Angad and then whosoever came in touch with him (Guru Angad) was liberated.

column2: ਅਕਲ ਕਲਾ ਭਰਪੂਰ; ਸੂਤ੍ਰ ਗਤਿ ਓਤ ਪੋਤ ਮਹਿ॥
column5: Guru Angad Ji integrated himself like warp and weft with Guru Nanak, the possessor of Divine power of the Lord.

column2: ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਰੂਪ ਜੋਤਿ; ਮਿਲਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਹਿ॥੯॥੩॥
column5: Light merged with light so much that whosoever came in contact with light embodiment (Guru Angad) , became resplendent too. (3)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨਿਵਾਸ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਅਨਹਦ ਸਬਦ॥
column5: n the divine glimpse of Guru Amar Das dwells the elixir of life. (On whomsoever he casts his look, he makes him immortal). His elixir-like words are like unstruck music.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ; ਮਿਲਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਭਏ॥੧੦॥
column5: Resplendent True Guru Amar Das Ji became elixir-like after meeting Guru Angad Dev Ji. He now makes others calm and mortal.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਅਨਹਦ ਸਬਦ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨਿਵਾਸ॥
column5: Meditating on unstruck melodious Divine Word, the sight and utterances of Guru Amar Das Ji, started showering elixir of life.

column2: ਮਿਲਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਭਏ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ॥੧੧॥
column5: Meeting Guru Angad Dev Ji, elixir-like cool, tranquil and granter of emancipation, Satgur Amar Das too became the same.

column2: ਛੰਦ॥ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਮਰ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ; ਤਾਸ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪਾਵੈ॥
column5: Satgur Amar Das Ji who is light refulgent, whosoever partakes nectar-like wash of his feet,

column2: ਕਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ; ਪਰਮਪਦ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਵੈ॥
column5: Becomes free from all desires is absorbed in high state of spirituality and equipoise.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਸੁਗੰਧਿ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਨਿਜ ਆਸਨ॥
column5: By the fragrance of Naam Simran of Guru Amar Das Ji, the Guru's obedient seeker finds stability in the company of holy men and Lord's devotees

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨਿਵਾਸ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮੁਖ ਬਚਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸਨ॥੧੨॥੪॥
column5: In the elixir-like vision of Guru Amar Das lies the nectar of life and his words provide elixir-like luminance of Naam of the Lord. (4)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਸਨ ਬਿਸ੍ਰਾਮ; ਗੁਰ ਭਏ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲਿ॥
column5: Satguru who resides in Waheguru (Brahm), meeting such Guru-conscious person (Guru Amar Das), and becoming one with him he too acquired all traits of Guru.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਮਤਾ ਰਾਮ; ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭਏ॥੧੩॥
column5: Through the blessings of Naam Simran of the principal Guru Satguru (Amar Das Ji), Guru Ram Das Ji too became prime Guru.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਗੁਰ ਭਏ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲਿ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਸਨ ਬਿਸ੍ਰਾਮ॥
column5: In the association of prime Guru (Guru Amar Das Ji) he too became Guru and found refuge in the holy feet of the Lord.

column2: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭਏ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਮਤਾ ਰਾਮ॥੧੪॥
column5: The Guru-conscious person whose name is Ram Das, by perpetual meditation on Lord's name, became Guru-oriented and virtuous (Satguru)

column2: ਛੰਤ॥ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਮਤਾ ਰਾਮ; ਨਾਮ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਇਓ॥
column5: Through God-conscious Guru Amar Das Ji and by the blessing of meditation on His name, the virtuous Ram Das emerged as Guru Ram Das (slave of the Lord).

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਗੁਰ ਗੁਰੂ ਕਹਾਇਓ॥
column5: Because of knowledge of Guru Shabad and consciously uniting with Him, Guru Ram Das became known as principal Guru.

column2: ਦੀਪ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਦੀਪ ਜੋਤਿ; ਜਗਮਗ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਉਰ॥
column5: The flame of a beacon lights up another lamp.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਮਤਾ ਰਾਮ; ਸੰਧਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਭਏ ਗੁਰ॥੧੫॥੫॥
column5: Thus Guru Ram Das became principal Guru through the blessings of Simran of Lord's name and his association with Guru Amar Das Ji. (5)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ; ਫਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਗਤਿ॥
column5: Just as the puzzle of seed and tree as to who came first is strange and perplexing, similarly strange is understanding the meeting of Guru and Sikh.

column2: ਆਦਿ ਪਰਮ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ; ਅੰਤ ਅਨੰਤ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥੧੬॥
column5: This mystery of the beginning and end is beyond comprehension. Lord is beyond, away and infinite.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਫਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਗਤਿ; ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ॥
column5: Guru Ram Das caused the meeting of Guru and Sikh in the same wondrous way of fruit and tree.

column2: ਅੰਤ ਅਨੰਤ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ; ਆਦਿ ਪਰਮ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ॥੧੭॥
column5: That perspective is infinite and no one can understand it. It is beyond, away and still away out of the reach of mortals.

column2: ਛੰਤ॥ ਆਦਿ ਪਰਮ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ; ਨਾਦ ਮਿਲਿ ਨਾਦ ਸਬਦ ਧੁਨਿ॥
column5: Just as the sound of musical instruments melds with the words (of song/hymns), similarly Guru Ram Das and Guru Arjan became indistinguishable.

column2: ਸਲਿਲਹਿ ਸਲਿਲ ਸਮਾਇ; ਨਾਦ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਾਗਰ ਸੁਨਿ॥
column5: Just as the river water becomes inseparable from the water of the ocean, Guru Arjan became one with Guru Amar Das by engrossing himself in his precepts and following them obediently.

column2: ਨਰਪਤਿ ਸੁਤ ਨ੍ਰਿਪ ਹੋਤ; ਜੋਤਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਰਜਨ॥
column5: Just as son of a king becomes king, similarly Guru Arjan born as son to Guru Ram Das became an enlightened soul by singing the eulogies of Lord-a boon blessed to him by Satguru.

column2: ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਪਰਸਾਦਿ ਭਏ; ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਗੁਰੁ ਅਰਜਨ॥੧੮॥੬॥
column5: By the grace of Guru Ram Das, Arjan Dev succeeded him as Guru Arjan Dev.

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ; ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੁਇ॥
column5: Being supreme conscious and knowledgeable; it seems God Himself has descended as light divine in the form of Guru Hargobind.

column2: ਨਾਮ ਦੋਇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਏਕ; ਗੁਰ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥੧੯॥
column5: One may hold them (Guru Hargobind) and Gobind different due to their names, but in reality, God Himself has appeared in the form of Hargobind.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੁਇ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ॥
column5: : Guru Hargobind the enlightened is manifest of Lord. He is granter of spiritual knowledge.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ; ਨਾਮ ਦੋਇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਏਕ॥੨੦॥
column5: Guru and Gobind are just two separate names, but in reality it is Lord Himself who is manifest.

column2: ਛੰਤ॥ ਨਾਮ ਦੋਇ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਏਕ; ਟੇਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਠਹਰਾਈ॥
column5: Guru and Gobind though are two different names, in reality He himself is refulgent

column2: ਆਦਿ ਭਏ ਗੁਰ ਨਾਮ; ਦੁਤੀਅ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਬਡਾਈ॥
column5: Sikhs ever in attendance of Guru has this strong belief that, first He was called Guru Arjan and then he gave this honour of Guruship to Hargobind.

column2: ਹਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਹਰਿਗੋਬਿੰਦ; ਰਚਨ ਰਚਿ ਥਾਪਿ ਉਥਾਪਨ॥
column5: God Lord is Guru Arjan Himself and then He Himself became Hargobind.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ; ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਹੁਇ ਆਪਾ ਆਪਨ॥੨੧॥੭॥
column5: The Omnipotent Lord who created the cosmos, established it and has singular authority of destroying it; it seems He Himself has taken the form of Hargobind to reveal all knowledge about Himself. (7)

column2: ਸੋਰਠਾ॥ ਬਿਸਮਾਦਹਿ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ; ਅਸਚਰਜਹਿ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਗਤਿ॥
column5: God - manifest Satguru's play is ecstatic and blissful, astonishing beyond astonishment,

column2: ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ; ਅਦਭੁਤ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਭਏ॥੨੨॥
column5: unimaginably wondrous, and amazing beyond perception.

column2: ਦੋਹਰਾ॥ ਅਸਚਰਜਹਿ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਗਤਿ; ਬਿਸਮਾਦਹਿ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ॥
column5: (Describing the wondrous state of Guru who is immanent of the Lord), we have reached the awesome of awesome state, in the most enrapturing ecstatic state,

column2: ਅਦਭੁਤ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਭਏ; ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ॥੨੩॥
column5: wonderfully strange condition of transcendence seeing the magnificence of the Lord .

column2: ਛੰਤ॥ ਆਦਿ ਪੁਰਖ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ; ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਰਸ ਗੰਧ ਅਗੋਚਰ॥
column5: Primordial Lord (God) has no beginning. He is beyond and still farther. He is free of such mundane worldly pleasures like taste, desires and fragrances.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਅਸਪਰਸ; ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਤਿ ਸਬਦ ਮਨੋਚਰ॥
column5: He is beyond vision, touch, reach of mind, intelligence and words.

column2: ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਤਿ ਗਿਆਨ; ਲਖੇ ਨਹੀਂ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵਾ॥
column5: The imperceptible and unattached Lord cannot be known by study of Vedas and through other earthly knowledge.

column2: ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ; ਨਮ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੇਵਾ॥੨੪॥੮॥
column5: Satguru who is embodiment of Lord and inhabits His divine radiance is infinite. Thus, he is worthy of salutation and obeisance in all the three times-past, present and future. (8)

column2: ਦਰਸਨ ਦੇਖਤ ਹੀ ਸੁਧਿ ਕੀ ਨ ਸੁਧਿ ਰਹੀ; ਬੁਧਿ ਕੀ ਨ ਬੁਧਿ ਰਹੀ. ਮਤਿ ਮੈ ਨ ਮਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A glimpse of (Satguru) left me bereft of all my consciousness, senses, intelligence, cleverness and all other considered wisdom of the world.

column2: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮੈ ਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਉ ਧਿਆਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਰਹਿਓ; ਗਿਆਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਰਹਿਓ. ਗਤਿ ਮੈ ਨ ਗਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: I lost my awareness, attachment of mind with insignificant matters, desires to acquire base or futile ego inflating knowledge and other worldly predicaments.

column2: ਧੀਰਜ ਕੋ ਧੀਰਜ. ਗਰਬ ਕੋ ਗਰਬੁ ਗਇਓ; ਰਤਿ ਮੈ ਨ ਰਤਿ ਰਹੀ. ਪਤਿ ਰਤਿ ਪਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: My patience was lost and so was my vanity. There was no life in me and I was bereft of my existence even.

column2: ਅਦਭੁਤ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ. ਬਿਸਮੈ ਬਿਸਮ; ਅਸਚਰਜੈ ਅਸਚਰਜ. ਅਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਹੈ॥੯॥
column5: The glimpse of Satguru is capable of amazing one with wondrous feelings. These are astonishing and marvellous and there is no end to this amazement. (9)

column2: ਦਸਮ ਸਥਾਨ ਕੇ ਸਮਾਨਿ ਕਉਨ ਭਉਨ ਕਹੋਂ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਸੁ ਤਉ. ਅਨਤ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: What other place of mystic abode can I say than the tenth concealed opening of a human being? Only the Guru conscious person can reach it by the grace of True Guru by meditating on His name.

column2: ਉਨਮਨੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਪਟੰਤਰ ਦੀਜੈ ਕਉਨ ਜੋਤਿ; ਦਇਆ ਕੈ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਜਾਹੀ. ਤਾਹੀ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: What light can be equated with the radiance one receives at the time of spiritual enlightenment?

column2: ਅਨਹਦ ਨਾਦ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਕਉਨ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰੂ ਸੁਨਾਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ. ਸੋਈ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਈ॥
column5: What melodious musical sound can be equal to the melodious unstruck musical sound of divine word?

column2: ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਤੁਲਿ ਨ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸ; ਅਪਿਉ ਪੀਆਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ. ਤਾਹੀ ਮੈ ਸਮਾਵਈ॥੧੦॥
column5: There is no other elixir capable of making one immortal than the one that flows perpetually in the concealed opening (Dasam Duar) of a human being. And one who is blessed by the True Guru (Satguru) to receive this elixir of immortality obtains it by His g

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ. ਬੀਸ ਇਕ ਈਸ ਈਸ; ਇਤ ਤੇ ਉਲੰਘਿ. ਉਤ ਜਾਇ ਠਹਰਾਵਈ॥
column5: Meeting with Guru, a Sikh receives the Lord's word to meditate upon and by his untiring and resolute efforts become one with Him. He frees himself from the worldly matters and lives in harmony in the realm of the Lord.

column2: ਚਰਮ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਮੂੰਦ. ਪੇਖੈ ਦਿਬ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕੈ; ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਉਨਮਨੀ ਸੁਧਿ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: He shuts his eyes from mundane worldly attractions and lives in the spiritual wisdom that helps him feel His presence in everything.

column2: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੰਕੋਚਤ ਹੀ. ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਖੋਲਿ; ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਪਰੈ. ਅਨਹਤ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਈ॥
column5: Weaning his thoughts away from the worldly attractions, his doors of ignorance are opened; he is distracted from all sources of worldly pleasures and he gets engrossed in listening to celestial songs and music.

column2: ਬਚਨ ਬਿਸਰਜਿਤ ਅਨ ਰਸ ਰਹਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਅਪਿਉ ਪੀਆਵਈ॥੧੧॥
column5: Renouncing the worldly matters and shedding all attachment with worldly pleasures, he drinks deep the elixir that flows continuously in his (Dasam Duar) the celestial door of the body. (11)

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਅਨਰਸ ਬਸਿ. ਤਉ ਲਉ ਨਹੀ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸੁ; ਜਉ ਲਉ ਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ. ਆਪਿ ਆਪੁ ਨਹੀ ਦੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: So long a human being remains absorbed in worldly attractions and pleasures, he cannot know love. So long his attention is focused on something else, he cannot realize self.

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ. ਤਉ ਲਉ ਨਹੀ ਅਧ੍ਯਾਤਮ ਗਿਆਨ; ਜਉ ਲਉ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ. ਨ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਬਿਸੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: (Renouncing Lord) as long as one is busy acquiring knowledge of mundane worldly things, he remains bereft of spiritual wisdom. As long as one remains involved in worldly pleasures one cannot hear the unstruck celestial music of divine word.

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਅਹੰਬੁਧਿ. ਸੁਧਿ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਅੰਤਰਗਤਿ; ਜਉ ਲਉ ਨ ਲਖਾਵੈ. ਤਉ ਲਉ ਅਲਖ ਨ ਲੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: As long as one remains proud and arrogant, one cannot realise self. Till such time True Guru does not initiate a person with the boon of Lord's name and propitiate the Lord, one cannot realise the 'formless God'.

column2: ਸਤਿ ਰੂਪ ਸਤਿਨਾਮ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਏਕ ਹੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਮੇਕ. ਏਕ ਏਕ ਭੇਖੀਐ॥੧੨॥
column5: The knowledge of Almighty lies in the consecrating words of the True Guru that leads one to reality of His name and form. By uniting his mind with His name, the Lord who prevails in various forms gets revealed. (12)

column2: ਨਾਨਾ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ. ਬਹੁ ਬਿੰਜਨਾਦਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ; ਸੀਂਚਤ ਸਰਬ ਰਸ. ਰਸਨਾ ਕਹਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Tongue that enjoys many forms of sweet and savoury foods, drinks and is relisher of all the tastes is called gustation. Eyes see good and bad, beautiful and ugly and is therefore known as vision power.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਅਰੁ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਿਮਰਨ. ਅਮਿਤ ਬਡਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Ears for their ability to hear all sorts of sounds, melodies etc. are called hearing power. With the use of all these faculties, one obtains knowledge of various things, focuses one's mind in meaningful thoughts and earns worldly respect.

column2: ਸਕਲ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਸਪਰਸ ਅਉ ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ; ਬੁਧਿ ਬਲ ਬਚਨ. ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: The skin brings awareness of things through touch. Enjoyment of music and songs, intellect, strength, speech and dependence on discrimination is the boon of the Lord.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿਨਾਮ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਸਫਲ ਹੋਇ; ਬੋਲਤ ਮਧੁਰ ਧੁਨਿ. ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ਹੈ॥੧੩॥
column5: But all these senses of knowledge are useful if a person obtains the boon of Guru's wisdom, dwells his mind in the name of the Immortal Lord and sings the sweet paeans of me Lord's name. Such tune and melody of His name is bestower of bliss and happiness.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸਿ ਹੁਇ ਪਤੰਗ ਸੰਗਮ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਛੋਹ ਮੀਨ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: In order to become one with my dear beloved, I, a deceitful lover, not possessed by his love, did not learn from a mot how to die in separation from him, nor did I learn from fish how to die in the separation of beloved.

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਜੋਤਿ ਮੈ. ਨ ਹੁਇ ਜੋਤੀ ਸਰੂਪ; ਚਰਨ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਹੋਇ. ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਠਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: And here I am who is not making any effort to merge in my Lord by keeping his refulgence in my heart; and yet with all this recalcitrance, I am alive.

column2: ਮਿਲਿ ਬਿਛਰਤ ਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨ ਬਿਰਹ ਜਾਨੀ; ਮੀਨ ਅਉ ਪਤੰਗ ਮੋਹਿ ਦੇਖਤ ਲਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: I have not understood the intensity of love and consequence of death as is in the case of a moth and flame or fish and water, and therefore both moth and fish feel ashamed of my; fraudulent love.

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ. ਧੰਨੁ ਹੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਗਦ ਜੋਨਿ; ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਦੇਹ. ਨਰਕ ਨ ਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੧੪॥
column5: Being a deceitful friend, my human life is damnable, whereas the reptilian species are worth appreciation for their love of their beloveds like the moth and fish. Because of my fraudulent love I will not even get a place in hell. (14)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਅਤਿ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਕਹਿਤ ਨ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: The spiritual happiness of a devout Sikh of Guru who meditates on Lord's name, the bliss and his spiritual happiness is wonderful beyond explanation.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਗੰਧ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ; ਸੀਤਲ ਕੋਮਲ ਪਰਸਤ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: The peace and joy of a Guru-conscious person diffuses wonderful fragrance. Its tranquility and softness can only be realised when it is relished. There is no limit of the divine peace and wisdom of such a Guru-oriented person. It can understood best when

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧ; ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: One who is a devout Sikh of Guru, the glory of his spiritual, knowledge gets reflected in every limb of his body umpteen times. Every hair of his body becomes alive with divine effulgence.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਕੋਟਿ ਸੋਭਾ; ਮਇਆ ਕੈ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਤੋ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਧਾਵਈ॥੧੫॥
column5: By his grace, whosoever is shown this state of spiritual bliss, does not wander about anywhere. (15)

column2: ਉਲਟਿ ਪਵਨ ਮਨ ਮੀਨ ਕੀ ਚਪਲ ਗਤਿ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪਰਚੇ ਪਰਮਪਦ ਪਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By practicing Naam Simran (meditation on Lord's name) one can turn the wind-like wayward mind into sharp and swift movement of fish. Developing association with the word of True Guru, one attains noble state.

column2: ਸੂਰ ਸਰ ਸੋਖਿ. ਪੋਖਿ ਸੋਮ ਸਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਕੈ; ਬੰਧਨ ਦੈ ਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਸਰ ਅਪਿਅ ਪੀਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Nectar of life (blissful peace) is obtained only by meditation. By burning away the indestructible ego and by killing the imperishable mind, leaving all doubts and suspicions, those who stabilise their body, their life-force finds a direction.

column2: ਅਜਰਹਿ ਜਾਰਿ. ਮਾਰਿ ਅਮਰਹਿ ਭ੍ਰਾਤਿ ਛਾਡਿ; ਅਸਥਿਰ ਕੰਧ ਹੰਸ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਧਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By burning away the indestructible ego and by killing the imperishable mind, leaving all doubts and suspicions, those who stabilise their body, their life-force finds a direction.

column2: ਆਦੈ ਆਦਿ ਨਾਦੈ ਨਾਦਿ ਸਲਿਲੈ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਿਲਿ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥੧੬॥
column5: As the space merges with space, air with air and water mixes up with its source, so does the life-force integrates with the radiance of the Lord and supreme bliss is experienced. (16)

column2: ਚਿਰੰਕਾਲ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਨਿਰਮੋਲੁ ਪਾਏ; ਸਫਲ ਜਨਮ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: After wandering many births, this human life is obtained. But the birth becomes successful only when one takes the refuge the holy feet of a True Guru.

column2: ਲੋਚਨ ਅਮੋਲ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਅਮੋਲ ਦੇਖੇ; ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਅਮੋਲ ਗੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਧਰਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Eyes are invaluable only when they see a glimpse of the Lord ugh the form of Sat guru. Ears are fruitful if they listen the precepts and command of Satguru attentively.

column2: ਨਾਸਕਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਚਰਨਾਰਬਿੰਦ ਬਾਸਨਾ ਕੈ; ਰਸਨਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਗੁਰਮੰਤ੍ਰ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Nostril’s are worthy only when they smell the fragrance of dust of Satguru's lotus-feet. The tongue becomes invaluable when it recites the word of the Lord given as consecration by Satguru Ji.

column2: ਹਸਤ ਅਮੋਲ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਕੈ ਸਫਲ; ਚਰਨ ਅਮੋਲ ਪਰਦਛਨਾ ਕਰਨ ਕੈ॥੧੭॥
column5: Hands are invaluable only when they involve in the comforting service of Satguru and feet become precious when they ever stroll about in the vicinity of Satguru. (17)

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਦਿਬ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਈ; ਕਰੁਨਾ ਕਟਾਛ ਦਿਬ ਦੇਹ ਪਰਵਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Focusing mind on the form of True Guru, one is enlightened with celestial vision of knowledge. By the grace of True Guru, the human form acquires Godly refulgence making its coming to this world a success.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਖੁਲੇ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਰਸਨ ਕੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Concentrating the mind on the divine word, the rock strong doors of ignorance become ajar. The acquisition of knowledge then blesses one with the treasure of Lord's name.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਬਾਸਨਾ ਸੁਬਾਸ; ਹਸਤ ਪੂਜਾ ਪ੍ਰਨਾਮ ਸਫਲ ਸੁਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The touching and feeling of the dust of True Guru's feet enlivens the fragrance of Lord's name in the mind. Involving the hands in His prayer and service, one is blessed with true and real spiritual knowledge.

column2: ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਬਿਸਮ ਸ੍ਰਬੰਗ ਮੈ ਸਮਾਇ ਭਏ; ਮਨ ਮਨਸਾ ਥਕਤ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥੧੮॥
column5: Thus every hair of a person becomes glorious and he merges with the light divine. All his vices and desires end and his mind dwells in the love of Lord's feet. (18)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਮੈ; ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਬਿਸਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The contentment that a devout Sikh gets from meditating on His name is so mystical that he (Gursikh) forgets all other worldly pleasures.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਗੰਧ ਰਸ ਬਿਸਮ ਹੁਇ; ਅਨਰਸ ਬਾਸਨਾ ਬਿਲਾਸ ਨ ਹਿਤਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the fragrance of spiritual peace Guru-conscious person lives in a state of bliss and forgets all other worldly enjoyments.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅਦਭੁਤ ਅਸਥਾਨ; ਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮੰਡਲ ਅਸਥਲ ਨ ਲੁਭਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who live in the conscious presence of True Guru live a state of perpetual bliss. The perishable pleasures of the destructible world entice and attract them no more

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਦੇਖਿ; ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਭ ਨਿਰਸ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੧੯॥
column5: In the company of spiritually elevated souls and seeing their state of ecstasy of uniting with the Lord, they consider all wisdom and attractions of the world as worthless. (19)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਦਇਆ ਕੈ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ; ਤਾਹਿ ਆਨ ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਦੇਖੇ ਨਾਹੀ ਭਾਵਈ॥
column5: One who is blessed by Satguru with spiritual wisdom, he does not like to see any other form or attraction. Nothing else can give tranquility and peace to such a blessed person.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਮਇਆ ਕੈ ਚਖਾਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ; ਤਾਹਿ ਅਨਰਸ ਨਹੀਂ ਰਸਨਾ ਹਿਤਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: One who is blessed with spiritual pleasure by the True Guru, he does not relish any other pleasures.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅਗਹੁ ਗਹਾਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ; ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਰਸਨ ਕਉ ਨ ਧਾਵਈ॥
column5: A devout Sikh who is blessed with the spiritual pleasure that no one can reach to, he need not run after other worldly relishments.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਵੈ ਜਾਹਿ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਵਾਹੀ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਈ॥੨੦॥
column5: Only he who is blessed with self-realization (spiritual knowledge) can feel its pleasure and this cannot be explained. The devotee himself can only appreciate the pleasure of that state. (20)

column2: ਸਿਧ ਨਾਥ ਜੋਗੀ ਜੋਗ ਧਿਆਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਆਨ ਸਕੇ; ਬੇਦ ਪਾਠ ਕਰਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿਕ ਨ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Supreme, absolute, true Lord whom Sidhs, Yogis and Naths could not bring in their perception, who could not be known by Brahma and other deities despite contemplation of Vedas;

column2: ਅਧ੍ਯਾਤਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੈ ਨ ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿ ਪਾਏ; ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਮੈ ਨ ਇੰਦ੍ਰਾਦਿਕ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord who could not be realised by Shiva and four sons of Brahma, nor by Indra and other such deities who resorted myriad yags and penances;

column2: ਨਾਮ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਕੈ ਸੇਖਾਦਿਕ ਨ ਸੰਖਯਾ ਜਾਨੀ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮਚਰਜ ਨਾਰਦਾਦਿਕ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Whom Shesh Naag with his thousand tongues could not comprehend and speak out all names of the Lord; bewildered by His magnificence, even the celibate sage Narad gave up the search out of frustration,

column2: ਨਾਨਾ ਅਵਤਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਪਾਰ ਕੋ ਨ ਪਾਰ ਪਾਇਓ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮਨਿ ਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੨੧॥
column5: About the infinitude of which Lord, Vishnu in spite of manifesting in so many incarnations, could know nothing. Satguru manifests Him in the heart of his obedient devotee. (21)

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਰਿਦੈ ਨਿਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਜਾਸੁ; ਧਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਕੈ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Sikh in whose heart resides the percept of Guru, and by focusing his mind in the holy feet of the Lord through Simran, the omnipresent Lord dwells in him;

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਗਿਆਨ; ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਇ ਸਰਬਾਤਮ ਕੈ ਸਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who lodges the holy word of the True Guru, contemplates on spiritual knowledge and in the process realises that One Supreme Lord exists in all, thus treats all as equals;

column2: ਹਉਮੈ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਤਿਆਗੀ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਕੈ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਭਏ; ਮਨ ਉਨਮਨ ਲਿਵ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਅਗੰਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who sheds his ego and becomes an ascetic by virtue of Simran, yet live a detached worldly life; reaches the inaccessible Lord,

column2: ਸੂਖਮ ਅਸਥੂਲ ਮੂਲ ਏਕ ਹੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਮੇਕ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਨਮ ਹੈ॥੨੨॥
column5: He who recognises one Lord manifested in all things subtle and absolute; that Guru-conscious person is emancipated even when living a worldly life. (22)

column2: ਦਰਸਨ ਜੋਤਿ ਨ ਜੋਤੀ ਸਰੂਪ ਹੁਇ ਪਤੰਗ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like a moth, I do not sacrifice myself over the radiant glimpse True Guru, nor do I know the method of lodging the music of True Guru's words as is the wont of a deer;

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਨ ਮਧੁਪ ਗਤਿ; ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਹੁਇ ਨ ਮੀਨ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like a bumble bee mad for the nectar of lotus flower loses his life when the flower closes, but I have not sacrificed myself unto the lotus like feet of my Satguru, nor have Known the pangs of separation from my Satguru like a fish when out of water;

column2: ਏਕ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਨ ਟਰਤ ਹੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਗਦ ਜੋਨਿ; ਚਾਤੁਰ ਚਤੁਰ ਗੁਨ ਹੋਇ ਨ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The living being of lower species do not backtrack their steps dying for their love that is based on just one virtue. But I with all my wisdom do not carry any trait like these beings, I do not sacrifice myself unto my True Guru creatures do;

column2: ਪਾਹਨ ਕਠੋਰ ਸਤਿਗੁਰੁ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਮੈ; ਸੁਨਿ ਮਮ ਨਾਮ ਜਮ ਨਰਕ ਲਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੨੩॥
column5: Satguru is ocean of peace and tranquillity but I am like a stone (who is least influenced by any precept of the True Guru) despite living near Him. Hearing the name of a sinner like the messenger of hell would feel ashamed of me. (23)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਚੰਚਲ ਅਚਲ ਭਏ; ਮਹਾਂ ਮਲ ਮੂਤ੍ਰ ਧਾਰੀ. ਨਿਰਮਲ ਕੀਨੇ ਹੈਂ॥
column5: Those frolicsome persons who toil on the name of the Lord with devotion and love become peaceful and tranquil. Those who are filled with dross become neat and clean.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਜੋਨਿ ਕੈ ਅਜੋਨਿ ਭਏ; ਕਾਲ ਸੇ ਅਕਾਲ ਕੈ ਅਮਰ ਪਦ ਦੀਨੇ ਹੈਂ॥
column5: Those who practiced the consecration of True Guru saved themselves from the repeated births in the life of various species and achieved immortality.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਖੋਇ ਹੋਇ ਰੇਨੁ; ਤ੍ਰਿਕੁਟੀ ਤ੍ਰਿਬੇਨੀ ਪਾਰਿ ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਚੀਨੇ ਹੈਂ॥
column5: Those who toil on the Naam Simran of the Lord with full devotion and love, become humble by renouncing ego and crossing all hurdles merge into Him.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਬਰਨ ਅਬਰਨ ਭਏ; ਭੈ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਨਿਵਾਰਿ ਡਾਰਿ ਨਿਰਭੈ ਕੈ ਲੀਨੇ ਹੈਂ॥੨੪॥
column5: They are free from caste, creed, race and colour-based social inequalities and becoming fearless merge with the Fearless Lord. (24)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਜੰਤ ਸੰਤ ਨਾਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: By adopting and accepting the Guru's word as true and immortal, a lowly and base person can become pious. By concentrating on the precepts of Guru, even a lowly and trivial person can rise to become a holy man.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਅਬਿਬੇਕੀ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: The thoughtless and ignorant person becomes rational and considerate once he accepts the truth of Guru's wisdom. He also becomes free from all desires and wants.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਅਗਿਆਨੀ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨੀ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਸਹਜ ਬਿਸ੍ਰਾਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who is wandering in the darkness of ignorance becomes a Brahm Gyani once he accepts the truth of Guru's wisdom and teachings. By practicing the teachings of Guru with full devotion and confidence, one reaches a state of equipoise.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਭਏ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਧਾਮ ਹੈ॥੨੫॥
column5: By accepting Guru's teachings as true and practicing them with concentration, devotion and faith, one attains salvation when still alive and secures a place in the higher realms of the Lord. (25)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਬੈਰ ਨਿਰਬੈਰ ਭਏ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਬ ਮੈ ਜਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who abide by Guru's teachings with faith and sincerity are without rancour. They carry no enmity for anyone because they have realised His presence in everybody.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਭੇਦ ਨਿਰਭੇਦ ਭਏ; ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਬਿਧਿ ਨਿਖੇਧ ਖੇਦ ਬਿਨਸਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who practice the teachings of the Guru are free from discriminatory temperament. All are alike for them. The sense of dualism and attitude of condemnation of others disappear from their mind.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਬਾਇਸ ਪਰਮਹੰਸ; ਗਿਆਨ ਅੰਸ ਬੰਸ ਨਿਰਗੰਧ ਗੰਧ ਠਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those crow-like dross-filled persons who adopt Guru's wisdom as truth are able to shed all the dross and become clean and pious. A miniscule of spiritual knowledge help them spread the fragrance of the Lord like Sandalwood.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਮ ਭਰਮ ਖੋਏ; ਆਸਾ ਮੈ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਹੁਇ. ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਉਰਿ ਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥੨੬॥
column5: Those who abide by the teachings of the Guru destroy all their doubts of rites and rituals. They become unattached with worldly desires and imbibe the intellect of the Guru in their hearts. (26)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਸਿੰਬਲ ਸਫਲ ਭਏ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਬਾਂਸ ਮੈ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who sincerely and faithfully follow the teachings of the True Guru turn into a fruit-bearing tree from a silk cotton tree (Simbal). That is to say that they become worthy from what good for nothing they were earlier. It is like egoistic bamboo tree

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਕੰਚਨ ਮਨੂਰ ਭਏ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਪਰਖਤ ਅੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who toil their lives on the teachings of Guru become glittering like gold (those who are highly noble and pious) from burnt out iron sludge (useless persons). The ignorants acquire assayer intellect and become knowledgeable.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਾਲ ਕੂਟ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਕਾਲ ਮੈ ਅਕਾਲ ਭਏ ਅਸਥਿਰ ਕੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who imbibe Guru's teachings as true are filled with spiritual happiness shedding all the attachment with maya. They do not fear death any more and their body rests in the memory of the Lord forever.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤ ਭਏ; ਮਾਯਾ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸ ਬਾਸ ਬੰਧ ਨਿਰਬੰਧ ਹੈ॥੨੭॥
column5: such people are emancipated from the love and attachment of worldly pleasures despite staying and living their life-span in this world. (27)

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਸਸਿ ਘਰਿ ਸੂਰੁ ਪੂਰਿ ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The union of Guru and Sikh leads the Sikh to focus his mind on the divine word. The irha, pingla and Sukhmana enter the tenth door of the Sikh making him realise himself and granting him spiritual peace.

column2: ਉਲਟਿ ਪਵਨ ਮਨ ਮੀਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਬੇਨੀ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗ; ਤ੍ਰਿਕੁਟੀ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Practicing Naam Simran, the frolicsome mind becomes peaceful and crossing all hurdles gets engrossed in the realm of peace and tranquillity-the Dasam Duar. They are not to bear the torments of yogic practices.

column2: ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚਤੁਰਥ ਪਦ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਕੈ; ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਅਮਿਅ ਚੁਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: A practitioner of Naam detaches himself from the three pronged influence of mammon i.e. the worldly attractions and reach the stage of absolute.

column2: ਚਕਈ ਚਕੋਰ ਮੋਰ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਅਨੰਦਮਈ; ਕਦਲੀ ਕਮਲ ਸੋ ਬਿਮਲ ਜਲ ਛਾਏ ਹੈ॥੨੮॥
column5: Just as Chakvi (Sun bird) seeing sun, Chakor (moon bird) seeing moon, rain bird and peacock seeing clouds get into wondrous stage of bliss, similarly a ·Gunnukh (Guru conscious person) who practices Naam Simran keeps progressing like a lotus flower in the

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਪੰਚ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਮਿਟੇ ਪੰਚ ਪਰਧਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the meeting of Guru and Sikh, and engrossment of the latter in the divine word, he is able to counter the deceit of five vices-kam, krodh, lobh, moh and ahankar. The five virtues of Truth, Contentment, Compassion, Devotion and Patience become paramou

column2: ਭਾਗੇ ਭੈ ਭਰਮ ਭੇਦ. ਕਾਲ ਅਉ ਕਰਮ ਖੇਦ; ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਉਦੋਤ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: All his doubts, fear and discriminatory feelings are destroyed. He is not hunted by the worldly discomforts those accrue from worldly activities.

column2: ਮਾਇਆ ਅਉ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਮ ਦਸਮ ਦੁਆਰ ਪਾਰਿ; ਅਨਹਦ ਰੁਣਝੁਣ ਬਾਜਤ ਨੀਸਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: With his conscious awareness firmly lodged in the mystical tenth opening, the worldly attractions and Lord appears alike to him. He sees the image of the Lord in every creature of the world. And in such a state, he remains engrossed in the celestial music

column2: ਉਨਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਗਗਨ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ; ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੨੯॥
column5: In such a high spiritual state, he enjoys heavenly bliss and the divine light shines in him. He is ever relishing the divine elixir of Naam. (29)

column2: ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਮਹਿ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤੀ ਹੁਇ ਪਾਇਓ ਨ ਸਹਜ ਘਰ; ਬਨ ਬਨਵਾਸ ਨ ਉਦਾਸ ਫਲ ਪਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Without Guru's teaching and all by himself a householder engrossed in all household duties cannot reach the state of oneness with Lord nor renouncing the world and living in jungles can he attain Him

column2: ਪੜ੍ਹਿ ਪੜ੍ਹਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਨ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਚਾਰੀ; ਸਿਧਾਸਨ ਕੈ ਨ ਨਿਜ ਆਸਨ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: By becoming a scholar, reading scriptures no one can become knowledgeable of the magnificence of the Lord and describe Him. Nor by doing Yogic practices can one merge in Him.

column2: ਜੋਗ ਧਿਆਨੁ ਧਾਰਨ ਕੈ ਨਾਥਨ ਨ ਦੇਖੇ ਨਾਥ; ਜਗਿ ਭੋਗ ਪੂਜਾ ਕੈ ਨ ਅਗਹੁ ਗਹਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Yogis, Naths could not realise Him by their strenuous yogic practices, nor can He be attained by doing yags etc.

column2: ਦੇਵੀ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਕੈ ਨ ਅਹੰਮੇਵ ਟੇਵ ਟਾਰੀ; ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸਮਝਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥੩੦॥
column5: Serving gods and goddesses one cannot get rid of one's ego. All this worship and offerings before these gods and goddesses only inflate the ego. The Lord who is beyond reach and description can only be reached with the teachings, knowledge and wisdom of t

column2: ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚਤੁਰਥ ਗੁਨ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਕੈ; ਪੰਚ ਤਤ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਪਰਮ ਤਤ ਵਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: detaching himself from the worldly attractions and the three its of maya, a Guru-conscious person acquires fourth state and discarding all adorations of the body lives in the memory the Lord.

column2: ਖਟ ਰਸ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਕਉ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤਿ ਭਏ; ਪੂਰਿ ਸਰ ਸਪਤ ਅਨਹਦ ਅਭਿਆਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is not enamoured by the tastes of worldly things, and enjoys the bliss of love of the Lord; and celestial music by keeping Him in his mind all the time

column2: ਅਸਟ ਸਿਧਾਂਤ ਭੇਦਿ ਨਾਥਨ ਕੈ ਨਾਥ ਭਏ; ਦਸਮ ਸਥਲ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਬਿਲਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: he renunciates yog and ways of Naths and surpasses them ;all-spiritually, and reaching the ultimate, enjoys all happiness and peace.

column2: ਉਨਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਗਗਨ ਹੁਇ ਨਿਝਰ ਝਰੈ; ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਚੈ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥੩੧॥
column5: because of his high spiritual state and residing his conscious awareness in the Dasam Duar, he gets detached from worldly things and remains in a state of bliss. (31)

column2: ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨਿਵਾਰਿ ਅਬਰਨ ਹੁਇ ਬਰਨ ਬਿਖੈ; ਪਾਂਚ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਨ ਦਰਸ ਅਦਰਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: By perpetual meditation on Lord's name, a Guru-conscious person distances himself from duality and caste discrimination. He frees himself from the grip of five vices (lust, anger, greed, ego and attachment) nor does he entangle himself in the rationales o

column2: ਪਰਮ ਪਾਰਸ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਿ ਪਾਰਸ ਭਏ; ਕਨਿਕ ਅਨਿਕ ਧਾਤ ਆਪਾ ਅਪਰਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as an iron piece when touched with a philosopher-stone becomes gold, similarly a devotee meeting Guru becomes a pious and a clean man.

column2: ਨਵ ਦੁਆਰ ਪਾਰਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਸਨ ਸਿੰਘਾਸਨ ਮੈ; ਨਿਝਰ ਝਰਨਿ ਰੁਚਤ ਨ ਅਨਰਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Overcoming the pleasures of the nine doors of the body, he rests his faculties in the tenth door, where the divine elixir flows perpetually that turn him away from all other pleasures.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਬੀਸ ਇਕਈਸ ਈਸ; ਅਨਹਦ ਗਦ ਗਦ ਅਭਰ ਭਰਸ ਹੈ॥੩੨॥
column5: Be assured that the meeting of Guru and a disciple, makes a disciple realise Lord and virtually becomes like Him. His heart then remains immersed in the celestial music. (32)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਭਜਿ ਕਮਲ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਏ; ਦਰਸ ਦਰਸਿ ਸਮਦਰਸ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By taking refuge of Sat guru's lotus feet, a devotee's mind )0 blooms like lotus flower. By the blessings of a True Guru, he treats and conducts himself alike with all and sundry. He carries no rancour for anyone.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਨਹਦ ਲਿਵਲੀਨ ਭਏ; ਉਨਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਗਗਨ ਪੁਰ ਛਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such Guru-conscious person attaches his mind in the unstruck celestial music and enjoying heavenley bliss, rests his mind in the Dasam Duar.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸਿ ਹੋਇ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਦੇਹ ਭਏ; ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਮੈ ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Enamoured by the love of Lord, he does not remain conscious of his body any more. This is such a wondrous state that surprises everyone.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖਫਲ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧਿ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ. ਕਹਤ ਨ ਆਏ ਹੈ॥੩੩॥
column5: The spiritually ecstatic state of a Guru's disciple cannot even be praised. It is beyond contemplation and indescribable too. (33)

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮੇਟਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੀ; ਖੋਇ ਕੈ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਜਾਨੇ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: When a disciple meets his Guru and he works hard and toil himself on his precepts, he gets rid of base intellect and divine intelligence is revealed to him. He sheds his ignorance and acquires His knowledge.

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਬਿਸਿਮਰਨ ਕੈ; ਸਬਰ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮੋਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਤ ਪਰਵਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the glimpse of the True Guru and focusing his mind, he weans away his attention from worldly pleasures and concentrates the divine word in his consciousness and closes his mind from all other attractions.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੁਇ ਅਨਰਸ ਰਹਤ ਹੁਇ; ਜੋਤੀ ਮਹਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਰੂਪ. ਸੋਹੰ ਸੁਰ ਤਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: In His love, leaving all worldly pleasures, getting absorbed in His Naam, he keeps on remembering Him all the time.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਬੀਸ ਇਕਈਸ ਈਸ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਏਕ ਹੀਯੇ ਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥੩੪॥
column5: Believe it for Sure that by meeting with Guru, a Guru conscious person becomes one with Lord and all his life depends on Naam Simran-an exclusive support of the Lord. (34)

column2: ਰੋਮ ਰੋਮ ਕੋਟਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਂਡ ਕੋ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਜਾਸੁ; ਮਾਨਸ ਅਉਤਾਰ ਧਾਰਿ ਦਰਸੁ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Supreme Lord, whose each hair support millions of cosmos has incarnated as Satguru in human form.

column2: ਜਾ ਕੈ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਕੇ ਅਕਾਰ ਹੈ ਨਾਨਾ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ; ਸ੍ਰੀਮੁਖ ਸਬਦੁ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਸੁਨਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Omniprotector Lord who has many forms, appearing as Guru has given sermon to his disciples in person.

column2: ਜਗ ਭੋਗ ਨਈਬੇਦ ਜਗਤ ਭਗਤ ਜਾਹਿ; ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਲਡਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The God for whose propitiation yags are performed, food and offerings are made, the same Lord takes the form of Guru now coddling his Sikhs by distributing food and to his disciples.

column2: ਨਿਗਮ ਸੇਖਾਦਿਕ ਕਥਤ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਕਰਿ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਲਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥੩੫॥
column5: The supreme Creator, whom Shesh Nag and others have calling by innumerable names, now manifests as Guru showing Himself to His devotees (The Sikhs). (35)

column2: ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਸਰਗੁਨ ਕੈ ਅਲਖ ਅਬਿਗਤ ਰੂਪ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਰੂਪ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Supreme Lord whose countenance is beyond perception, who is indestructible, despite being formless took human form and revealed Himself as Guru.

column2: ਸਰਗੁਨ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਕੈ ਧਿਆਨ ਰੂਪ; ਅਕੁਲ ਅਕਾਲ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: God in His immanent form as Satguru who is beyond all castes, creed and race make the Sikhs realise the true form of God.

column2: ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਅਨਹਦ ਧੁਨਿ; ਸਬਦ ਬੇਧੀ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਸੁਨਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The heart piercing melodious tune which Satguru sings to his Sikhs is in fact manifestation of True Lord.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਗਤਿ ਲਪਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥੩੬॥
column5: Fragrance of the dust (of the lotus feet of such Satguru) with which the Sikhs remain attached is capable of destroying all worldly desires. (36)

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰੁ ਬੇਲ ਹੁਇ ਚੰਬੇਲੀ ਗਤਿ; ਮੂਲ ਸਾਖਾ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਕਰਿ ਬਿਬਿਧ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Satguru, manifestation of Almighty God, is like jasmine creeper of which He Himself is the root and all His devotees and pious persons are its leaves and branches.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਪੁਹਪ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਨਿਜ ਰੂਪ ਤਾ ਮੈ; ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਹੁਇ ਕਰਤ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕੋ ਉਧਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Pleased at the services of His devotees (like Bhai Lehna Ji, Baba Amar Das Ji, etc.) Satguru turns those devotees by His grace and makes them into fragrance-spreading flowers and by manifesting in them is liberating the world.

column2: ਤਿਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਾਸਨਾ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਕੋ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਕਰਿ; ਆਪਾ ਖੋਇ ਹੋਇ ਹੈ ਫੁਲੇਲ ਮਹਿਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as sesame seed loses its existence and becomes scent when unites with the fragrance of the flowers, the devotees too lose themselves to the Lord through meditation and spread divine fragrance in the world.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗ ਮੈ ਪਤਿਤ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਰੀਤਿ; ਸੰਸਾਰੀ ਹੁਇ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੩੭॥
column5: Sikhism has a tradition of changing sinners into holy persons. And in this path, this is a very righteous task and service towards others. Those engrossed in material world are converted into God-loving and godly persons. They are detached from maya (mamm

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰੁ ਬਿਰਖ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਧਾਰ; ਮੂਲ ਕੰਦ ਸਾਖਾ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Satguru, a total form of complete Lord is like a fragrant tree, whose spread has many branches, leaves, flowers in the form of Sikhs.

column2: ਤਾ ਮੈ ਨਿਜ ਰੂਪ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਫਲ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ; ਬਾਸਨਾ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਅਉ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the strenuous toil of devoted Sikhs like Bhai Lehna Ji and Baba Amar Das Ji, the True Guru illuminated His own light in them. Engulfed in the desire of worship and fragrance of the Lord, these pious souls are keen to spread and distribute the elixir-li

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੁਇ; ਚਾਖੇ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕੋ ਉਧਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such Gursikhs enjoying the fragrance of the dust of Lord's lotus-feet emancipate others from the world.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ; ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੩੮॥
column5: The glory of the path of Sikhism cannot be described. All that we can say is that He is infinite, infinite and beyond and is worthy of our salutes myriad times. (38)

column2: ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਬਹੁ ਬਰਨ ਗੋਬੰਸ ਜੈਸੇ; ਏਕ ਹੀ ਬਰਨ ਦੁਹੇ ਦੂਧ ਜਗ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as the cows are of many breeds and colours, yet the whole world knows that they all yield milk of the same colour.

column2: ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਫਲ ਫੂਲ ਕੈ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਏਕੈ ਰੂਪ ਅਗਨਿ ਸਰਬ ਮੈ ਸਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: There are many species of fruit and flower trees but all carry the same latent fire in them.

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਬਰਨ ਪਾਨ ਚੂਨਾ ਅਉ ਸੁਪਾਰੀ ਕਾਥਾ; ਆਪਾ ਖੋਇ ਮਿਲਤ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਠਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Four different colours-beetle leaf, Supari (beetle nut), Kattha (extract of the bark of accacia) and lime shed their own colour and merge in each other in a Paan and make beautiful red colour.

column2: ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੩੯॥
column5: Similarly the Guru-conscious person (Gurmukh) renounces various worldly pleasures and adopts one colour of the formless God. And because of the blessings of his Guru who has taught him to unite with the divine word and his mind, he achieves higher spiritu

column2: ਸੀਂਚਤ ਸਲਿਲ ਬਹੁ ਬਰਨ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਏਕੈ ਚੰਦਨ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: By irrigation, several kinds of plants and vegetation can be grown but when they come in touch with sandalwood they are all called sandalwood (because they have the same fragrance).

column2: ਪਰਬਤ ਬਿਖੈ ਉਤਪਤ ਹੁਇ ਅਸਟ ਧਾਤ; ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਿ ਏਕੈ ਕੰਚਨ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Eight metals are obtained from mountain but when each one of them are touched by philosopher-stone become gold.

column2: ਨਿਸ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਤਾਰਾ ਮੰਡਲ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ; ਦਿਨ ਦਿਨਕਰ ਜੋਤਿ ਏਕੈ ਪਰਵਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: In the darkness of the night, many stars shine but during the day, the light of one Sun alone is considered authentic.

column2: ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੪੦॥
column5: Similarly a Sikh who lives life according to the advice of his Guru becomes divine in all respects, even when he is living life as a worldly person. Due to the lodging of the divine word in his mind, he is known to be living in heavenly state. (40)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਗੁਰ ਜਨ ਮੈ ਘੂਘਟ ਪਟ; ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਸਮੈ ਅੰਤਰੁ ਨ ਪੀਅ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a daughter-in-law covers herself with veil in front of the elders of the house, but keeps no distance from her husband at the time of sharing his bed;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਣਿ ਅਛਤ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਹੀ ਸਹਿਤ ਅਹਿ; ਬੰਕ ਤਨ ਸੂਧੋ ਬਿਲ ਪੈਸਤ ਹੁਇ ਜੀਅ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a snake remains crooked when with female snake and his family, but becomes straight when it enters in the burrow;

column2: ਮਾਤਾ ਪਿਤਾ ਅਛਤ ਨ ਬੋਲੈ ਸੁਤ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਸੈ; ਪਾਛੈ ਕੈ ਦੈ ਸਰਬਸ ਮੋਹੁ ਸੁਤ ਤੀਅ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a son avoids talking to his wife in front of his parents, but when alone showers all his love upon her,

column2: ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਉਨਮਨ ਮਨ ਹੀਅ ਸੈ॥੪੧॥
column5: Similarly a devoted Sikh appears worldly among others but having attached his mind with Guru's word, he rises spiritually and realises the Lord. Quintessence: One may maintain oneself as a worldly person outwardly but inwardly one keeps oneself attached w

column2: ਜੋਗ ਬਿਖੈ ਭੋਗ ਅਰੁ ਭੋਗ ਬਿਖੈ ਜੋਗ ਜਤ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥ ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਸੈ ਅਤੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Where yogic practitioners have innate desire for worldly relishments and the worldly people desire to become yogi, but those treading the path of Guru maintain a very different and unique desire in their hearts than the yogis.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਧਿਆਨ ਅਰੁ ਧਿਆਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਬੇਧੇ ਗਿਆਨ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ ਅਜੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those following the path of Gyan (knowledge) keep their mind focused on contemplation while those in contemplation are wandering for Gyan. But the state of a person treading the path of his Guru is above those persons who are pursuing Gyan or Dhyan (conte

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੈ ਭਗਤਿ ਅਰ ਭਗਤਿ ਕੈ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ; ਅਲਖ ਭਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰੀਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Followers of path of love yearn for devotion and those on path of devotion desire love, but the innate desire of Guru-conscious person is to remain engrossed in loving worship of God.

column2: ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਸਰਗੁਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਰਿਦੈ; ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਪਾਰਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤਿ ਹੈ॥੪੨॥
column5: Many seekers hold a faith on the worship of Transcendental Lord while others hold a strange view of worship of God. Perhaps their belief and understanding is half baked. But the disciples of Guru hold their faith on the Lord much above these strange devot

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਦਿਬਿ ਦੇਹ ਦਿਬਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਹੁਇ; ਦਿਬਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੋ ਧਿਆਨ ਦਿਬਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਾਂਤ ਕੈ॥
column5: With just a miniscule of clement look of Satguru, the body and looks of a disciple of the Guru become divine. He then starts seeing the presence of the Lord all around him.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਹੁਇ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ; ਅਨਹਦ ਗੰਮਿ ਉਨਮਨੀ ਕੋ ਮਤਾਂਤ ਕੈ॥
column5: By meditating on Gur Shabad (Guru's Word) and taking its refuge, Guru's precepts are revealed to him. When he reaches the state of listening to unstruck melody of the divine word, he enjoys the bliss of higher state of equipoise.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਕਰਨੀ ਕੈ ਉਪਜਤ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸੁ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਨਿਜ ਕ੍ਰਾਂਤਿ ਕੈ॥
column5: Concentrating on the knowledge of the True Guru, listening to his counsel, practicing contemplation and living life according to His command, a feeling of love grows and blossoms. And in living this life of love, a Guru-conscious person realises the radia

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਦਲ ਸੰਪਟ ਮਧੁਪ ਗਤਿ; ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਮਧੁ ਪਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਕੈ॥੪੩॥
column5: As the bumble bee attains the divine bliss by drinking the elixir and getting shut in the box-like petals of a lotus flower, similarly in order to provide spiritual peace to his life, a true seeker enjoins the lotus-like feet of Guru and drinks deep by co

column2: ਸੂਆ ਗਹਿ ਨਲਿਨੀ ਕਉ ਉਲਟਿ ਗਹਾਵੈ ਆਪੁ; ਹਾਥ ਸੈ ਛਡਾਏ ਛਾਡੈ ਪਰ ਬਸਿ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: A parrot catcher fixes a rotating pipe/tube on which a parrot comes and sits. The pipe rotates and the parrot hangs upside down. He does not let go the pipe. The parrot catcher then comes and frees his claws. Thus he becomes a slave.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਬਾਰੰਬਾਰ ਟੇਰਿ ਟੇਰਿ ਕਹੇ ਪਠੋ ਪਠੋ; ਆਪਨੋ ਹੀ ਨਾਉ ਸੀਖਿ ਆਪ ਹੀ ਪੜਾਵਈ॥
column5: As the parrot is trained and taught to say words, he repeatedly speak those words. He learns to speak his own name and he teaches it to others as well.

column2: ਰਘੁਬੰਸੀ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਗਾਲ ਜਾਮਨੀ ਸੁ ਭਾਖਾ; ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਗਤਿ ਬੁਧਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਵਈ॥
column5: A parrot learns to pronounce the name of Ram from the devotees of Ram. From the wicked and unrighteous, he learns bad names. In the company of Greeks, he learns their language. He develops his intellect according to the company he keeps.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮਿਲੇ; ਆਪਾ ਆਪੁ ਚੀਨਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਵਈ॥੪੪॥
column5: Similarly in the company of holy men, and taking the refuge of the lotus-like feet of the Satguru, the Sikh in attendance of his Guru realises his self and enjoys the true bliss and peace. (44)

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਮਹਿ ਦਰਸ. ਦਰਸ ਮਹਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ; ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਅਦਰਸ ਗੁਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the vision of Guru-conscious persons rests the image of the True Guru, and in the eyes of the True Guru, rests the glimpse of the disciple. Because of this attention of Satguru, these disciples keep away from the worldly attractions.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਮਹਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ. ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਹਿ ਸਬਦ ਧੁਨਿ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਗਮਿਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: They remain engrossed in the words of the Guru and the tune of these words remain lodged in their consciousness. But the knowledge of word and consciousness is beyond reach.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਕਰਨੀ ਕੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਤ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸੁ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਨਿਰਬਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By following the teachings of the True Guru and moulding one's character in accordance with the contemplation of Lord's qualities, a sense of love develops. The well defined routine of Guru's philosophy, leads one to free oneself from the worldly shackles

column2: ਪਿੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨਪਤਿ ਬੀਸ ਕੋ ਬਰਤਮਾਨ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਇਕਈਸ ਮੋ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥੪੫॥
column5: Living life in the world, a Guru-conscious person always believes that his life belongs to the Master of Life-God. Remaining engrossed in One Lord is the wealth of happiness of the Guru-conscious persons. (45)

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਹੁਇ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਛਤ੍ਰਪਤਿ ਭਏ; ਸਹਜ ਸਿੰਘਾਸਨ ਕੈ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਰਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Guru-conscious person feels like a mighty king when he is able to concentrate his mind on words and acts as per the teachings of Gum. When he is able to rest in a state of equipoise, he feels like an emperor of infallible kingdom.

column2: ਸਤ੍ਯ ਅਉ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਦਇਆ ਧਰਮ ਅਰਥ ਮੇਲਿ; ਪੰਚ ਪਰਵਾਨ ਕੀਏ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: By imbibing the five virtues of Truth, Contentment, Compassion, Righteousness and Purpose in accordance with the teachings of the Gum, he becomes acceptable and an honourable person.

column2: ਸਕਲ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਅਉ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਸਭਾ; ਸਿਵ ਨਗਰੀ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਕੋਟ ਛਬਿ ਛਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: All materials and worldly treasures are his. The divine abode of Dasam Duar is his fort where the continuous presence of melodious Naam makes him a unique and glorious person.

column2: ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿ ਰੀਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਪਰਜਾ ਕੈ ਸੁਖੈ ਸੁਖ; ਪੂਰਨ ਮਨੋਰਥ ਸਫਲ ਸਭ ਕਾਜ ਹੈ॥੪੬॥
column5: The loving and affectionate treatment of such a king-like disciple of the True Guru with other human beings is his statesmanship that spreads happiness, peace and success all around him. (46)

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਹੁਇ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ; ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਸੁਧਿ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: When a Guru-conscious person achieves harmony with his mind, words and actions, and by the blessings of the refuge of True Guru, he acquires knowledge of times and the three worlds.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਸਾਧਿ ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਕਥਾ; ਅੰਤਰਿ ਦਿਸੰਤਰ ਨਿਰੰਤਰੀ ਜਤਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: By practicing on Naam, a Guru-conscious person lives in a state of equipoise. Any description of that state is beyond our comprehension. It is indescribable. By dint of that state, he becomes aware of all that is happening in every nook and corner of the

column2: ਖੰਡ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਪਿੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨਪਤਿ ਗਤਿ; ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਸੋਹੰ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the union of Guru and Sikh, the seeker feels the presence of the Lord of Cosmos in his body and his life-giving support; and when he achieves oneness with God, he remains engrossed in the memory of Lord.

column2: ਦਰਪਨ ਦਰਸ ਅਉ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ ਧੁਨਿ ਜੰਤ੍ਰੀ ਬਿਧਿ; ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਸੂਤ ਏਕੈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥੪੭॥
column5: As the mirror and image in it, music and the musical instrument, waft and woof of a cloth are all part of each other and inseparable, so does the Guru-conscious person becomes one with God and is freed of all doubts of duality. (47)

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਹੁਇ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ; ਤਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਗਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Because of the refuge of True Guru and moulding his mind, words and actions as per His teachings, a Guru-conscious person learns the happenings of the three worlds innately. He recognises the true Lord residing within.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕਰਮ. ਕਰਮ ਮਨ ਬਚਨ ਕੈ; ਬਚਨ ਕਰਮ ਮਨ ਉਨਮਨੀ ਛਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the harmony of actions, mind and words, the thoughts of the mind, the utterance of the words and actions performed are influenced.

column2: ਗਿਆਨੀ ਧਿਆਨੀ ਕਰਨੀ ਜਿਉ ਗੁਰ ਮਹੂਆ ਕਮਾਦ; ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਭਾਠੀ ਕੈ ਚੁਆਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: As wine is brewed from jaggery, sugarcane and Madhuca Indica flowers, so does a Guru-conscious person obtains the unique flow of elixir of Naam when Gyan of his Guru's precepts, Dhyan (concentration of mind) on these precepts and clean actions are perform

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਪੂਰਨ ਹੁਇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਈ ਹੈ॥੪੮॥
column5: Guru-conscious person satiates himself by drinking deep the loving elixir of Lord's name and by his union with the divine word of the True Guru, he resides in a state of equipoise. (48)

column2: ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਰਖ ਬਲੀ ਫਲ ਫੁਲ ਮੂਲ ਸਾਖਾ; ਰਚਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The vegetation is seen in many forms like trees, creepers, fruit, flowers, roots and branches. This beautiful creation of the Lord unfolds itself in many forms of wonderful artistic skills.

column2: ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਫਲ. ਬਹੁ ਬਿਧਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਰਸ; ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਫੂਲ. ਬਾਸਨਾ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: These trees and creepers bear fruits of different tastes and flavour, flowers of myriad shape and colour. All of them spread various types of fragrance.

column2: ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਮੂਲ. ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਸਾਖਾ; ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਸੁਗਨ ਅਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The trunks of the trees and creepers, their branches and leaves are of many kind and each leaves a different effect.

column2: ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਨਾਸਪਤਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਜੈਸੇ; ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਏਕੈ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੯॥
column5: As the latent fire in all these types of vegetation is the same, so do the God-loving persons find One Lord dwelling in the hearts of all the living beings in this world. (49)

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਲਿਵ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ ਸਾਧ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: When the devoted Guru-conscious person becomes one with the True form of the True Lord, his vision enjoins the holy sight of Guru. He who practices meditation on Lord's name remains attached with the words of wisdom of the True Guru.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸੁਧਿ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the union of True Guru and his disciple (Gursikh) the disciple obeys the command of his Guru very sincerely and faithfully. By meditating on the Lord, he learns to reflect on the True Guru.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਸ੍ਵਾਮੀ ਸੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਕਰਨੀ ਕਮਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Thus union of a disciple with Guru imbibes the trait of service of the Master. He serves all without reward or desire as he has learnt that he is serving Him who resides in all.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਕਰਨੀ ਸੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਈ ਹੈ॥੫੦॥
column5: Such a person emerges as a person with ideal actions by virtue of the meditation and reflection on Lord. In the process, he attains equipoise and remains engrossed in it. (50)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਧਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ; ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਕਾਰ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: When a Guru-conscious person lives in harmony with his Guru, his mind is absorbed in the remembrance of God. He then realises that all forms are actually His forms.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ; ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: And when he establishes his relationship with Him, he realises through the medium of meditation on His name that Formless Lord has manifested Himself in various forms and shapes.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਸ੍ਵਾਮੀ ਸੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਅਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The union of a devoted Sikh with True Guru renders him with an attitude of service and benevolence and he yearns to be available at His service. He then develops character of loving devotion and divine reflection.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਗਤਿ; ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੫੧॥
column5: The state of union of a God-conscious person and his True Guru is glorious and full of astonishment. No other state can equal it. He is worthy of salutation infinite time, again and again. (51)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕਰਮ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਭਏ; ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਬਿਸਮ ਸ੍ਰਬੰਗ ਮੈ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By practicing the teachings of Guru with mind, words and action, the devoted Sikh in attendance keeps every limb of his body in the memory of blissful Omnipresent Lord all the time.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕੈ ਮਦੋਨ; ਰਸਨਾ ਥਕਤ ਭਈ. ਕਹਿਤ ਨ ਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: He remains in a state of trance by drinking the loving elixir of Naam: He relishes not any other pleasure of life anymore.

column2: ਜਗਮਗ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਜੋਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਲੋਚਨ ਚਕਿਤ ਭਏ ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The wonderful elixir that has caused him to live such a celestial state of trance is indescribable.

column2: ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਧੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ; ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਬਿਲੈ ਬਿਲੈ ਬਿਲਾਏ ਹੈ॥੫੨॥
column5: The radiance of love for Naam Simran exists in him a strange form that surprises all the beholders. (52)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕਰਮ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਭਏ; ਪੂਰਨ ਪਰਮਪਦ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By virtue of the harmonious state of mind, words and actions, a Guru's disciple who is blessed with the loving elixir of Naam Simran, reaches a highly conscious state.

column2: ਲੋਚਨ ਮੈ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਰਸ ਗੰਧਿ ਸੰਧਿ; ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗੰਧ ਰਸ ਪਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By virtue of the fragrance of Naam relishment, he is blessed with True Guru-like glimpse. His ears perpetually hear His celestial music.

column2: ਰਸਨਾ ਮੈ ਰਸ ਗੰਧ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮੇਲ;
column5: This harmonious integration of word and consciousness renders his tongue to be sweet and comfort-giving.

column2: ਨਾਸ ਬਾਸ ਰਸ ਸ੍ਰੁਤਿ ਸਬਦ ਲਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The exhaling of his breath too is fragrant and reflects his high state of harmonious relationship between his mental faculties and Naam.

column2: ਰੋਮ ਰੋਮ ਰਸਨਾ. ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ ਨਾਸਾ ਕੋਟਿ; ਖੰਡ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਪਿੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਮੈ ਜਤਾਏ ਹੈ॥੫੩॥
column5: Thus by perpetual meditation on Him, with the relishing fragrance of Lord's name residing on his tongue, eyes, ears and nostrils, a Guru-conscious person realises the presence of Lord who abides in millions cosmoses within himself. (53)

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਆਪਿ ਆਪਨ ਹੀ ਆਪੁ ਸਾਜਿ; ਆਪਨ ਰਚਿਓ ਹੈ ਨਾਉ ਆਪ ਹੀ ਬਿਚਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Omniscient and Omnipotent God Himself has created His own form and has named Himself as (Guru) Nanak.

column2: ਆਦਿ ਗੁਰ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਨਾਮ ਕੈ ਕਹਾਇਓ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਰਚਨਾ ਅਕਾਰ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: The second name that He called Himself is Gobind. The transcendental Lord took the immanent form to appear as the first Guru.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਾਦ ਬੇਦ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਭੇਦ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਲੀਲਾਧਾਰੀ ਅਨਿਕ ਅਉਤਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Lord Himself is the precept of Vedas and He Himself knows all the secrets that lie therein. Lord Himself has created this wonderful act and is manifesting in many forms and bodies

column2: ਗੁਰ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਅਉ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੁਰੂ ਏਕ ਮੇਕ; ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਸੂਤ੍ਰ ਗਤਿ ਅੰਬਰ ਉਚਾਰ ਕੈ॥੫੪॥
column5: Like waft and woof of a cloth, both Guru and Gobind (God) are not different to each other. (54)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬੀਜ ਬੋਏ ਹੋਤ ਬਿਰਖ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਗੁਰ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the seed sown develops into a tree and with time it expands, so has a True Guru emerged out of the one divine form of the all knowing, all powerful, Omnipotent God.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਬਿਰਖ ਸੈ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਫਲ; ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a tree yields countless fruits, so is the gathering of many disciples (Gursikhs) of the True Guru.

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ; ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਸਰਗੁਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Focusing mind on the holy form of True Guru who is the immanent manifestation of Lord, his percepts in the shape of word, its contemplation and understanding of the Transcendental form of God is in reality the contemplation of immanent Lord.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਥਾਨ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਉਧਾਰ ਹੈ॥੫੫॥
column5: By assembling in the holy congregation at the appointed place and meditating on Lord's name with total concentration and loving worship, can one sail through the worldly ocean. (55)

column2: ਫਲ ਮੂਲ. ਮੂਲ ਫਲ. ਮੂਲ ਫਲ. ਫਲ ਮੂਲ; ਆਦਿ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ ਅਰੁ ਅੰਤ ਕੈ ਅਨੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: From fruit one begets a seed and seed develops into a tree to give fruit, and this process continues. This system of growth has been in vogue before the beginning. Its end is beyond the end.

column2: ਪਿਤ ਸੁਤ. ਸੁਤ ਪਿਤ. ਸੁਤ ਪਿਤ. ਪਿਤ ਸੁਤ; ਉਤਪਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਗੂੜ ਮੂਲਮੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Father begets a son and the son then becomes a father and begets son. Thus continues the system of father-son-father. This convention of creation has very deep quintessence.

column2: ਪਥਿਕ ਬਸੇਰਾ ਕੋ ਨਿਬੇਰਾ ਜਿਉ ਨਿਕਸ ਬੈਠ; ਇਤ ਉਤ ਵਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਿਧੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the end of journey of a traveller depends upon his embarking a boat and then de-embarking from it, crossing the river defines its near and far ends, and these ends keep changing depending upon which direction a traveller is crossing the river from.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਗੁਰ; ਅਬਿਗਤ ਗਤਿ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਤ ਹੈ॥੫੬॥
column5: Similarly all powerful, all knowing Guru is God Himself. He is both Guru and God. This incomprehensible state can best be understood by a Guru-conscious person. (56)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥ ਗਹੇ. ਜਮਪੁਰਿ ਪੰਥ ਮੇਟੇ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਿ ਪੰਚ ਦੂਤ ਸੰਗ ਤਿਆਗੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Treading the path of Guru's percepts, a Sikh is freed from the fear of death. By keeping company of the holy Sangat (congregation) even vices like lust, anger, avarice, attachment and pride are shed.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਕਰਮ ਭਰਮ ਖੋਏ; ਦਰਸ ਅਕਾਲ ਕਾਲ ਕੰਟਕ ਭੈ ਭਾਗੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: By taking the refuge of Satguru, one destroys all effects of the past deeds. And beholding the God-like form of Satguru, the fear of death disappears.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਵੇਸ ਬੱਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਖੁਲੇ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮੂਰਛਤ ਮਨ ਜਾਗੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Abiding by the sermons of the Satguru, all desires and apprehensions vanish. By engrossing the mind in the holy words of the Guru, the mammon-gripped unconscious mind becomes alert.

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਏ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੇ ਹੈ॥੫੭॥
column5: Even a subtle element of grace of the Satguru is no less than all the worldly treasures. By engrossing the mind in the word and Naam blessed by the Satguru, one achieves salvation while still alive and living life. (57)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥ ਸੁਖ ਚਾਹਤ ਸਕਲ ਪੰਥ; ਸਕਲ ਦਰਸ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਅਧੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: All religions yearn for the comfort and peace of the path of Guru-conscious people. All cults and religions are subservient and in attendance to the path of Guru

column2: ਸੁਰ ਸੁਰਸਰਿ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨ ਚਾਹੈ; ਬੇਦ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿਕ ਸਬਦ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: All gods and their holy rivers yearn for the refuge of Satguru Ji. Brahma the creator of Vedas also longs to attach his mind in the words of Guru.

column2: ਸਰਬ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਅਵਗਾਹਨ ਮੈ; ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗੁਰ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: All the religions are seekers of the Naam Simran. By the blessings of the Guru, one gets all the treasures of the world just as a fish receives life-supporting water.

column2: ਜੋਗੀ ਜੋਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਮੈ. ਭੋਗੀ ਭੋਗ ਭੁਗਤਿ ਮੈ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਕੁਲ ਅਕੁਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥੫੮॥
column5: Just as the Yogis are perpetually immersed in practicing yogic exercises and a worldly man is ever engrossed in enjoying the relishments, similarly the devoted Sikhs remain engrossed in the higher spiritual state through Naam Simran and keep themselves un

column2: ਉਲਟਿ ਪਵਨ ਮਨ ਮੀਨ ਕੀ ਚਪਲ ਗਤਿ; ਸੁਖਮਨਾ ਸੰਗਮ ਕੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਥਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the practice of Naam Simran, Guru-conscious disciples are able to control the wayward and frolicsome mind and with sharp fish-like movement harbour their consciousness in the Dasam Duar (tenth opening), the meeting place of Irha, Pingla and Sukhmana. T

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਸਲਿਲ ਗਹਿ ਗਗਨ ਘਟਾ ਘਮੰਡ; ਉਨਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਲਗਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: With their consciousness resting in the Dasam Duar, they integrate themselves in the light eternal of the Lord just as the river merges with the water of the ocean. They remain in the ecstatic state of Naam Simran and all their interest and devotion remai

column2: ਜੋਤਿ ਮੈ ਜੋਤੀ ਸਰੂਪ ਦਾਮਿਨੀ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ; ਗਰਜਤ ਅਨਹਦ ਸਬਦ ਨੀਸਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By melding into the super radiance of the Lord, they enjoy the blissful electric radiance of the union. They hear the sound of unstruck music loud and clear.

column2: ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਬਰਖਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਜਲ; ਸੇਵਕ ਸਕਲ ਫਲ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥੫੯॥
column5: They perpetually enjoy the continuous flow of the divine elixir in the Dasam Duar and the seekers obtain all the fruits and treasures. (59)

column2: ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ. ਬੇਦਨ ਮੈ ਬੇਦ ਬਿਚਾਰ; ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਬੀਸ. ਇਕਈਸ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Guru-conscious seeker lives like a worldly being in the society and conducts himself as a knowledgeable person among the scholars. And yet for him, all these are worldly deeds and keeps him unsullied from them. He remains engrossed in the memory of the

column2: ਜੋਗ ਮੈ ਨ ਜੋਗ. ਭੋਗ ਮੈ ਨ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ; ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗਾਤੀਤ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The yogic practices do not provide a seeker with the true union of the Lord. The worldly pleasures are also devoid of true comfort and peace. Thus a Guru-conscious person keeps himself free from such distractions and enjoys the true bliss by engrossing hi

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ. ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗਿਆਨ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਲਖਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Guru-conscious person's vision is always focused on the glimpse of his Guru. His mind is always engrossed in repeated remembrance of Lord's name. In acquiring such divine awareness, he is able to receive the divine treasure of love of the Lord.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਸ੍ਰਮ ਸਾਧਨਾਧ੍ਯਾਤਮ ਕ੍ਰਮ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਸਰਬੋਤਿਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥੬੦॥
column5: Whatever good he does with mind, words and actions, is all spiritual. He enjoys all the happiness in the supreme treasure of Naam Simran. (60)

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਧਾਵਤ ਬਰਜਿ ਰਾਖੈ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਮਤਿ ਮਨ ਉਨਮਨ ਭੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By engrossing the mind in the divine word, a Guru-conscious seeker is able to arrest his wandering mind. That stabilises his memory in the meditation of Naam raising him to a higher spiritual state.

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਲਹਰ ਗਤਿ ਆਤਮ ਤਰੰਗ ਰੰਗ; ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The sea and the waves are one and the same. Similarly by becoming one with the Lord, the spiritual waves experienced are astonishing and gloriously unique. Guru-conscious people are only capable of understanding and experiencing the spiritual state.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਨਿਰਮੋਲਕ ਰਤਨ ਧਨ; ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Guru-conscious person obtains the invaluable jewel like treasure of Naam by the precepts of Guru. And once he obtains it, he remains engrossed in the practicing of Naam Simran.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਸੋਹੰ ਹੰਸੋ ਏਕ ਮੇਕ ਆਪਾ ਆਪੁ ਚੀਨ ਹੈ॥੬੧॥
column5: By the harmonious union of Guru and Sikh (disciple) the Sikh attaches his mind in the divine word that enables his self to become one with the Supreme soul. Thus he is able to recognise what he truly is. (61)

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਵਗਾਹਨ ਬਿਮਲ ਮਤਿ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Sikh who remains in service of his Guru, whose mind is engrossed in his teachings, who practices remembering Lord; his intellect becomes sharp and high. That enlightens his mind and soul with the light of Guru's knowledge.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਮਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕੈ ਦਿਬਿ ਜੋਤਿ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: With Guru's word residing in the memory, seeing and treating all alike, he experiences the divine refulgence in his soul. By attachment of his mind in the divine word, he becomes a practitioner of Fearless Lord's Naam Simran.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਪਰਮਪਦ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: By this union a Guru-conscious person achieves emancipation, the supreme spiritual state. He then rests in a state of perpetual comfort and peace and lives in a state of blissful equipoise.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੁਇ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥੬੨॥
column5: And by imbibing the divine word in his memory, Guru conscious person lives in the love of the Lord. He relishes the divine elixir forever. An astonishing devotion for the Lord develops in his mind then. (62)

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਕਾਸ ਜਲ ਅੰਤਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: When a devoted Sikh meets True Guru, his vision gets absorbed in the sight/glimpse of the Guru. And then his soul recognises everyone as if He resides in all; like the sky/space resides equally in all the water pitchers.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਜੰਤ੍ਰ ਧੁਨਿ ਜੰਤ੍ਰੀ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The union of a True Guru and a Sikh blesses the Sikh with the capability of remaining engrossed in the words/precepts of the Guru. As a musician gets totally engrossed in the tune that he is playing, so is the case of a Sikh's absorption in his Guru.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਭਏ; ਤਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਗਤਿ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the concentration of mind and Guru's words in a Guru devotee, he realises all the happenings of the three worlds within his body.

column2: ਏਕ ਅਉ ਅਨੇਕ ਮੇਕ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ; ਸ੍ਰੋਤ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਆਤਮ ਸਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥੬੩॥
column5: By the help of divine knowledge, the soul of a Guru devotee becomes harmonious with One Lord who is present in every bit of His creation. This union is like the merging of a river water in the ocean. (63)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਭਏ; ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਗਤਿ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The obedient slaves of Guru, being dyed in the hue of Naam Simran (with their mind, speech and actions being harmonious) sees the astonishing and transcendental Lord God conspicuous.

column2: ਅੰਤਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਦਿਬ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੋ ਉਦੋਤ ਭਇਓ; ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਰੂਪ ਘਟ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: And when he looks inward (concentrates his faculties within), he sees the divine light refulgent within. He sees the happenings of the three worlds in his consciousness.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਇਓ; ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਗਤਿ ਜਤਨ ਜਤਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: When the supreme treasure of Guru's Gyan (Divine knowledge) becomes effulgent in the mind of a Guru conscious person, he becomes aware of all the three worlds. And even then, he does not go astray from his objective of absorption of self into the vastness

column2: ਆਤਮ ਤਰੰਗ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਮਧ ਪਾਨ ਮਤ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਏ ਹੈ॥੬੪॥
column5: Such a devotee remains in a state of trance drinking deep the divine elixir of ecstasy. This wonderful state is beyond description. One feels amazed at this state. (64)

column2: ਬਿਨ ਰਸ ਰਸਨਾ ਬਕਤ ਹੀ ਬਹੁਤ ਬਾਤੈ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸ ਭਏ ਮੋਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਤ ਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Without tasting the elixir of Naam, an insipid tongue speaks much trash. On the contrary, by indulging in repeated utterance of His name, a devotee becomes sweet of tongue and pleasant of disposition.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕੈ ਮਦੋਨ; ਅੰਤਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨ ਚੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By drinking the elixir-like Naam, a devotee remains in a state of exhilaration. He starts seeing inward and depends not on anyone else.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਅਨਹਦ ਲਿਵ; ਦੁਤੀਆ ਸਬਦ ਸ੍ਰਵਨੰਤਰਿ ਨ ਕੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The devoted traveller on the path of Naam stays in a state of equipoise and remains absorbed in the celestial melody of divine words music. He hears no other sound in his ears.

column2: ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਦੇਹ ਜਗ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਭਏ; ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਅਉ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ ਹੈ॥੬੫॥
column5: And in this blissful state, he is free of body and still alive. He is free from all worldly things and is emancipated while still living. He becomes capable of knowing the happenings of the three worlds and of the three periods. (65)

column2: ਸਕਲ ਸੁਗੰਧਤਾ ਮਿਲਤ ਅਰਗਜਾ ਹੋਤ; ਕੋਟਿ ਅਰਗਜਾ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਿਸਮ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: When sandalwood, musk, camphor and saffron are mixed; a fragrant paste is formed, But millions of such pastes are worthless before the fragrance of the lotus like feet of Satguru Ji.

column2: ਸਕਲ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਕਮਲ ਬਿਖੈ ਸਮਾਤ; ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਤ ਕੋਟਿ ਕਮਲਾ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: All the beauties of the world are absorbed in Lakshmi (the consort of Vishnu) but the beautiful radiance of Lord's feet is many times more blissful and pleasant than millions of Lakshmis,

column2: ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਮਿਲਿ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਭਏ; ਕੋਟਿਕ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੁਇ ਚਕਿਤ ਬਿਲਾਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: The wealth of the world put together becomes supreme and invaluable assets. But all the peace and comforts obtainable from many times more wealth is not even a counterpart of the comforts obtained from the spiritual bliss of the Lord,

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧਿ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸ ਕੈ॥੬੬॥
column5: The glory of the lotus feet of a True Guru is beyond the perception of a man. The devoted Sikhs enjoy and relish the elixir of the lotus feet of Fearless God through engrossing themselves in Naam Simran. (66)

column2: ਰਤਨ ਪਾਰਖ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਤਨ ਪਰੀਖਾ ਹੋਤ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹਾਟ ਸਾਟ ਰਤਨ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The genuineness of a gem can only be assayed by some connoisseur of the trade. Similarly an alert and attentive Sikh of the Guru trades in the purchase of jewel like Naam at the shop of a True Guru.

column2: ਮਾਨਕ ਹੀਰਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਮਨਿ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਕੈ; ਗਾਹਕ ਚਾਹਕ ਲਾਭ ਲਭਤਿ ਅਪਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who is genuinely interested in the trading of the diamonds, pearls, rubies and precious stones, he alone makes maximum profit out of it. Similarly true devotees and disciples of the Guru trade in the commodity of True Naam and make their life profitabl

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਅਵਗਾਹਨ ਬਿਸਾਹਨ ਕੈ; ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਗੁਰਦੁਆਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: By engrossing the mind in the divine word and trading in the commodity of Naam and Shabad (divine word), True Guru blesses his disciple with the treasure of love.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੰਗਮ ਸਮਾਗਮ ਕੈ; ਮਾਇਆ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸ ਭਵ ਤਰਤ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਹੈ॥੬੭॥
column5: When a true servant meets with True Guru; when he joins the loving and devoted congregation of the Guru, such a disciple who is ever in attendance of the Guru remains aloof and unsullied of maya (mammon). He sails across the worldly ocean with impunity. (

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਨਿਜ ਘਰਿ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਗੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Gursikh (disciple of the Gum) immersed in the pleasure of the elixir of Naam of the Lord remains stable of mind and fully conscious of his self. His mind is ever absorbed in the memory of God.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਰਿਦੈ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਜਾਗੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who remains engrossed in the elixir-like Naam of the Lord is blessed by the wisdom of Gum. The higher wisdom and his toil of remembering the Lord perpetually reveals the supernatural form of God radiance in his mind.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ. ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਭਾਗੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who is absorbed in the lotus-like holy feet of the True Guru, keeps drinking the elixir Naam from the inexhaustible source of the Lord. Thus he destroys his sullied wisdom.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਮਾਇਆ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸ ਬਾਸ ਬਿਰਲੋ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਹੈ॥੬੮॥
column5: One who remains absorbed in the lotus-like holy feet of the True Guru remains unsoiled by the effect of maya (mammon). Only a rare person achieves renunciation from material attractions of the world. (68)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਾਉ ਬੂਡਤ ਸੈ. ਜੋਊ ਬਚੈ ਸੋਈ ਭਲੋ; ਬੂਡਿ ਗਏ ਪਾਛੈ ਪਛਤਾਇਓ ਰਹਿ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Blessed are those who are saved from a capsizing boat. If drowned, there would be nothing but repentance.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਰ ਲਾਗੇ ਆਗਿ. ਜੋਈ ਬਚੈ ਸੋਈ ਭਲੋ; ਜਰਿ ਬੁਝੈ ਪਾਛੈ ਕਛੁ ਬਸੁ ਨ ਬਸਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: All those who escape from a burning house are blessed people. Nothing can be done if one is burnt into ashes.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚੋਰ ਲਾਗੇ ਜਾਗੇ. ਜੋਈ ਰਹੈ ਸੋਈ ਭਲੋ; ਸੋਇ ਗਏ ਰੀਤੋ ਘਰ ਦੇਖੈ ਉਠਿ ਪ੍ਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As one wakes up when the thief is committing theft, whatever is left behind by him is bonus and blessing. Otherwise one would find the house empty in the morning.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਆਵੈ; ਪਾਵੈ ਮੋਖ ਪਦਵੀ ਨਾਤਰੁ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥੬੯॥
column5: Similarly if a wayward person comes to the refuge of Guru even at the near end of his life, he can achieve a state of emancipation. Otherwise he would fall in the hands of angels of death and keep wailing. (69)

column2: ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਕੈ ਸਤੀ ਹੋਇ; ਧੰਨਿ ਧੰਨਿ ਕਹਤ ਹੈ ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Controlling her mind and with utmost determination, when a woman jumps into the pyre of her husband and self immolates herself, the whole world applauds her effort of being a loving and devoted wife.

column2: ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਕੈ ਜੋਧਾ ਜੂਝੈ; ਇਤ ਉਤ ਜਤ ਕਤ ਹੋਤ ਜੈ ਜੈਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: As a brave warrior lays down his life fighting for his noble cause determinedly till the end, he is applauded here, there and everywhere as a martyr.

column2: ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਕੈ ਚੋਰੁ ਮਰੈ; ਫਾਸੀ ਕੈ ਸੂਰੀ ਚਢਾਏ ਜਗ ਮਹਿ ਧਿਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Contrary to this, as a thief determinedly makes up his mind to commit theft, if caught, he is jailed, hanged or punished, he is degraded and rebuked the world over

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਕੈ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਗਤਿ ਮਾਨਸ ਅਉਤਾਰ ਜੀ॥੭੦॥
column5: Similarly one becomes bad and wicked with base wisdom whereas accepting and abiding by Guru's wisdom makes a person noble and virtuous. A human being makes his life a success or failure according to the company he keeps or his devotion to the holy congreg

column2: ਆਦਿ ਕੈ ਅਨਾਦਿ ਅਰੁ ਅੰਤ ਕੈ ਅਨੰਤ ਅਤਿ; ਪਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਪਾਰ ਨ ਅਥਾਹ ਥਾਹ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the imperishable God is beyond beginning though He is the beginning of all; as He is beyond end since He is the end of all; as He is beyond the farthest conceivable extent as He is unfathomable, so is the praise of the True Guru just like that of Lord.

column2: ਮਿਤਿ ਕੈ ਅਮਿਤਿ ਅਰੁ ਸੰਖ ਕੈ ਅਸੰਖ ਪੁਨਿ; ਲੇਖ ਕੈ ਅਲੇਖ ਨਹੀ ਤੋਲ ਕੈ ਤੁਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the imperishable God is beyond measurement, beyond counting, beyond perception, beyond· weighing; so is the praise of a True Guru.

column2: ਅਰਧ ਉਰਧ ਪਰਜੰਤ ਕੈ ਅਪਾਰਜੰਤ; ਅਗਮ ਅਗੋਚਰ. ਨ ਮੋਲ ਕੈ ਮੁਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the Almighty is limitless, inaccessible, beyond the perception of senses and evaluation, so is the praise of the True Guru.

column2: ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਤਿ; ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੀ ਬਡਾਈ ਹੈ॥੭੧॥
column5: As God Almighty is absolutely marvellous, astonishing and very strange, so is the praise of True Guru. (71)

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਤੀਰਥ ਪੁਰਬ ਕੋਟਿ; ਦੇਵੀ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Refuge of a True Guru is equivalent to pilgrimage of millions of holy places. Service of millions of gods and goddesses is also equivalent to living in the service of True Guru.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਸਕਲ ਫਲ; ਰਿਧਿ ਸਿਧਿ ਨਿਧਿ ਅਵਤਾਰ ਅਮਰਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: All desires fructify in the holy refuge of True Guru. All the miraculous powers remain in attendance forever.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ; ਭਗਤਿ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਤਾਰਨ ਤਰਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Meditation on Lord's name carried out in the refuge of the True Guru but with no reward at the back of the mind, is the place of all the comforts and peace in the world. A devoted Sikh absorbs himself in Naam Simran and sails across the worldly ocean besi

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧ; ਹਰਨ ਭਰਨ ਗਤਿ ਕਾਰਨ ਕਰਨ ਹੈ॥੭੨॥
column5: The glory of the refuge of a True Guru is beyond perception. Like eternal Lord, it destroys all base deeds and vices and fills a person with virtues. (72)

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਏਕ ਮੇਕ ਰੋਮ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਨੰਤ; ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰ ਗੁਰ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: By virtue of inexhaustible toil of Naam Simran in the service of a True Guru, the praise of a hair of a Gursikh is infinite. Then the inaccessible Satguru with countless virtues is a treasure house of praises.
