column2: ਨਿਰਵੈਰ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ਨਿਰਾਧਾਰ ਨਿਰਲੰਭ; ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਨਿਰਬਿਕਾਰ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਚੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: God-like True Guru is free of all animosity. He is beyond the influence of maya (mammon). He requires nobody's support, nor takes anyone's refuge. He is formless, beyond the grip of five vices and is ever stable of mind.

column2: ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਿਰਮੋਲ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ ਨਿਰਾਹਾਰ; ਨਿਰਮੋਹ ਨਿਰਭੇਦ ਅਛਲ ਅਜੀਤ ਹੈ॥੧੫੦॥
column5: God-like True Guru is free of dross. He cannot be evaluated. He is beyond the smudge of maya (mammon). He is free of all bodily needs like food and sleep etc; He has no attachment with anyone and is free of all differences. He tricks no one, nor can be tr

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਤਿ. ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੋ ਸਬਦੁ ਸਤਿ; ਸਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: True Lord (Satguru) is truth. His word is truth. His holy congregation is truth but this truth is realized only when one presents oneself before the True Lord (Satguru).

column2: ਦਰਸਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਤਿ. ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਤਿ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗ ਸਤਿ. ਸਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Contemplation on His vision is truth. Union of consciousness with Guru's word is truth. Company of the Sikhs of the Guru is truth but all this reality can be accepted only by becoming an obedient Sikh.

column2: ਦਰਸ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਧਿਆਨ ਸਬਦ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ; ਸੰਗਤਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਥਾਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Vision of the True Guru is like vision and meditation of Lord. The sermon of the True Guru is the divine knowledge. The congregation of the Sikhs of the True Guru is the abode of the Lord. But this truth can only be realised when love resides in the mind.

column2: ਸਤਿਰੂਪ ਸਤਿਨਾਮ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਕਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੧੫੧॥
column5: The remembrance of the eternal and true name of the True Lord is contemplation and awareness of the True Guru. But this can only be realised after becoming bereft of all lusts and worldly desires and raising the soul to a higher realm. (151)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਦੇਖੇ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕੈ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦ ਕੈ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: An obedient Sikh of the Guru sees the Lord pervading everywhere. By his utterances and expressions, he shows His presence to others as well.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸ੍ਰੁਤਿ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਕੈ; ਮਧੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਕਹਿ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਬਿਸਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: Guru's obedient slave hears the melodious sound of the complete God with his own ears by his very sweetly spoken words. He makes supplications which have marvellous sweetness in them.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਰਸ ਗੰਧ ਸੰਧਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਸ ਰਸ ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਗਮਾਗਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: Guru-conscious person always relishes the elixir of Lord's name even if he is lured by the combined attractions of his sense of smell and taste. The wondrous elixir obtained as a result of his love for the Lord is far more fragrant than the Sandalwood.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਬ ਮੈ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਨਮੋ ਨਮ ਹੈ॥੧੫੨॥
column5: Guru-oriented person considers the True Guru as a form of all-pervading Lord God. He makes his salutations and supplications to him again and again. (152)

column2: ਦਰਸ ਅਦਰਸ ਦਰਸ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ. ਦ੍ਰਿਗ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਅਗਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: A vision of the Lord is beyond the knowledge of the six philosophies (of Hinduism). That vision is astonishing and wondrous. One is amazed at its sight. But that marvellous sight is beyond the capabilities of these eyes that can only see outwardly.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਅਗੋਚਰ ਸਬਦ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਕੈ ਸ੍ਰੁਤਿ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਬਿਸਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: The form of divine word of the Lord is beyond speech and language. It is extremely wonderful. Even a description made and heard with ears is capable of sending one into a trance.

column2: ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਰਸ ਰਹਿਤ. ਅਪਿਅ ਪੀਆ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ; ਰਸਨਾ ਥਕਤ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: For His vision, relishing the elixir of Naam with love is beyond the worldly tastes. It is indeed unique. The tongue feels tired of making salutations to Him repeatedly and saying-Thou art infinite! Thou art infinite.

column2: ਨਿਰਗੁਨ ਸਰਗੁਨ ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਨ ਗਹਨਗਤਿ; ਸੂਖਮ ਸਥੂਲ ਮੂਲ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਹੈ॥੧੫੩॥
column5: No one can reach the latent and patent characteristics of the Transcendental and Immanent God who is complete in both forms: The complete and absolute God is the source of all the visible and invisible cosmos. (153)

column2: ਖੁਲੇ ਸੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਭਲੋ ਹੈ ਸੀਚਾਨੋ ਜਾ ਤੇ; ਜੀਵ ਘਾਤ ਕਰੈ ਨ ਬਿਕਾਰੁ ਹੋਇ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: A falcon is better in captivity as it would keep him away from killing other birds.

column2: ਖੁਲੇ ਸੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਚਕਈ ਭਲੀ ਜਾ ਤੇ; ਰਾਮ ਰੇਖ ਮੇਟਿ ਨਿਸਿ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਸੰਗੁ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: A red-legged partridge (Chakvi) is better in captivity that enables her meet her partner at night contrary to the curse of Sri Ram Chander.

column2: ਖੁਲੇ ਸੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਭਲੋ ਹੈ ਸੂਆ ਪ੍ਰਸਿਧ; ਸੁਨਿ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਈ॥
column5: A parrot is better in cage where he can receive sermons from his master and repeat the name of the Lord perpetually.

column2: ਮੋਖ ਪਦਵੀ ਸੈ ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਭਲੋ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਧਿਆਵਈ॥੧੫੪॥
column5: Similarly being born in a human body is better since it helps an individual to be an obedient slave of the True Guru and remembers the Lord in the holy company of Lord's beloveds rather than acquiring emancipation outwardly. (154)

column2: ਜੈਸਾ ਸੂਆ ਉਡਤ ਫਿਰਤ ਬਨ ਬਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਜੈਸੇ ਈ ਬਿਰਖ ਬੈਠੇ. ਤੈਸੋ ਫਲੁ ਚਾਖਈ॥
column5: As a parrot flies from one tree to the other and eats fruit that is available on them;

column2: ਪਰ ਬਸਿ ਹੋਇ ਜੈਸੀ ਜੈਸੀਐ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲੈ; ਸੁਨਿ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਤੈਸੀ ਭਾਖਾ ਲੈ ਸੁ ਭਾਖਈ॥
column5: In captivity, the parrot speaks language that he learns from the company that he keeps;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਚਿਤ ਚੰਚਲ ਚਪਲ ਜਲ ਕੋ ਸੁਭਾਉ; ਜੈਸੇ ਰੰਗ ਸੰਗ ਮਿਲੈ. ਤੈਸੋ ਰੰਗ ਰਾਖਈ॥
column5: So is the nature of this frolicsome mind that like water is very unstable and instable since it acquires colour that it mixes with.

column2: ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਰੁਨੀ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਕਾਲ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਗੰਗ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੁਜਨ ਭਿਲਾਖਈ॥੧੫੫॥
column5: A lowly person and a sinner desires for liquor on his death bed, while a noble person desires company of noble and saintly persons when time for this departure from the world approaches. (155)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜੈਸੇ ਰੰਗ ਸੰਗਿ ਮਿਲਤ ਸੇਤਾਂਬਰ ਹੁਇ; ਤੈਸੋ ਤੈਸੋ ਰੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਲਪਟਾਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Every fibre of a white cloth in contact with any colour acquires the same hue.

column2: ਭਗਵਤ ਕਥਾ ਅਰਾਧਨ ਕਉ ਧਾਰਨੀਕ; ਲਿਖਤ ਕ੍ਰਿਤਾਸ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਬੰਧ ਮੋਖਦਾਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Paper made of kritas leaf (considered to be impious) when used for recording the praises and paeans of the Lord, becomes capable of freeing one from the bondage of repeated births.

column2: ਸੀਤ ਗ੍ਰੀਖਮਾਦਿ ਬਰਖਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਰਖ ਮੈ; ਨਿਸਿ ਦਿਨ ਹੋਇ ਲਘੁ ਦੀਰਘ ਦਿਖਾਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The periods of day light and the ambient conditions varies during summer, rainy season and winter;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਚਿਤ ਚੰਚਲ ਚਪਲ ਪਉਨ ਗਉਨ ਗਤਿ; ਸੰਗਮ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਬਿਰਗੰਧ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਇ ਹੈ॥੧੫੬॥
column5: So is the instable and frolicsome mind that blows like breeze. The air acquires fragrance or foul smell when it passes over stacks of flowers or heap of filth. Similarly a human mind acquires good traits in the company of good persons and ill traits when

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਪਹਰ ਦਿਨ. ਜਗਤਿ ਚਤੁਰ ਜੁਗ; ਨਿਸਿ ਮਹਾ ਪਰਲੈ ਸਮਾਨਿ. ਦਿਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the four epoch world, consider the four quarters of day of life and the four quarters of night as great calamity, a game that is being played regularly.

column2: ਉਤਮ ਮਧਿਮ ਨੀਚ ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਣ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਗਤਿ; ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਆਸਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like the dice of Chaoparh-a black-gammon like game, the progress of the worldly game is sometimes supreme, modest or low. People living in the three traits of maya are entangled in debating about the worldly and spiritual knowledge.

column2: ਰਜਿ ਤਮਿ ਸਤਿ ਗੁਨ ਅਉਗਨ ਸਿਮ੍ਰਤ ਚਿਤ; ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਬਿਰਲੋਈ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A rare Guru-oriented, Guru follower holds these three traits of maya (Rajas, Tamas and Satv) as evil and strives to get rid of them.

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਬਰਨ ਸਾਰ ਚਉਪਰ ਕੋ ਖੇਲੁ ਜਗੁ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਜੁਗਲ ਹੁਇ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥੧੫੭॥
column5: The world is a game of four coloured dice. As in the game of Chaoparh where two dice are used and often fall favourably, one can achieve salvation from repeated births by keeping and adopting the company of godly men. (157)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰੰਗ ਸੰਗ ਮਿਲਤ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਹੋਇ ਤੈਸੋ; ਤੈਸੋ ਰੰਗ ਜਗਤੁ ਮੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: As water acquires colour that it comes in contact with, so is the effect of good and bad company regarded in the world.

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਮਿਲਿ ਪਵਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਸੰਗਿ; ਮਲ ਮੂਤ੍ਰ ਸੂਤ੍ਰ ਬ੍ਰਿਗੰਧ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Air in contact with sandalwood acquires fragrance, while it becomes foul smelling when in contact with filth.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜੈਸੇ ਪਾਕ ਸਾਕ ਬਿੰਜਨ ਮਿਲਤ ਘ੍ਰਿਤ; ਤੈਸੋ ਤੈਸੋ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਰਸੁ ਰਸਨਾ ਕੈ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Clarified butter acquires the taste of the vegetable and other items cooked and fried in it.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਗਤਿ; ਮੂਰੀ ਅਉ ਤੰਬੋਲ ਰਸ ਖਾਏ ਤੇ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥੧੫੮॥
column5: The nature of good and bad people is not latent; like the taste of a radish leaf and betel leaf that is recognised on eating. Similarly good and evil persons may look alike outwardly but their good and bad characteristics can be known by keeping their com

column2: ਬਾਲਕ ਕਿਸ਼ੋਰ ਜੋਬਨਾਦਿ ਅਉ ਜਰਾ ਬਿਵਸਥਾ; ਏਕ ਹੀ ਜਨਮ ਹੋਤ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as one go through childhood, adolescence, youth and old age in one lifespan.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਸਿ ਦਿਨ ਤਿਥਿ ਵਾਰ ਪਛ ਮਾਸ ਰੁਤਿ; ਚਤੁਰ ਮਾਸ ਤ੍ਰਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਰਖ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As days, nights, dates, weeks, months, four seasons are the spread of one year;

column2: ਜਾਗ੍ਰਤ ਸੁਪਨ ਅਉ ਸਖੋਪਤਿ ਅਵਸਥਾ ਕੈ; ਤੁਰੀਆ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As wakefulness, dream sleep, deep slumber and a state of nothingness (Turi) are different states;

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਤ; ਭਗਤ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ ਜਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥੧੫੯॥
column5: Similarly meeting with saintly people and contemplating on Lord's glory and grandeur during human life, one becomes a godly person, a saint, a devotee and a wise man. (159)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚਕਈ ਮੁਦਿਤ ਪੇਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬ ਨਿਸਿ; ਸਿੰਘ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬ ਦੇਖਿ ਕੂਪ ਮੈ ਪਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a red-legged partridge (chakvi) feels happy seeing its image and regarding it as her paramour, whereas a lion jumps in the well when he sees his image in the water and regards it as his rival;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਾਚ ਮੰਦਰ ਮੈ ਮਾਨਸ ਅਨੰਦਮਈ; ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਪੇਖਿ ਆਪਾ ਆਪੁ ਭੂਸ ਕੈ ਮਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a person feels ecstatic watching his image in the mirror-studded house while a dog barks perpetually considering all images as other dogs;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਵਿ ਸੁਤ ਜਮ ਰੂਪ ਅਉ ਧਰਮਰਾਇ; ਧਰਮ ਅਧਰਮ ਕੈ ਭਾਉ ਭੈ ਕਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the son of Sun becomes an object of fear for the unrighteous people in the form of angel of death, but loves the righteous people by projecting himself as the king of righteousness;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਕੈ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਆਪਾ ਆਪੁ ਚੀਨਤ ਨ ਚੀਨਤ ਚਰਤ ਹੈ॥੧੬੦॥
column5: So do the deceiver and trickster not recognise themselves due to their base wisdom. On the contrary, Godly people acquire the wisdom of the True Guru and recognise their real self. (160)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਬਿਖੈ; ਜਾਹੀ ਜਾਹੀ ਰੰਗ ਮਿਲੈ ਸੋਈ ਹੁਇ ਦਿਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: As water acquires the hue of colour that is mixed in it, As the clarified butter conveys to the tongue the taste of the vegetable and other items cooked in it,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘ੍ਰਿਤ ਜਾਹੀ ਜਾਹੀ ਪਾਕ ਸਾਕ ਸੰਗ ਮਿਲੈ; ਤੈਸੋ ਤੈਸੋ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਰਸ ਰਸਨਾ ਚਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: As a mimic has a definite character of his own adopts different characters for mimicry but he is known by the character that he is mimicking at that moment,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਗੀ ਏਕ ਹੁਇ. ਅਨੇਕ ਭਾਂਤਿ ਭੇਖ ਧਾਰੈ; ਜੋਈ ਜੋਈ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਗ ਕਾਛੈ ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: So does a man of frolicsome mind takes to vices in the company of those whose minds are restless and playful.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਚਿਤ ਚੰਚਲ ਚਪਲ ਸੰਗ ਦੋਖ ਲੇਪ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਹੋਇ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਠਹਿਰਾਵਈ॥੧੬੧॥
column5: But an obedient Sikh of the True Guru becomes God-oriented in the company and teachings of the True Guru. (161)

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਮਥਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਕਸੇ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਿਖੁ; ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਨ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Churning of ocean produced nectar and poison. Despite coming out of the same ocean, the goodness of nectar and harm of poison is not the same.

column2: ਬਿਖੁ ਅਚਵਤ ਹੋਤ ਰਤਨ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਕਾਲ; ਅਚਏ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮੂਏ ਜੀਵਨ ਅਮਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Poison ends the jewel-like life whereas nectar resuscitates or revives the dead making him immortal.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਾਰੋ ਤਾਰੀ ਏਕ ਲੋਸਟ ਸੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਹੁਇ; ਬੰਧ ਮੋਖ ਪਦਵੀ ਸੰਸਾਰਿ ਬਿਸਥਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the key and the lock are made of the same metal, but a lock results in bondage whereas a key frees the bonds.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਸਨ ਅਉ ਮਜੀਠ ਗਤਿ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਟੇਵ ਸੈ ਨ ਟਰਿ ਹੈ॥੧੬੨॥
column5: Similarly a man does not give up his base wisdom but a person of Godly disposition never falters from the wisdom and teachings of the Guru. (162)

column2: ਬਰਖਾ ਸੰਜੋਗੁ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਓਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ; ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰੀ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: In the rainy season, both pearl and hailstones are produced. Being of the same form, a pearl is considered good doer while the hailstone causes damage.

column2: ਓਰਾ ਬਰਖਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕੋ ਬਿਨਾਸ; ਮੁਕਤਾ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਸਭਾ ਸੋਭਾ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Hailstones destroy/damage crops and other vegetation, whereas a pearl is praised for its beauty and lustrous form.

column2: ਓਰਾ ਤਉ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਧਾਰਿ ਦੇਖਤ ਬਿਲਾਇ ਜਾਇ; ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਜਿਉ ਠਹਿਰਾਵਈ॥
column5: Being damaging in nature, a hailstone melts away in no time, whereas a good doer pearl remains stable.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਗਤਿ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੁਰੈ ਨ ਦੁਰਾਵਈ॥੧੬੩॥
column5: Similar is the effect of the company of vice/evil and virtuous people. The supreme wisdom acquired by the teachings of a True Guru and polluted intellect due to base wisdom cannot be hidden. (163)

column2: ਲਜਾ ਕੁਲ ਅੰਕਸੁ ਅਉ ਗੁਰ ਜਨ ਸੀਲ ਡੀਲ; ਕੁਲਾਬਧੂ ਬ੍ਰਤਿ ਕੈ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Because of the good of the family honour, displaying calm and tranquil behaviour before the elders of the house and following the right ethos expected of a married woman, a daughter-in-law of a good family is called faithful and virtuous.

column2: ਦੁਸਟ ਸਭਾ ਸੰਜੋਗ. ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਸੰਗ; ਬਹੁ ਬਿਬਿਚਾਰ ਧਾਰਿ, ਗਨਿਕਾ ਬੁਲਾਵਈ॥
column5: A woman who keeps company of evil people, doing acts highly condemnable and indulging in licentious deeds is called a whore.

column2: ਕੁਲਾਬਧੂ ਸੁਤ ਕੋ ਬਖਾਨੀਅਤ ਗੋਤ੍ਰਾਚਾਰ; ਗਨਿਕਾ ਸੁਅਨ ਪਿਤਾ ਨਾਮ ਕੋ ਬਤਾਵਈ॥
column5: The son of a virtuous woman furthers the family lineage but who can tell the name of the father of a whore's son.

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਲਾਗਿ ਜੈਸੇ ਕਾਗ ਬਨ ਬਨ ਫਿਰੈ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਹੰਸ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਜਸੁ ਪਾਵਈ॥੧੬੪॥
column5: As a self-willed person of crow-like temperament wanders all over, a Guru-oriented person of swan-like attitude enjoys respect by taking the refuge of Lords name as taught and initiated to him by his Guru. (164)

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਧਾਰਿ. ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਗਤਿ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਧਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the human birth, one is influenced by good or bad company. Thus the teachings of Guru instills virtues whereas bad company fills a person with base wisdom.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਪਦਵੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਅਉ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ ਜਨ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਸਾਧੂ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the company of true people, one attains a position of a devotee, an analytical person, liberated alive and possessor of divine knowledge.

column2: ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਸੰਗ ਚੋਰ ਜਾਰ ਅਉ ਜੂਆਰੀ; ਠਗ ਬਟਵਾਰਾ ਮਤਵਾਰਾ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Association with evil and vice-ridden people turns a man into thief, gambler, deceitful, dacoit, addict and arrogant.

column2: ਅਪੁਨੇ ਅਪੁਨੇ ਰੰਗ ਸੰਗ ਸੁਖੁ ਮਾਨੈ ਬਿਸੁ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥੧੬੫॥
column5: The whole world enjoys peace and pleasures in their own way. But a rare person has understood the intensity of the blessing of Guru's teaching and happiness it gives. (165)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਅਸਟ ਧਾਤੁ ਡਾਰੀਅਤ ਨਾਉ ਬਿਖੈ; ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰੈ ਤਾਹਿ. ਤਊ ਵਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਸੋਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a bundle of eight metals loaded in a boat will reach the other bank without any change in its form or colour during transit,

column2: ਸੋਈ ਧਾਤੁ ਅਗਨਿ ਮੈ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਅਗਨਿ ਰੂਪ; ਤਊ ਜੋਈ ਸੋਈ ਪੈ ਸੁ ਘਾਟ ਠਾਟ ਹੋਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: When these metals are put in fire, they melt and acquire the form of fire. It is then turned into beautiful ornaments of metal better looking than each individually.

column2: ਸੋਈ ਧਾਤੁ ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਿ ਪੁਨਿ ਕੰਚਨ ਹੁਇ; ਮੋਲ ਕੈ ਅਮੋਲਾਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਅਵਲੋਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: But when it comes in contact with philosopher-stone, it turns into gold. Beside becoming invaluable, it also becomes beautiful and attractive to look at.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਪਾਰਸ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਸਿ ਪਾਰਸ ਹੋਤ; ਸੰਗਤਿ ਹੁਇ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਸਤਸੰਗ ਪੋਈ ਹੈ॥੧੬੬॥
column5: Similarly in the company of God-oriented and holy men, one becomes holy. Meeting with True Guru, the Supreme of all philosopher-stones, one becomes like a philosopher-stone. (166)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਰ ਲਾਗੈ ਆਗਿ. ਭਾਗਿ ਨਿਕਸਤ ਖਾਨ; ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਪਰੋਸੀ ਧਾਇ ਜਰਤ ਬੁਝਾਵਈ॥
column5: As the owner of the house that catches fire escapes from the inferno to save his life, but sympathetic neighbours and friends rush to put the fire off,

column2: ਗੋਧਨ ਹਰਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਕਰਤ ਪੁਕਾਰ ਗੋਪ; ਗਾਉ ਮੈ ਗੁਹਾਰ ਲਾਗਿ ਤੁਰਤ ਛਡਾਵਈ॥
column5: As a herdsman shouts for help when his cattle are being stolen, the village folks chase the thieves and recover the cattle,

column2: ਬੂਡਤ ਅਥਾਹ ਜੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਬਿਖੈ; ਪੇਖਤ ਪੈਰਊਆ ਵਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਲੈ ਲਗਾਵਈ॥
column5: As a person may be drowning in rapid and deep water and an expert swimmer rescues him and reaches him on the other bank to safety,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਜਮ ਜਾਲ ਕਾਲ ਬਿਆਲ ਗ੍ਰਸੇ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਤ ਸੰਕਟ ਮਿਟਾਵਈ॥੧੬੭॥
column5: Similarly, when death-like snake is entangling a person in the throes of death, seeking help of saintly and holy persons allay that distress. (167)

column2: ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਨਿਹਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਨਿਰਲੋਭ ਨਿਰਮੋਹ; ਨਿਹਮੇਵ ਨਿਹਟੇਵ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਵਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: An obedient disciple of True Guru is free of lust, anger, avarice, attachment, arrogance, base habits and other vices.

column2: ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ਨਿਰਬਾਨ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਿਰਵੈਰ; ਨਿਰਬਿਘਨਾਇ ਨਿਰਾਲੰਬ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is free of the influence of mammon (maya), bondage, dross, animosity, hindrances and support. He is indestructible of form.

column2: ਨਿਰਾਹਾਰ ਨਿਰਾਧਾਰ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਨਿਰਬਿਕਾਰ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਨਿਹਭ੍ਰਾਂਤਿ ਨਿਰਭੈ ਨਿਰਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is free of all desires of taste, not dependent on the grace of gods and goddesses, transcendental of form, independent of all support, free of vices and doubts, fearless and stable of mind.

column2: ਨਿਹਕਰਮ ਨਿਹਭਰਮ ਨਿਹਸਰਮ ਨਿਹਸੁਆਦ; ਨਿਰਬਿਵਾਦ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ ਸੁਨਿ ਮੈ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਹੈ॥੧੬੮॥
column5: He is a recluse beyond rites and rituals, infatiguiable, undesirous of all worldly tastes and relishments, beyond all worldly disputes and discords, not smudged by the mammon (maya), who lives in a state of trance and tranquil thoughts. (168)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Supernatural love grows in the heart of an obedient disciple of the Guru when he lodges the divine word in his consciousness and keeps the company of holy men.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰੰਗ ਮੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਰੰਗ ਜਿਉ ਤਰੰਗ ਗੰਗ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਮੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਰਸ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਲਾਸੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The company of saintly persons and perpetual Naam Simran, creates a loving hue like the waves of river Ganges that generates multi-coloured effects. The Guru-conscious person enjoys many elixirs in this loving state.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਗੰਧ ਸੰਧਿ ਮੈ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਸਨਬੰਧ ਕੋਟਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਸ੍ਰੂਤਿ ਅਨਿਕ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਉਲਾਸੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Due to the practicing of Naam Simran, that fragrance is the combination of millions of fragrances. And the unstruck Music emerging from the loving fragrance of God, contains the pleasure of many modes of singing.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਅਸਪਰਸ ਕੋਮਲਤਾ ਸੀਤਲਤਾ ਕੈ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੇ ਹੈ॥੧੬੯॥
column5: None can reach the sensitivity and coolness of that love generated by Naam Simran). The pleasures and ecstasy are describable. It generates marvellous faith. (169)

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰੰਗ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨ ਕੋਊ ਰੰਗੁ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨ ਅਨਰਸ ਸਮਾਨਿ ਕੈ॥
column5: No colour or shade can reach the hue of love nor anyone can reach near the elixir of love.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਗੰਧ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨ ਆਨ ਕੋਊਐ ਸੁਗੰਧ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪ੍ਰਭੁਤਾ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਪ੍ਰਭੁਤਾ ਨ ਆਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: The loving fragrance produced as a result of contemplation on Guru's words cannot be reached by any other fragrance in the world, nor any praise of the world can match the praise of love resulted from Naam Simran.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਤੋਲੁ ਤੁਲਿ ਨ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਤੋਲ ਤੁਲਾਧਾਰ; ਮੋਲ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: The mergence of Guru's words in the consciousness cannot be measured by any balance or measures. The invaluable love cannot be reached by any treasure of the world.

column2: ਏਕ ਬੋਲ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੈ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨਹੀ ਬੋਲੁ ਕੋਊ: ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਅਸ ਥਕਤ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕੈ॥੧੭੦॥
column5: A loving word resulting from the Naam Simran cannot be matched by any explanation or elucidation of the world. Millions of volumes have consumed themselves trying to estimate this state. (170)

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਜਸ; ਆਨਦ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਖ ਬਿਸਮ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Millions of comforts of the world fall inadequate before the calming ecstasy of singing the praises of the True Guru, the epitome of Lord, the celestial knower.

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਸੋਭ ਲੋਭ ਕੈ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੋਇ; ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਛਬਿ ਛਬਿ ਕੈ ਲੁਭਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Millions of grandeurs of the world are allured by the glory of the holy feet of the True Guru. Millions of worldly beauties go into trance over the beauty of the feet of True Guru.

column2: ਕੋਮਲਤਾ ਕੋਟਿ ਲੋਟ ਪੋਟ ਹੁਇ ਕੋਮਲਤਾ ਕੈ; ਸੀਤਲਤਾ ਕੋਟਿ ਓਟ ਚਾਹਤ ਹਿਰਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Millions of tenderness’s of the world are sacrificed over the tenderness of feet of the True Guru. Millions of calmness’s seek His refuge and are amazed.

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਅਨਹਦ ਗਦ ਗਦ ਹੋਤ; ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਤਿਹ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥੧੭੧॥
column5: Millions of nectars are going gaga over the nectar of the holy feet of True Guru. As a bumble bee enjoys the sweet nectar of the flower by sucking deep into it, so does a Guru-conscious person remains immersed in the fragrance of the holy feet of the True

column2: ਸੋਵਤ ਪੈ ਸੁਪਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਦੇਖਿਓ ਚਾਹੈ; ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਬਿਖੈ ਉਨਮਨੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: If someone desires to see the happenings of a dream in reality, it is not possible. Similarly the divine radiance of the celestial light generated due to Naam Simran cannot be described.

column2: ਸੁਰਾਪਾਨ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਮਤਵਾਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਨ ਜਿਉ; ਨਿਝਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਅਨਭੈ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a drunkard feels satisfied and happy drinking liquor and he alone knows about it, similarly the continuous flow of the elixir of Naam generates divine awareness that is indescribable.

column2: ਬਾਲਕ ਪੈ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸਬਦ ਬਿਧਾਨ ਚਾਹੈ; ਅਨਹਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਰੁਨਝੁਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸ੍ਰੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a child is unable to explain the notes of music in various modes, similarly a Guru-conscious person who listens to the unstruck music cannot describe its sweetness and melody.

column2: ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਸੋਈ ਜਾਨੈ ਜਾ ਮੈ ਬੀਤੈ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਜਿਉ ਤਰੋਵਰ ਨ ਗੋਤ ਹੈ॥੧੭੨॥
column5: The melody of unstruck music and continuous fall of elixir as a result is beyond description. One who has the process going in his mind, experiences it. Just as the trees which are fragranced by a Sandalwood are not regarded different than the Sandalwood

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਸੋਈ ਜਾਨੈ ਜਾ ਮੈ ਬੀਤੇ; ਮਦਨ ਮਦੋਨ ਮਤਿਵਾਰੋ ਜਗ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: He alone can appreciate the greatness of Lord's love-elixir who experiences it. It is just like a drunkard who is considered crazy by the world.

column2: ਘੂਰਮ ਹੁਇ ਘਾਇਲ ਸੋ ਘੂਮਤ ਅਰੁਨ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ; ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਸਤ੍ਰੁਤਾ ਨਿਲਜ ਲਜਾ ਹੂ ਲਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a warrior wounded in the battlefield wanders around with his eyes blazing red, he shams the feeling of friendship and animosity,

column2: ਰਸਨਾ ਰਸੀਲੀ ਕਥਾ ਅਕਥ ਕੈ ਮੋਨ ਬ੍ਰਤ; ਅਨ ਰਸ ਰਹਿਤ. ਨ ਉਤਰ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: One enamoured by the love of God has his speech nectar-like due to perpetual recitation of Lord's indescribable traits. He adopts silence and is free from all other desires. He talks to no one and remains relishing the sweetness of Lord's naam.

column2: ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੰਕੋਚ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਅਸਤੁਤਿ ਨਿੰਦਾ; ਪਗ ਡਗਮਗ ਜਤ ਕਤ ਬਿਸਮਾਨੀਐ॥੧੭੩॥
column5: He keeps all his desires under wrap. Praise and insult is all alike to him. In the stupor of Naam he is seen living a life of wonders and marvels. (173)

column2: ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਜਾਮਨ ਕੈ ਦੂਧ ਦਧਿ ਹੋਤ ਜੈਸੇ; ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਕਾਂਜੀ ਪਰੈ ਦੂਧ ਫਾਟਿ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a little coagulant turns the milk into curd, whereas a little citric acid would split it;

column2: ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਬੀਜ ਬੋਏ ਬਿਰਖ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੋਇ; ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਚਿਨਗ ਪਰੈ ਭਸਮ ਹੁਇ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a small seed grows into a mighty tree, but a spark of fire falling on such a mighty tree reduces it to ashes,

column2: ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਖਾਏ ਬਿਖੁ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਕਾਲ; ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ ਅਮਰੁ ਹੁਇ ਗਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a small quantity of poison causes death, whereas a little elixir makes a person imperishable,

column2: ਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਗਨਿਕਾ ਬਿਵਾਹਿਤਾ ਜਿਉ; ਤਨਕ ਮੈ ਉਪਕਾਰ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਘਾਤ ਹੈ॥੧੭੪॥
column5: So is the company of self-willed and Guru-willed people that can be compared with a harlot and a loyal married woman respectively. Company of self-willed/self-oriented persons cause much damage and destruction to good deeds. On the contrary the company of

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਕੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਧਿਆਨ; ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਅਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: When the vision rests on the congregation of holy people, one's consciousness attaches with the Lord. The same vision turns into vices in the company of self-willed people.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ; ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਅਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਬਾਦ ਅਹੰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the holy company, one realises the Lord through the union of the True Guru's words and the consciousness. But the same consciousness becomes a cause of arrogance and discord in the company of ill reputed persons.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਕੈ ਮਹਾਪ੍ਰਸਾਦ; ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਅਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਬਿਖਮ ਅਹਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: By virtue of the company of Guru-conscious persons simplicity in life and eating becomes supreme blessing. But eating (of meat etc.) in the company of ill-famed and self-willed people become painful and distressing.

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਹੁਇ ਅਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਦੁਆਰ ਹੈ॥੧੭੫॥
column5: Due to base wisdom, the company of the self-willed people become a cause of birth and death repeatedly. On the contrary, adopting Guru's wisdom and keeping company of holy persons become a cause of emancipation. (175)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਚਰਮ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਿਬਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਹੁਇ; ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਲੋਚਨ ਅਛਤ ਅੰਧ ਕੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: Accepting the initiation sermon of the True Guru turns the outward vision of a person into divine vision. But the base wisdom makes a person blind despite the presence of eyes. Such a person is bereft of knowledge.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕੈ ਬਜਰ ਕਪਾਟ ਖੁਲੈ; ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਕਠਿਨ ਕਪਾਟ ਸਨਬੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the sermon of the True Guru, the tight shut doors of the consciousness become ajar whereas this does not happen in the case of a person of base wisdom and self will.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ; ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਮੁਖਿ ਦੁਰਬਚਨ ਦੁਰਗੰਧ ਹੈ॥
column5: By adopting the counsel of the True Guru, one relishes the elixir of God's love perpetually. But base wisdom emanates foul smell from the mouth as a result of ill and bad words spoken.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਇ ਨ ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ; ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਬਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਬਿਰੋਧ ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਸੰਧਿ ਹੈ॥੧੭੬॥
column5: Adopting the wisdom of True Guru produces true love and peace. He is never touched by happiness or sorrow in this state. However, base wisdom remains a cause of discord, quarrels and distress. (176)

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਕਾਮ ਚੇਸਟਾ ਸੰਜੋਗ. ਜਤ ਸਤਵੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The impure intelligence and company of evil persons generates lust and passion but adopting the teachings of True Guru, makes a person disciplined and chaste.

column2: ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਕੇ ਬਿਰੋਧ ਬਿਖੈ. ਸਹਜ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਮੋਖ; ਲੋਭ ਲਹਰੰਤਰ ਧਰਮ ਧੀਰ ਜੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The impure wisdom entangles a person in the waves of hatred and greed under the influence of anger, whereas in the company of saints he attains, humility, patience and kindness.

column2: ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਦ੍ਰੋਹ ਕੈ ਅਰਥ ਪ੍ਰਮਾਰਥ ਸੈ; ਅਹੰਮੇਵ ਟੇਵ. ਦਇਆ ਦ੍ਰਵੀਭੂਤ ਸੰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A person with base wisdom is ever engrossed in the love of maya (mammon). He becomes deceitful and arrogant. But with the intellect of the True Guru, one becomes clement, kind, humble and saintly.

column2: ਦੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਚਿਤ. ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਸਤ੍ਰਤਾ ਸੁਭਾਵ; ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਮੂਲ ਮੰਤ ਹੈ॥੧੭੭॥
column5: One with impure intellect remains absorbed in base deeds and is ridden with animosity. On the contrary a Guru-conscious person is friendly and of good dispositions. Welfare and good of all is his mission in life, whereas a person of nefarious intellect en

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਰਿਦੈ. ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕੈ ਬਸੈ; ਤਾ ਪਾਛੈ ਕਰਤ ਆਗਿਆ ਮਇਆ ਕੈ ਮਨਾਵਈ॥
column5: The True Guru becomes clement and enters the heart of a Sikh first. Then he asks the Sikh to meditate on Naam and showers his kindness to make him meditate.

column2: ਆਗਿਆ ਮਾਨਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਦਾਨ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Obeying the command of the True Guru, a Guru-conscious person indulges in Naam Simran- a supreme treasure of the Lord and enjoys the spiritual comfort. He also attains the ultimate spiritual state.

column2: ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਲਿਵ; ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਕਥਾ ਕਹਤ ਨ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: In that spiritual realm, he achieves that high state of Naam where all desires of reward or fruit vanish. Thus he gets engrossed in a deep concentration. This state is beyond description.

column2: ਜੈਸੋ ਜੈਸੋ ਭਾਉ ਕਰਿ ਪੂਜਤ ਪਦਾਰਬਿੰਦ; ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕੈ ਮਨੋਰਥ ਪੁਜਾਵਈ॥੧੭੮॥
column5: With whatever desires and sentiments one worships the True Guru, He fulfils all his wants and desires. (178)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਿਆ ਭੇਟਤ ਅਧਾਨ ਨਿਰਮਾਨ ਹੋਤ; ਬਾਂਛਤ ਬਿਧਾਨ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਅਗ੍ਰਭਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: As a wife presents herself to her husband with humility and becomes pregnant, the husband brings her all the foods of her liking and taste.

column2: ਜਨਮਤ ਸੁਤ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕੋ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਕਰੈ; ਸੁਤ ਹਿਤ ਰਸ ਕਸ ਸਕਲ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: On birth of a son, she abstains from eating all that may be harmful to the child.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪੁਜਾਇ; ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਲਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Similarly taking the refuge of the True Guru with devotion; the desires of a Gursikh are fulfilled. He is blessed with the Naam by the True-Guru which is the source of desirelessness. One craves for nothing more and observes no rituals.

column2: ਨਿਸਿ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਭਵ ਸਾਗਰ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਪੰਚ ਤਸਕਰ ਜੀਤਿ ਸਿਖ ਹੀ ਸੁਜਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥੧੭੯॥
column5: A Sikh who has received the boon of elixir-like Naam can win over the five evils cautiously and swims across worldly ocean that is frightening like a dark night. (179)

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਆਗਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲਕ ਬਾਲਕ ਸਿਖ; ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਪਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: A devotee who obeys the command of the True Guru with the innocence of a child, the glory of the dust of his feet is infinite.

column2: ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿਕ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿਕ ਨ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਹੈ; ਨਿਗਮ ਸੇਖਾਦਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਕੈ ਉਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Shiv, Sanak etc., the four sons of Brahma and other gods of Hindu triology cannot reach the praise of the Sikh of Guru who obeys the command of doing Naam Simran. Even the Vedas and Shesh Naag praise the glory of such a disciple saying-great, limitless.

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਚਾਹੈ; ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਸੁਰਸਰਿ ਸਰਧਾ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: All the four desirable goals-dharam, arth, kam and mokh, three times (past, present and future) desire refuge of such a devotee. The Yogis, householders, river Ganges the river of the gods and the whole world devotion craves for the dust of the feet of su

column2: ਪੂਜਨ ਕੇ ਪੂਜ ਅਰੁ ਪਾਵਨ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਕਰੈ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਬਿਮਲ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥੧੮੦॥
column5: The dust of the feet of a disciple of the True Guru blessed with the Naam Simran is sacred even for those who are believed to be pious souls as it purifies them further. The state of such a person is beyond elucidation and his views are pure and clear. (1

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਚਾਖਤ ਭਈ ਉਲਟੀ; ਤਨ ਸਨਾਤਨ ਮਨ ਉਨਮਨ ਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Enjoying the taste of the pleasure-giving True Guru blessed Naam elixir, practicing the command of the Guru diligently, the inclinations of such Sikhs of the Guru turn away from the worldly attractions.

column2: ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਉਲਟਿ ਭਈ ਹੈ. ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਰਿਦੈ; ਦੁਰਜਨ ਸੁਰਜਨ ਕਰਿ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The base intellect is shed and the wisdom of Guru comes and resides in them. They are then known not as unworthy of trust but persons of divine traits.

column2: ਸੰਸਾਰੀ ਸੈ ਉਲਟਿ ਪਲਟਿ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਭਏ; ਬਗ ਬੰਸ ਹੰਸ ਭਏ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Freeing themselves from the affairs of the world, the mammon entangled people become the devotees of the formless God. By the True Guru blessed knowledge, they become praise worthy like a swan from that of a heron like inclination.

column2: ਕਾਰਨ ਅਧੀਨ ਦੀਨ ਕਾਰਨ ਕਰਨ ਭਏ; ਹਰਨ ਭਰਨ ਭੇਦ ਅਲਖ ਲਖਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੧੮੧॥
column5: By obeying the command of Guru of doing Naam Simran, those who were under the influence of worldly affairs now become their masters. They become aware of the Lord's indefinable traits who is the creator, the sustainer and destroyer of all things in the Un

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਚਾਖਤ ਉਲਟੀ ਭਈ; ਜੋਨਿ ਕੈ ਅਜੋਨਿ ਭਏ ਕੁਲ ਅਕੁਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: State of Guru-conscious disciples blessed by the True Guru with elixir of Naam turns opposite from the worldly involvements and are rid of the cycle of birth and death, ego and attachment.

column2: ਜੰਤਨ ਤੇ ਸੰਤ ਅਉ ਬਿਨਾਸੀ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ ਭਏ; ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਭਏ ਸਾਧ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such persons who are ever relishing the Naam like elixir of the True Guru become saintly from the worldly beings. The mortal beings become immortal. They become noble and worthy persons from their ill bred and low status.

column2: ਲਾਲਚੀ ਲਲੂ ਜਨ ਤੇ ਪਾਵਨ ਕੈ ਪੂਜ ਕੀਨੇ; ਅੰਜਨ ਜਗਤ ਮੈ ਨਿਰੰਜਨਈ ਦੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The pleasure giving Naam elixir turns the greedy and covetous people into pure and worthy beings. Living in the world, makes them untouchable and unaffected by the worldly attractions.

column2: ਕਾਟਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਫਾਸੀ ਗੁਰ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਕੀਨੋ; ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸੀ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਭੀਨ ਹੈ॥੧੮੨॥
column5: With the initiation of a Sikh by the True Guru, his bondage of maya (mammon) is sheared. He becomes indifferent from it. The practice of Naam Simran makes a person fearless, and immerses him in the love-elixir of beloved Lord. (182)

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਦਰਸਨੀ ਹੋਤ ਖਟ ਦਰਸ ਅਤੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The contemplation on the vision of True Guru for a devotee is wonderful. Those who see the True Guru in their vision go beyond the teachings of the six philosophies (of Hinduism).

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ; ਸੇਵਕੁ ਨ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਕੀ ਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The refuge of the True Guru is the home of desirelessness. Those in the refuge of True Guru hold no love for serving any other god.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਮੂਲਮੰਤ੍ਰ; ਆਨ ਤੰਤ੍ਰ ਮੰਤ੍ਰ ਕੀ ਨ ਸਿਖਨ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Engrossing the mind in the words of the True Guru is the supreme incantation. The true disciples of the Guru hold no faith in any other form of worship.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਪੰਗਤਿ ਸੁਖ; ਹੰਸ ਬੰਸ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਚੀਤ ਹੈ॥੧੮੩॥
column5: It is by the grace of True Guru that one gets the pleasure of sitting and enjoying the holy gathering. The swan-like Guru-conscious people attach their mind in the highly respected divine company of holy people and nowhere else. (183)

column2: ਘੋਸਲਾ ਮੈ ਅੰਡਾ ਤਜਿ ਉਡਤ ਅਕਾਸਚਾਰੀ; ਸੰਧਿਆ ਸਮੈ ਅੰਡਾ ਹੇਤ ਚੇਤਿ ਫਿਰ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a bird flies away in the open sky from the comfort of its nest, leaving its egg behind but returns due to its concern for the baby bird in the egg,

column2: ਤਿਰੀਆ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਸੁਤ ਜਾਤ ਬਨਖੰਡ ਬਿਖੈ; ਸੁਤ ਕੀ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਆਇ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a labour woman leaves her child home under compulsion and goes to the jungle to pick up firewood, but keeps the memory of her child in the mind and finds comfort on returning home;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਕੁੰਡ ਕਰਿ ਛਾਡੀਅਤਿ ਜਲਚਰੀ; ਜਬ ਚਾਹੇ ਤਬ ਗਹਿ ਲੇਤ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a pool of water is made and fish released in it to be caught again at one's will.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਚਿਤ ਚੰਚਲ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਹੈ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਬਿਹੰਗ ਠਹਿਰਾਵਈ॥੧੮੪॥
column5: So does the frolicsome mind of a human being wanders in all the four directions. But due to the ship-like Naam blessed by the True Guru, the wandering bird-like mind comes and rests in the self. (184)

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਬਰਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ ਬਰਨ ਤੈਸੋ; ਖਟ ਦਰਸਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਦਰਸਨ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: There is nothing as marvellous available for the Guru-conscious persons in the four castes (Brahmin, Khatri et al.) like the wonderful elixir-like Naam of the Lord. Even the six philosophical scriptures do not have the glory and grandeur of the divine rad

column2: ਸਿੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਪੁਰਾਨ ਬੇਦ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਸਮਾਨ ਖਾਨ; ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਮੈ ਨ ਸਬਦ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The treasure that Guru-conscious people possess is not available in the Vedas, Shastras and Simritis. The melody that is available with them as a result of Guru's words is not found in any musical mode.

column2: ਨਾਨਾ ਬਿੰਜਨਾਦਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ; ਸਕਲ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਮੈ ਨ ਗੰਧਿ ਸੰਧਿ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The relishment that Guru-conscious persons enjoy is so wonderful that it is not available in any type of food. The ecstatic fragrance that they enjoy is not available in any other form of fragrances.

column2: ਉਸਨ ਸੀਤਲਤਾ ਸਪਰਸ ਅਪਰਸ ਨ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਤੁਲਿ ਓਤ ਪੋਤ ਹੈ॥੧੮੫॥
column5: The pleasure of Naam-like elixir that the Guru-conscious people enjoy is beyond all comforts of allaying or relieving hot or cold conditions by cool or hot means respectively. The hot and cold conditions keep altering but the relishment of Naam elixir rem

column2: ਲਿਖਨੁ ਪੜ੍ਹਨੁ ਤਉ ਲਉ ਜਾਨੈ ਦਿਸੰਤਰ ਜਉ ਲਉ; ਕਹਿਤ ਸੁਨਤ ਹੈ ਬਿਦੇਸ ਕੇ ਸੰਦੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: So long the husband is away on business or work tour, the wife keeps receiving his commands and news of well being through letters. They exchange their emotions through letters.

column2: ਦੇਖਤ ਅਉ ਦੇਖੀਅਤ ਇਤ ਉਤ ਦੋਇ ਹੋਇ; ਭੇਟਤ ਪਰਸਪਰ ਬਿਰਹ ਅਵੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: So long the husband and wife are not together, they indulge in looking here and there. But when they meet they become one in the wake of their separation. Similarly so long a seeker remains away from his deity Guru, he indulges in other means of spiritual

column2: ਖੋਇ ਖੋਇ ਖੋਜੀ ਹੋਇ ਖੋਜਤ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਮਦ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਨਤ ਪ੍ਰਵੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a deer keeps wandering and searching for the musk that he keeps smelling and is unaware of the means to find it, so would a seeker keep wandering till he meets with True Guru and learns the way of God-realisation.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ; ਸ੍ਵਾਮੀ ਸੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨਿਰੰਤਰਿ ਆਦੇਸ ਕੈ॥੧੮੬॥
column5: When a disciple meets with Guru, the all knowing Lord then comes and resides in the heart of the disciple. He then meditates, contemplates and worships the Master Lord as a slave and serves His command and will. (186)

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਸੰਗ ਪ੍ਰੀਤ ਇਕ ਅੰਗੀ ਹੋਇ; ਚੰਦ੍ਰਮਾ ਚਕੋਰ. ਘਨ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਨ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Love of a lamp and a moth (winged insect) is one-sided. Similarly is the love of Chakor with the moon and of rain bird (Papiha) with clouds;

column2: ਚਕਈ ਅਉ ਸੂਰ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਜਿਉ ਕਮਲ ਅਲਿ; ਕਾਸਟ ਅਗਨਿ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਨਾਦ ਕੋ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as love of Casarca ferruginea (Chakv) with Sun, fish with water, a bumble bee with lotus flower, wood and fire, a deer and musical sound is one-sided,

column2: ਪਿਤ ਸੁਤ ਹਿਤ ਅਰੁ ਭਾਮਨੀ ਭਤਾਰ ਗਤਿ; ਮਾਇਆ ਅਉ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਦੁਆਰ ਮਿਟਤ ਨ ਛੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: So is the love of father with son, wife and husband, attachment with worldly attractions is one-sided and like chronic contagious disease cannot be eradicated.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਸਾਚੋ; ਲੋਕ ਪਰਲੋਕ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥੧੮੭॥
column5: Contrary to the above union and grandeur of the True Guru with his Sikhs is True. It is uniform like warp and woof of a cloth. It is comforting in the world beyond. (187)

column2: ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਪਿਆਰ; ਮਿਥਨ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: There are several types of worldly loves but all these are false and considered a source of distress.

column2: ਬੇਦ ਮਰਜਾਦ ਮੈ ਕਹਤ ਹੈ ਕਥਾ ਅਨੇਕ; ਸੁਨੀਐ ਨ ਤੈਸੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਮਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Several love episodes are found used in the Vedas in order to explain certain point but none is heard or believed to be anywhere near the love of a Sikh with his Guru and holy congregation.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਜਗਤ ਭਗਤ ਬਿਖੈ; ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦਿ. ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਹੂੰ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Such true love cannot be found in methods and statements of knowledge, in saying of pious persons in the melodies sung in various modes with accompaniment of musical instruments from one end of world to the other.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਜੈਸੋ; ਤੈਸੋ ਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਕ ਬਿਖੈ ਅਉਰ ਉਰ ਆਨੀਐ॥੧੮੮॥
column5: The expression of love between the Sikhs and the holy congregation of the True Guru has unique grandeur and such love cannot find its match in anyone's heart in the three worlds. (188)

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ. ਪੂਰਨ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਜਉ ਕਰੈ; ਹਰੈ ਹਉਮੈ ਰੋਗੁ. ਰਿਦੈ ਨਿਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: When the True Guru, an embodiment of complete and the only Lord becomes clement, he destroys the melody of ego, instilling humility in the heart.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਮਿਲਿ; ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the kindness of the True Guru, one gets attached with Word Guru (Shabad Guru) in the company of saintly persons. The sentiment of loving worship destroys the duality from the mind.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਪੂਰਨ ਹੁਇ; ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਬਿਖੈ ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the magnificence of the True Guru, the relishing of loving elixir-like Naam, one feels satiated. Becoming wondrous and devoted, one indulges in the meditation on the name of fearless Lord.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਇ ਚਾਇ. ਚਿੰਤਾ ਮੈ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਦਾਸ ਹੈ॥੧੮੯॥
column5: With the kindness of True Guru renouncing fear and worry one gets into a state of ecstasy and by adopting consecration of True Guru one becomes a slave of the Guru. (189)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ ਆਸਾ ਮੈ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: An obedient disciple of the True Guru lodges the Guru's word in his consciousness in the holy company of God-loving people. He protects his mind from the influence of maya (mammon) and remains free from the worldly options and conceptions.

column2: ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਧਾਮ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਇ ਰਿਦੈ; ਬਰਤੈ ਬਰਤਮਾਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Living and dealing with the world, the Naam of the Lord which is treasure-house of indifference to the worldly attractions gets lodged in his mind. Thus the divine light effulges in his heart.

column2: ਸੂਖਮ ਸਥੂਲ ਏਕ. ਏਕ ਅਉ ਅਨੇਕ ਮੇਕ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Supreme Lord who manifests in perceptible and subtle ways in everything of the world becomes his support when he contemplates on Him. He reposes his confidence in that Lord alone.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਲਿਵ. ਆਪਾ ਖੋਇ ਹੋਇ ਰੇਨ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਦਾਸ ਹੈ॥੧੯੦॥
column5: By engrossing and attaching the mind in the refuge of the holy feet of the True Guru, one destroys his egocentricity and adopts humility. He lives in the service of holy men and becomes a true servant of the Guru by accepting the teachings of the True Gur

column2: ਹਉਮੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨ ਕੈ ਅਗਿਆਨਤਾ ਅਵਗਿਆ ਗੁਰ; ਨਿੰਦਾ ਗੁਰ ਦਾਸਨ ਕੈ. ਨਾਮ ਗੁਰਦਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Under the influence of self-pride, ego and ignorance, I show scant respect to the Guru and indulge in slander of his servants. Yet I have named myself the slave of Guru.

column2: ਮਹੁਰਾ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਮੀਠਾ. ਗਈ ਸੋ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਆਈ; ਰੂਠੀ ਕਉ ਕਹਿਤ ਤੂਠੀ ਹੋਤ ਉਪਹਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: This is like poisonous root or tuber of Aconytum Ferox (Mitha Mauhra) that is called sweet or an infected eye that is called 'akh ai hai' and one who suffers from smallpox is said to have been visited and blessed by mother (Mata). This is a big joke.

column2: ਬਾਂਝ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਸਪੂਤੀ. ਦੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਸੁਹਾਗਨਿ; ਕੁਰੀਤਿ ਸੁਰੀਤਿ. ਕਾਟਿਓ ਨਕਟਾ ਕੋ ਨਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just out of fun a barren woman is called Saputi (one blessed with sons), an abandoned woman is called happily married, it is no different than calling an evil rite as auspicious or one with chopped nose as beautiful.

column2: ਬਾਵਰੋ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਭੋਰੋ. ਆਂਧਰੈ ਕਹੈ ਸੁਜਾਖੋ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸਮੀਪ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਂਸੁ ਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਹੈ॥੧੯੧॥
column5: Just as a crazy person is addressed as simpleton, or a blind person who can see are all a crazy and wrong expressions, Similarly a bamboo tree even if it thrives in the close proximity of a Sandalwood tree cannot acquire its fragrance, so would a person l

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਏਕ ਮੇਕ ਰੋਮ ਨ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਕੋਟਿ; ਹੋਮ ਜਗਿ ਭੋਗ ਨਈਬੇਦ ਪੂਜਾਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: No one, not even millions of offerings to the fire, celestial feasts, offerings to the gods and other forms of worship, rites and rituals can reach even a hair of a Sikh who has become one with his True Guru.

column2: ਜੋਗ ਧਿਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਅਧਿਆਤਮ ਰਿਧਿ ਸਿਧਿ ਨਿਧੋ; ਜਪ ਤਪ ਸੰਜਮਾਦਿ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Many forms of Yogas contemplations, exercises to control the body and other disciplines of Yoga, miraculous powers and other forms of obstinate worships cannot reach to match a hair of a Guru's Sikh.

column2: ਸਿੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਪੁਰਾਨ ਬੇਦ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਅਉ ਸਾਅੰਗੀਤ; ਸੁਰਸਰਿ ਦੇਵ ਸਥਲ ਮਾਇਆ ਬਿਸਥਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: All the Simritis, Vedas, Purans, other scriptures, music, rivers like Ganges, abodes of gods and expanse of mammon in the entire Universe can reach the praise of a hair of a Guru's Sikh who has become one with the True Guru.

column2: ਕੋਟਨਿ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਸੰਖ ਜਾ ਕੈ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੧੯੨॥
column5: Countless are the congregations of such Sikhs of the Guru. Such a True Guru is beyond count. He is infinite. We salute at His holy feet again and again. (192)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮਾਥੈ ਲਾਗੀ; ਬਾਛਤ ਸਕਲ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਪਗ ਰੇਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Sikh of the Guru who is blessed by the holy dust of the feet of the True Guru (who receives the boon of Naam Simran from the True Guru), the entire Universe crave for the dust of his feet.

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕੋਟਿ ਕਮਲਾ ਕਲਪਤਰੁ; ਪਾਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਚਿੰਤਾਮਨਿ ਕਾਮਧੇਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Millions of goddesses of wealth, tree of Indra's heavenly garden (Kalap- Variksh), philosopher stones, elixirs, distress removing forces and heavenly cows (Kamdhenu) desire the touch of such a Sikh of the Guru.

column2: ਸੁਰਿ ਨਰ ਨਾਥ ਮੁਨਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਅਉ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ; ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਜੇਨ ਕੇਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Millions of gods, humans, sages, master yogis, all the three worlds, the three times, wondrous knowledge of Vedas and many such estimates beg for the holy dust of the feet of such a disciple of the Guru.

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਸੰਖ ਜਾ ਕੈ; ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਦੇਨ ਹੈ॥੧੯੩॥
column5: There are numerous congregations of such Sikhs of the True Guru. I bow again and again before such True Guru who is the blesser of such elixir-like Naam that provides comfort and peace. (193)

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਅਤਿ; ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਚਾਇ ਕੈ ਚਈਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The glory and grandeur of the Sikhs of Guru who are one with the True Guru and are perpetually in touch with His holy feet is beyond mention. Such Sikhs are ever motivated to meditate more and more on the Lord's name.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਲਿਵ ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਬਚਨ ਤੰਬੋਲ ਸੰਗ ਰੰਗ ਹੁਇ ਰੰਗੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The vision of the Sikhs of the Guru is ever fixed in the astonishing form of the True Guru. Such Sikhs are ever dyed in the hue of Naam Simran that they repeatedly meditate on like perpetually chewing betel leaf and nut.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ. ਲੀਨ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਗਤਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like a fish meeting water, the divine word of the True Guru when lodged in the mind, they remain engrossed in Lord's name. They themselves become nectar-like by constant meditation on the elixir-like Naam that they keep relishing all the time.

column2: ਸੋਭਾ ਨਿਧਿ ਸੋਭ ਕੋਟਿ ਓਟ ਲੋਭ ਕੈ ਲੁਭਿਤ; ਕੋਟਿ ਛਬਿ ਛਾਹ ਛਿਪੈ. ਛਬਿ ਕੈ ਛਬੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥੧੯੪॥
column5: These pious Sikhs are store-house of adulations. Millions of adulations crave for their praise and seek their refuge. They are so handsome and beautiful that millions of beautiful forms are nothing before them. (194)

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਏਕ ਮੇਕ ਰੋਮ ਕੀ ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਕੋ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: The glory of a hair of a Sikh who has become one with the True Guru cannot be narrated. Then who can fathom the greatness of a congregation of such glorious Sikhs?

column2: ਏਕ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਕੇ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਕੋ ਨ ਪਾਰਾਵਾਰੁ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਮਾਵਈ॥
column5: The One Formless God whose expanse is limitless is always permeating in the congregation of devotees absorbed in His name.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮੈ ਨਿਵਾਸ; ਦਾਸਨ ਦਾਸਾਨ ਮਤਿ ਆਪਾ ਨ ਜਤਾਵਈ॥
column5: The True Guru who is manifest of the Lord resides in the congregation of holy men. But such Sikhs who are united with True Guru are very humble and they remain servants of Lord's servants. They shed all their ego.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗੁਰ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਹੈ; ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਵਾ ਕੀ ਵਾਹੀ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਈ॥੧੯੫॥
column5: True Guru is great and so are His disciples who constitute his holy congregation. The light divine of such a True Guru. entangled in the holy gathering like warp and 'weft of a cloth. Grandeur of such True Guru befits only Him and nobody can reach Him. (1

column2: ਪਵਨਹਿ ਪਵਨ ਮਿਲਤ ਨਹੀ ਪੇਖੀਅਤ; ਸਲਿਲੈ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਿਲਤ ਨਾਹਿ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Air mixed with air and water mixed with water cannot be distinguished.

column2: ਜੋਤੀ ਮਿਲੇ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੋਤ ਭਿੰਨ ਭਿੰਨ ਕੈਸੇ ਕਰਿ; ਭਸਮਹਿ ਭਸਮ ਸਮਾਨੀ. ਕੈਸੇ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: How can light merging with another light be seen separately? How can ashes mixed with ashes be distinguished?

column2: ਕੈਸੇ ਪੰਚਤਤ ਮੇਲ ਖੇਲੁ ਹੋਤ ਪਿੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ; ਬਿਛੁਰਤ ਪਿੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਕੈਸੇ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Who knows how a body constituted of five elements take shape? How can one discern what happens to the soul when it leaves the body?

column2: ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਅਗਮਿਤਿ ਕੈਸੇ ਉਰ ਆਨੀਐ॥੧੯੬॥
column5: Similarly no one can assess the state of such Sikhs who have become one with the True Guru. That state is astonishing and wonderful. It cannot be known through the knowledge of scriptures nor through contemplation. One cannot even make an estimate or a gu

column2: ਚਾਰ ਕੁੰਟ. ਸਾਤ ਦੀਪ ਮੈ. ਨ ਨਵ ਖੰਡ ਬਿਖੈ; ਦਹਦਿਸਿ ਦੇਖੀਐ ਨ ਬਨ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Glory of the union of True Guru and the devotees cannot be known or estimated in all the four directions, seven seas, in all the forests and nine regions.

column2: ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਕੈ ਨ ਦੇਖਿਓ ਸੁਨਿਓ; ਸ੍ਵਰਗ ਪਇਆਲ ਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮੰਡਲ ਨ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: This grandeur has not been heard or read in wondrous knowledge of the Vedas. It is not believed to exist in heavens, nether regions nor in the worldly regions.

column2: ਭੂਤ ਅਉ ਭਵਿਖ ਨ ਬਰਤਮਾਨ ਚਾਰੋ ਜੁਗ; ਚਤੁਰ ਬਰਨ ਖਟ ਦਰਸ ਨ ਧਿਆਨੀਐ॥
column5: It cannot be perceived in the four eons, three periods, four sections of the society and even in the six philosophical Scriptures.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਜੈਸੋ; ਤੈਸੋ ਅਉਰ ਠਉਰ ਸੁਨੀਐ ਨ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥੧੯੭॥
column5: The union of True Guru and his Sikhs is so indescribable and marvellous that such state is not heard or seen anywhere else. (197)

column2: ਊਖ ਮੈ ਪਯੂਖ ਰਸ. ਰਸਨਾ ਰਹਿਤ ਹੋਇ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਤਾਸ ਨਾਸਕਾ ਨ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Sugarcane has elixir-like sweet juice but it has no tongue to enjoy it. Sandalwood has fragrance but the tree is without nostrils to enjoy the smell.

column2: ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਬਿਹੂਨ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਗਤਿ; ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਰਨ ਬਿਨੁ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸੁ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Musical instruments produce sound to bring awe to the listeners but it is without the ears that can listen to its melody. Myriads of colour and shapes are there to attract the eyes but they are without any ability to see such beauty themselves.

column2: ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਨ ਸਪਰਸ ਉਸਨ ਸੀਤ; ਕਰ ਚਰਨ ਹੀਨ ਧਰ ਅਉਖਧੀ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Philosopher-stone has power to turn any metal into gold but it is without any sense of touch even to feel cold or heat. Many herbs grow in the earth but without hands and feet, it can do nothing to reach anywhere.

column2: ਜਾਹਿ ਪੰਚ ਦੋਖ. ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਮੋਖ ਪਾਵੈ ਕੈਸੇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਹਜ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਹੁਇ ਅਛੋਤ ਹੈ॥੧੯੮॥
column5: A person who has all the five senses of knowledge and is also deeply infected by the five vices of relishment, smell, hearing, touch and seeing, how can he achieve salvation that is viceless. Only the obedient Sikhs of Guru who obey the command of a True

column2: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਹੈ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਆਦ ਹੀਨ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨ ਅਨਹਦ ਨਾਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: A tongue without savouring the elixir-like Naam and ears that are without hearing the unstruck melody of recitation of Lord's name are useless and vain.

column2: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨ ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਦੇਖੀਅਤਿ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਸੁਆਸ ਨਹੀ ਬਾਸੁ ਪਰਮਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: The eyes that do not see the true vision of thyself and the breaths that do not smell of Lord's fragrance are no good either.

column2: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਕਰ ਗੁਰ ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਬਿਨੁ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਾਰਗ ਬਿਹੂਨ ਪਗ ਬਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: The hands that have not touched the philosopher-stone like feet of the True Guru are of no use. Those feet which have not treaded towards the door of the True Guru are no good either.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਪੰਗ ਸਰਬੰਗ ਲਿਵ; ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥੧੯੯॥
column5: Every limb of the Sikhs who are obedient to the True Guru are pious. By the grace of the company of holy people, their mind and vision remains focused in meditation on Naam and glimpse of the True Guru. (199)

column2: ਪਸੂਆ ਮਨੁਖ ਦੇਹ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅੰਤਰੁ ਇਹੈ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕੋ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਅਬਿਬੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: The only difference in human and animal body is that a human is aware of the union of consciousness and the holy word of the Guru but animal has no such knowledge nor any ability.

column2: ਪਸੁ ਹਰਿਆਉ ਕਹਿਓ ਸੁਨਿਓ ਅਨਸੁਨਿਓ ਕਰੈ; ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਰਿਦੈ ਟੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: If an animal is asked to stay away from green fields, or pasture land, it ignores it but a human being lodges the teachings of the True Guru in his heart and adheres to it.

column2: ਪਸੂਆ ਸਬਦ ਹੀਨ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਨ ਬੋਲਿ ਸਕੈ; ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਬੋਲੈ ਬਚਨ ਅਨੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: Devoid of words, an animal cannot speak with its tongue but a human can speak several words.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸਮਝਿ ਬੋਲੈ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ; ਨਾਤੁਰ ਅਚੇਤ ਪਸੁ ਪ੍ਰੇਤ ਹੂੰ ਮੈ ਏਕ ਹੈ॥੨੦੦॥
column5: If a man listens, understands and speaks the words of Guru, he is a wise and intelligent person. Otherwise he too is one among the ignorant animal and a fool. (200)

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੀਨ ਪਸੂਆ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਦੇਹ; ਖੜ ਖਾਏ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਕੋ ਸੁਆਉ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who has no perception of Guru’s words is far inferior than an animal who eats hay and grass and yields nectar like milk.

column2: ਗੋਬਰ ਗੋਮੂਤ੍ਰ ਸੂਤ੍ਰ ਪਰਮਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਭਏ; ਮਾਨਸ ਦੇਹੀ ਨਿਖਿਧ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਅਪਿਆਉ ਹੈ॥
column5: According to Hindu mythology, cowdung and cow's urine are considered sacred but cursed is a human body that eats elixir- like food and spreads filth all around.

column2: ਬਚਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਸਾਧਨ ਕੈ ਸਾਧ ਭਏ; ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਖਲ ਬਚਨ ਦੁਰਾਉ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who take the support of the knowledgeable sermons of the True Guru and practice these in their life are superb saintly persons. On the contrary, those who shy away from the teachings of the True Guru are low of status, evil and foolish.

column2: ਰਸਨਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸਾਇਨ ਹੁਇ; ਮਾਨਸ ਬਿਖੈ ਧਰ ਬਿਖਮ ਬਿਖੁ ਤਾਉ ਹੈ॥੨੦੧॥
column5: By meditation on His name, such saintly persons themselves become the fountains of elixir-like Naam. Those who are bereft of the words of the Guru and are engrossed in maya are scary like poisonous snakes and full of venom. (201)

column2: ਪਸੂ ਖੜ ਖਾਤ ਖਲ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੀਨ; ਮੋਨ ਕੋ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਪੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: An animal eats green grass and hay. He is bereft of all knowledge of Lord's word. Due to his inability to speak, gives nectar-like milk.

column2: ਨਾਨਾ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਮਾਨਸ ਮੁਖ; ਰਸਨ ਰਸੀਲੀ ਹੋਇ ਸੋਈ ਭਲੀ ਤਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: A man eats and enjoys many types of food materials with his tongue but he becomes praiseworthy only if his tongue is sweetened with the sweetness of Lord's name.

column2: ਬਚਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਫਲ; ਬਚਨ ਬਿਹੂਨ ਪਸੁ ਪਰਮਿਤਿ ਆਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: Purpose of human life is to take refuge in the meditation of His Naam. But one devoid of the teachings of the True Guru is the worst kind of animal.

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਗਤਿ ਬਚਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਹੀਨ; ਬਿਖਧਰ ਬਿਖਮ ਚਕਤ ਚਿਤ ਚਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥੨੦੨॥
column5: One who is bereft of teachings of True Guru, craves and wanders in search of worldly pleasure and remains vexed for their acquisition. His state is like a dangerous poisonous snake. (202)

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਆਪੈ ਦ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਹੁਇ; ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਬਿਰਹੁ ਬਿਆਪੈ ਮਧੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a married woman temporarily separated from her husband feels the pangs of separation, her inability to hear the sweet sound of her husband distresses her, so do the Sikhs suffer the pangs of separation.

column2: ਸੰਗਮ ਸਮਾਗਮ ਬਿਰਹੁ ਬਿਆਪੈ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਕੈ; ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਅੰਕਮਾਲ ਕੀ ਰਚਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife feels a strong desire to speak to her husband after a long separation, her fond desire to feel her husband against her breast troubles her, so do the Sikhs long to feel the divine embrace of their True Guru.

column2: ਸਿਹਜਾ ਗਵਨ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਆਪੈ ਚਰਨ ਹੁਇ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਿਰਹ ਸ੍ਰਬੰਗ ਹੁਇ ਸਚਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: As reaching the nuptial bed of her husband troubles the wife when her husband is not there but she is filled with passion and love; so does a Sikh separated from his Guru craves like a fish out of water to touch the True Guru.

column2: ਰੋਮ ਰੋਮ ਬਿਰਹ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਕੈ ਬਿਹਬਲ ਭਈ; ਸਸਾ ਜਿਉ ਬਹੀਰ ਪੀਰ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਤਚਨ ਕੈ॥੨੦੩॥
column5: A separated wife feels love sickness in every hair of her body and remains distressed like a rabbit that has been surrounded by hunters from all sides. So does a Sikh feel the pangs of separation and longs to meet his True Guru at the earliest. (203)

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਬਦਨ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ; ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਕਹਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: A momentary look of the True Guru brings a very attractive and ecstatic look on the face of wife-like Sikh of the True Guru. She (Sikh) then is honoured to be an astoundingly beautiful heroine.

column2: ਲੋਚਨ ਕੀ ਪੁਤਰੀ ਮੈ ਤਨਕ ਤਾਰਕਾ ਸਿਆਮ; ਤਾ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬ ਤਿਲ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਬਨਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the casting of a look of grace by the True Guru, the small black spot in the eyes of True Guru leaves a mole on the face of the wife-like Sikh. Such a mole enhances the beauty of the wife-like Sikh further.

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਛਬਿ ਤਿਲ ਛਿਪਤ ਛਾਹ; ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਸੋਭ ਲੋਭ ਲਲਚਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Beauties of the world get hidden in the shadow of that mole and millions desire eagerly to covet glory of that mole.

column2: ਕੋਟਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਕੇ ਨਾਇਕ ਕੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਭਈ; ਤਿਲ ਕੇ ਤਿਲਕ ਸਰਬ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥੨੦੪॥
column5: The grace that a wife-like Sikh gets by an impression of True Guru's kind glance makes her the maid of the Master of millions of celestial regions. Because of that mole, she surpasses all other seeker-wives in beauty. None can match her. (204)

column2: ਸੁਪਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ. ਬਾਨਕ ਬਨੇ ਬਚਿਤ੍ਰ; ਪਾਵਨ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਆਜ ਮੋਰੇ ਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Himself holy and capable of making other pious-the friendly True Guru has come into my dream beautifully attired and adored. It is indeed a wonderful marvel for me.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਦਇਆਲ ਲਾਲ. ਲੋਚਨ ਬਿਸਾਲ; ਮੁਖ ਬਚਨ ਰਸਾਲ. ਮਧੁ ਮਧੁਰ ਪੀਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Beloved Lord is sweet of words, big eyed and clement of form. Believe me! It is like He blessing us with honeyed elixir.

column2: ਸੋਭਿਤ ਸਿਹਜਾਸਨ ਬਿਲਾਸਨ ਦੈ ਅੰਕਮਾਲ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਿਸਮ ਹੁਇ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: He looked pleased and honoured me by occupying my bedlike heart. I was lost in the love filled trance of Nam Amrit that merged me into a state of equipoise.

column2: ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਨਿ ਅਖੀਆਂ ਉਘਰ ਗਈ; ਭਈ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਗਤਿ. ਬਿਰਹ ਜਗਾਏ ਹੈ॥੨੦੫॥
column5: Enjoying the bliss of divine dream, I was woken up by the voice of rain-bird and that broke my celestial dream. The awe and marvel of love-filled state disappeared reawakening the pangs of separation. I was restless like a fish out of water. (205)

column2: ਦੇਖਬੋ ਕਉ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨ ਦਰਸ ਦਿਖਾਇਬੇ ਕਉ; ਕੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਿਯ ਦਰਸਨ ਦੇਖੀਐ ਦਿਖਾਈਐ॥
column5: I neither have enlightened eyes to have a glimpse of my unique, radiant and dear lover nor have I the power to show His glimpse to anyone. Then how can one see or even show a glimpse of the lover?

column2: ਕਹਿਬੇ ਕਉ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੈ ਨ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਸੁਨਬੇ ਕਉ; ਕੈਸੇ ਗੁਨਨਿਧਿ ਗੁਨ ਸੁਨੀਐ ਸੁਨਾਈਐ॥
column5: I do not have the wisdom to describe the virtues of my beloved who is treasure-house of goodness. Nor do I have ears to listen to his adulations. Then how should we listen and recite the panegyrics of the fountain of merits and excellence?

column2: ਮਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ. ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਮੈ ਨ ਮਨ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਹੁਇ ਨ ਉਨਮਨ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: The mind neither inhabits the teachings of the True Guru nor does it engross itself in the sermons of Guru. The mind does not achieve stability in the words of Guru. Then how can one get engrossed in higher spiritual state?

column2: ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਭੰਗ. ਰੰਗ ਰੂਪ ਕੁਲ ਹੀਨ ਦੀਨ; ਕੈਸੇ ਬਹੁਨਾਇਕ ਕੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਕਹਾਈਐ॥੨੦੬॥
column5: My whole body is aching. I, the meek and devoid of respect, has neither beauty nor high caste. Then how can I become and be known as the most favourite love of my Master Lord? (206)

column2: ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਰੋਗ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਰਹਨੀ; ਕਹਤ ਸੰਦੇਸ ਪਥਿਕਨ ਪੈ ਉਸਾਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: The to pangs of separation and disunion from her beloved husband, a distressed wife heaves big sighs and sends messages to her beloved husband through the wayfarers.

column2: ਦੇਖਹੁ ਤ੍ਰਿਗਦ ਜੋਨਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੈ ਪਰੇਵਾ; ਪਰ ਕਰ ਨਾਰਿ ਦੇਖਿ ਟੂਟਤ ਅਕਾਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: My beloved! Look how a lovelorn pigeon, a species of devious origin, impatiently flies down from high sky to his mate.

column2: ਤੁਮ ਤੋ ਚਤੁਰਦਸ ਬਿਦਿਆ ਕੇ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ; ਤ੍ਰਿਅ ਨ ਛੁਡਾਵਹੁ ਬਿਰਹ ਰਿਪੁ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: My beloved! You are a store-house of all the knowledge; why don't you rid your woman from the pangs of separation?

column2: ਚਰਨ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਤਾਰਿਕਾ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ; ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਹਿ ਰਵਿ ਦਰਸ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਤੇ॥੨੦੭॥
column5: The twinkling stars frighten everyone during the dark night, so am I being distressed by the separation from your holy feet. All these distressing twinkling stars will disappear soon as your Sun-like effulgent glimpse become visible. (207)

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਦੇਖਿ ਅਉ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਆਪ; ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੋਭਾ ਦੈ ਸੁਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: The seeker woman, who is liked by the True Guru Master, is glanced at with a look of clemency by the beloved Master who reveals Himself to her. By His clemency and glimpse, the hapless woman is blessed with goodness making her praiseworthy.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਮੁਖ ਬਚਨ ਸੁਨਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ; ਸਬਦਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਪਜਾਵਈ॥
column5: One who is liked by the beloved Master, is blessed with His divine words. By the union of His words and consciousness, He enlightens her with Guru's sermons.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਦਸਦਿਸਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ; ਸੋਈ ਬਹੁ ਨਾਇਕ ਕੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: The seeker woman who is loved by her True Guru, is revealed by Him in all the ten directions of the world. Then she is addressed and known as supreme beloved of the Master who is the master of many more seeker brides.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਸਿਹਜਾਸਨ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸ ਕਰਿ ਅਪਿਓ ਪੀਆਵਈ॥੨੦੮॥
column5: The seeker bride who is liked by beloved True Guru, is united with Him on the mind like divine bed. Enamoured by her love, He makes her drink deep the elixir of Naam Amrit. (208)

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਸੁੰਦਰਤਾ ਕੈ ਸੁਹਾਵੈ; ਸੋਈ ਸੁੰਦਰੀ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਛਬਿ ਕੈ ਛਬੀਲੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: The female living being (jeev Istri) who has found favour with the True Guru Master the manifest form of the Lord, becomes virtuous and praiseworthy due to the blessing of spiritual beauty to her. That truly is called the beauty.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਬਾਨਕ ਬਧੂ ਬਨਾਵੈ; ਸੋਈ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਰੰਗ ਮੈ ਰੰਗੀਲੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: She who is loved by her beloved master, is made into a highly adorable bride by Him. One who is ever engrossed in the hue of Lord's meditation is truly a blessed married woman.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾ ਕੀ ਸਬੈ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪੁਜਾਵੈ; ਸੋਈ ਕਾਮਨੀ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਸੀਲ ਕੈ ਸੁਸੀਲੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: The (seeker) female living being who gains the favour of her beloved master has all her desires fulfilled by Him. By virtue of her superior nature, she is well behaved and that makes her famous as beautiful lady in the true sense.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਭਾਵੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਲੈ ਪੀਆਵੈ; ਸੋਈ ਪ੍ਰੇਮਨੀ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸੀਲੀ ਹੈ॥੨੦੯॥
column5: The seeker woman who is liked by dear True Guru, she is blessed with relishing the Nam elixir of Lord's love. One who drinks deep the divine elixir is loved one in the true sense. (209)

column2: ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਰੋਗ ਸੇਤ ਰੂਪ ਹੁਇ ਕ੍ਰਿਤਾਸ; ਟੂਕ ਟੂਕ ਭਏ ਪਾਤੀ ਲਿਖੀਐ ਬਿਦੇਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: A sentient woman (devoted Sikh) separated from her beloved True Guru writes letter to her beloved stating that his separation and long disjunction has made her complexion paper white while her limbs are losing their strength to the extent of falling apart

column2: ਬਿਰਹ ਅਗਨਿ ਸੇ ਸਵਾਨੀ ਮਾਸੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਨ ਹੁਇ; ਬਿਰਹਨੀ ਭੇਖ ਲੇਖ ਬਿਖਮ ਸੰਦੇਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: The separated woman writes the state of her distress and the pangs she has been bearing. She wails that his separation has virtually turned the colour of her skin black.

column2: ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਰੋਗ ਲੇਖਨਿ ਕੀ ਛਾਤੀ ਫਾਟੀ; ਰੁਦਨ ਕਰਤ ਲਿਖੈ ਆਤਮ ਅਵੇਸ ਤੇ॥
column5: Crying from the core of her heart, the separated woman writes that because of the distress of bearing separation, even the breast of the pen that she is writing with has cracked.

column2: ਬਿਰਹ ਉਸਾਸਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸਨ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਗਤਿ; ਬਿਰਹਨੀ ਕੈਸੇ ਜੀਐ ਬਿਰਹ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਤੇ॥੨੧੦॥
column5: Heaving cold sighs and lamenting, she expresses her distressed state and asks how could anyone live when the weapon of separation had penetrated deep into her heart. (210)

column2: ਪੂਰਬ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਮਿਲਿ ਸੁਜਨ ਸਗਾਈ ਹੋਤ; ਸਿਮਰਤ ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਸੰਦੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: Deeds of previous births bring together noble people and they join in the form of holy congregation to establish union with True Guru. Such a maid who is betrothed thus hears the messages of her True Guru master from others and remembers them.

column2: ਬਿਧਿ ਸੇ ਬਿਵਾਹੇ ਮਿਲਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਲਿਵ; ਬਿਦ੍ਯਮਾਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਰਸ ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਭੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: When according to tradition, marriage is solemnised, that is to say she is consecrated by Guru and an accord gets established between them, then her mind is engrossed in the form, colour, attire and pleasure of the master True Guru.

column2: ਰੈਨ ਸੈਨ ਸਮੈ ਸ੍ਰੁਤਿ ਸਬਦ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ; ਆਤਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰਮਾਤਮ ਪ੍ਰਵੇਸ ਕੈ॥
column5: At night when it is time for people to sleep, a seeker of the Lord takes refuge in the knowledge of the divine words and achieving soulful ecstasy through practicing of Naam, unites in the holy feet of the Lord.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਇਕਤ੍ਰ ਹੋਇ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸਿ ਹੋਤ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਵੇਸ ਕੈ॥੨੧੧॥
column5: Contemplating thus she (jeev istri) crosses all the stages of knowledge and becomes one with dear beloved and influenced by His loving pleasure, she gets engrossed in wondrous and marvellous spiritual state. (211)

column2: ਏਕ ਸੈ ਅਧਿਕ ਏਕ. ਨਾਇਕਾ ਅਨੇਕ ਜਾ ਕੈ; ਦੀਨ ਕੇ ਦਿਆਲ ਹੁਇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਧਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: The dear beloved who has not one but many obedient consorts; the dispenser of kindness on the distressed, the beloved has been clement on me.

column2: ਸਜਨੀ ਰਜਨੀ ਸਸਿ. ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅਉਸਰ ਮੈ; ਅਬਲੇ ਅਧੀਨ ਗਤਿ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਉਚਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: That moonlit night (the auspicious moment) when the time for me to belong to and enjoy the loving elixir of the Lord came, this humble maid slave in all the humility· made a supplication before the beloved True Guru;

column2: ਜੋਈ ਜੋਈ ਆਗਿਆ ਹੋਇ. ਸੋਈ ਸੋਈ ਮਾਨਿ ਜਾਨਿ; ਹਾਥ ਜੋਰੈ ਅਗ੍ਰਭਾਗਿ ਹੋਇ ਆਗਿਆਕਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Oh beloved! Whatever will be your command, I shall obey implicitly. I shall ever serve you obediently and with humility.

column2: ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਚਾਇ ਕੈ ਚਈਲੋ ਭਜਉ; ਸਫਲ ਜਨਮ ਧੰਨਿ ਆਜ ਮੇਰੀ ਬਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥੨੧੨॥
column5: I shall serve you with dedication and devotion of loving worship in my heart. This moment when you have so kindly blessed me with your consecration, my human birth has become purposeful since my turn to meet my beloved Lord has come. (212)

column2: ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਕੀ ਪੁਤਰੀ ਮੈ ਤਨਿਕ ਤਾਰਿਕਾ ਸਿਆਮ; ਤਾ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬੁ ਤਿਲ ਤਿਲਕ ਤ੍ਰਿਲੋਕ ਕੋ॥
column5: When the True Guru casts a sight of grace on the seeker woman, the thin black star-like line in the pupil of my beloved True Guru has cast a very subtle image on the face of the seeker woman that is adoring and beseeming like a mole in the three worlds.

column2: ਬਨਿਤਾ ਬਦਨ ਪਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਬਨਾਇ ਰਾਖਿਓ; ਕਾਮਦੇਵ ਕੋਟਿ ਲੋਟ ਪੋਟ ਅਵਿਲੋਕ ਕੋ॥
column5: That subtle image of the star like shinning line has made beauty so conspicuous and astonishing that millions of cupids are becoming restless. Heaving cold sighs and lamenting, she expresses her distressed state and asks how could anyone live when the wea

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਰੂਪ ਕੀ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਛਬਿ; ਸਕਲ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਕੋ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਸਰਬ ਥੋਕ ਕੋ॥
column5: The unique beauty as a result of that mole cannot be matched by millions of beautiful forms. The beauty of that mole is beyond the beauty of all adoring things of the world.

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਤਿਲ ਕੀ ਅਤੁਲ ਸੋਭਾ; ਸੁਰਸਤੀ ਕੋਟਿ ਮਾਨ ਭੰਗ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੋਕ ਕੋ॥੨੧੩॥
column5: By virtue of a little kindness of the True Guru, the fame and glory of that miniscule mole is infinite. It is capable of shattering the pride of millions of goddesses of beauty. Even a red legged partridge (Allectoris graeca) engrossed in looking at the m

column2: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਖਟ ਦਰਸ ਨ ਦੇਖੇ; ਸਕਲ ਦਰਸ ਸਮ ਦਰਸ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who has fixed his attention on the vision of the True Guru. is not reassured by the six schools of philosophy nor towards other religious sects. He sees all philosophies in the vision of one True Guru.

column2: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਸਬਦ ਪੰਚ ਸਬਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੰਮਿ; ਸਰਬ ਸਬਦ ਅਨਹਦ ਸਮਝਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who has received Guru's consecration hears melodies of five types of musical instruments deep in his soul because the unstruck music that has appeared in his being due to perpetual meditation on Lord's name has all the melodies in it.

column2: ਮੰਤ੍ਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਕੈ ਅਵੇਸ ਰਿਦੈ; ਆਦਿ ਕਉ ਅਦੇਸ ਕੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By practicing meditation on the Lord, He comes and resides he in the heart. In this state an initiated disciple sees the all-pervading Lord everywhere.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੋਇ; ਏਕ ਅਉ ਅਨੇਕ ਕੇ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥੨੧੪॥
column5: The Sikh who is blessed with knowledge, contemplation and Simran by the True Guru and who relishes the loving elixir, learns the truth of one Lord who pervades in all despite being one. (214)

column2: ਸਤਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੰਜਮ ਨ ਪਤਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਪੂਜਾ ਹੋਇ; ਸਚ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੋਚ ਨ ਜਨੇਊ ਜਤ ਹੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Except the name of the stable and firm Lord, no other deed is righteous. Except for the prayer and worship of Master Lord, worship of gods/goddesses is futile. No piety is beyond the truth and wearing of sacred thread without morality is futile.

column2: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਦੀਖਿਆ ਗਿਆਨ. ਬਿਨੁ ਦਰਸਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਭਾਉ ਬਿਨੁ ਭਗਤਿ ਨ ਕਥਨੀ ਭੈ ਭੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Without obtaining initiation from a True Guru, no knowledge is worthwhile. No contemplation is useful except that of the True Guru. No worship is worth anything if not performed love, nor any view point expressed can invite respect.

column2: ਸਾਂਤਿ ਨ ਸੰਤੋਖ ਬਿਨੁ. ਸੁਖੁ ਨ ਸਹਜ ਬਿਨੁ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Without patience and contentment, peace cannot reside. No true peace and comfort is achievable without acquiring a state of equipoise. Similarly no love can be stable without the union of the word and mind (consciousness).

column2: ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਬਿਨੁ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਨ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ; ਬਿਨੁ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਨ ਰੰਗ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥੨੧੫॥
column5: Without deliberation on His name, one cannot establish faith in the heart and without the holy congregation of divine and saintly persons, engrossment in Lord's name is not possible. (215)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਤਾਹਿ ਜਗ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Sikh who is engrossed in the elixir-like name of the Lord by virtue of the holy dust of the feet of True Guru (due to His company) has the whole world becoming his devotees.

column2: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਗਦ ਗਦ ਹੋਇ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਤਾਹਿ ਜਗਤ ਉਧਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Sikh of the Guru whose every hair blooms hearing the melody of True Guru blessed Naam Simran, his elixir-like words can sail the world across the worldly ocean.

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਗੁਰ ਦਇਆ ਨਿਧਾਨ; ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਦਾਨ ਦੋਖ ਦੁਖ ਹਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A Sikh of the Guru who receives even a very small blessing of the True Guru, becomes capable of giving away all the treasures and allaying the distresses of the others.

column2: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਾਸਨ ਦਾਸ. ਦਾਸਨ ਦਾਸਾਨ ਦਾਸ; ਤਾਸੁ ਨ ਇੰਦ੍ਰਾਦਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਹੈ॥੨੧੬॥
column5: A Sikh who serves the servants of the slaves of the True Guru (who becomes down to earth humble) cannot even be equated with god Indra, Brahma and all the gods and goddesses put together. (216)

column2: ਜਬ ਤੇ ਪਰਮ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਆਏ; ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਲਿਵ ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Since the time a human being takes the refuge in the holy feet of the True Guru, the people of the world then start contemplating in the refuge of his feet.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ; ਚਾਹਤ ਚਰਨ ਰੇਨੁ ਸਕਲ ਆਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: By taking the foot-wash of the True Guru while staying in His refuge, the entire mankind desires to be blessed by his holy feet.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਹਜ ਘਰਿ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਮਤਿ ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: By living in the peaceful refuge of the lotus-like feet of the True Guru, one gets absorbed in a state of equipoise. Because of higher spiritual wisdom, they become stable of mind and consciousness.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧ; ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਕੈ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੨੧੭॥
column5: The glory of the lotus-like feet of the True Guru is beyond comprehension, It is limitless, infinite. He is worthy of salutation again and again. (217)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਜਬ ਤੇ ਰਿਦੈ ਬਸਾਏ; ਤਬ ਤੇ ਅਸਥਿਰ ਚਿਤ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਧਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Since the time a human being attaches his mind with the lotus like feet of the True Guru, his mind becomes stable and it does not wander anywhere.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ; ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤਿ ਅਮਰ ਪਦ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: The refuge of the True Guru's feet provides one with the foot-wash of the True Guru that helps him acquire inimitable state and engrossment in equipoise.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਸੁਖ ਮਨ ਮੈ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਕੀਉ; ਆਨ ਸੁਖ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Since the holy feet of the True Guru got lodged in the heart of a devotee (the devotee took His refuge), the mind of the devotee has shed all other comforts and is absorbed in the meditation of His name.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਵਾਸਨਾ ਨਿਵਾਸ; ਆਨ ਵਾਸ ਫੀਕੀ ਭਈ ਹਿਰਦੇ ਨ ਭਾਵਈ॥੨੧੮॥
column5: Since the fragrance of the holy lotus-feet of the True Guru got lodged in the mind of the devotee, all other fragrances have become prosaic and indifferent for him. (218)

column2: ਬਾਰੀ ਬਹੁ ਨਾਇਕ ਕੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਪਿਆਰੀ ਕੇਰੀ; ਘੇਰੀ ਆਨਿ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਹੁਇ ਨਿੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਨੈਨ ਛਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Known as favourite and loved one of the Master who has many women, when her turn to receive her Master's blessings came she was over-powered by the sleep of ignorance. The sleep-filled eyes made me unaware of everything.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮਨੀ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਚਈਲੀ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਆਗਮ ਕੀ; ਨਿੰਦ੍ਰਾ ਕੋ ਨਿਰਾਦਰ ਕੈ ਸੋਈ ਨ ਭੈ ਭਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: But those Sikh sentient beings who were filled with love in their hearts when they heard that their Master was coming, they forsake sleep and remained alert in their faith and love for the meeting.

column2: ਸਖੀ ਹੁਤੀ ਸੋਤ ਥੀ ਭਈ. ਗਈ ਸੁਖਦਾਇਕ ਪੈ; ਜਹਾ ਕੇ ਤਹਾ ਲੇ ਰਾਖੇ ਸੰਗਮ ਸੁਲਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Despite being favourite of my Master, I remained sleeping in ignorance. I remained bereft of meeting my comfort-giving beloved. I remained wherever I was, separated and bereft of His love and blessings. This is what sleep of ignorance did to me.

column2: ਸੁਪਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਮੈ ਨ ਮ੍ਰਿਤਹਿ ਮਿਲਨ ਦੀਨੀ; ਜਮ ਰੂਪ ਜਾਮਿਨੀ ਨ ਨਿਬਰੈ ਬਿਹਾਇ ਕੈ॥੨੧੯॥
column5: This dream like happening did not let me meet my beloved. Now the death-like night of separation neither ends nor terminates. (219)

column2: ਰੂਪ ਹੀਨ ਕੁਲ ਹੀਨ ਗੁਨ ਹੀਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੀਨ; ਸੋਭਾ ਹੀਨ ਭਾਗ ਹੀਨ ਤਪ ਹੀਨ ਬਾਵਰੀ॥
column5: I the sentient seeker am without attractive looks, do not belong to high caste as deemed of Sikhs of the Guru, without the virtues of Naam, empty of Guru's knowledge, without any praiseworthy traits, unlucky because of vices, bereft of the service of Guru

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਹੀਨ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੀਨ; ਬੁਧਿ ਬਲ ਹੀਨ ਸੂਧੇ ਹਸਤ ਨ ਪਾਵ ਰੀ॥
column5: I am bereft of the kind looks and glimpse of the True Guru, without meditation, weak of power and wisdom, of warped hands and feet because of not doing service of the Guru.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਹੀਨ ਰੀਤਿ ਹੀਨ ਭਾਇ ਭੈ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤ ਹੀਨ; ਚਿਤ ਹੀਨ ਬਿਤ ਹੀਨ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਰੀ॥
column5: I am vacuous of my beloved's love, unaware of Guru's teachings, hollow of devotion, unstable of mind, poor of the wealth of meditation and even lacking in calmness of nature.

column2: ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਹੀਨ ਦੀਨਾ ਧੀਨ ਪਰਾਚੀਨ ਲਗਿ; ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਕੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤਿ ਹੁਇ ਰਾਵਰੀ॥੨੨੦॥
column5: I am inferior from every aspect of life. I don't become humble in order to please my beloved. With all these shortcomings, O my True Guru! How can I acquire the refuge of your holy feet. (220)

column2: ਜਨਨੀ ਸੁਤਹਿ ਬਿਖੁ ਦੇਤ. ਹੇਤੁ ਕਉਨ ਰਾਖੈ; ਘਰੁ ਮੂਸੈ ਪਾਹਰੂਆ. ਕਹੋ ਕੈਸੇ ਰਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: If a mother poisons her son then who will love him? If a watchman robs the house then how can it be protected?

column2: ਕਰੀਐ ਜਉ ਬੋਰੈ ਨਾਵ. ਕਹੋ ਕੈਸੇ ਪਾਵੈ ਪਾਰੁ; ਅਗੂਆਊ ਬਾਟ ਪਾਰੈ. ਕਾ ਪੈ ਦੀਨ ਭਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: If a boatman sinks the boat, then how can the passengers reach the shore beyond? If the-leader cheats on the way, then who can be prayed for justice?

column2: ਖੇਤੈ ਜਉ ਖਾਇ ਬਾਰ. ਕਉਨ ਧਾਇ ਰਾਖਨਹਾਰੁ; ਚਕ੍ਰਵੈ ਕਰੈ ਅਨਿਆਉ. ਪੂਛੈ ਕਉਨੁ ਸਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: If the protecting fence starts eating the crop (caretaker starts destroying the crop) then who will take care of it? If a king becomes unjust who will examine the witness?

column2: ਰੋਗੀਐ ਜਉ ਬੈਦ ਮਾਰੈ. ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਜਉ ਕਮਾਵੈ ਦ੍ਰੋਹੁ; ਗੁਰ ਨ ਮੁਕਤੁ ਕਰੈ. ਕਾ ਪੈ ਅਭਿਲਾਖੀਐ॥੨੨੧॥
column5: If a physician kills the patient, a friend betrays his friend, then who can be trusted? If a Guru does not bless his disciple with salvation, then who else can be expected to be saved? (221)

column2: ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਗਤਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਈਐ॥
column5: The mind wanders like a bumble bee in all four directions. But by coming into the refuge of True Guru and by the blessings of Naam Simran, he merges into peace and comfort of equipoise.

column2: ਸੀਤਲ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਅਤਿ ਕੋਮਲ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ; ਮਧੁ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ. ਅਨਤ ਨ ਧਾਈਐ॥
column5: Once the calming, scented, delicate and very beautiful elixir-like holy dust of the feet of True Guru is received, the mind does not wander in any direction.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਉਨਮਨ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ; ਅਨਹਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਰੁਨ ਝੁਨਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: On account of his association with the holy feet of the True Guru, by remaining in a state of divine will and tranquil state of meditation and ever enjoying a glimpse of the light effulgent, he remains engrossed in the melodious unstruck celestial music.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਬੀਸ ਇਕਕੀਸ ਸੋਹੰ ਸੋਈ ਜਾਨੈ; ਆਪਾ ਅਪਰੰਪਰ ਪਰਮਪਦੁ ਪਾਈਐ॥੨੨੨॥
column5: Believe it! An obedient Sikh of the True Guru becomes aware of the One Lord who is beyond all limits. And thus he reaches the supreme spiritual state. (222)

column2: ਮਨ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਮਦ ਅਛਤ ਅੰਤਰਗਤਿ; ਭੂਲਿਓ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਖੋਜਤ ਫਿਰਤ ਬਨ ਮਾਹੀ ਜੀ॥
column5: Human mind is like a fast running deer who has Naam-like musk within him. But under various doubts and misgivings, he keeps searching it in forest.

column2: ਦਾਦਰ ਸਰੋਜ ਗਤਿ ਏਕੈ ਸਰਵਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਅੰਤਰਿ ਦਿਸੰਤਰਿ ਹੁਇ ਸਮਝਤ ਨਾਹੀ ਜੀ॥
column5: Frog and lotus flower live in the same pond but despite that the frog-like mind does not know lotus as if he is residing in a foreign land. The frog eats moss and not lotus flower. Such is the state of mind who is not aware of the Naam Amrit co-existing w

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਖਿਆਧਰ ਤਜੈ ਨ ਬਿਖੁ ਬਿਖਮ ਕਉ; ਅਹਿਨਿਸ ਬਾਵਨ ਬਿਰਖ ਲਪਟਾਹੀ ਜੀ॥
column5: As a snake never sheds his venom although he keeps coiled around a sandalwood tree so is the state of that person who does not shed his vices even in the holy congregation.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਰਪਤਿ ਸੁਪਨੰਤਰਿ ਭੇਖਾਰੀ ਹੋਇ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਗਤ ਮੈ ਭਰਮ ਮਿਟਾਹੀ ਜੀ॥੨੨੩॥
column5: The state of our wandering mind is like a king who becomes a beggar in his dream. But the mind of a Sikh of the Guru dispels all his doubts and misgivings with the power of Naam Simran and recognising his self, lives a purposeful, contented and a happy li

column2: ਬਾਇ ਹੁਇ ਬਘੂਲਾ ਬਾਇ ਮੰਡਲ ਫਿਰੈ ਤਉ ਕਹਾ; ਬਾਸਨਾ ਕੀ ਆਗਿ ਜਾਗਿ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: So what if someone through spiritual powers become a whirlwind of air and wanders in the atmosphere when all desires are kindred in his mind and he does not know how to get rid of them?

column2: ਕੂਪ ਜਲੁ ਗਰੋ ਬਾਧੇ ਨਿਕਸੈ ਨ ਹੁਇ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ; ਚੀਲ ਹੁਇ ਉਡੈ ਨ ਖਗਪਤਿ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as water drawn out of a well with a pitcher tied to a rope does not become an ocean and a vulture, who roams about in the sky looking for corpses, cannot be accepted as god of birds, similarly, an evil-filled man cannot claim to be a spiritually awak

column2: ਮੂਸਾ ਬਿਲ ਖੋਦਿ ਨ ਜੋਗੀਸੁਰ ਗੁਫਾ ਕਹਾਵੈ; ਸਰਪ ਹੁਇ ਚਿਰੰਜੀਵ ਬਿਖੁ ਨ ਬਿਲਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: A rat living in a burrow cannot be called a saint in a cave. Similarly, a person who has done no good to anyone is like a rat even if he puts himself through rigorous penance to realise his beloved God. If one acquires long life like a snake, one cannot d

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ ਹੁਇ ਅਤੀਤ; ਹਉਮੈ ਖੋਇ ਹੋਇ ਰੇਨੁ ਕਾਮਧੇਨ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥੨੨੪॥
column5: But an obedient Sikh of the Guru keeps himself clear from the effect of tri-traits of maya and is a recluse by heart. He loses his ego and becomes an epitome of humility by serving all and accomplishing others' tasks admirably. (224)

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ; ਆਤਮ ਅਵੇਸ ਪ੍ਰਮਾਤਮ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the union of a disciple coming in the refuge of True Guru and when his mind is engrossed in the divine word, he becomes an adept in uniting his self with the Supreme soul.

column2: ਤਤੈ ਮਿਲਿ ਤਤ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤ ਬੂੰਦ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਹੋਇ; ਪਾਰਸ ਕੈ ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਪਰ ਕੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the mythical rain-drop (Swati) turns into a pearl when it falls on an Oyster shell and becomes highly valuable, so would a person become when his heart is filled with the elixir-like Naam of the Lord. Uniting with Supreme, he too becomes like Him. Like
