column5: A frog and lotus flower, a bamboo and sandalwood tree, a crane and a swan, an ordinary stone and a philosopher-stone, nectar and poison may come together, yet do not adopt each other's characteristics.

column2: ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਮਦ. ਅਹਿ ਮਨਿ. ਮਧੁ ਮਾਖੀ ਸਾਖੀ; ਬਾਂਝ ਬਧੂ ਨਾਹ ਨੇਹ ਨਿਹਫਲ ਭੋਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: Deer has musk in its naval, a cobra has a pearl in its hood, a bee lives with honey, a sterile woman gets to meet with her husband with love but all in vain.

column2: ਦਿਨਕਰ ਜੋਤਿ ਉਲੂ. ਬਰਖੈ ਸਮੈ ਜਵਾਸੋ; ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਜੈਸੇ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾਵੰਤ ਰੋਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as Sun's light for an owl, rain for a wild herb (javran-alhogi maunosum) and clothes and food for a patient are like disease.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਬੀਜ ਜਮਤ ਨ ਕਾਲਰ ਮੈ; ਅੰਕੁਰ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੋਤ ਨਾਹਿਨ. ਬਿਓਗ ਹੈ॥੨੯੯॥
column5: Similarly sullied and vice-ridden hearts cannot be fertile to the seeds of Guru's sermons and teachings. It just does not sprout. Such a person remains separated from his God. (299)

column2: ਸੰਗਮ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਕਉ ਪਤੰਗੁ ਜਾਨੈ; ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਸੋਗ ਮੀਨ ਭਲ ਜਾਨਈ॥
column5: The loving atmosphere that gets generated when a lover is about to meet his beloved can best be known by a moth. The pang of separation is best described by a fish that has been separated from its beloved water.

column2: ਇਕ ਟਕ ਦੀਪਕ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਪਰਹਰੈ; ਸਲਿਲ ਬਿਓਗ ਮੀਨ ਜੀਵਨ ਨ ਮਾਨਈ॥
column5: A moth burns itself for the love of the flame that he keeps watching and playing with. Similarly a fish separated from water has no meaning of life. She dies when out of it.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਿਛੁਰੈ ਮਧੁਪਮਨੁ; ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਜਨਮ ਅਗਿਆਨਈ॥
column5: These living beings i.e. moth and fish lay down their lives in love of their beloveds. On the other hand an evil person's mind is like a black bee that hops from one flower to the other. It separates from the holy feet of True Guru, even after meeting Him

column2: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਜੀਵਨ ਮਰਨ ਗੁਰ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਹੁਇ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਅਰੁ ਬਿਰਹ ਨ ਦੋਊ ਉਰ ਆਨਈ॥੩੦੦॥
column5: A follower of his own heart turned away from the refuge of the Guru, who does not feel the pangs of separation and love of the holy feet of the. True Guru, has wasted away his birth and death thus living a worthless life. (300)

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਲਿਵ ਦੇਖੈ ਅਉ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਸੋਈ; ਸਰਬ ਦਰਸ ਏਕ ਦਰਸ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: An obedient disciple of the Guru who has focused his vision in the glimpse of the True Guru, he observes the impermeable Lord everywhere and every place. He makes others see Him too. He regards and understands that all philosophies are present in His sigh

column2: ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਕਹਤ ਸੁਨਤ ਸੋਈ; ਸਰਬ ਸਬਦ ਏਕ ਸਬਦ ਕੈ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: When a Guru-oriented person acquires the teachings of the True Guru, his mind gets absorbed in the practicing of Lord's Naam Simran. He then speaks and hears the words of the True Guru deep in his soul. He regards all singing modes engrossed in the melody

column2: ਕਾਰਨ ਕਰਨ ਕਰਤਗਿ ਸਰਬਗਿ ਸੋਈ; ਕਰਮ ਕਰਤੂਤਿ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: In this state of immersion in the elixir of Naam, a Guru oriented slave recognises the cause of all causes, the knower of all deeds and capable of knowing all; who is the doer of all the deeds-the Doer and Creator,

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਏਕ ਹੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਮੇਕ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਏਕੈ ਉਰਿ ਆਨੀਐ॥੩੦੧॥
column5: And thus a Guru-conscious person becomes aware of One God through the knowledge blessed by the True Guru and perpetual contemplation of Him, Such a person leans on none else for support except One All-pervading Lord, (301)

column2: ਕਿੰਚਤ ਕਟਾਛ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹੇ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਖੰਡ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਰੰਗ ਮੈ ਬਿਮੋਹਿਤ ਮਗਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord whose one transverse glance can infatuate millions of people of the regions and realms in maya, that God, enamoured by the love of the assembly of true God-loving meditating people remains absorbed in them.

column2: ਜਾਂ ਕੈ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਕਾਰ ਹੈ ਨਾਨਾ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ; ਕੀਰਤਨ ਸਮੈ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਸੌ ਲਗਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord whose expanse and forms are indescribable, He remains involved in the pious people through the singing of His hymns of praise.

column2: ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿ ਆਗਿਆਕਾਰੀ ਜਾ ਕੇ; ਅਗ੍ਰਭਾਗ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਗੁਨਨੁ ਅਗਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord who has the services of all the three deities and the four sons of Brahma at His beck and call and obedience, that Lord of countless traits remains obedient in the company of the holy and saintly persons who are ever engrossed in Him.

column2: ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰ. ਸਾਧ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਅਪਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਅਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਅਭਗਨ ਹੈ॥੩੦੨॥
column5: Praise of congregation immersed in His loving remembrance is beyond comprehension. A Guru-conscious devotee remains in love with Him like a fish in water. (302)

column2: ਨਿਜ ਘਰ ਮੇਰੋ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ. ਨਾਰਦਮੁਨਿ; ਦਰਸਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮੇਰੋ ਨਿਜ ਰੂਪ ਹੈ॥
column5: Lord (Bhagwan) tells sage Narad, 0 dear devotee! Congregation of Guru-conscious and true people is my abode if company is my glimpse.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮੇਰੋ ਮਾਤਾ ਪਿਤਾ ਅਉ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਖਾ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮੇਰੋ ਸੁਤੁ ਸ੍ਰੇਸਟ ਅਨੂਪ ਹੈ॥
column5: Company of godlike people of the True Guru is like my friends and whole family. The company of true is my beautiful and supreme son.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੈ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਸੇਵਾ ਦੀਪ ਧੂਪ ਹੈ॥
column5: Congregation is a treasure-house of all comforts and happiness. It is my life-support. Congregation of true people is means of gaining higher spiritual state. It is also a place of performing service which is true worship.

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਰੰਗ ਰਸ ਭੋਗ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜ ਮੈ; ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਸੋਭਾ ਅਤਿ ਉਪਮਾ ਅਉ ਊਪ ਹੈ॥੩੦੩॥
column5: Company of Guru beloveds is a place of relishing the elixir of Naam Simran and enjoying spiritual peace. The glory and grandeur of the holy congregation is beyond praise unique and wondrous. (303)

column2: ਅਗਮ ਅਪਾਰ ਦੇਵ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ ਅਤਿ; ਅਨਿਕ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: The Lord who is highly inaccessible, infinite, light effulgent and beyond comprehension, cannot be reached by controlling the senses with all available means.

column2: ਪਾਈਐ ਨ ਜਗ ਭੋਗ. ਪਾਈਐ ਨ ਰਾਜ ਜੋਗ; ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਬੇਦ ਕੈ ਅਗਹੁ ਨ ਗਹਾਈਐ॥
column5: He cannot even be realised through holding/performing of Yag, hom (offerings to fire god), holding of feast for holy men, nor through Raj Yog. He cannot be reached through playing of musical instruments nor recitation of Vedas.

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਪੁਰਬ ਦੇਵ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੈ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ; ਕਰਮ ਧਰਮ ਬ੍ਰਤਿ ਨੇਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: Such God of gods can also not be reached by visiting places of pilgrimage, celebrating days considered auspicious or by the service of gods. Even fasts of myriad kind cannot even bring Him closer. Contemplations are futile as well.

column2: ਨਿਹਫਲ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਚਾਰ ਸਬੈ; ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਹੁਇ ਸਬਦ ਸੁ ਗਾਈਐ॥੩੦੪॥
column5: All the methods of God-realisation are of no use. He can only be realised by singing His paeans in the company of holy men and meditating on Him with concentrated and singular mind. (304)

column2: ਸੁਫਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਜੋਈ ਦੇਖੈ ਸੋਈ ਜਾਨੈ; ਦੂਸਰੋ ਨਾ ਦੇਖੈ ਪਾਵੈ. ਕਹੁ ਕੈਸੇ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: The miracle of a dream is known to the one who has seen it. No one else can see it. Then how can anyone else know about it?

column2: ਨਾਲ ਬਿਖੈ ਬਾਤ ਕੀਏ ਸੁਨੀਅਤ ਕਾਨ ਦੀਏ; ਬਕਤਾ ਅਉ ਸ੍ਰੋਤਾ ਬਿਨੁ ਕਾ ਪੈ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: If something is spoken in one end of a tube and the other end is put in one's own ears, then only he alone would know who has said or heard what. No one else can know.

column2: ਪਘੂਲਾ ਕੇ ਮੂਲ ਬਿਖੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਪਾਨ ਕੀਜੈ; ਲੀਜੀਐ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ ਪੀਏ ਮਨ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a lotus flower or any other plant draws water through its roots from the soil, the flower or plant alone knows about the state of its bloom, who drinks according to his desire.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਧਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਗੁਹਜ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਧਾਨੀਐ॥੩੦੫॥
column5: The event of a Sikh meeting with his Guru and obtaining initiation from him is very wondrous, blissful and mysterious. The description of the knowledge obtained from the True Guru, contemplation on Him, His love and ecstasy is very strange to describe. No

column2: ਨਵਨ ਗਵਨ ਜਲ ਸੀਤਲ ਅਮਲ ਜੈਸੇ; ਅਗਨਿ ਉਰਧ ਮੁਖ ਤਪਤ ਮਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as water flowing downward remains cool and clear of contamination but fire that goes upward causes heat and pollution;

column2: ਸਫਲ ਹੋਇ ਆਂਬ ਝੁਕੇ. ਰਹਿਤ ਹੈ ਚਿਰੰਕਾਲ; ਨਿਵੈ ਨ ਅਰਿੰਡ. ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਆਰਬਲਾ ਛੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a mango tree bends down when it yields fruit, and lives a long life, but a castor oil seed plant does not bend. It would break if we bend it, it breaks. Thus it has a short span of life.

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਸੀਐ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਬਾਂਸ ਤਉ ਬਡਾਈ ਬੂਡਿਓ ਸੰਗ ਲਿਵਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the sweet smell of a small sized sandalwood tree gets infused in the vegetation around it, but a tall and high bamboo plant arrogant of its size absorbs no fragrance of the sandalwood tree.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਅਹੰਬੁਧਿ ਨਿਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਕੈ; ਸਨ ਅਉ ਮਜੀਠ ਗਤਿ ਪਾਪ ਪੁੰਨ ਕੀਨ ਹੈ॥੩੦੬॥
column5: Similarly evil and apostate people bound by their pride and ego commit sins. On the contrary those good people who live in the way of the Guru and are humble, do good work like Rubia munjista (Majith). (The fibre for making a rope grows high up and is use

column2: ਸਕਲ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ ਬਿਖੈ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਦੀਰਘ ਦੁਇ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਭਏ ਬੂਡੇ ਬਹੁਤ ਬਡਾਈ ਕੈ॥
column5: Amongst all the vegetation, both Silk cotton (Simhal) and Bamboo are the tallest but feeling proud of their size and greatness, they remain failures.

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਬਾਸਨਾ ਕੈ ਸੇਂਬਲ ਸੁਬਾਸੁ ਹੋਤ; ਬਾਂਸ ਨਿਰਗੰਧ ਬਹੁ ਗਾਂਠਨ ਢਿਠਾਈ ਕੈ॥
column5: At least a Silk cotton tree do acquire some fragrance from a Sandalwood tree but due to the obstinacy of the knots, a Bamboo tree remains bereft of Sandalwood smell.

column2: ਸੇਂਬਲ ਕੇ ਫਲ ਤੂਲ ਖਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਛਾਇਆ ਤਾ ਕੈ; ਬਾਂਸ ਤਉ ਬਰਨ ਦੋਖੀ ਜਾਰਤ ਬੁਰਾਈ ਕੈ॥
column5: The cotton of the Silk cotton tree is put to use. The vast expanse of the tree provides shade for birds and other animals, but a Bamboo is family destroyer and due to its evil nature, it burns the other Bamboos that it rubs with.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਹੋਤ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਕੈ; ਤ੍ਰਿਸਟੈ ਨ ਗੁਰ ਗੋਪਿ ਦ੍ਰੋਹ ਗੁਰਭਾਈ ਕੈ॥੩੦੭॥
column5: Similarly an apostate Sikh becomes an obedient of the Guru by obtaining his sermons and enjoying the company of godly persons. But one who turns his face despite belonging to Guru, who is guilty of doing wrong to his Guru-brothers pushed around from door

column2: ਬਿਰਖ ਬਲੀ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਸਫਲ ਸਘਨ ਛਾਇਆ; ਬਾਂਸ ਤਉ ਬਰਨ ਦੋਖੀ ਮਿਲੈ ਜਰੈ ਜਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Many fruit-bearing trees along with creepers climbing upon them become dense of shade. They provide comfort to all wayfarers. But Bamboo that rubs with each other becomes cause of its own destruction through fire and for others as well who are near it.

column2: ਸਫਲ ਹੁਇ ਤਰਹਰਿ ਝੁਕਤਿ ਸਕਲ ਤਰੁ; ਬਾਂਸ ਤਉ ਬਡਾਈ ਬੂਡਿਓ ਆਪਨੁ ਸੰਮਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: All other fruit-bearing trees bow down but a Bamboo tree sublimed in his own praise keep accumulating pride.

column2: ਸਕਲ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ ਸੁਧਿ ਰਿਦੈ ਮੋਨਿ ਗਹੇ; ਬਾਂਸ ਤਉ ਰੀਤੋ ਗਠੀਲੋ ਬਾਜੇ ਧਾਰਿ ਮਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: All fruit trees remain at peace at heart and are silent of disposition. They produce no sounds. But the tall Bamboo is hollow from inside and is knotted. It wails and produces noise.

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸਮੀਪਿ ਹੀ ਅਛਤ ਨਿਰਗੰਧ ਰਹੇ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ, ਦੋਖੀ ਬਜਰ ਪ੍ਰਾਨੀ ਨ ਉਧਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥੩੦੮॥
column5: He who remains proud and hypocrite despite living in the close proximity of Sandalwood like True Guru, (remains fragrance-less) and does not acquire Guru's wisdom, such a person whoever wishes ill of Guru's disciples can never sail across the worldly ocea

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਐਸੋ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੈ ਪਰਸਪਰ ਪਗ ਲਪਟਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: The glory of the meeting of obedient and truthful persons of the True Guru is such that they bend down to touch each others feet regardless of their high or low status or age.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਅਰੁ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Having seen the True Guru and by the divine effect of words residing in their mind, such Sikhs of the Guru remain engrossed in perfect Lord by virtue of Guru's knowledge and contemplation. The effect is always visible on them.

column2: ਏਕ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਲਾਵਤ ਮਹਾਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ; ਏਕ ਗੁਰਪੁਰਬ ਕੈ ਸਿਖਨ ਬੁਲਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Many of these devotees of the Guru bring dainty dishes for the consumption of the saintly persons of the congregation. Others send invitations to the Sikhs of Guru and hold Religious functions on the days associated with their Gurus.

column2: ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿ ਬਾਛੈ ਤਿਨ ਕੇ ਉਚਿਸਟ ਕਉ; ਸਾਧਨ ਕੀ ਦੂਖਨਾ ਕਵਨ ਫਲ ਪਾਵਹੀ॥੩੦੯॥
column5: Even gods like Shiv, Sanak crave for the leftovers of such Sikhs of the Guru who are blessed with divine traits of Naam Simran. What good will one reap who thinks ill of such godly ones? It is evident that such a person will be severely 'shed in the court

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬੋਝ ਭਰੀ ਨਾਵ. ਆਂਗੁਰੀ ਦੁਇ ਬਾਹਰਿ ਹੁਇ; ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰੈ ਪੂਰ. ਸਬੈ ਕੁਸਲ ਬਿਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A fully loaded boat remains above water-level by no more than two fingers. Everyone rejoices when all the travellers are able to disembark on the other bank/shore;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕਾਹਾਰੀ ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਪਾਕਸਾਲਾ ਬੈਠਿ; ਭੋਜਨ ਕੈ ਬਿੰਜਨਾਦਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਦਿ ਕੈ ਅਘਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a person who eats food once in 24 hours (though hungry) feels his hunger satiated when he spends sometime in the kitchen where the food is being prepared;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜ ਦੁਆਰ ਜਾਇ ਕਰਤ ਜੁਹਾਰ ਜਨ; ਏਕ ਘਰੀ ਪਾਛੈ ਦੇਸ ਭੋਗਤਾ ਹੁਇ ਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a servant shows much respect at the door of the king or his master, and later on, he reaps the fruit of his service when he himself becomes landlord.

column2: ਆਠ ਹੀ ਪਹਰ ਸਾਠ ਘਰੀ ਮੈ ਜਉ ਏਕ ਘਰੀ; ਸਾਧ ਸਮਾਗਮੁ ਕਰੈ ਨਿਜ ਘਰ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੧੦॥
column5: Similarly, if a person keeps company of holy men who are perpetually meditating on Lord's name for a watch out of 24 hrs (24 hrs=60 watches), he is able to rest in his own self and would realise the God gradually. (310)

column2: ਕਾਰਤਿਕ ਜੈਸੇ ਦੀਪਮਾਲਿਕਾ ਰਜਨੀ ਸਮੈ; ਦੀਪ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੋ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੋਤ ਹੀ ਬਿਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as on festival of Diwali, that falls in the Indian month of Kartik, many earthen lamps are lit at night, and their light go off after a short period;

column2: ਬਰਖਾ ਸਮੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬੁਦਬੁਦਾ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ; ਤਾਸ ਨਾਮ ਪਲਕ ਮੈ ਨ ਠਹਿਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as bubbles appear on water when rain-drops on it, and very soon these bubbles explode and disappear from the surface;

column2: ਗ੍ਰੀਖਮ ਸਮੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ; ਝਾਈ ਸੀ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦੇਤ ਉਪਜਿ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a thirsty deer is disillusioned of the presence of water, the hot shimmering sand (mirage) that disappears in time then he reaches that spot;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮੋਹ ਮਾਇਆ ਛਾਇਆ ਬਿਰਖ ਚਪਲ ਛਲ; ਛਲੈ ਛੈਲ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਲਪਟਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੧੧॥
column5: So is the love of Maya that keeps changing its master like the shadow of a tree. But the Naam practitioner devotee of the Guru who remains engrossed in the holy feet of the True, he is able to control the attractive and trickster maya with ease. (311)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਸਨ ਅੰਗ ਸੰਗ ਮਿਲਿ ਹੋਇ ਮਲੀਨ; ਸਲਿਲ ਸਾਬੁਨ ਮਿਲਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as clothes become soiled by their touching the body but are washed clean with water and soap

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਰੋਵਰ ਸਿਵਾਲ ਕੈ ਅਛਾਦਿਓ ਜਲੁ; ਝੋਲਿ ਪੀਏ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਦੇਖੀਐ ਅਛੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the water in a pond is covered with thin film of algae and dropped leaves, but by brushing aside the film with hand, clean drinkable water appears.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਸ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਤਾਰਕਾ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ; ਹੋਤ ਉਜੀਆਰੋ ਦਿਨਕਰ ਕੇ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the night is dark even with the twinkling of stars but with the rising Sun-light spreads all over.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਮਲੀਨ ਮਤਿ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ॥੩੧੨॥
column5: So does the love of maya sully the mind. But by the teachings of True Guru and His contemplation, it becomes radiant. (312)

column2: ਅੰਤਰ ਅਛਿਤ ਹੀ ਦਿਸੰਤਰਿ ਗਵਨ ਕਰੈ; ਪਾਛੈ ਪਰੇ ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਕ ਜਉ ਧਾਵਈ॥
column5: Despite well hidden in the body, mind still reaches far off places. If someone tries to chase it, he just cannot reach it.

column2: ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਰਥ ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਗਜਰਾਜ ਬਾਜੁ; ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਖਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਫਾਂਧਤ ਉਡਾਵਈ॥
column5: No chariot, a swift horse or even Airawat (a legendary elephant) can reach it. Neither a fast flying bird nor a galloping deer can match it.

column2: ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਪਵਨ. ਗਵਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਅਰਧ ਉਰਧ ਅੰਤਰੀਛ ਹੁਇ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Even the wind which has its reach in the three worlds cannot reach it. One who is capable of reaching the land of the world beyond, cannot win the race of mind.

column2: ਪੰਚ ਦੂਤ ਭੂਤ ਲਗਿ ਅਧਮੁ ਅਸਾਧੁ ਮਨੁ; ਗਹੇ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਬਸਿ ਆਵਈ॥੩੧੩॥
column5: Inveigled by the five vices of maya that has embraced it like a demon, the low and incorrigible mind can only be controlled and disciplined if it accepts the initiation of the True Guru through the kind blessings of saintly and true devotees of the Lord.

column2: ਆਂਧਰੇ ਕਉ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕਰ ਚਰ ਟੇਕ; ਬਹਰੈ ਚਰਨ ਕਰ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਬਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: A blind person has support of the words, ability to hear, hands and feet. A deaf has much reliance on his hands feet, vision of the eyes and the words that he speaks.

column2: ਗੂੰਗੇ ਟੇਕ ਚਰ ਕਰ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ; ਲੂਲੇ ਟੇਕ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਬਦ ਸ੍ਰੁਤਿ ਪਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: A dumb has support of ears for listening, feet, hands vision of the eyes. A handless person relies much on eyes speech, hearing and feet.

column2: ਪਾਂਗੁਰੇ ਕਉ ਟੇਕ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕਰ; ਟੇਕ ਏਕ ਏਕ ਅੰਗ ਹੀਨ ਦੀਨਤਾ ਅਛਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: One who is lame or without legs rely on sight of his eyes speech, ability to hear, and use of his hands. Despite capacitated of one limb or faculty, the dependence on others remain hidden.

column2: ਅੰਧ ਗੁੰਗ ਸੁੰਨ ਪੰਗ ਲੁੰਜ ਦੁਖ ਪੁੰਜ ਮਮ; ਅੰਤਰ ਕੇ ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ਪਰਬੀਨ ਸਦ ਹੈ॥੩੧੪॥
column5: But I am blind, dumb, deaf, maimed of hand and feet a mass of suffering. 0 my True Lord! You are the wisest and completely informed of all my innate pains. 0 My Lord, please be merciful and remove all my pains. (314)

column2: ਆਂਧਰੇ ਕਉ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕਰ ਚਰ ਟੇਕ; ਅੰਧ ਗੁੰਗ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕਰ ਚਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: A blind person has the-support of power of speech, hands and feet. And if someone is blind and dumb as well, then he is dependent on others for listening power, hands and feet.

column2: ਅੰਧ ਗੁੰਗ ਸੁੰਨ ਕਰ ਚਰ ਅਵਿਲੰਬ ਟੇਕ; ਅੰਧ ਗੁੰਗ ਸੁੰਨ ਪੰਗ ਟੇਕ ਏਕ ਕਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: If someone is blind, deaf and dumb, he has support of hands and feet. But if one is blind, deaf, dumb and lame, he has support only of hands.

column2: ਅੰਧ ਗੁੰਗ ਸੁੰਨ ਪੰਗੁ ਲੁੰਜ ਦੁਖ ਪੁੰਜ ਮਮ; ਸਰਬੰਗ ਹੀਨ ਦੀਨ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਅਧਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: But I am a bundle of pains and sufferings, because I am blind, deaf, dumb, crippled and have no support. I am deeply distressed.

column2: ਅੰਤਰ ਕੀ ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ਜਾਨੈ ਅੰਤਰਗਤਿ; ਕੈਸੇ ਨਿਰਬਾਹੁ ਕਰੈ ਸਰੈ ਨਰਹਰ ਹੈ॥੩੧੫॥
column5: Oh Omnipotent Lord! You are Omniscient. How can I tell you my pain, how will I live and how will I cross this worldly ocean of life. (315)

column2: ਚਕਈ ਚਕੋਰ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ. ਮੀਨ ਭ੍ਰਿੰਗ ਅਉ ਪਤੰਗ; ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਇਕ ਅੰਗੀ ਬਹੁ ਰੰਗੀ ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: The love of a Ruddy Sheldrake with Sun, Allictoris gracea with Moon, deer with melody of Ghandey Herhey, fish with water, a black bee with lotus flower and of a moth with light is one-sided. Such one-sided love is often painful in many ways.

column2: ਏਕ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਸੈ ਟਰਤ ਨ. ਮਰਤ ਸਬੈ; ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤਿ ਕੀ ਸੁ ਚਾਲ ਚਲੀ ਜਗ ਆਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: All these lovers do not abstain from their faith of one-sided love and in the process give away their lives. This tradition of worldly love is going on since the beginning of ages.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਐਸੋ; ਲੋਗ ਪਰਲੋਗ ਸੁਖਦਾਇਕ ਸਹਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: But the importance of two-sided love of a Sikh of the Guru and his True Guru is such that can prove helpful and peaceful in this world and the world beyond.

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸੁਨਿ. ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਨ ਮਿਟਤ ਜਾ ਕੀ; ਅਹਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਚੰਦਨ ਜਿਉ ਬਿਖੁ ਨ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥੩੧੬॥
column5: With such comforting love of the Guru available close at hand, if one does not listen to the teachings of the Guru and who does not dispel one's base wisdom, then such a person is no better than a snake who does not shed his venom even after embracing San

column2: ਮੀਨ ਕਉ ਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ. ਜਲ ਕਉ ਸਬਦ ਗਿਆਨ; ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਿਟਾਇ ਨ ਸਕਤ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: A fish is not a ware that the water will assist nor does water have knowledge of speech or hearing in order to help the fish in distress. Therefore the water cannot allay its pain while it is in distress.

column2: ਸਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਅਥਾਹ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਬਸੈ; ਗ੍ਰਸੈ ਲੋਹ. ਰਾਖਿ ਨ ਸਕਤ ਮਤਿ ਹੀਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: Fish resides in the vast and rapid flow of the river. But when it swallows the iron bait of an angler, the bemused fish cannot be saved by water-her beloved.

column2: ਜਲ ਬਿਨੁ ਤਰਫਿ ਤਜਤ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਮੀਨ; ਜਾਨਤ ਨ ਪੀਰ ਨੀਰ ਦੀਨਤਾਈ ਦੀਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: Removed from water, a fish writhes in pain for life having been separated from its beloved (life support). But the water is not aware of the sufferings of the fish.

column2: ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤਿ ਮੀਨ ਕੁਲ ਦ੍ਰਿੜ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਬੰਸ ਧ੍ਰਿਗ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਪਰਧੀਨ ਕੀ॥੩੧੭॥
column5: The entire clan of fish is bearing with this one-sided love for eons. But the love of a Guru and his disciple is always two-sided. Guru helps the Sikh in distress. But one who being in the clan, leaves the love of True Guru, submits himself and serves spu

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਪੈ ਆਵਤ ਪਤੰਗ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਰੀਤਿ ਲਗਿ; ਦੀਪਕਹਿ ਮਹਾਂ ਬਿਪਰੀਤਿ ਮਿਲੇ ਜਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A moth approaches a light out of love but the attitude of a lamp is to the contrary. It singes him to death.

column2: ਅਲਿ ਚਲਿ ਆਵਤ ਕਮਲ ਪਹਿ ਸਨੇਹ ਕਰਿ; ਕਮਲ ਸੰਪੁਟਿ ਬਾਂਧਿ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਪਰਹਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Fulfilling his desire for love, a black bee approaches a lotus flower. But as the Sun sets, the lotus flower closes its petals and snuffs out life from the black bee.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਜਲ ਮੀਨ ਲਿਵਲੀਨ ਗਤਿ; ਬਿਛੁਰਤਿ ਰਾਖਿ ਨ ਸਕਤ ਗਹਿ ਡਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: It is the character of fish to stay in water but when a fisherman or angler catches it with the help of a net or a hook, and throws it out of the water, the water does not help it in anyway.

column2: ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤਿ ਕੈ ਮਰੈ. ਨ ਟਰੈ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕਿਉ ਬਿਸਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥੩੧੮॥
column5: Despite being one-sided, the painful love of moth, black bee and fish is full of faith and trust. Every lover dies for its beloved but doesn't give up loving. Contrary to this one-sided love, the love of Guru and his Sikh is two-sided. True Guru loves His

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਦਿਬਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਹੀਨ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ॥
column5: What light of vision could he have found in an oil beacon, the moth becomes devoid of even seeing it since it dies on its flame. But the contemplation of True Guru's sight illuminates the vision of the slave of Guru that he is able to see all the happenin

column2: ਬਾਸਨਾ ਕਮਲ ਅਲਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਨ ਰਾਖਿ ਸਕੈ; ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ. ਅਨਤ ਨ ਰੰਮਿਤਾ॥
column5: A black bee is fascinated by the smell of a lotus flower. However a lotus flower cannot stop him from visiting other flowers. But a devoted Sikh coming into the refuge of True Guru does not go anywhere else.

column2: ਮੀਨ ਜਲ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਅੰਤ ਨ ਸਹਾਈ ਹੋਤ; ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਹੈ ਇਤ ਉਤ ਸੰਮਿਤਾ॥
column5: A fish sees through her love for water till the end. But when hooked to a bait, the water does not help her and can't save her. However a Sikh who is ever swimming in the safe ocean of True Guru is always helped by Him here and in the world beyond.

column2: ਏਕ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਸੈ ਟਰਤ ਨ. ਮਰਤ ਸਬੈ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਬੰਗੀ ਸੰਗੀ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਾ॥੩੧੯॥
column5: The love of moth, black bee and fish is one-sided. They never give up this one-sided infatuation and die living in the love of their beloved. But the love of True Guru emancipates one from the cycle of birth and death. Why should anyone turn his face away

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਮਿਲਿ ਜਰਤ ਨ ਰਾਖਿ ਸਕੈ; ਜਰੇ ਮਰੇ ਆਗੇ ਨ ਪਰਮਪਦ ਪਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Coming near the flame of oil lamp, the lamp cannot save the moth from burning. This type of death cannot provide salvation in the world beyond.

column2: ਮਧੁਪ ਕਮਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਨ ਰਾਖਿ ਸਕੈ; ਸੰਪੁਟ ਮੈ ਮੂਏ ਸੈ ਨ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: A lotus flower cannot stop a black bee from visiting other flowers. So, if a black bee gets shut up in the box of lotus petals when the Sun sets, it cannot merge with Lord Almighty.

column2: ਜਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਮੀਨ ਕੀ ਨ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਿਟਾਇ ਸਕੀ; ਬਿਛੁਰਿ ਮਰਤ ਹਰਿਲੋਕ ਨ ਪਠਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Separating from water, the pain suffered by a fish cannot be removed by water. Thus, a death of this type cannot land the fish in heaven.

column2: ਇਤ ਉਤ ਸੰਗਮ ਸਹਾਈ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ਗੁਰ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪੀਆਏ ਹੈ॥੩੨੦॥
column5: Meeting the True Guru provides support and assistance in this world and the world beyond. Such a love is the outcome of contemplation and meditation on the teachings and consecration of the True Guru. It fills the Sikh with elixir-like love of the True Gu

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਅਲਿ ਕਮਲ. ਸਲਿਲ ਮੀਨ; ਚਕਈ ਚਕੋਰ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ. ਰਵਿ ਸਸਿ ਨਾਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: A moth, black bee, fish, ruddy Sheldrake, (Allectoris gracea) and a deer loves flame of a lamp, lotus flower, water, Sun, moon and sound of music produced by Ghanda Herha respectively.

column2: ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਇਕ ਅੰਗੀ ਬਹੁ ਰੰਗੀ ਨਹੀ ਸੰਗੀ ਕੋਊ; ਸਬੈ ਦੁਖਦਾਈ. ਨ ਸਹਾਈ ਅੰਤਿ ਆਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: All their love being one-sided is very painful that does not help neither in the beginning nor in the end.

column2: ਜੀਵਤ ਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ. ਮੂਏ ਨ ਪਰਮਗਤਿ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ. ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: These living beings of subhuman life can neither be blessed with holy congregation of true devotees nor salvation after death. They cannot even be the recipient of Guru's teachings, His contemplation and the divine nectar that the grace of True Guru can d

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮੁ ਪਾਇ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਇਆਨਿਧਾਨ; ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਸੁਖਫਲ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਹੈ॥੩੨੧॥
column5: Coming to the refuge of the True Guru, the store-house of mercy and that too in the life of a human being and practicing on the True Guru-given Naam Simran can bless one with that unique fruit of comfort and peace. (321)

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥ ਗੁਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਰਹੈ; ਲਹੈ ਨਿਜੁ ਘਰੁ ਅਰੁ ਸਹਜ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: Treading the path of Sikhism, he who remains alert in the form of the True Guru, recognises his self and lives in a state of equipoise thereafter.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਮਤਿ; ਮਧੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: By the single support of the teachings of True Guru, his mind becomes stable. As a result of his comforting utterances, his practice of Naam Simran blossoms.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸਿ ਭਏ. ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: By the acquisition of the initiation of the True Guru and elixir-like Naam, nectar-like love resides in his mind. Unique and wondrous devotion grows in his heart.

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਨੇਮ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਤੀਤਿ ਚੀਤਿ; ਬਨ ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸ ਜੀ॥੩੨੨॥
column5: Fulfilling all the loving requirements with devotion and love, he who stays alert in the teachings and the presence of the True Guru, living in the jungle or in the house is alike for him. He remains unsullied from the effects of maya despite living in it

column2: ਮਾਰਬੇ ਕੋ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ ਦੇਖਿ ਚੋਰ ਨ ਤਜਤ ਚੋਰੀ; ਬਟਵਾਰਾ ਬਟਵਾਰੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਹੁਇ ਤਕਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Despite fear of death lurking around, a thief doesn't give up Stealing. A dacoit keeps targeting other wayfarers along with other members of his gang.

column2: ਬੇਸ੍ਵਾਰਤਿ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਭਏ ਮਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਸੰਕਾ ਮਾਨੈ; ਜੁਆਰੀ ਨ ਸਰਬਸੁ ਹਾਰੇ ਸੈ ਥਕਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Knowing that his visit to the house of a whore can cause him serious disease, a licentious person still doesn't feel hesitant to go there. A gambler never feels tired of gambling even after losing all his assets and the family.

column2: ਅਮਲੀ ਨ ਅਮਲ ਤਜਤ ਜਿਉ ਧਿਕਾਰ ਕੀਏ; ਦੋਖ ਦੁਖ ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਸੁਨਤ ਛਕਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: An addict keeps consuming drugs and intoxicants despite monitions, learning the effects of drug abuse from religious scriptures and people who have societal interests at heart, just can't give up his addiction.

column2: ਅਧਮ ਅਸਾਧ ਸੰਗ ਛਾਡਤ ਨ ਅੰਗੀਕਾਰ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਛਾਡਿ ਕਿਉ ਸਕਤ ਹੈ॥੩੨੩॥
column5: Even all these low and base people cannot give up their deeds, then how can an obedient devotee of Guru leave company of true and noble people? (323)

column2: ਦਮਕ ਦੈ ਦੋਖ ਦੁਖ. ਅਪਜਸ ਲੈ ਅਸਾਧ; ਲੋਕ ਪਰਲੋਕ ਮੁਖ ਸਿਆਮਤਾ ਲਗਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: A self-willed and base person acquires vices, sufferings and bad name after spending his wealth. He earns a stigma on himself both in this world and the world hereafter.

column2: ਚੋਰ ਜਾਰ ਅਉ ਜੂਆਰ ਮਦਪਾਨੀ ਦੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਸੈਂ; ਕਲਹ ਕਲੇਸ ਭੇਸ. ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਕਉ ਧਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: A thief, immoral person, gambler and an addict is always involved in some discord or dispute on account of his base and infamous deeds.

column2: ਮਤਿ ਪਤਿ ਹਾਨਿ. ਕਾਨਿ ਮੈ ਕਨੌਡੀ ਸਭਾ; ਨਾਕ ਕਾਨ ਖੰਡ ਡੰਡ ਹੋਤ ਨ ਲਜਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Such an evil-doer loses his intellect, respect, esteem and glory; and after bearing the punishment of cutting of nose or ear, he feels no shame in the society despite the stigma that he carries. Becoming more shameless, he keeps indulging in his nefarious

column2: ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਦਾਨਦਾਇਕ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਾਧ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧੂ ਜਨ ਕਿਉ ਨ ਚਲਿ ਆਵਹੀ॥੩੨੪॥
column5: When such ill-doers and infamous people do not abstain from doing bad deeds, then why should a Sikh of the Guru not come to the congregation of true and saintly persons that is capable of blessing one with all the treasures? (If they do not feel shy of do

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਅਕਸਮਾਤ ਬਾਦਰ ਉਦੋਤ ਹੋਤ; ਗਗਨ ਘਟਾ ਘਮੰਡ ਕਰਤ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as deep dark clouds appear in the sky all of a sudden and spread themselves in all the directions.

column2: ਤਾਹੀ ਤੇ ਸਬਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਘਨ ਗਰਜਤ ਅਤਿ; ਚੰਚਲ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਦਾਮਨੀ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Their thunder produces a very strong sound and sprightly lightning flashes.

column2: ਬਰਖਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਜਲ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਕਪੂਰ ਤਾ ਤੇ; ਅਉਖਧੀ ਉਪਾਰਜਨਾ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Then sweet, cold, nectar-like raindrops from where a swati drop falls on oyster to produce a pearl, camphor when it falls on a plantain beside producing many useful herbs.

column2: ਦਿਬ ਦੇਹ ਸਾਧ. ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਰਹਿਤ ਜਗਿ; ਪ੍ਰਗਟਤ ਕਰਬੇ ਕਉ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥੩੨੫॥
column5: Like good-doer cloud, the body of Guru-conscious disciple is divine. He is free from the cycle of birth and death. He comes to this world to do good. He helps others reach and realise Lord. (325)

column2: ਸਫਲ ਬਿਰਖ ਫਲ ਦੇਤ ਜਿਉ ਪਾਖਾਨ ਮਾਰੇ; ਸਿਰਿ ਕਰਵਤ ਸਹਿ ਗਹਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a tree laden with fruit drops fruit to the person who throws stone at it, then it bears the pain of a saw on its head and in the form of a raft or boat takes the iron saw across the river;

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਮੈ ਕਾਢਿ. ਮੁਖ ਫੋਰੀਅਤਿ ਸੀਪ ਕੋ ਜਿਉ; ਦੇਤ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ. ਅਵਗਿਆ ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as an oyster is taken out of the sea, is broken and it yields a pearl to the one who breaks it open and feels not the insult that it faces;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਖਨਵਾਰਾ ਖਾਨਿ ਖਨਤ ਹਨਤ ਘਨ; ਮਾਨਿਕ ਹੀਰਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a labourer strives the ore in a mine with his shovel and pick-axe and the mine rewards him with precious stones and diamonds;

column2: ਊਖ ਮੈ ਪਿਊਖ ਜਿਉ ਪਰਗਾਸ ਹੋਤ ਕੋਲੂ ਪਚੈ; ਅਵਗੁਨ ਕੀਏ ਗੁਨ. ਸਾਧਨ ਕੈ ਦੁਆਰ ਹੈ॥੩੨੬॥
column5: Just as sweet nectar-like juice is extracted out by putting it through a crusher, so are the evil-doers treated with sympathy and welfare by the true and saintly persons when they come to them. (326)

column2: ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਦਰਸਨ ਕੋ ਹੈ ਨਿਤਨੇਮ ਜਾ ਕੋ; ਸੋਈ ਦਰਸਨੀ ਸਮਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who· is regular in seeing and visiting the saintly persons, is the contemplator of the Lord in the true sense. He sees all alike and feels the presence of Lord in everyone.

column2: ਸਬਦ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਮਨਿ ਬਸੈ; ਮਾਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ. ਸੋਈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who holds the contemplation of Guru's words as his primary support and lodges it in his heart is the true follower of the Guru's teachings and knower of the Lord in true sense.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਅਰੁ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮੀ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He whose vision is focused on seeing the True Guru and hearing power concentrated on hearing the divine words of Guru, is a lover of his beloved Lord in the true sense.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਇਕ ਰੰਗ ਜੋਈ; ਸੋਈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਿਰਬਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥੩੨੭॥
column5: He who is dyed in the love of one Lord engrosses himself deep meditation of the Lord's name in the company of the saintly persons is truly liberated and a clean Guru-oriented individual. (327)

column2: ਦਰਸ ਧਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨੀ. ਸਬਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਗਿਆਨੀ; ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਦ੍ਰਿੜ. ਮਾਇਆ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who focuses his mind on the vision of the True Guru is a true contemplator. He who is aware of the Guru's teachings is wise in the real sense. Such a person is free of all bondages of maya when he stays in the refuge of True Guru.

column2: ਹਉਮੈ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਤਿਆਗੀ. ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਕੈ ਬੈਰਾਗੀ ਭਏ; ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ. ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: A true renouncer is one who has forsaken ego and pride; and attached himself with the name of the Lord. He is an ascetic when he feels engrossed in the ecstatic hues of the Lord. Having kept his mind dross free from the effect of maya, he is the true prac

column2: ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਅਪਰਸ ਅਉ ਸਾਧ ਇੰਦ੍ਰੀ ਨਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਕੈ; ਆਤਮ ਪੂਜਾ ਬਿਬੇਕੀ. ਸੁੰਨ ਮੈ ਸੰਨਿਆਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Having lost his feelings of mine and yours, he is free of all touches. Since he has control over his senses, he is a saintly person or a hermit. Due to worshipping of· the Lord, he is full of true wisdom. Since he remains engrossed in absolute Lord, he is

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਕਰਿ ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਭਏ; ਸੇਵਾ ਸਰਬਾਤਮ ਕੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥੩੨੮॥
column5: Since he is naturally involved in the worldly duties, he is liberated while still alive (Jeevan Mukt). Seeing the divine light pervading in all, and serving His creation, he holds total faith on Almighty God. (328)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਜੁਗੰਤਰਿ ਬਸੈ ਪਾਖਾਨ; ਭਿਦੈ ਨ ਰਿਦੈ ਕਠੋਰ. ਬੂਡੈ ਬਜਰ ਭਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a stone stays in water for ages, yet it never softens since it is hard-hearted. Because of its density and solid mass, it sinks;

column2: ਅਠਸਠਿ ਤੀਰਥ ਮਜਨ ਕਰੈ ਤੋਂਬਰੀ ਤਊ; ਮਿਟਤ ਨ ਕਰਵਾਈ ਧੋਏ ਵਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as colocynth (Tumma) does not lose its bitterness even it is washed both from inside and outside at sixty-eight places of pilgrimage

column2: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਅਹਿ ਲਪਟਾਨੋ ਰਹੈ ਚੰਦਨਹਿ; ਤਜਤ ਨ ਬਿਖੁ ਤਊ ਹਉਮੈ ਅਹੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a snake keeps entangled with the trunk of a Sandalwood tree all his life but because of the pride of long age, it does not shed its venom;

column2: ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਦੇਹ ਨਿਹਫਲ ਭਏ ਜਗਤ ਮੈ; ਸੰਤਨ ਕੋ ਹੈ ਦੋਖੀ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਕੈ॥੩੨੯॥
column5: Similarly, he who is debased and spurious at heart, has deceitful and suspicious love. His life in the world is useless and futile. He is a slanderer of saintly and Guru-oriented persons and is caught in the mesh of vices and sins account of his 'mine' an

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਦਰਪਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਕਛੁ; ਸਕਲ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਦੇਖਤ ਦਿਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a clean mirror has no image in it, but when one looks in it, it shows all the details in their true colours,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਜਲ ਬਰਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਰੀਤਿ; ਸਕਲ ਬਰਨ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਰਨ ਬਨਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as clean water is bereft of all shades of colours, but acquires the colour that it mixes with,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਸੁੰਧਰਾ ਸੁਆਦ ਬਾਸਨਾ ਰਹਿਤ; ਅਉਖਧੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਰਸ ਗੰਧ ਉਪਜਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as Earth is free of all tastes and desires but produces myriads of herbs of different effects, plants capable of giving many types of medicinal and aromatic extracts,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਅਲਖ ਅਭੇਵ ਗਤਿ; ਜੈਸੋ ਜੈਸੋ ਭਾਉ ਤੈਸੀ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪੁਜਾਵਈ॥੩੩੦॥
column5: Similarly with whatever sentiment one performs service of indescribable and inaccessible Lord-like True Guru, one's desires are filled accordingly. (330)

column2: ਸੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਹਾਨਿ ਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪੂਰਬ ਲਿਖਤ ਲੇਖ; ਜੰਤ੍ਰਨ ਕੈ ਨ ਬਸਿ ਕਛੁ. ਜੰਤ੍ਰੀ ਜਗਦੀਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: All events of happiness and sorrow, gain and loss, birth and death etc., take place according to what has been written by the Almighty or is preordained. Nothing is in the hands of living beings. It is all in the hands of the Almighty.

column2: ਭੋਗਤ ਬਿਵਸਿਮੇਵ ਕਰਮ ਕਿਰਤ ਗਤਿ; ਜਸਿ ਕਰ ਤਸਿ. ਲੇਪ ਕਾਰਨ ਕੋ ਈਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: All living beings bear the fruits of what they had done. Whatever deeds they perform, they are rewarded accordingly. He the Almighty Himself involves the human beings in the performance of various deeds/actions.

column2: ਕਰਤਾ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ. ਕਿਧੌ ਕਰਮ. ਕਿਧੌ ਹੈ ਜੀਉ; ਘਾਟਿ ਬਾਢਿ ਕਉਨ. ਕਉਨ ਮਤਿ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾ ਬੀਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: And thus surprised, a question rises in everyone's minds who is the primary cause, God, human being or the action itself? Which of these causes is more or less? What is definitely right? Nothing can be said with any degree of assurance.

column2: ਅਸਤੁਤਿ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕਹਾ ਬਿਆਪਤ ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ; ਹੋਨਹਾਰ ਕਹੌ. ਕਹਾ ਗਾਰਿ ਅਉ ਅਸੀਸ ਹੈ॥੩੩੧॥
column5: How does one go through praise and slander, pleasure or sorrow? What is a blessing and what is a curse? Nothing can be said conclusively. One can only reason that all is happening and being caused by the Lord Himself. (331)

column2: ਮਾਨਸਰ ਪਰ ਜਉ ਬੈਠਾਈਐ ਲੇ ਜਾਇ ਬਗ; ਮੁਕਤਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਤਜਿ ਮੀਨ ਬੀਨਿ ਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: If a heron is taken to Mansarover lake, he will only be picking up small fish instead of invaluable pearls.

column2: ਅਸਥਨ ਪਾਨ ਕਰਬੇ ਕਉ ਜਉ ਲਗਾਈਐ ਜੋਕ; ਪੀਅਤ ਨ ਪੈ. ਲੈ ਲੋਹੂ ਅਚਏ ਅਘਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: If a leech is put to the teats of a cow, it will not suckle milk but suck blood to satiate its hunger.

column2: ਪਰਮ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਪਰਿ ਮਾਖੀ ਨ ਰਹਿਤ ਰਾਖੀ; ਮਹਾਂ ਦੁਰਗੰਧ ਪਰਿ ਬੇਗ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A fly when placed on a fragrant article does not stay there but hurriedly reaches where filth and stench is present.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਗਜ ਮਜਨ ਕੈ ਡਾਰਤ ਹੈ ਛਾਰੁ ਸਿਰਿ; ਸੰਤਨ ਕੈ ਦੋਖੀ ਸੰਤਸੰਗੁ ਨ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੩੨॥
column5: Just as an elephant sprinkles dust on its head after bathing in clean water, so do the slanderers of saintly persons do not like the company of true and noble persons. (332)

column2: ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਤਿ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ. ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ; ਸਿਵ ਨ ਸਕਤਿ ਗਤਿ. ਅਨਭੈ ਅਭਿਆਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Obedient disciple of the True Guru considers the support of Guru teachings and wisdom as authentic and true. He has no one else in his heart other than One God. He does not recognise god-Shiv or goddess-Shakti as a means of emancipation. He remains a medi

column2: ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ. ਜੀਤ ਹਾਰ ਨ ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ; ਸੰਜੋਗ ਬਿਓਗ ਮੇਟਿ. ਸਹਜ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He remains unsullied of the influence of maya. Defeat or victory, happiness or sorrow does not disturb or please him. He remains absorbed in supreme spiritual state discarding all thoughts of achievements and failures.

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਬਰਨ ਇਕ ਬਰਨ ਹੁਇ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ; ਪੰਚ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: By joining true congregation he destroys the differences of high low caste and belongs to One God. Detaching from the love of five elements, he takes to Naam Simran of the wondrous God Lord and holds his faith in Him.

column2: ਖਟ ਦਰਸਨ ਪਰੈ ਪਾਰ ਹੁਇ ਸਪਤਸਰ; ਨਵ ਦੁਆਰ ਉਲੰਘਿ ਦਸਮਈ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਹੈ॥੩੩੩॥
column5: A Gursikh stays in the company of true seekers beyond the garbs of the six philosophical schools. He frees himself from the bonds of nine doors of the body and lives blissfully in of the tenth door (Dasam Duar). (333)

column2: ਨਦੀ ਨਾਵ ਕੋ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਸੁਜਨ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਲੋਗੁ; ਹੋਇਗੋ ਜੋ ਦੀਓ. ਸੋਈ ਮਿਲੈ ਆਗੈ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: The union with friends, family members and other acquaintances in this world is like voyagers in a boat that lasts for a short period of time. Therefore whatever is donated for good deeds in. this world will be received in the world beyond.

column2: ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਧਨ ਸੰਗਿ ਨ ਚਲਤ. ਚਲੇ; ਅਰਪੇ ਦੀਜੈ ਧਰਮਸਾਲਾ ਪਹੁਚਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Food, clothing and wealth does not go with one in the next world. Whatever has been assigned to the Guru in the true company is what one's wealth or earnings are for life beyond.

column2: ਆਠੋ ਜਾਮ ਸਾਠੋ ਘਰੀ ਨਿਹਫਲ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ; ਸਫਲ ਪਲਕ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਮਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Spending all the time in the love of maya and its actions are futile but enjoying the company of saintly persons even for a few seconds is a big achievement and useful.

column2: ਮਲ ਮੂਤ੍ਰਧਾਰੀ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ. ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਹੋਤ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਇ ਕੈ॥੩੩੪॥
column5: By uniting the words/teachings of the Guru with mind, and by the grace of holy company, this filth-filled and vice-ridden human being becomes an obedient disciple of the Guru. (334)

column2: ਹਉਮੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨ ਅਸਥਾਨ ਤਜਿ ਬੰਝ ਬਨ; ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: The black bee like mind of a Guru-oriented person who is attached with the congregation of saintly persons, forsakes pride and ego which is like a jungle of bamboos. He leaves attachment and infatuations. Enamoured by the lotus-like feet of the True Guru,

column2: ਅਤਿ ਹੀ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ. ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ; ਅਨਹਦ ਗੁੰਜਤ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਹੂ ਸਿਰਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Seeing the most beautiful form of the True Guru, his eyes are amazed. Listening to the pleasing and enchanting notes of the words of Guru, his ears feel calm and tranquil.

column2: ਰਸਨਾ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਤਿ ਮਧੁ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ; ਨਾਸਿਕਾ ਚਕਿਤ ਹੀ ਸੁਬਾਸੁ ਮਹਿਕਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Relishing the sweet elixir-like dust of the feet of the True Guru, the tongue enjoys strange bliss and pleasure. The nostrils are amazed by the sweet smell of that dust of the True Guru.

column2: ਕੋਮਲਤਾ ਸੀਤਲਤਾ ਪੰਗ ਸਰਬੰਗ ਭਏ; ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਪੁਨਿ ਅਨਤ ਨ ਧਾਏ ਹੈ॥੩੩੫॥
column5: Experiencing the calmness and tenderness of the sweet smell of the holy feet of the True Guru, all limbs of the body become stable. The black bee like mind then does not wander anywhere else and remains attached with lotus-like feet. (335)

column2: ਬਾਸਨਾ ਕੋ ਬਾਸੁ. ਦੂਤ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਕਾਲ; ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who has taken the refuge of the lotus-like feet of the True Guru, is freed from attraction of all other smells and involvement in the five vices.

column2: ਭੈਜਲ ਭਇਆਨਕ ਲਹਰਿ ਨ ਬਿਆਪਿ ਸਕੈ; ਨਿਜ ਘਰ ਸੰਪਟ ਕੈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: The worldly waves of wants and desires cannot influence him anymore. Having engrossed himself in the Self, he has destroyed all type of duality.

column2: ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਬਿਸਿਮਰਨ ਕੈ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸਿ ਬਸਿ. ਆਸਾ ਮਨਸਾ ਨ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: The black bee like lover of the lotus-feet of the True Guru, forgets all other type of knowledge, contemplations and incantations of meditations. He has destroyed all his wants and desires by virtue of his love for the lotus feet of the True Guru.

column2: ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ. ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਮਤਿ; ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਉਨਮਨਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥੩੩੬॥
column5: A Sikh of the Guru who is lover of the lotus feet (of the Guru) sheds his duality. He remains absorbed in the refuge of the lotus feet. In the higher spiritual state, he is absorbed in the stable contemplation of the Lord. (336)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਲੇਪਨ ਕੈ; ਭਰਮ ਕਰਮ ਲੇਖ ਸਿਆਮਤਾ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: The application of the holy dust of the lotus-like feet of the true Guru obliterates the dross of all deeds performed in the previous births under the influence of doubts, suspicions and lack of faith.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮਲੀਨ ਮਨਿ; ਕਰਿ ਨਿਰਮਲ. ਦੂਤ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: By quaffing the nectar-like elixir of the holy feet of the True Guru, the dross of the mind is dispensed and one becomes clean (of heart). He is also freed from the influence of the five evils and other dualities.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਹਜ ਘਰਿ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਮਤਿ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਠਹਿਰਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: Engrossed in the meditation of the holy name, one lives in abode of God. The consciousness becomes stable and in the refuge of the Lord.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਗੁਰ ਮਹਮਾ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧਿ; ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਅਉ ਸਕਲ ਫਲਦਾਈ ਹੈ॥੩੩੭॥
column5: The knowledge of the glory of True Guru's holy feet is limitless and vast. He is the store-house of all material goods and a perfect and complete donor. (337)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਜਨ ਕੈ ਦਿਬਿ ਦੇਹ; ਮਹਾ ਮਲਮੂਤ੍ਰਧਾਰੀ. ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਕੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Bathing in the holy dust of the True Guru's feet, the body of an individual acquires golden hue. One who is evil of thoughts, becomes Guru-oriented and divine of temperaments.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਚਰਨਾਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ; ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ. ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਚੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: By relishing the elixir of the True Guru's feet, the mind is freed from the triple traits of maya (mammon). He then recognises his self.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਨਿਜ ਆਸਨ ਸਿੰਘਾਸਨ ਕੈ; ਤ੍ਰਿਭਵਨ ਅਉ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: By instilling the lotus-like holy feet of the True Guru in self, I.e. mind, one becomes aware of all the three times and the three worlds.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਸ ਗੰਧ ਰੂਪ ਸੀਤਲਤਾ; ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ. ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਲਿਵ ਲੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥੩੩੮॥
column5: By relishing the coolness, sweetness, fragrance and beauty of the lotus-like feet of the True Guru, the duality disappears from the mind. One remains absorbed in the refuge and support of the holy feet (of the True Guru). (338)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਜਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਅਤਿ; ਪੁਰਬ ਤੀਰਥ ਕੋਟਿ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Bathing in the holy dust of the lotus feet of the True Guru has great significance. Millions of places of pilgrimage reside in the refuge of the True Guru. One is deemed to have visited all the holy places by the touch of the dust of His holy feet.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਜਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਅਤਿ; ਦੇਵੀ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ ਪੂਜਤ ਚਰਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The glory and grandeur of the dust of True Guru's holy feet supreme. All gods and goddesses worship Him as His humble servants. (worship of all gods and goddesses lie in feet of the True Guru).

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਜਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਅਤਿ; ਕਾਰਨ ਅਧੀਨ ਕੀਨ. ਕਾਰਨ ਕਰਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The significance of bathing in the dust of the holy feet of True Guru is so great· that he who is ever under the causes, himself becomes the creator of those causes, by becoming a devoted slave of the True Guru.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਰਜ ਮਜਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਅਤਿ; ਪਤਿਤ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਭਏ. ਤਾਰਨ ਤਰਨ ਹੈ॥੩੩੯॥
column5: Importance of touching of True Guru's holy feet is so supreme that a human being badly soiled in sins of maya becomes pious in his refuge. He even becomes a ship for others to sail across the worldly ocean. (339)

column2: ਮਾਨਸਰ ਹੰਸ. ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਪਰਮਹੰਸ; ਧਰਮਧੁਜਾ ਧਰਮਸਾਲਾ ਚਲਿ ਆਵਹੀ॥
column5: Just as swans visit lake Mansarover, so do the righteous people with divine wisdom visit the holy congregation of Lord's loving servants/devotees.

column2: ਉਤ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਅਹਾਰ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ; ਇਤ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: There, at Mansarover, the swans relish pearls as their food and nothing else; so do these devotees engross their minds in the holy Naam of the Lord and remains attached with His divine words.

column2: ਉਤ ਖੀਰ ਨੀਰ ਨਿਰਵਾਰੋ ਕੈ ਬਖਾਨੀਅਤ; ਇਤ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਸਮਝਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: The swans are believed to disintegrate milk in its constituents of water and milk; while here in the holy congregation, one learns about those who are Guru-oriented and self-oriented.

column2: ਉਤ ਬਗ ਹੰਸ ਬੰਸ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨ ਮੇਟਿ ਸਕੈ; ਇਤ ਕਾਗ ਪਾਗਿ ਸਮ ਰੂਪ ਕੈ ਮਿਲਾਵਹੀ॥੩੪੦॥
column5: The temperament of herons cannot be changed into those of swans but here in the holy congregation, those who are like filth-eating crows are transformed into holy and devoted persons through the hue of Naam blessed by the True Guru. (340)

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਛਿਨ; ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿਕ ਨ ਪਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Even gods like Shiv, Brahma, Sanak etc. are unable to acquire the importance of the congregation that one achieves by keeping the company of the obedient and devoted disciples of the True Guru even for a second.

column2: ਸਿੰਮ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਪੁਰਾਨ ਬੇਦ ਸਾਸਤ੍ਰ ਅਉ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ; ਰਾਗ ਰਾਗਨੀ ਹੂ ਨੇਤਿੁ ਨੇਤਿ ਕਰਿ ਗਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: A very short while spent in the holy congregation is sung as infinite, infinite by various religious scriptures like Simritis, Purans, Vedas beside the musical instruments, and various modes of singing.

column2: ਦੇਵੀ ਦੇਵ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਅਉ ਸਕਲ ਫਲ; ਸ੍ਵਰਗ ਸਮੂਹ ਸੁਖ ਧਿਆਨ ਧਰਿ ਧਿਆਵਹੀ॥
column5: All goddesses, gods, treasures, fruits and comforts of heaven sing and remember the peace that they enjoyed even with a fractional association with congregation of the saints.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਜਾਨਿ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵਹੀ॥੩੪੧॥
column5: The obedient disciples attach their mind and engross themselves in the words of the True Guru with singular mind considering the True Guru to be complete and perfect form of Lord. (341)

column2: ਰਚਨਾ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ. ਬਿਸਮ ਬਚਿਤ੍ਰਪਨ; ਕਾਹੂ ਸੋ ਨ ਕੋਊ ਕੀਨੋ. ਏਕ ਹੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: Miracle of His creation is wondrous and astonishing. No human being has been created like another. Yet His light prevails in all.

column2: ਨਿਪਟ ਕਪਟ. ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਨਟਵਟ ਨਟ; ਗੁਪਤ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਅਟਪਟ ਜਾਵਦੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: This world is an illusion. But every creation that is part of this entangled illusion, He, Himself is causing these wondrous acts both conspicuously and latently like a juggler.

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਸੀ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਨ ਦਰਸਨ ਸੋ ਦਰਸੁ; ਬਚਨ ਸੋ ਬਚਨ ਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸਮੇਕ ਹੈ॥
column5: In this creation, no one looks alike, talks alike, thinks alike or sees alike. No one's wisdom is the same.

column2: ਰੂਪ ਰੇਖ ਲੇਖ ਭੇਖ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਨਾਨਾ ਬਿਧਿ; ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਹੈ॥੩੪੨॥
column5: Living beings are of myriad forms, fortune, posture, sound and rhythm. All this is beyond comprehension and knowledge. In fact it is beyond human capability to understand the strange and wondrous creation of the Lord. (342)

column2: ਸਤਿਰੂਪ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਧਿਆਨ; ਸਤਿਨਾਮੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਪਾਰਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: True of form, the True Guru is the embodiment of the perfect Lord. Focusing on True Guru is truly focusing on the Lord. True Guru helps us realise the Lord of Eternal Name.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਬਦ ਅਨਾਹਦ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਗਿਆਨ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪੰਥ ਸਤਿ. ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਅਗਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Guru-blessed unstruck word is eternal of form and this is the means of divine knowledge and His realisation. The Guru-wise path defined by the True Guru is eternal of form, but this path is beyond reach.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਸਥਾਨ ਸਤਿ; ਕੀਰਤਨ ਸਮੈ ਹੁਇ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਸਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: The assembly of Guru's obedient and saintly disciples is the abode of the eternal Lord. Singing His praises through Gurbani with singular mind, a devoted disciple becomes one with God, the Lord.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਉ ਚਾਉ ਸਤਿ; ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਉ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮ ਹੈ॥੩੪੩॥
column5: The heart of a Guru-conscious disciple of the Guru is ever filled with loving devotion and enthusiasm of His worship. Salute such a cool-natured Guru-conscious disciple again and again. (343)

column2: ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰ ਨਿਰਾਧਾਰ ਨਿਰਾਹਾਰ ਨਿਰਬਿਕਾਰ; ਅਜੋਨੀ ਅਕਾਲ ਅਪਰੰਪਰ ਅਭੇਵ ਹੈ॥
column5: The eternal form of the Lord whose embodiment is True Guru, is formless, who is bereft of all support, who has no desire of any food, who is free of all vices, free of entering wombs to take birth, who is imperishable, limitless and who cannot be fathomed

column2: ਨਿਰਮੋਹ ਨਿਰਵੈਰ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ; ਨਿਰਭੈ ਨਿਰੰਜਨ. ਅਤਹ ਪਰ ਅਤੇਵ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is sans attachment, animosity, free of all allurements and stigmas, fearless, uninfluenced by maya and is beyond the beyond.

column2: ਅਬਿਗਤਿ ਅਗਮ ਅਗੋਚਰ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧਿ; ਅਚੁਤ ਅਲਖ ਅਤਿ ਅਛਲ ਅਛੇਵ ਹੈ॥
column5: Whose extent cannot be known, is imperceptible, beyond the senses, whose expanse is unknowable, who is ever stable, beyond perceptions, is beyond deception nor can be hurt by anyone.

column2: ਬਿਸਮੈ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਸਚਰਜੈ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਅਦਭੁਤ ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਹੈ॥੩੪੪॥
column5: Knowing Him is most baffling, amazing and astonishing that can make anyone blissfully ecstatic. The effulgent of form the True Guru is the form of such an eternal and radiant God Lord. (344)

column2: ਕਾਰਤਕ ਮਾਸ ਰੁਤਿ ਸਰਦ ਪੂਰਨਮਾਸੀ; ਆਠ ਜਾਮ ਸਾਠਿ ਘਰੀ ਆਜ ਤੇਰੀ ਬਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: It is the month of Karlik, and winter season with full moon night. In these eight watches, here is your chance to meet your beloved at any time. (Guru Nanak Dev Ji was born on this day).

column2: ਅਉਸਰ ਅਭੀਚ ਬਹੁਨਾਇਕ ਕੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ; ਹੁਇ ਰੂਪ ਗੁਨ ਜੋਬਨ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਅਧਿਕਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Therefore, you too be the rightful person to meet the beloved Lord of myriad other female-like seekers, with your loving devotion, love, worship like beauty and adornment of youthful virtues on this auspicious moment of star configuration.

column2: ਚਾਤਿਰ ਚਤੁਰ ਪਾਠ. ਸੇਵਕ ਸਹੇਲੀ ਸਾਠਿ; ਸੰਪਦਾ ਸਮਗ੍ਰੀ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਚਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: You are alert and adept in Naam Simran, the sixty main veins of your body are your friends and in your obedience, and you are the possessor of equipoise, beauteous treasure and other items of great value.

column2: ਸੁੰਦਰ ਮੰਦਰ. ਸੁਭ ਲਗਨ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ; ਜੀਵਨ ਜਨਮ ਧੰਨਿ. ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਪਿਆਰੀ ਹੈ॥੩੪੫॥
column5: In this auspicious occasion, acquiring the union of the temple-like body with the beloved Lord on the nuptial bed of the heart will make your human birth and life a blessed one. And thus you become the beloved of your dear and loving husband (God). (345)

column2: ਦਿਨਕਰ ਕਿਰਨਿ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਸੁਖਦਾਈ ਅੰਗ; ਰਚਤ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਅਭਰਨ. ਸਖੀ ਆਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: (Before the girl is married off, the bride is adorned with ornaments and jewellery) and the rays of Sun falling on her, make her look more good. Her friends come to embellish her more.

column2: ਪ੍ਰਿਥਮ ਉਬਟਨਾ ਕੈ. ਸੀਸ ਮੈ ਮਲਉਨੀ ਮੇਲਿ; ਮਜਨ ਉਸਨ ਜਲ. ਨਿਰਮਲ ਭਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Paste of herbs, oil and salts is rubbed on her body, the hair are massaged with scents and oil and then shampooed with lukewarm water. Her body then starts radiating like gold.

column2: ਕੁਸਮ ਅਵੇਸ ਕੇਸ. ਬਾਸਤ ਫੁਲੇਲ ਮੇਲਿ; ਅੰਗ ਅਰਗਜਾ ਲੇਪ. ਹੇਤ ਉਪਜਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Adoring the hair with flowers, applying the mixture of fragranced and scented mixture on the body, the feeling of romance and love is incited.

column2: ਚੀਰ ਚਾਰ ਦਰਪਨ ਮਧਿ ਆਪਾ ਆਪੁ ਚੀਨਿ; ਬੈਠੀ ਪਰਜੰਕ ਪਰਿ ਧਾਵਰੀ ਨ ਧਾਇ ਕੈ॥੩੪੬॥
column5: Wearing beautiful dresses, seeing her beautiful form in the mirror, she occupies the bed of her dear husband. Then her wandering mind does not wander any more and becomes stable and restful. (346)

column2: ਕਕਹੀ ਦੈ ਮਾਂਗ ਉਰਝਾਏ ਸੁਰਝਾਏ ਕੇਸ; ਕੁੰਕਮ ਚੰਦਨ ਕੋ. ਤਿਲਕ ਦੈ ਲਿਲਾਰ ਮੈ॥
column5: She should comb her entangled hair and create a neat parting in her hair, apply a dot of saffron and sandalwood on her forehead.

column2: ਅੰਜਨ ਖੰਜਨ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ. ਬੇਸਰਿ ਕਰਨ ਫੂਲ; ਬਾਰੀ ਸੀਸ ਫੂਲ ਦੈ. ਤਮੋਲ ਮੁਖ ਦੁਆਰ ਮੈ॥
column5: Put collyrium in her frolicsome eyes, a ring in the nose, earrings, wear dome-shaped ornament on the head and wait at the main entrance chewing betel leaf.

column2: ਕੰਠ ਸਰੀ ਕਪੋਤਿ ਮਰਕਤ ਅਉ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ; ਬਰਨ ਬਰਨ ਫੂਲ ਸੋਭਾ ਉਰ ਹਾਰ ਮੈ॥
column5: Wear diamond and pearl-studded necklace and adorn her heart with colourful flowers of virtuous qualities,

column2: ਚਚਰ ਚਰੀ ਕੰਕਨ ਮੁੰਦ੍ਰਿਕਾ ਮਹਦੀ ਬਨੀ; ਅੰਗੀਆ ਅਨੂਪ. ਛੁਦ੍ਰਪੀਠਿ ਕਟਿ ਧਾਰ ਮੈ॥੩੪੭॥
column5: Wear colourful rings in her fingers, bracelets, bangles on her wrists, apply henna on her hands, wear a beautiful bodice and a black thread with trinkets around her waist. Note: All the above adornments are related to the virtues and Naam Simran of the Si

column2: ਸੋਭਿਤ ਸਰਦ ਨਿਸਿ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਸਿ; ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਸਹੇਲੀ ਕਹੈ. ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸੁ ਚਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: Note: Abandon bashfulness and enjoy the love of the beloved husband at the time of meeting Him. It is a wintry night and the Moon is spreading its light all around. A friend of the holy congregation urges to obtain Guru's sermons in order to enjoy .

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕੈ ਤੇਰੈ ਆਏ ਹੈ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਨਿਧਾਨ; ਮਿਲੀਐ ਨਿਰੰਤਰ ਹੁਇ. ਅੰਤਰੁ ਨ ਰਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: And when the clement Lord in His complete blessings comes and rests on your bed-like heart, then meet Him without any reservations and inhibitions.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ; ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਟ ਭਿਲਾਖੀਐ॥
column5: May the frolicsome mind remain longing for the fragrant dust of the lotus feet of the Lord.

column2: ਜੋਈ ਲਜਾਇ. ਪਾਈਐ ਨ ਪੁਨਿ ਪਦਮ ਦੈ; ਪਲਕ ਅਮੋਲ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਸੰਗ ਮੁਖ ਸਾਖੀਐ॥੩੪੮॥
column5: Guru-conscious persons testify that any seeker bride who remains shy and bashful at the time of meeting with husband Lord, loses that rare opportunity. She is then unable to obtain the invaluable moment even after spending countless money. (348)

column2: ਕੰਚਨ ਅਸੁਧ ਜੈਸੇ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਕੁਠਾਰੀ ਬਿਖੈ; ਸੁਧ ਭਏ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਨ. ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: As impure gold when heated in a crucible, keep on moving here and there but when purified becomes stable and glitters like fire.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਰ ਕੰਕਨ ਅਨੇਕ ਸੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਧੁਨਿ; ਏਕੈ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਪੁਨਿ ਧੁਨਿ ਕੋ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: If many bangles are worn in one arm, they keep making noise by striking with each other but when melted and made into one become quiet and noiseless.

column2: ਖੁਧਿਆ ਕੈ ਬਾਲਕ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਅਤਿ; ਅਸਥਨ ਪਾਨ ਕਰਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a child cries when hungry but becomes quiet and peaceful after suckling milk from his mother's breasts.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ ਧਾਵੈ; ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਨਿਹਚਲ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਪਦ ਬਾਸ ਹੈ॥੩੪੯॥
column5: Similarly a human mind engulfed in worldly attachments and love keeps on wandering all over. But by the sermons of the True Guru, he becomes stable and calm. (349)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦੀਪ ਦਿਪਤ ਭਵਨ ਉਜਿਆਰੋ ਹੋਤ; ਸਗਲ ਸਮਗ੍ਰੀ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿ ਦਿਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a house is lit up when a lamp is lighted in it, it makes everything visible clearly;

column2: ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੋਤ. ਕਾਰਜ ਬਾਛਤ ਸਿਧਿ; ਆਨੰਦ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜਿ ਬਿਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: With light spreading all around, all the tasks can be accomplished with ease and the time passes in peace and happiness;

column2: ਲਾਲਚ ਲੁਭਾਇ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਨਾਨਾ ਪਤੰਗ; ਬੁਝਤ ਹੀ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਭਏ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as many moths are enamoured by the light of the lamp but are distressed when the light goes off and darkness descends;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਬਿਦਮਾਨਿ ਜਾਨੀਐ ਨ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਮਹਾਂਤ; ਅੰਤਿਰੀਛ ਭਏ ਪਾਛੇ ਲੋਗ ਪਛੁਤਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੫੦॥
column5: Just as the living beings do not appreciate the importance of the lighted lamp, but repent for not taking advantage of it when the lamp goes off, similarly the people repent and feel sad for not taking advantage of the presence of the True Guru after they

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦੀਪ ਦਿਪਤ ਮਹਾਤਮੈ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਕੋਊ; ਬੁਝਤ ਹੀ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਭਟਕਤ ਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the significance of a lit lamp is not appreciated by anyone, but when it is extinguished, one has to wander in the darkness.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਆਂਗਨਿ ਅਛਿਤ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਕਟਤ ਹੀ ਛਾਂਹਿ ਬੈਠਬੇ ਕਉ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a tree in the courtyard is not appreciated, but when felled or uprooted one longs for its shade.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿ ਬਿਖੈ ਚੈਨ ਹੁਇ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਛਤ੍ਰ ਢਾਲਾ ਚਾਲ ਭਏ ਜਤ੍ਰ ਕਤ੍ਰ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as enforcement of law and order of the kingdom ensures peace and prosperity everywhere, but chaos prevails when enforcement is compromised.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਮ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਜਗਿ; ਅੰਤਿਰੀਛ ਭਏ ਪਾਛੇ ਲੋਗ ਪਛੁਤਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੫੧॥
column5: So is the unique opportunity for the Sikhs of the Guru to meet with the saintly True Guru. Once missed, everyone repents. (351)

column2: ਜਉਪੈ ਜਾਨੈ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਦ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਕੈ ਦੇਖੀਅਤ; ਲੋਚਨ ਅਛਤ ਅੰਧ ਕਾਹੇ ਨ ਪੇਖਹੀ॥
column5: If we believe that we see the beauty of the nature because of our eyes, then why can't a blind person who has no eyes, enjoy the same spectacle?

column2: ਜਉ ਜਾਨੈ ਸਬਦ ਰਸ ਰਸਨਾ ਬਖਾਨੀਅਤ; ਜਿਹਬਾ ਅਛਤ ਕਤ ਗੁੰਗ ਨ ਸਰੇਖ ਹੀ॥
column5: If we believe that we speak sweet words because of our tongue, then why can't a dumb person with his tongue intact speak these words?

column2: ਜਉਪੈ ਜਾਨੈ ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਸੁਨੀਅਨ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਕੈ; ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਸਹਤ ਕਿਉ ਬਹਰੋ ਬਿਸੇਖ ਹੀ॥
column5: If we accept that we hear sweet music because of ears, then why can't a deaf person hear it with his ears intact?

column2: ਨੈਨ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਕੋ ਨ ਕਛੂਐ ਬਸਾਇ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੋ ਅਲੇਖ ਲੇਖ ਲੇਖਹੀ॥੩੫੨॥
column5: In fact, eyes, tongue and ears have no power of their own. Only the union of the consciousness with the words can describe or enable us to enjoy what we see, speak or hear. This is also true for knowing the indescribable Lord. Engrossing the consciousness

column2: ਜਨਨੀ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ ਜੁਗਵੈ ਜਠਰ ਰਾਖੈ; ਤਾ ਤੇ ਪਿੰਡ ਪੂਰਨ ਹੁਇ ਸੁਤ ਜਨਮਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a pregnant woman takes all possible care of herself during her pregnancy and on completion of the period gives birth to a baby boy;

column2: ਬਹੁਰਿਓ ਅਖਾਦਿ ਖਾਦਿ ਸੰਜਮ ਸਹਿਤ ਰਹੈ; ਤਾਹੀ ਤੇ ਪੈ ਪੀਅਤ ਅਰੋਗਪਨ ਪਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Then she observes and controls her eating habits meticulously and rigidly that helps the young child to grow healthy by consuming his mother's milk.

column2: ਮਲ ਮੂਤ੍ਰ ਧਾਰ ਕੋ ਬਿਚਾਰ ਨ ਬਿਚਾਰੈ ਚਿਤ; ਕਰੈ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲ ਬਾਲੁ. ਤਊ ਤਨ ਗਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The mother cares not about all the filth of the child and brings him up to give him a healthy body.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਅਰਭਕ ਰੂਪ ਸਿਖ ਹੈ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਮਧਿ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਇਆਲ ਕੀ ਦਇਆ ਕੈ ਸਨ ਗਤ ਹੈ॥੩੫੩॥
column5: So is a disciple (Sikh), like a child in this world who like the mother is blessed by the Guru with Naam Simran that ultimately emancipates him. (353)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਜਨਨੀ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕਉ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਕਰੈ; ਤਾ ਤੇ ਸੁਤ ਰਹੈ ਨਿਰਬਿਘਨ ਅਰੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a would-be mother takes care of what she eats so that the child in her womb remains healthy.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿ ਰੀਤਿ ਚਕ੍ਰਵੈ ਚੈਤੰਨ ਰੂਪ; ਤਾ ਤੇ ਨਿਹਚਿੰਤ ਨਿਰਭੈ ਬਸਤ ਲੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a good ruler remains alert in the enforcement of law and order so as to keep his subjects safe, fearless of any harm and happy.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਰੀਆ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਮੈ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ; ਤਾ ਤੇ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਹੁੰਚਤ ਪਥਿਕ ਅਸੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a sailor is ever alert while sailing his boat in the ocean so that he takes all his passengers safely to other shore.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਲਿਵ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਸਿਖ ਨਿਜਪਦ ਜੋਗ ਜੀ॥੩੫੪॥
column5: Similarly, the God-like True Guru is ever alert to bless his loving and devoted servant with knowledge and ability to focus his mind in Lord's name. And thus a Sikh of the Guru keeps himself free of all vices and becomes eligible for higher spiritual stat

column2: ਜਨਨੀ ਸੁਤਹਿ ਜਉ ਧਿਕਾਰ ਮਾਰਿ. ਪਿਆਰੁ ਕਰੈ; ਪਿਆਰ ਝਿਰਕਾਰ ਦੇਖਿ ਸਕਤ ਨ ਆਨ ਕੋ॥
column5: The mother scolds and spanks the child but cannot tolerate anyone else scolding, spanking and loving him.

column2: ਜਨਨੀ ਕੋ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਅਉ ਧਿਕਾਰ ਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੇਤ; ਆਨ ਕੋ ਧਿਕਾਰ ਪਿਆਰ ਹੈ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਕੋ॥
column5: Mothers scolding and spanking of the child is for his benefit but when someone else does it, it is indeed painful.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਅਗਨਿ ਮੈ ਪਰੈ. ਬੂਡ ਮਰੈ ਜਰੈ; ਤੈਸੇ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕ੍ਰੋਪ ਆਨ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਕੋ॥
column5: (Although water is cold and fire is hot) falling in water drowns while jumping into fire burns a person to death. Similarly it is foolish to believe on the kindness or anger of another woman. (It is utter foolishness to lay faith on any other god/goddess

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਕੋ ਜੁਗਵਤ ਜਤਨ ਕੈ; ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨ ਬਿਆਪੈ. ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਕੋ॥੩੫੫॥
column5: Just like mother, the True Guru makes every effort and attaches the Sikhs in the love of the Supreme Lord, the source of everything. And thus they are never infatuated or attracted by the love or anger of any god/goddess or spurious saint. (355)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਰ ਗਹਤ ਸਰਪ ਸੁਤ ਪੇਖਿ ਮਾਤਾ; ਕਹੈ ਨ ਪੁਕਾਰ. ਫੁਸਲਾਇ ਉਰ ਮੰਡ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as seeing a snake in the hands of her son, the mother does not shout but very calmly endears him to herself.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬੈਦ ਰੋਗੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਕਹੈ ਨ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ; ਸੰਜਮ ਕੈ ਅਉਖਦਿ ਖਵਾਇ ਰੋਗ ਡੰਡ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a physician does not disclose the details of ailment to the patient but serves him medicine within strict preventions and makes him well.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਭੂਲਿ ਚੂਕਿ ਚਟੀਆ ਕੀ ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰੈ ਪਾਂਧਾ; ਕਹਿ ਕਹਿ ਸੀਖਿਆ ਮੂਰਖਤ ਮਤਿ ਖੰਡ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the teacher does not take the mistake of his student to heart, and instead he removes his ignorance by imparting him with necessary lesson.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪੇਖਿ ਅਉਗੁਨ ਕਹੈ ਨ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕਾਹੂ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਸਮਝਾਵਤ. ਪ੍ਰਚੰਡ ਹੈ॥੩੫੬॥
column5: Similarly, the True Guru says nothing to a vice-infested disciple. Instead, he is blessed with complete knowledge. He makes him understand and changes him into a sharp-minded wise person. (356)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਪੋਖਿ ਤੋਖਿ ਬਾਲਕਹਿ; ਅਸਥਨ ਪਾਨ ਬਾਨਿ ਜਨਨੀ ਮਿਟਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a mother weans away the child from sucking her breast by feeding him with sweet meats.

column2: ਮਿਸਰੀ ਮਿਲਾਇ ਜੈਸੇ ਅਉਖਦ ਖਵਾਵੈ ਬੈਦੁ; ਮੀਠੋ ਕਰਿ ਖਾਤ ਰੋਗੀ. ਰੋਗਹਿ ਘਟਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a physician serves medicine coated with sugar to his patient who readily swallows it, the physician thus cures the patient.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲੁ ਸੀਚਿ ਸੀਚਿ ਧਾਨਹਿ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਾਨ ਪਾਲੈ; ਪਰਪਕ ਭਏ ਕਟਿ ਘਰ ਮੈ ਲਿਆਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a farmer irrigates his fields and brings up crops or rice and wheat and when ripe, harvests them and brings it home.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪੁਜਾਇ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਕਰਿ; ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਨਾਮੁ ਧਾਮੁ ਸਿਖੈ ਪਹੁਚਾਵਈ॥੩੫੭॥
column5: So does a True Guru free a Sikh from the worldly affairs and fulfils his desire of consecration. Thus he raises the Sikh high up spiritually through perpetual Naam Simran. (357)

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਸੁਤ ਰਾਖਤ ਜਨਨੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਅਵਗੁਨ ਗੁਨ ਮਾਤਾ ਚਿਤ ਮੈ ਨ ਚੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a son leaves his understanding, perception and protection of his life in the care of his mother, and she too does not think of her son's merits and demerits.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਭਰਤਾਰਿ ਭਾਰਿ ਨਾਰਿ ਉਰ ਹਾਰਿ ਮਾਨੈ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਲਾਲੁ ਲਲਨਾ ਕੋ ਮਾਨੁ ਮਨਿ ਲੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife filled with love of her husband, bears all the load of her husband on her mind, the husband too makes loving and respectful room for her in his heart.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚਟੀਆ ਸਭੀਤਿ ਸਕੁਚਤ ਪਾਂਧਾ ਪੇਖਿ'; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਭੂਲਿ ਚੂਕਿ ਪਾਂਧਾ ਛਾਡਤ ਨ ਹੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a student feels petrified at the sight of the teacher and as a reaction, the teacher too ignores his mistakes under the influence of this reverential fear and does not give up loving him.

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਸਿਖ; ਤਾ ਤੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਜਮਦੂਤਹਿ ਨ ਦੇਤ ਹੈ॥੩੫੮॥
column5: Similarly, a Sikh of the Guru who takes the refuge of the True Guru with devotion and love in his heart, the True Guru does not let him fall in the hands of angels of death when he is about to leave for the world beyond. The True Guru provides him a place

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕਾਮ ਕਟਕ ਹੁਇ ਕਾਮਾਰਥੀ; ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਕ੍ਰੋਧੀਵੰਤ ਆਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: If countless means of inciting lust in a consecrated and practitioner of meditation on Lord's name befall upon a Sikh of the Guru, he is also invaded by unlimited means that can put him in rage;

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਲੋਭ ਲੋਭੀ ਹੁਇ ਲਾਲਚ ਕਰੈ; ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਮੋਹ ਮੋਹਿ ਅਵਗਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: If he is visited by millions and millions of allurements of greed and attachments to entangle him;

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਅਹੰਕਾਰ ਅਹੰਕਾਰੀ ਹੁਇ; ਰੂਪ ਰਿਪੁ ਸੰਪੈ ਸੁਖ ਬਲ ਛਲ ਚਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: Millions and millions of such temptations are visited upon him like enemies that would make him proud, enticing him with wealth, luxuries and physical power;

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਕੇ ਰੋਮਹਿ ਨ ਚਾਪ ਸਕੈ; ਜਾ ਮੈ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਸਤ੍ਰ ਸਨਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥੩੫੯॥
column5: These evil forces cannot even harm a hair of the body of these Sikhs of the Guru who are blessed with the weapons and armour of the knowledge and consecration of True Guru. (In other words, no amount of temptations and worldly allurements can influence th

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸੁਮੇਰ ਊਚ ਅਚਲ ਅਗੰਮ ਅਤਿ; ਪਾਵਕ ਪਵਨ ਜਲ ਬਿਆਪ ਨ ਸਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: As Sumer mountain is believed to be very high, immovable and inaccessible, it is least influenced by fire, air and water;

column2: ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਤਾਸ ਬਾਨੀ ਚਉਗਨੀ ਚੜ੍ਹਤ; ਪਉਨ ਗਉਨ ਧੂਰਿ ਦੂਰਿ ਹੋਇ ਚਮਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: It shines and blazes in fire many times more while the air removes its dust making it glitter far more,

column2: ਸੰਗਮ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਲੁ ਧੋਇ. ਨਿਰਮਲ ਕਰੈ; ਹਰੈ ਦੁਖ ਦੇਖਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਜਸ ਬਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Water pouring on it makes it clean washing away all its dross. It dispels the distresses of the world by providing them with many herbs and medicinal plants. Because of all these virtuous qualities, people sing the glory of Sumer mountain.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਜੋਗੀ ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਣ ਅਤੀਤ ਚੀਤ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਛਕਤਿ ਹੈ॥੩੬੦॥
column5: Similarly the mind of the Sikhs attached with the lotus feet of Guru is free from the triple influence of maya (mammon). He accumulates no dross. Like Sumer mountain, he is stable, inaccessible, pious, free of all dross of vices and who allay others suffe

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਸੁਕਦੇਵ ਕੇ ਜਨਮ ਸਮੈ ਜਾ ਕੋ ਜਾ ਕੋ; ਜਨਮੁ ਭਇਓ. ਤੇ ਸਕਲ ਸਿਧ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: According to a story in Mahabharat, everyone born at the time of the birth of sage Sukdev is considered divine and liberated.

column2: ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਜੋਈ ਜੋਈ ਪਰਤ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਸੀਪ ਕੈ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Every drop of rain that falls in the sea during the Nakshatra of Swati is believed to become a pearl when it comes in contact with an oyster.

column2: ਬਾਵਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਸਨਬੰਧ ਪਉਨ ਗਉਨ ਕਰੈ; ਲਾਗੈ ਜਾਹੀ ਜਾਹੀ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ. ਚੰਦਨ ਸਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: When wind blows touching the sandalwood trees, it spreads its fragrance among all the trees who also start smelling like Sandalwood.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੰਗ ਜੋ ਜੋ ਜਾਗਤ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਜੋਗ; ਸਬਦ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਮੋਖ ਪਦ ਪਰਵਾਨੀਐ॥੩੬੧॥
column5: Similarly, all those Sikhs of the Guru who wake up in the ambrosial hour in order to enjoy the holy company of the Sikh blessed by the True Guru with the practicing of Lord's name, become eligible for salvation by virtue of consecration of Naam that they

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ਸਮੈ ਨ ਏਕਸੈ ਆਵਤ ਸਬੈ; ਕਾਹੂ ਸਾਧੂ ਪਾਛੈ ਪਾਪ ਸਬਨ ਕੇ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: All pilgrims on pilgrimage are not alike. But when a rare hermit of higher spiritual state enjoins them, the sins of all of them perish.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨ੍ਰਿਪ ਸੈਨਾ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਨ ਸਕਲ ਹੋਤ; ਏਕ ਏਕ ਪਾਛੇ ਕਈ ਕੋਟਿ ਪਰੇ ਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As all soldiers in a king's army are not equally valiant, but together under a brave and courageous general they become a force to reckon with.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਮੁਦ੍ਰ ਜਲ ਬਿਮਲ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਬਸੈ; ਏਕ ਏਕ ਪੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਹੁਚਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a ship leads the other ships to the safety of the shore through tumultuous ocean, many passengers of these ship also reach the safety of the other end.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸਾਖਾ ਅਨਿਕ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਦੁਆਰ; ਸਨਮੁਖ ਓਟ ਗਹੇ ਕੋਟਿ ਬਿਆਸਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੬੨॥
column5: Similarly, there are numerous teachers and disciples at the worldly level, but one who has taken the refuge of the True Guru, an embodiment of the Lord, millions sail across the worldly ocean with his support. (362)

column2: ਭਾਂਜਨ ਕੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਕੋਊ ਦੀਪਕੈ ਦੁਰਾਇ ਰਾਖੈ; ਮੰਦਰ ਮੈ ਅਛਤ ਹੀ ਦੂਸਰੋ ਨ ਜਾਨਈ॥
column5: If a beacon is lit but kept under cover, no one can see anything in that room despite the presence of an oil lamp there.

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਰਖਵਈਆ ਪੁਨਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਕਰੈ; ਹਰੈ ਤਮ ਤਿਮਰ. ਉਦੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਠਾਨਈ॥
column5: But he who has hidden the lamp removes its cover and light up the room, the darkness of room is dispelled.

column2: ਸਕਲ ਸਮਗ੍ਰੀ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਪੇਖੀਐ ਪ੍ਰਤਛਿ ਰੂਪ; ਦੀਪਕ ਦਿਪਈਆ ਤਤਖਿਨ ਪਹਿਚਾਨਈ॥
column5: Then one is able to see everything and even he who has lit the lamp can be recognised.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਅਵਘਟ ਘਟ ਗੁਪਤ ਜੋਤੀ ਸਰੂਪ; ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਉਨਮਾਨੀ ਉਨਮਾਨਈ॥੩੬੩॥
column5: Similarly, God resides latently in the tenth door of this sacred and invaluable body. By the incantation blessed by the True Guru and perpetual practicing on it, one realises Him and feels His presence there. (363)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾਵੰਤ ਜੰਤ ਅਉਖਧ ਹਿਤਾਇ ਰਿਦੈ; ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਬਲ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਹੋਇ. ਸਹਜਿ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a medicine suits a person, he-gets cured and becomes peaceful and comfortable.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਧਾਤ ਮੈ ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਕਲੰਕ ਡਾਰੇ; ਅਨਿਕ ਬਰਨ ਮੇਟਿ ਕਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as adding some chemicals in the metals give them a shinning lustre and their original colour vanishes.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੋਟਿ ਭਾਰ ਕਰਿ ਕਾਸਟਿ ਇਕਤ੍ਰਤਾ ਮੈ; ਰੰਚਕ ਹੀ ਆਂਚ ਦੇਤ. ਭਸਮ ਉਦਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a small amount of fire can reduce millions of heaps of woods into ashes and destroy it.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਉਰ ਅੰਤਰ ਪ੍ਰਵੇਸ ਭਏ; ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਦੁਖ ਦੋਖਨ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਹੈ॥੩੬੪॥
column5: Similarly, when the teachings of the True Guru resides in the mind of a seeker, his cycle of birth and death and all his sins are destroyed. (364)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਅਨੀ ਬਾਨ ਕੀ ਰਹਿਤ ਟੂਟਿ ਦੇਹੀ ਬਿਖੈ; ਚੁੰਬਿਕ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਤਤਕਾਲ ਨਿਕਸਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the tip of an arrow breaks inside the wound on the body and it is pulled out with the help of a magnet.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜੋਕ ਤੋਂਬਰੀ ਲਗਾਈਅਤ ਰੋਗੀ ਤਨ; ਐਂਚ ਲੇਤ ਰੁਧਿਰ. ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਸ੍ਰਮ ਖਸਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a leech is put on the boil of a patient that sucks out all the dirty blood and pus thus relieving the patient of the pain.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜੁਵਤਿਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਮਰਦਨ ਕਰੈ ਦਾਈ; ਗਰਭ ਸਥੰਭਨ ਹੁਇ. ਪੀੜਾ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਸਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a midwife massages the stomach of a pregnant lady to relieve her of pain and discomfort.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪਾਂਚੋ ਦੂਤ ਭੂਤ ਬਿਭ੍ਰਮ ਹੁਇ ਭਾਗਿ ਜਾਤ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮੰਤ ਜੰਤ ਰਸਨਾ ਰਸਤ ਹੈ॥੩੬੫॥
column5: Similarly, he who has been blessed with the divine word by the True Guru to meditate on and he practices it ardently enjoying the elixir-like Naam with his tongue, is able to dispel the influence of the five demons i.e., lust, anger, attachment, greed and

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਫਲ ਬਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਬਿਰਖਾ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ; ਜਾ ਕੋ ਫਲੁ ਮੀਠੋ. ਖਗ ਤਾ ਪੈ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a fruit orchard has many types of fruit trees, but birds fly only to the one which has sweet fruit.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰਬਤ ਬਿਖੈ ਦੇਖੀਐ ਪਾਖਾਨ ਬਹੁ; ਜਾ ਮੈ ਤੋ ਹੀਰਾ ਖੋਜੀ ਖੋਜ ਖਨਵਾਰਾ ਲਲਚਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Numerous types of stones are available in the mountains but one in search of diamond longs to see the stone that can yield one a diamond.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਜਲਧਿ ਮਧਿ ਬਸਤ ਅਨੰਤ ਜੰਤ; ਮੁਕਤਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਜਾ ਮੈ. ਹੰਸ ਖੋਜ ਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a lake is inhabited by many forms of marine life, but a swan visits only that lake which has pearls in its oyster.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਹੈ ਅਸੰਖ ਸਿਖ; ਜਾ ਮੈ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਤਾਹਿ ਲੋਕ ਲਪਟਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੬੬॥
column5: Similarly-numerous Sikhs reside in the refuge of the True Guru. But he who has the knowledge of Guru residing in his heart, people feel attracted and enamoured to him. (366)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਸਸਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੋਤ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਤਾਸ; ਚਿਤਵਤ ਚਕ੍ਰਤ ਚਕੋਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਧਾਰਹੀ॥
column5: Just as a geek partridge is spellbound by the radiation of moonlight and keep on looking at it with rapt attention.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਦੀਪ ਹੀ ਦਿਪਤ ਦੇਖਿ; ਅਨਿਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਓਤਿ ਪੋਤਿ ਹੁਇ ਗੁੰਜਾਰਹੀ॥
column5: Just as countless moths and insects gather around the flame of lamp lighted in dark place.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਜਾਨ ਕਾਜ ਭਾਂਜਨ ਮੈ; ਰਾਖਤ ਹੀ ਚੀਟੀ ਕੋਟਿ ਲੋਭ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਅਪਾਰ ਹੀ॥
column5: Just as ants gather around the pot in which some sweet meats have been kept.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪਰਮ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਪਰਵਾਨ ਜਾ ਮੈ; ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਤਾਸ ਚਰਨ ਨਮਸਕਾਰਹੀ॥੩੬੭॥
column5: Similarly, the whole world bows at the feet of that Sikh of the Guru who is blessed with the supreme treasure i.e. divine word by the true guru and is well lodged in the heart of the Sikh by perpetual practice. (367)

column2: ਜੇਤੇ ਫੂਲ ਫੂਲੇ. ਤੇਤੇ ਫਲ ਨ ਲਾਗੇ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ; ਲਾਗਤ ਜਿਤੇਕੁ. ਪਰਪਕ ਨ ਸਕਲ ਹੈ॥
column5: All the flowers that blossom on a tree do not yield fruit. And whatever number of fruits appear, do not ripe to be eaten ultimately.

column2: ਜੇਤੇ ਸੁਤ ਜਨਮਤ. ਜੀਅਤ ਰਹੈ ਨ ਤੇਤੇ; ਜੀਅਤ ਹੈ ਜੇਤੇ. ਤੇਤੇ ਕੁਲ ਨ ਕਮਲ ਹੈ॥
column5: All sons born do not survive to live but all those who live do not bring name and fame to their family.

column2: ਦਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਜਾਤ ਜੇਤੇ. ਸੁਭਟ ਨ ਹੋਹਿ ਤੇਤੇ; ਜੇਤਕ ਸੁਭਟ. ਜੂਝਿ ਮਰਨ ਨ ਥਲ ਹੈ॥
column5: All those who join the army are not valiant soldiers. And those who are brave warriors do not die fighting in the battlefield.

column2: ਆਰਸੀ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸਭ ਹੀ ਕਹਾਵੈ; ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਏ ਬਿਰਲੇ ਅਚਲ ਹੈ॥੩੬੮॥
column5: A glass embedded in a finger ring cracks when brought near fire but a genuine stone is unaffected. Similarly like a genuine stone, everyone is known as a Sikh but a few emerge genuine when put through traits. (368)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਅਹਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਕਉ ਬਾਲਕ ਬਿਲੋਕ ਧਾਵੈ; ਗਹਿ ਗਹਿ ਰਾਖੈ ਮਾਤਾ. ਸੁਤ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Because of its shining characteristic, a child runs to catch hold of snake and fire, but his mother keeps stopping him from doing so resulting in the wailing of the child.

column2: ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾਵੰਤ ਜੰਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਚਾਹਤ ਅਖਾਦਿ ਖਾਦਿ; ਜਤਨ ਕੈ ਬੈਦ ਜੁਗਵਤ. ਨ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as an ailing person wishes to eat food that is not good for his recovery and the physician constantly persuades him to exercise control and prevention and that helps the patient recover.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪੰਥ ਅਪੰਥ ਬਿਬੇਕਹਿ ਨ ਬੂਝੈ ਅੰਧ; ਕਰਿ ਗਹੇ ਅਟਪਟੀ ਚਾਲ ਚਲਿਓ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a blind person is unaware of the good and bad paths, and walks in a zig zag manner even by feeling the path with his walking stick.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਕਰਤ ਕਨਿਕ ਅਉ ਕਾਮਨੀ ਕੀ; ਰਾਖੈ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ਗੁਰ. ਸਿਖ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੬੯॥
column5: So does a Sikh longs to enjoy the pleasure of a woman and others wealth and is ever anxious to possess them, but the True Guru wants to keep his Sikh free from these allurements. (369)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਾਤਾ ਪਿਤਾ ਅਨੇਕ ਉਪਜਾਤ ਸੁਤ; ਪੂੰਜੀ ਦੈ ਦੈ ਬਨਜ ਬਿਉਹਾਰਹਿ ਲਗਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Just as parents give birth and raise many children and then support them with money and material to put them into trading business;

column2: ਕਿਰਤ ਬਿਰਤ ਕਰਿ ਕੋਊ ਮੂਲ ਖੋਵੈ ਰੋਵੈ; ਕੋਊ ਲਾਭ ਲਭਤਿ ਕੈ ਚਉਗੁਨੋ ਬਢਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: And out of them, one may lose all that he has invested in business and cry while other may earn much profit to enhance his investment fourfold;

column2: ਜੈਸੋ ਜੈਸੋ ਜੋਈ ਕੁਲਾਧਰਮ ਹੈ ਕਰਮ ਕਰੈ; ਤੈਸੋ ਤੈਸੋ ਜਸੁ ਅਪਜਸੁ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Every member of the family works and conducts himself according to the family traditions, and every son earns good or bad name according to the deeds performed by them.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਪੁਹਪ ਗਤਿ; ਸਿਖ ਸਾਖਾ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਰਖ. ਫਲ ਪਾਵਹੀ॥੩੭੦॥
column5: Similarly, the True Guru is like a flower that offers fragrance to all in equal measures but because of their higher or lower consciousness, the Sikhs obtain many types of blessings from him. Those who abide by His sermon, benefit while others who may get

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਰਪਤਿ ਬਹੁ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਬਿਵਾਹ ਕਰੈ; ਜਾ ਕੈ ਜਨਮਤ ਸੁਤ. ਵਾਹੀ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਰਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a king marries many young maids, but she who produces him a son has the kingdom in her home.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਉਦਧਿ ਮਧਿ ਚਹੂੰ ਓਰ ਮੈ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਚਲੈ; ਜੋਈ ਪਾਰ ਪਹੁੰਚੈ. ਪੂਰਨ ਸਰਬ ਕਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as ships sail in the ocean from all directions, but the ship that reaches its destination safely and on time, its travellers are benefited the most.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਖਾਨਿ ਖਨਤ ਅਨੰਤ ਖਨਵਾਰਾ ਖੋਜੀ; ਹੀਰਾ ਹਾਥ ਚੜੈ ਜਾ ਕੈ. ਤਾ ਕੈ ਬਾਜੁ ਬਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the miners dig the mines and one who is able to dig out or locate a diamond indulges in merry-making and festivities.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਨਵਤਨ ਅਉ ਪੁਰਾਤਨ; ਜਿਨ ਪਰਿ ਕਟਾਛਿ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ. ਤਾ ਕੈ ਛਬਿ ਛਾਜ ਹੈ॥੩੭੧॥
column5: So are many old and new Sikhs of the True Guru. But those who are blessed with His clemency and look of grace, become noble, beautiful, wise and respectable through meditation of Naam. (371)

column2: ਬੂੰਦ ਬੂੰਦ ਬਰਖ ਪਰਨਾਰੇ ਬਹਿ ਚਲੈ ਜਲ; ਬਹੁਰਿਓ ਉਮਗਿ ਬਹੈ ਬੀਥੀ ਬੀਥੀ ਆਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Every drop of rain joins the other and together they flow from the roof-tops into the streets and then in the storm water drains; And overflowing its banks, the water flows through many rivulets and join the main stream or rivers;

column2: ਤਾ ਤੇ ਨੋਰਾ ਨੋਰਾ ਭਰਿ ਚਲਤ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਮਿਲਤ ਹੈ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: And all the water of the rivers flow to achieve union with sea and once it falls in it, become one with it. It loses its individuality. The truth is, whatever are the traits of a person, he is praised and recognised accordingly (Some may behave mean, frol

column2: ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਕਲ ਜਲ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਚਲਿ; ਸੰਗਮ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਹੋਤ. ਸਮਤ ਸਮਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a diamond held in hand seems very small but when evaluated and sold, fills the coffers. Just as a cheque/draft carried on person has no weight but when cashed at the other end yields much money

column2: ਜਾ ਮੈ ਜੈਸੀਐ ਸਮਾਈ ਤੈਸੀਐ ਮਹਮਾ ਬਡਾਈ; ਓਛੋ ਅਉ ਗੰਭੀਰ ਧੀਰ. ਬੂਝੀਐ ਬੁਲਾਇ ਕੈ॥੩੭੨॥
column5: Just as seed of a banyan tree is very small but when sown grows into big tree and spreads all over. Similar is the significance of the lodging of true Guru's teachings in the hearts of obedient Sikhs of the Guru. This is reckoned only on reaching the divi

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਹੀਰਾ ਹਾਥ ਮੈ ਤਨਕ ਸੋ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦੇਤ; ਮੋਲ ਕੀਏ ਤੇ ਦਮਕਨ ਭਰਤ ਭੰਡਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a diamond held in hand seems very small but when evaluated and sold, fills the coffers.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਰ ਬਾਧੇ ਹੁੰਡੀ. ਲਾਗਤ ਨ ਭਾਰ ਕਛੂ; ਆਗੈ ਜਾਇ ਪਾਈਅਤ ਲਛਮੀ ਅਪਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a cheque/draft carried on person has no weight but when cashed at the other end yields much money

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਟਿ ਬੀਜ ਅਤਿ ਸੂਖਮ ਸਰੂਪ ਹੋਤ; ਬੋਏ ਸੈ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਕਰੈ ਬਿਰਖਾ ਬਿਸਥਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as seed of a banyan tree is very small but when sown grows into big tree and spreads all over.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਸਚਨ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਮੈ; ਜਾਨੀਐ ਮਹਾਤਮ. ਗਏ ਹੀ ਹਰਿਦੁਆਰ ਜੀ॥੩੭੩॥
column5: Similar is the significance of the lodging of true Guru's teachings in the hearts of obedient Sikhs of the Guru. This is reckoned only on reaching the divine court of the Lord. (The practitioners of Naam are honoured in His court). (373)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਦ ਪੀਅਤ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ ਮਰੰਮੁ ਤਾ ਕੋ; ਪਾਛੈ ਮਤਵਾਰੋ ਹੋਇ. ਛਕੈ ਛਕ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a man drinking wine is unaware of its effect on him and he keeps consuming more till he falls unconscious.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਾਰਿ ਭੇਟਤ ਭਤਾਰਹਿ ਨ ਭੇਦੁ ਜਾਨੈ; ਉਦਿਤ ਅਧਾਨ ਆਨ ਚਿਹਨ ਦਿਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife making love with her husband is unaware of the effect at that time but it appears in the form of her pregnancy.

column2: ਕਰ ਪਰਿ ਮਾਨਕੁ. ਨ ਲਾਗਤ ਹੈ ਭਾਰੀ ਤੋਲ; ਮੋਲ ਸੰਖਿਆ ਦਮਕਨ ਹੇਰਤ ਹਿਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as one feels no weight of a diamond on one's hand but when sold, amazes all with the money that it brings forth.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਸੁਨਿ ਮਾਨੈ ਸਿਖ; ਜਾਨੈ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਜਉ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥੩੭੪॥
column5: So does a Sikh of the Guru listens to the elixir-like sermon of the True Guru and adopts it with mind, words and deeds. He then realises its greatness and merges in the Lord-the ocean of all comforts and peace. (A Naam practitioner only knows the ecstasy

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਛ ਕਛ ਬਗ ਹੰਸ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਪਖਾਨ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਿਖੈ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਉਦਧਿ ਸੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: As the marine dependent life like fish, tortoise, heron, swan, pearls precious stones and nectar is known to be related to water (like, sea etc.)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਾਰੋ ਤਾਰੀ ਤਉ ਆਰਸੀ ਸਨਾਹ ਸਸਤ੍ਰ; ਲੋਹ ਏਕ ਸੇ ਅਨੇਕ ਰਚਨਾ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a lock, key, sword, armour jacket and other weapons are made from the same iron,

column2: ਭਾਂਜਨ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਜੈਸੇ ਹੋਤ ਏਕ ਮ੍ਰਿਤਕਾ ਸੈ; ਖੀਰ ਨੀਰ ਬਿੰਜਨਾਦਿ ਅਉਖਧਿ ਸਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as many type of earthenware are made from the clay in which milk, water, eatables and medicines are stored;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਦਰਸਨ ਬਹੁ ਬਰਨ ਆਸ੍ਰਮ ਧਰਮ; ਸਕਲ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤ ਕੀ ਸਾਖਾ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੩੭੫॥
column5: Similarly, many forms of philosophical tomes, the four caste system, the four abodes of life and religions are known as the branches of the household life. (They all are there because of the household life). (375)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਸਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਕਲ ਮੈ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਬਡੋ; ਮੇਰ ਮੈ ਸੁਮੇਰ ਬਡੋ ਜਗਤੁ ਬਖਾਨ ਹੈ॥
