column5: Just as in the world, the sea is considered biggest among the lakes, rivers etc.; and Sumer mountain amongst all the mountains.

column2: ਤਰਵਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਚੰਦਨ ਬਿਰਖੁ ਬਡੋ; ਧਾਤੁ ਮੈ ਕਨਿਕ ਅਤਿ ਉਤਮ ਕੈ ਮਾਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a sandalwood tree and gold is considered supreme amongst the trees and metals respectively.

column2: ਪੰਛੀਅਨ ਮੈ ਹੰਸੁ. ਮ੍ਰਿਗਰਾਜਨ ਮੈ ਸਾਰਦੂਲ; ਰਾਗਨ ਮੈ ਸਿਰੀਰਾਗੁ. ਪਾਰਸ ਪਖਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as swan is supreme amongst the birds, the lion amongst the feline family, Sri Rag amongst the mode of singing and philosopher-stone among the stones.

column2: ਗਿਆਨਨ ਮੈ ਗਿਆਨ ਅਰੁ ਧਿਆਨਨ ਮੈ ਧਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ; ਸਕਲ ਧਰਮ ਮੈ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤੁ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥੩੭੬॥
column5: Just as the knowledge imparted by the True Guru is supreme of all knowledge, and concentration of mind on True Guru is superb, so is the family life ideal and superior of all the religions (ways of life). (376)

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਮਜਨ ਕਰਬੈ ਕੋ ਹੈ ਇਹੈ ਗੁਨਾਉ; ਨਿਰਮਲ ਤਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਖਾ ਤਪਤਿ ਨਿਵਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: The significance of bathing at places of pilgrimage is that body becomes clean and free of all desires and attractions.

column2: ਦਰਪਨ ਦੀਪ ਕਰ ਗਹੈ ਕੋ ਇਹੈ ਗੁਨਾਉ; ਪੇਖਤ ਚਿਹਨ ਮਗ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੰਭਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: Holding of mirror in the hand shows one the shape of features and body structure. Carrying a lamp in hand makes one aware of the path one walks on.

column2: ਭੇਟਤ ਭਤਾਰਿ ਨਾਰਿ ਕੋ ਇਹੈ ਗੁਨਾਉ; ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿਬੂੰਦ ਸੀਪ ਗਤਿ ਲੈ ਗਰਭ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: The union of husband and wife is like swati drop falling in an oyster that develops into a pearl. The wife becomes pregnant and she takes care of her pearl-like child in her womb.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਕੋ ਇਹੈ ਗੁਨਾਉ; ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਕਰਿ ਹਾਰ ਉਰਧਾਰੀਐ॥੩੭੭॥
column5: Similarly, a disciple taking refuge of the True Guru and getting initiation from him is that the Sikh of the Guru adopts the teachings of the True Guru in his heart and lives his life accordingly. (377)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਾਤਾ ਪਿਤਾ ਨ ਬਿਚਾਰਤ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਸੁਤ; ਪੋਖਤ ਸਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਬਿਹਸਤ ਬਿਹਸਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as the parents take no notice of the faults of their son and bring him up in happy and pleasant atmosphere.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾਵੰਤ ਜੰਤ ਬੈਦਹਿ ਬ੍ਰਿਤਾਂਤ ਕਹੈ; ਪਰਖ ਪਰੀਖਾ ਉਪਚਾਰਤ ਰਸਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a patient suffering from pain explains his ailment to the physician, ignoring his carelessness in maintaining his health, the physician administers medicine lovingly after thorough investigation,

column2: ਚਟੀਆ ਅਨੇਕ ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਚਟਿਸਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਬਿਦਿਆਵੰਤ ਕਰੇ ਪਾਂਧਾ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਸੈ ਪੜ੍ਹਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as there are many students in a school, the teacher does not look into their childish pranks and nuisance but teaches them devotedly to make them knowledgeable,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਕੈ ਅਉਗੁਨੁ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਮੇਟੈ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਸੈ ਸਹਜ ਸਮਝਾਇ ਕੈ॥੩੭੮॥
column5: So does the True Guru bless the Sikhs in His refuge with divine knowledge and high state of equipoise, thus obliterating their ill deeds performed in ignorance. (378)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਕਰਤ ਸੁਤ ਅਨਿਕ ਇਆਨਪਨ; ਤਊ ਨ ਜਨਨੀ ਅਉਗਨ ਉਰਿ ਧਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a mother ignores many of her son's amateurish deeds and brings him up with love and care.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਰਨਿ ਸੂਰਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਗਿਆ ਰਾਖੈ; ਅਨਿਕ ਅਵਗਿਆ ਕੀਏ ਮਾਰਿ ਨ ਬਿਡਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a warrior abides by his woe/pledge in respect of one who comes in his refuge and despite his showing disrespect does not kill him.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਜਲੁ ਕਾਸਟਹਿ ਨ ਬੋਰਤ; ਕਰਤ ਚਿਤ ਲਾਜ ਅਪਨੋ ਈ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a log of wood does not sink in river, since it carries a latent respect that he (river) has helped the tree grow by providing it with life-giving water.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਪਰਮ ਗੁਰ ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਗਤਿ; ਸਿਖਨ ਕੋ ਕਿਰਤੁ ਕਰਮੁ ਕਛੁ ਨ ਬਿਚਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥੩੭੯॥
column5: So is the great benefactor True Guru who like a philosopher stone is capable of turning the Sikhs into gold-like metal. He does not dwell on their erstwhile deeds and by blessing them with Naam Simran, make them virtuous like Himself. (379)

column2: ਜੋਈ ਕੁਲਾ ਧਰਮ ਕਰਮ ਕੈ ਸੁਚਾਰ ਚਾਰ; ਸੋਈ ਪਰਵਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਸ੍ਰੇਸਟ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: He who performs all deeds according to his family traditions, behaves well and kindly is known as an ideal person in the family.

column2: ਬਨਜੁ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਸਾਚੋ. ਸਾਹ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਸਦਾ; ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਬਨਉਟਾ. ਨਿਹਕਪਟ ਕੈ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: He who is honest of all his dealings, alone is considered guileless and sincere before his master, the rich merchant.

column2: ਸੁਆਮਿ ਕਾਮ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ. ਮਾਨਤ ਨਰੇਸ ਆਨ; ਸੋਈ ਸੁਆਮਿ ਕਾਰਜੀ. ਪ੍ਰਸਿਧ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: He who acknowledges the authority of his king and performs his master's tasks with care and devotion is always recognised as the ideal servant of the master (king).

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਰਿਦ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਹੈ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਸੋਈ ਸਿਖ ਜਗ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥੩੮੦॥
column5: Similarly, an obedient Sikh of the Guru who lodges the teachings of True Guru in his mind and engrosses his consciousness in the divine word is known the world over. (380)

column2: ਜਲ ਕੈ ਧਰਨਿ ਅਰੁ ਧਰਨਿ ਕੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲੁ; ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਕੈ ਪਰਸਪਰ ਸੰਗਮੁ ਸਮਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as water has love for earth and earth for water, both respond and acknowledge their love for each other.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਸੀਂਚ ਕੈ ਤਮਾਲਿ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲੀਅਤ; ਬੋਰਤ ਨ ਕਾਸਟਹ ਜ੍ਵਾਲਾ ਮੈ ਨਾ ਜਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as water irrigates useful trees like Tamal, brings them up, and it does not sink the tree (wood) that it has reared, nor let it burn in fire.

column2: ਲੋਸਟ ਕੇ ਜੜਿ ਗੜਿ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਬਨਾਈਅਤ; ਲੋਸਟਹਿ ਸਾਗਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਪਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Iron is forged and moulded to fix wooden planks together to make boats and ships. Because of its association with wood, iron too is able to cross the ocean to the other side.

column2: ਪ੍ਰਭ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਜੈ ਜਨ. ਜਨ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਜੈ ਪ੍ਰਭ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਜਨ ਕੋ ਨ ਗੁਨ ਅਉਗੁਨ ਬੀਚਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥੩੮੧॥
column5: A devoted disciple is known from his Master God and God is recognised through His servant. That is why Master Lord does not recognise the virtues and vices of his slave (He even takes those seekers across the worldly ocean who keep the company of His slav

column2: ਬਿਆਹ ਸਮੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਦੁਹੂੰ ਓਰ ਗਾਈਅਤਿ ਗੀਤ; ਏਕੈ ਹੁਇ ਲਭਤ ਏਕੈ ਹਾਨਿ ਕਾਨਿ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as in celebration of marriage, songs are sung both in the bride and bridegroom's house, the bridegroom's side stands to gain through dowry and arrival of the bride whereas the bride's family loses in wealth and their daughter.

column2: ਦੁਹੂੰ ਦਲ ਬਿਖੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਜਤ ਨੀਸਾਨ ਤਾਨ; ਕਾਹੂ ਕਉ ਜੈ ਕਾਹੂ ਕੋ ਪਰਾਜੈ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as drums are beaten by both sides before commencement of battle, one wins and the other loses ultimately.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦੁਹੂੰ ਕੂਲ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਮੈ ਭਰਿ ਨਾਉ ਚਲੈ
column5: Just as a boat sets out fully loaded with passengers from both banks of a river,

column2: ਕੋਊ ਮਾਝਿਧਾਰਿ ਕੋਊ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰਵਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: one sails across while the other may sink half way.

column2: ਧਰਮ ਅਧਰਮ ਕਰਮ ਕੈ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਊਚ ਨੀਚ ਪਦਵੀ ਪ੍ਰਸਿਧ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੩੮੨॥
column5: Similarly, by virtue of their good deeds, the obedient Sikhs of the Guru achieve high status in society while those who indulge in vices are easily recognised by their bad deeds. (382)

column2: ਪਾਹਨ ਕੇ ਰੇਖ ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤ ਨਿਰਬਾਹੁ ਕਰੈ; ਟਰੈ ਨ ਸਨੇਹ ਸਾਧ ਬਿਗ੍ਰਹ ਅਸਾਧ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as a line drawn on a stone cannot be effaced and lasts till the stone perishes, so is the love of holy men with the Lord's feet and of vicious persons with evil.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਮੈ ਲਕੀਰ ਧੀਰ ਨ ਧਰਤ ਤਤ; ਅਧਮ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਅਉ ਬਿਰੁਧ ਜੁਧ ਸਾਧ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as line drawn on water does not last for a moment, so does the love of a wicked person and opposition or discord of a noble man vanishes in a wink.

column2: ਥੋਹਰਿ ਉਖਾਰੀ ਉਪਕਾਰੀ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ; ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਸਾਧ ਅਧਮ ਉਪਾਧ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as cactus is painful due to its thorns and sugarcane is comforting and pleasant for its sweet juice, so is the temperament of an evil person who raises unpleasant situations while a saintly person remains peaceful and Endeavours to spread peace and h

column2: ਗੁੰਜਾ ਫਲ ਮਾਨਕ ਸੰਸਾਰਿ ਤੁਲਾਧਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਤੋਲਿ ਕੈ ਸਮਾਨਿ ਮੋਲ ਅਲਪ ਅਗਾਧ ਕੋ॥੩੮੩॥
column5: Just as a ruby and a seed of Abrus Precatorius (Ratti) both being red in colour may look alike but the seed of Abrus Precatorius (Ratti) is insignificant in value as compared to ruby. Similarly a noble and an evil person may look same but an evil person i

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਅੰਗ ਰਚਤ ਸੀਗਾਰ ਖੋੜਸ; ਤੇਈ ਗਨਿਕਾ ਰਚਤ ਸਕਲ ਸੀਗਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a lady of a noble house adorns herself with sixteen types of embellishments and even a harlot does the same;

column2: ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸਮੈ ਰਮੈ ਭਤਾਰ ਏਕ; ਬੇਸ੍ਵਾ ਤਉ ਅਨੇਕ ਸੈ ਕਰਤ ਬਿਭਚਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: The lady of the noble house enjoys the bed of one person her husband, whereas a harlot shares her bed with many persons;

column2: ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਸੰਗਮੁ ਸੁਜਸ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਮੋਖ; ਬੇਸ੍ਵਾ ਪਰਸਤ ਅਪਜਸ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਜੀ॥
column5: For her love of her husband, the lady of the noble house is praised, admired and is free of any calumny whereas a prostitute earns notoriety for her blemishes and offering herself to others.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਕਉ ਪਰਮਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਮਾਇਆ; ਸੋਈ ਦੁਖਦਾਇਕ ਹੁਇ ਦਹਤਿ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਜੀ॥੩੮੪॥
column5: Similarly the mammon (maya) becomes good for obedient Sikhs of the Guru who use it for good of others according to the teachings of the Guru. But the same mammon becomes troublesome to the worldly people and causes them distress and sufferings. (384)

column2: ਸੋਈ ਲੋਹਾ ਬਿਸ੍ਵ ਬਿਖੈ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬੰਧਨ ਰੂਪ; ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਕੰਚਨ ਜੋਤਿ ਪਾਰਸ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: Iron is used for making handcuffs, chains and fetters while the same iron when brought in contact with the philosopher stone becomes gold and glitters.

column2: ਸੋਈ ਤਉ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਅਤਿ ਸੋਭਤ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਕਉ; ਸੋਈ ਅਭਰਨੁ ਗਨਿਕਾ ਰਚਿਤ ਅੰਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: A noble lady adorns herself with various embellishments and these make her more respectable and impressive whereas the same adornments are condemned on a lady of ill repute and bad character.

column2: ਸੋਈ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਗਰ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਫਲ; ਸੋਈ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਬਿਖੁ ਭੇਟਤ ਭੁਇਅੰਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: A drop of rain during Swati constellation when falls on an oyster in the sea and becomes an expensive pearl whereas it becomes venom if it drops in the mouth of a snake.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਕਿਰਤ ਬਿਰਤ ਹੈ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਜਗ; ਪਰਉਪਕਾਰ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਸ੍ਰਬੰਗ ਹੈ॥੩੮੫॥
column5: Similarly, mammon is evil of character for the worldly people but for the obedient Sikhs of the True Guru, it is highly philanthropic since it does good to many in their hands. (385)

column2: ਕਊਆ ਜਉ ਮਰਾਲ ਸਭਾ ਜਾਇ ਬੈਠੇ ਮਾਨਸਰ; ਦੁਚਿਤ ਉਦਾਸ ਬਾਸ ਆਸ ਦੁਰਗੰਧ ਕੀ॥
column5: If a crow joins the company of swans on the bank of lake Mansarover (a sacred lake in Himalayas) he will feel morose and in two minds since he cannot find any sullage there.

column2: ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਜਿਉ ਬੈਠਾਈਐ ਸੁਭਗ ਪ੍ਰਜੰਕ ਪਰਿ; ਤਿਆਗਿ ਜਾਇ ਚਾਕੀ ਚਾਟੈ ਹੀਨ ਮਤਿ ਅੰਧ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a dog is made to sit on a comfortable bed, being of base wisdom and foolish he will leave it and go to lick millstone.

column2: ਗਰਧਭ ਅੰਗ ਅਰਗਜਾ ਜਉ ਲੇਪਨ ਕੀਜੈ; ਲੋਟਤ ਭਸਮ ਸੰਗਿ ਹੈ ਕੁਟੇਵ ਕੰਧ ਕੀ॥
column5: If a donkey is applied with paste of sandalwood, saffron and musk etc., he will still go and roll in the dust as is his character.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਚੀਤਿ; ਮਨਸਾ ਉਪਾਧ ਅਪਰਾਧ ਸਨਬੰਧ ਕੀ॥੩੮੬॥
column5: Similarly, those of base wisdom and turned away from the True Guru have no love or attraction for the company of saintly persons. They are ever engrossed in creating troubles and doing ill deeds. (386)

column2: ਨਿਰਾਧਾਰ ਕੋ ਆਧਾਰੁ ਆਸਰੋ ਨਿਰਾਸਨ ਕੋ; ਨਾਥੁ ਹੈ ਅਨਾਥਨ ਕੋ ਦੀਨ ਕੋ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਹੈ॥
column5: God is support of all those who are without any support. He is refuge of those who have no one to take care of them. He is the master of all those who are orphans. He is the haven of mercy for the destitutes.

column2: ਅਸਰਨਿ ਸਰਨਿ ਅਉ ਨਿਰਧਨ ਕੋ ਹੈ ਧਨ; ਟੇਕ ਅੰਧਨ ਕੀ ਅਉ ਕਿਰਪਨ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਲੁ ਹੈ॥
column5: Those who are unable to obtain shelter anywhere, He provides them refuge. For the poor, His name is the real treasure. For blind, He is the walking stick. He showers His kindness even on misers.

column2: ਅਕ੍ਰਿਤਘਨ ਕੇ ਦਾਤਾਰ ਪਤਿਤ ਪਾਵਨ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ; ਨਰਕ ਨਿਵਾਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਗਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲੁ ਹੈ॥
column5: To the ungrateful, he is the provider of their needs. He makes the sinners pious. He saves the sinners from hell fire and abides by His character of kind, clement, benevolent and sustainer.

column2: ਅਵਗੁਨ ਹਰਨ ਕਰਨਿ ਕਰਤਗਿਆ ਸੁਆਮੀ; ਸੰਗੀ ਸਰਬੰਗਿ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸਾਲੁ ਹੈ॥੩੮੭॥
column5: He destroys the vices and knows all the latent deeds of everyone. He is a companion who stands by in all thick and thin situations. Such a Lord is the treasure-house of elixir for those who relish His divine nectar. (387)

column2: ਕੋਇਲਾ ਸੀਤਲ ਕਰ ਕਰਤ ਸਿਆਮ ਗਹੈ; ਪਰਸਿ ਤਪਤ ਪਰਦਗਧ ਕਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A burnt out coal when held in hand blackens it but causes burns when held if burning. (Coal is problematic both when cold or burning)

column2: ਕੂਕਰ ਕੇ ਚਾਟਤ ਕਲੇਵਰਹਿ ਲਾਗੈ ਛੋਤਿ; ਕਾਟਤ ਸਰੀਰ ਪੀਰ ਧੀਰ ਨ ਧਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as dog's lick is contagious and causes unbearable pain when it bites. (Dogs lick and bite both are troublesome).

column2: ਫੂਟਤ ਜਿਉ ਗਾਗਰ ਪਰਤ ਹੀ ਪਖਾਨ ਪਰਿ; ਪਾਹਨ ਪਰਤ ਪੁਨਿ ਗਾਗਰ ਹਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a pitcher breaks when dropped on a stone, and it also breaks when stone drops on it. (A stone is destroyer of a pitcher in every way).

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਸਾਧ ਸੰਗਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਹੂੰ ਬਿਰੋਧ ਬੁਰੋ; ਲੋਕ ਪਰਲੋਕ ਦੁਖ ਦੋਖ ਨ ਟਰਤ ਹੈ॥੩੮੮॥
column5: So is developing loving relationship with evil-minded persons. Loving him or showing dissent towards him is equally bad. Thus one cannot escape from pain and sufferings of this world and the world hereafter. (388)

column2: ਛਤ੍ਰ ਕੇ ਬਦਲੇ ਜੈਸੇ ਬੈਠੇ ਛਤਨਾ ਕੀ ਛਾਹਿ; ਹੀਰਾ ਅਮੋਲਕ ਬਦਲੇ ਫਟਕੁ ਕਉ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: Sitting under a small umbrella leaving the royal canopy and taking a glass crystal in place of a diamond would be a foolish act.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਨਿ ਕੰਚਨ ਕੇ ਬਦਲੇ ਕਾਚ ਗੁੰਜਾ ਫਲ; ਕਾਂਬਰੀ ਪਟੰਬਰ ਕੇ ਬਦਲੇ ਓਢਾਈਐ॥
column5: Accepting glass pieces in place of rubies, seeds of Abrus Precatorius in place of gold or wearing a tattered blanket in place of silk apparel would be an indication of base wisdom.

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕੇ ਬਦਲੇ ਕਰੀ ਫਲ; ਕੇਸਰ ਕਪੂਰ ਜਿਉ ਕਚੂਰ ਲੈ ਲਗਾਈਐ॥
column5: Leaving aside dainty dishes, one eats insipid fruits of Acacia tree, and apply paste of wild turmeric in place of fragrant saffron and camphor, would be an act of total ignorance.

column2: ਭੇਟਤ ਅਸਾਧ ਸੁਖ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਸੂਖਮ ਹੋਤ; ਸਾਗਰ ਅਥਾਹ ਜੈਸੇ ਬੇਲੀ ਮਹਿ ਸਮਾਈਐ॥੩੮੯॥
column5: Similarly, meeting with an evil and vice-ridden person, all comforts and good deeds shrink to such a size as if an ocean has been reduced to the size of a small cup. (389)

column2: ਕੰਚਨ ਕਲਸ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਕੋ ਭਏ ਸੂਧੋ ਹੋਇ; ਮਾਟੀ ਕੋ ਕਲਸੁ ਫੂਟੇ ਜੁਰੈ ਨ ਜਤਨ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a golden pitcher if dented can be set right whereas an earthenware pitcher can never be restored to its original shape when broken.

column2: ਬਸਨ ਮਲੀਨ ਧੋਏ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਹੋਤ ਜੈਸੇ; ਊਜਰੀ ਨ ਹੋਤ ਕਾਰੀ ਕਾਂਬਰੀ ਪਤਨ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a dirty cloth can be made clean by washing, whereas a black blanket can never become white till reduced to tatters.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਲਕੁਟੀ ਅਗਨਿ ਸੇਕਤ ਹੀ ਸੂਧੀ ਹੋਇ; ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਪੂਛਿ ਪਟੰਤਰੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਮਨ ਤਨ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as a wooden stick when heated on fire can be straightened, but a dog's tail can never be straightened despite numerous efforts.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਸੁਭਾਉ ਜਲ ਮੈਨ ਗਤਿ; ਸਾਕਤ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਲਾਖ ਪਾਹਨ ਗਤਨ ਸੈ॥੩੯੦॥
column5: So is the nature of True Guru-oriented obedient Sikhs who are tender and malleable like water and wax. On the other hand, the temperament of mammon-loving person is rigid and hard like shellac and stone and thus is destructive. (390)

column2: ਕੋਊ ਬੇਚੈ ਗੜਿ ਗੜਿ ਸਸਤ੍ਰ ਧਨੁਖ ਬਾਨ; ਕੋਊ ਬੇਚੈ ਗੜਿ ਗੜਿ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਸਨਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Someone manufactures bows and arrows used for killing while others fabricate armour coats and shields to defend against these weapons.

column2: ਕੋਊ ਬੇਚੈ ਗੋਰਸ ਦੁਗਧ ਦਧਿ ਘ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਤ; ਕੋਊ ਬੇਚੈ ਬਾਰੁਨੀ ਬਿਖਮ ਸਮ ਚਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Someone sells nourishing foods like milk, butter, curd etc. to make the body strong while others produce items like wine etc. that are harmful and destructive to the body.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ ਉਪਕਾਰੀ ਹੈ ਅਸਾਧ ਸਾਧ; ਬਿਖਿਆ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਨ ਦੇਖੇ ਅਵਗਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: So is a base and low person who spreads evil whereas an obedient Guru-oriented saintly person of the True Guru desires and endeavours to dispense good for all. Treat it like bathing in a sea of poison or jumping into a reservoir of nectar.

column2: ਆਤਮਾ ਅਚੇਤ ਪੰਛੀ ਧਾਵਤ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ; ਜੈਸੇ ਈ ਬਿਰਖ ਬੈਠੇ ਚਾਖੇ ਫਲ ਤਾਹ ਜੀ॥੩੯੧॥
column5: Like an innocent bird, a human mind wanders in all four directions. Whatever tree it sits on, it would get that fruit to eat. In the company of evil-doers, the mind will only pick up dross whereas one collects virtues from the company of Guru-conscious sa

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਜਨਨੀ ਕੈ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਸੁਤ; ਸਬ ਹੀ ਮੈ ਅਧਿਕ ਪਿਆਰੋ ਸੁਤ ਗੋਦ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as a mother has many sons but one in her lap is the dearest to her;

column2: ਸਿਆਨੇ ਸੁਤ ਬਨਜ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਕੇ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਗੋਦ ਮੈ ਅਚੇਤੁ ਹੇਤੁ ਸੰਪੈ ਨ ਸਹੋਦ ਕੋ॥
column5: The elder sons remain engrossed in their commercial activities but one in lap is ignorant of all allurements of wealth, commodities and love of brothers and sisters;

column2: ਪਲਨਾ ਸੁਵਾਇ ਮਾਇ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਕਾਜ ਲਾਗੈ ਜਾਇ; ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਤ ਰੁਦਨ ਪੈ ਪੀਆਵੈ ਮਨ ਮੋਦ ਕੋ॥
column5: Leaving the innocent baby in a cradle, the mother keeps attending other domestic chores but listening to the baby's cry, she comes running and feeds the child.

column2: ਆਪਾ ਖੋਇ ਜੋਈ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਗਹੇ; ਰਹੇ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਮੋਖ ਅਨਦ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਕੋ॥੩੯੨॥
column5: Like the innocent child, he who loses his self and takes the refuge of the holy feet of the True Guru, is blessed with the consecration of Naam-Simran-Mantar that saves him from the worldly vices; and relishing the bliss of Naam Simran he achieves salvati

column2: ਕਰਤ ਨਾ ਇਛਾ ਕਛੁ ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਸਤ੍ਰਤਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਬਾਲ ਬੁਧਿ ਸੁਧਿ ਨਾਹਿ ਬਾਲਕ ਅਚੇਤ ਕਉ॥
column5: Due to his childlike wisdom and unawareness of all sorts, a child is innocent, He desires nothing, nor does he harbour any hostility or friendship with anyone;

column2: ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਲੀਏ ਮਾਤਾ ਪੀਛੈ ਲਾਗੀ ਡੋਲੈ; ਬੋਲੈ ਮੁਖਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਸੁਤ ਹੇਤ ਕਉ॥
column5: His mother out of love keeps wandering behind him with food and clothing and utters elixir-like loving words for her son;

column2: ਬਾਲਕੈ ਅਸੀਸ ਦੈਨਹਾਰੀ ਅਤਿ ਪਿਆਰੀ ਲਾਗੈ; ਗਾਰਿ ਦੈਨਹਾਰੀ ਬੋਲਿਹਾਰੀ ਡਾਰੀ ਸੇਤ ਕਉ॥
column5: The mother loves her friends who keep showering blessings on her son but one who abuses him or say ill words for him destroys her peace of mind and creates duality.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਅਨੰਦਮਈ; ਜੈਸੋ ਜਗੁ ਮਾਨੈ ਤੈਸੋ ਲਾਗੈ ਫਲੁ ਖੇਤ ਕਉ॥੩੯੩॥
column5: Like the innocent child, the obedient Sikh of the Guru maintains impartiality. He treats all alike and by virtue of the relishing of the Naam Ras blessed by the True Guru, stays in a state of bliss. Whatever way is he recognised and known by the worldly p

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦਰਪਨ ਦਿਬਿ ਸੂਰ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਰਾਖੈ; ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੋਤ ਕਿਰਨ ਚਰਿਤ੍ਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: As a magnifying lens placed before the rays of the sun produces fire.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੇਘ ਬਰਖਤ ਹੀ ਬਸੁੰਧਰਾ ਬਿਰਾਜੈ; ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ ਸਫਲ ਸੁਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as earth looks good with rainfall and like a good friend produces fruits and flowers.

column2: ਭੇਟਤ ਭਤਾਰ ਨਾਰਿ ਸੋਭਿਤ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਚਾਰੁ; ਪੂਰਨ ਅਨੰਦ ਸੁਤ ਉਦਿਤ ਬਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as the conjugal union of a well adorned and embellished woman with her husband gives birth to a son and the wife is highly pleased.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਪਰਸਿ ਬਿਗਸਤ ਸਿਖ; ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਪਾਵਨ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਕੈ॥੩੯੪॥
column5: Similarly an obedient disciple of the Guru feels pleased and blossoms at the sight of the True Guru. And by acquiring the treasure-house of divine knowledge and consecration of Naam Simran from his True Guru, he becomes a pious person. (394)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਬੁਧਿਵੰਤਿ ਸਸੁਰਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਚੇਤਨ ਰਹੈ ਅਚਾਰ ਚਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as an intelligent daughter-in-law of a good family deals with everybody attentively, consciously and decently in her in-laws house;

column2: ਸਸੁਰ ਦੇਵਰ ਜੇਠ ਸਕਲ ਕੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੈ; ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਜਾਨਿ ਪਤਿ ਪਰਵਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: Realizing that this is her husband's family, takes care of food and all other needs of her father-in-law, brothers-in-law and other members of the family diligently and respectfully;

column2: ਮਧੁਰ ਬਚਨ ਗੁਰਜਨ ਸੈ ਲਜਾ ਲਵੰਨਿ; ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸਮੈ ਰਸ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਪੂਰਨ ਭਤਾਰ ਕੈ॥
column5: She talks with all elders of the family respectfully, politely and abashedly. Similarly a devoted disciple of the True Guru is adept in observing respect towards all human beings.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸਰਬਾਤਮ ਪੂਜਾ ਪ੍ਰਬੀਨ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਧਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਅਪਾਰ ਕੈ॥੩੯੫॥
column5: But within himself, he remains focused on the divine sight of the God-like True Guru. (According to Bhai Gurdas Ji, practicing on the Guru's words and meditating on Lord's name given by the True Guru is contemplation on the vision of True Guru). (395)

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਪੁਰਬ ਦੇਵ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ ਜਗਤੁ; ਪੁਰਬ ਤੀਰਥ ਸੁਰ ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕੈ॥
column5: People of the world visit various places of pilgrimage on various days considered auspicious by them. But such days and holy places associated with gods are numerous.

column2: ਮੁਕਤਿ ਬੈਕੁੰਠ ਜੋਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਬਿਬਿਧ ਫਲ; ਬਾਂਛਤ ਹੈ ਸਾਧ ਰਜ ਕੋਟਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Millions of seekers of Salvation, heaven and many methods of performing Yoga, worldly knowledge and contemplations long for the holy dust of the feet of saintly True Guru.

column2: ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਅਸੰਖ ਸਿਖ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰੂ ਬਚਨ ਮਿਲੈ ਰਾਮ ਰਸ ਆਨਿ ਕੈ॥
column5: There are numerous devoted Sikhs of the True Guru in the holy assembly of the inaccessible and serene True Guru who receive the sermon of how to reach the blissful state of enjoying ambrosial name of the Lord through meditation.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਅਪਰੰਪਰ ਪੁਰਖ ਲਿਵ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਕੈ॥੩੯੬॥
column5: Such Sikhs of the Guru engross themselves in the silent meditation of Lord's name-an initiation that imperceptible, inaccessible, perfect and God-like True Guru has blessed them with. Their engrossment is highly attentive and in a state of serenity. (All

column2: ਦ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਕਉ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਜਉ ਮਿਲਹਿ; ਜੈਸੋ ਦੇਖੈ ਤੈਸੋ ਕਹਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: If eyes get tongue and ears, then whatever it sees and hears with ears, it will then describe the reality and convey it.

column2: ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਅਉ ਲੋਚਨ ਮਿਲੈ ਦਿਆਲ; ਜੈਸੋ ਸੁਨੈ ਤੈਸੋ ਦੇਖਿ ਕਹਿ ਸਮਝਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: If by the kindness of Almighty, the ears get tongue and eyes, then they will speak with tongue, what they see with eyes and hear.

column2: ਜਿਹਬਾ ਕਉ ਲੋਚਨ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਜਉ ਮਿਲਹਿ ਦੇਵ; ਜੈਸੋ ਕਹੈ ਤੈਸੋ ਸੁਨਿ ਦੇਖਿ ਅਉ ਦਿਖਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: If the Almighty God bless the tongue with eyes .and ears it will say what it sees with eyes and hears with ears.

column2: ਨੈਨ ਜੀਹ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ. ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਲੋਚਨ ਜੀਹ; ਜਿਹਬਾ ਨ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਲੋਚਨ ਲਲਚਾਵਹੀ॥੩੯੭॥
column5: Eyes need co-operation of tongue and ears, the ears need full co-operation of tongue and eyes but just as Guru Nanak on page 1091 of Guru Granth Sahib says “Jeebh rasayan chuni rati lal lavaaye” (Sucking the elixir-like Naam, meditating on Lord's name,

column2: ਆਪਨੋ ਸੁਅੰਨਿ ਜੈਸੇ ਲਾਗਤ ਪਿਆਰੋ ਜੀਅ; ਜਾਨੀਐ ਵੈਸੋ ਈ ਪਿਆਰੋ ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as one loves one's son by heart, so are their sons loved by all others in the world.

column2: ਆਪਨੋ ਦਰਬੁ ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਜਤਨ ਕਰਿ; ਵੈਸੋ ਈ ਸਮਝਿ ਸਭ ਕਾਹੂੰ ਕੇ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as one takes full care of one's wealth and assets, so should one treat other's business and profession monetarily.

column2: ਅਸਤੁਤਿ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਸੁਨਿ ਬਿਆਪਤ ਹਰਖ ਸੋਗ; ਵੈਸੀਐ ਲਗਤ ਜਗ ਅਨਿਕ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as one feels happy listening to one's praise and disturbed on listening slander about oneself, so should one acknowledge and think that others would feel the same.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੁਲ ਧਰਮੁ ਕਰਮੁ ਜੈਸੋ ਜੈਸੋ ਕਾ ਕੋ; ਉਤਮ ਕੈ ਮਾਨਿ ਜਾਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਕਉ॥੩੯੮॥
column5: Similarly, whatever is the business or profession of a person according to his family tradition, it should be accepted as supreme and most appropriate for him. (No one should be hurt on this account). This is enough to understand the Omnipresence of the L

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨੈਨ ਬੈਨ ਪੰਖ ਸੁੰਦਰ ਸਰਬੰਗ ਮੋਰ; ਤਾ ਕੇ ਪਗ ਓਰ ਦੇਖਿ ਦੋਖ ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a peacock's eyes, call, feathers and all other limbs are beautiful, one should not condemn him for his ugly feet. (see the merits alone).

column2: ਸੰਦਲ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਅਤਿ ਕੋਮਲ ਕਮਲ ਜੈਸੇ; ਕੰਟਕਿ ਬਿਲੋਕਿ ਨ ਅਉਗਨ ਉਰਧਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: Just as Sandalwood is very fragrant and lotus flower very delicate, one should not bring to mind their demerit of the fact that a snake generally wraps itself around the sandalwood tree while a lotus flower has thorn on its stem.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਫਲ ਮਿਸਟਿ ਗੁਨਾਦਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ; ਬੀਜ ਕਰਵਾਈ ਕੈ ਬੁਰਾਈ ਨ ਸਮਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a mango is sweet and delicious but its kernal's bitterness should not be thought of.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਦਾਨ ਸਬਹੂੰ ਸੈ ਮਾਂਗਿ ਲੀਜੈ; ਬੰਦਨਾ ਸਕਲ ਭੂਤ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਨ ਤਕਾਰੀਐ॥੩੯੯॥
column5: Similarly one should take Guru's word and his sermons from everyone and everywhere. Everyone should be respected also. No one should be ever slandered and condemned for his demerit.

column2: ਸਵੈਯਾ॥ ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਦਰਸ ਕਤ ਸਜਨੀ; ਕਤ ਵੈ ਨੈਨ ਬੈਨ ਮਨ ਮੋਹਨ॥
column5: O my Guru-conscious friend! like a philosopher-stone, the touch of which changes a metal into gold, where is the glimpse of the True Guru that makes a person supreme and valuable like gold? Where are those enamoring eyes and sweet invaluable words?

column2: ਕਤ ਵੈ ਦਸਨ ਹਸਨ ਸੋਭਾ ਨਿਧਿ; ਕਤ ਵੈ ਗਵਨ ਭਵਨ ਬਨ ਸੋਹਨ॥
column5: Where is that smiling face with beautiful teeth, where is the hearth and home and his majestic walk in the fields and gardens?

column2: ਕਤ ਵੈ ਰਾਗ ਰੰਗ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ; ਕਤ ਵੈ ਦਇਆ ਮਇਆ ਦੁਖ ਜੋਹਨ॥
column5: Where is the treasure of peace and comfort? The treasure of singing His praises through Naam and bani (Guru's compositions). Where is that look of kindness and benevolence that is sailing myriad devotees across the worldly ocean?

column2: ਕਤ ਵੈ ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਰਸ ਲੀਲਾ; ਕਤ ਵੈ ਸੰਤ ਸਭਾ ਛਬਿ ਗੋਹਨ॥੪੦੦॥
column5: Where is the engrossment in the Lord through practicing of Naam, strange and wondrous feeling of enjoying the bliss of Lord's name and where is that congregation assembled in the divine presence of the saintly True Guru that sings the praises of the might

column2: ਕਬ ਲਾਗੈ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਚਰਨਨ ਰਜ; ਦਰਸ ਦਇਆ ਦ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਕਬ ਦੇਖਉ॥
column5: When will my forehead be anointed with the holy dust of the feet of True Guru and when will I see the clement and merciful face of the True Guru with my own eyes?

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਚਨ ਸੁਨਉ ਕਬ ਸ੍ਰਵਨਨ; ਕਬ ਰਸਨਾ ਬੇਨਤੀ ਬਿਸੇਖਉ॥
column5: When will I hear the sweet ambrosial-like and elixir-giving words of my True Guru with my own ears? When will I be able to make a humble supplication of my distress with my own tongue before him?

column2: ਕਬ ਕਰ ਕਰਉ ਡੰਡਉਤਿ ਬੰਦਨਾ; ਪਗਨ ਪਰਿਕ੍ਰਮਾਦਿ ਪੁਨਿ ਰੇਖਉ॥
column5: When will I be able to lie prostrate like a staff before my True Guru and salute him with folded hands? When will I be able to employ my feet in the circumambulation of my True Guru?

column2: ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਤਛਿ ਪ੍ਰਾਨਪਤਿ; ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਜੀਵਨ ਪਦ ਲੇਖਉ॥੪੦੧॥
column5: True Guru who is the manifest of the Lord, provided of knowledge, contemplation, granter of salvation and sustainer of life when will I be able to realise Him clearly through my loving worship? (Bhai Gurdas Ii is expressing his pangs of separation from hi

column2: ਕਬਿਤ॥ ਬਿਰਖੈ ਬਇਆਰ ਲਾਗੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਹਰਰਾਤਿ ਪਾਤਿ; ਪੰਛੀ ਨ ਧੀਰਜ ਕਰਿ ਠਉਰ ਠਹਿਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the leaves and branches of a tree start trembling under the influence of fast wind and even the birds lose trust of their nests;

column2: ਸਰਵਰ ਘਾਮ ਲਾਗੈ ਬਾਰਜ ਬਿਲਖ ਮੁਖ; ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਅੰਤ ਹੰਤ ਜਲ ਜੰਤ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as lotus flowers whither away under the sharp heat of the Sun and aquatic life of the water feels distressed as if their lives were coming to an end;

column2: ਸਾਰਦੂਲ ਦੇਖੈ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਮਾਲ ਦਲ ਚਿਤ ਬਨ; ਵਾਸ ਮੈ ਨ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ ਕਰਿ ਆਸ੍ਰਮ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the herd of deers find solace and safety in their small hiding places in the jungle when they see the lion in the vicinity;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਆਂਗ ਸ੍ਵਾਂਗਿ ਭਏ ਭੈ ਚਕਿਤ ਸਿਖ; ਦੁਖਿਤ ਉਦਾਸ ਬਾਸ ਅਤਿ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੦੨॥
column5: Similarly, the Sikhs of the Guru are frightened, amazed, distressed and morose seeing the body/limbs of a spurious Guru marked with artificial marks of recognition. Even the Sikhs most close to the Guru feel restless. (402)

column2: ਓਲਾ ਬਰਖਨ ਕਰਖਨ ਦਾਮਿਨੀ ਬਯਾਰਿ; ਸਾਗਰ ਲਹਰਿ ਬਨ ਜਰਤ ਅਗਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: If hails be falling, lightening be making thunderous sounds, storm be raging. stormy waves be rising in the ocean and forests may be burning with fire;

column2: ਰਾਜੀ ਬਿਰਾਜੀ ਭੂਕੰਪਿਕਾ ਅੰਤਰ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਬਲ; ਬੰਦਸਾਲ ਸਾਸਨਾ ਸੰਕਟ ਮੈ ਮਗਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: The subjects be without their king, earthquakes be experienced, one may have been troubled by some deep innate pain and for some offence may have been lodged in the jail;

column2: ਆਪਦਾ ਅਧੀਨ ਦੀਨ ਦੂਖਨਾ ਦਰਿਦ੍ਰ ਛਿਦ੍ਰਿ; ਭ੍ਰਮਤਿ ਉਦਾਸ ਰਿਨ ਦਾਸਨਿ ਨਗਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Many tribulations may overpower him, may be distressed by false accusations, poverty may have crushed him, may be wandering for loan and caught in the slavery, may be straying aimlessly but in acute hunger;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਕੋ ਅਦ੍ਰਿਸਟ ਜਉ ਆਇ ਲਾਗੈ; ਜਗ ਮੈ ਭਗਤਨ ਕੇ ਰੋਮ ਨ ਭਗਨ ਹੈ॥੪੦੩॥
column5: And even if more of such worldly tribulations and distresses may befall upon Guru-loving, obedient and meditating persons dear to the True Guru, they are least troubled by them and live life ever in bloom and happiness. (403)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚੀਟੀ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਕੈ ਬਿਰਖ ਚੜੈ; ਪੰਛੀ ਉਡ ਜਾਇ ਬੈਸੇ ਨਿਕਟ ਹੀ ਫਲ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as an ant crawls up the tree very slowly to reach the fruit, whereas a bird flies and reaches it instantly.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਗਾਡੀ ਚਲੀ ਜਾਤਿ ਲੀਕਨ ਮਹਿ ਧੀਰਜ ਸੈ; ਘੋਰੋ ਦੌਰਿ ਜਾਇ ਬਾਂਇ ਦਾਹਨੇ ਸਬਲ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a bullock cart moving in the ruts of the path reaches its destination slowly but a horse moving on either side of the path moves fast and reaches the destination quickly.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੋਸ ਭਰਿ ਚਲਿ ਸਕੀਐ ਨ ਪਾਇਨ ਕੈ; ਆਤਮਾ ਚਤੁਰ ਕੁੰਟ ਧਾਇ ਆਵੈ ਪਲ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as one does not cover even a mile in a few seconds but the mind reaches and wanders around in four directions in a split second.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਭੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਪੱਛ; ਗੰਮ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਅਸਥਲ ਕੈ॥੪੦੪॥
column5: Similarly, the knowledge of Vedas and worldly affairs is based on arguments and exchange of views. This method is like the movement of an ant. But by taking the refuge of the True Guru, one reaches the infallible and stable places of the Lord in no time.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਨਰਾਇ ਪਰਫੁਲਤ ਫਲ ਨਮਿਤ; ਲਾਗਤ ਹੀ ਫਲ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਪੁਹਪ ਬਿਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as trees and other vegetation grow for fruits and flowers but as soon as they bear fruits, their leaves and fruits drop.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤ੍ਰੀਆ ਰਚਿਤ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਭਰਤਾਰ ਹੇਤਿ; ਭੇਟਤ ਭਰਤਾਰ ਉਰ ਹਾਰ ਨ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife adorns and embellishes herself for the love of her husband, but in his embrace, she doesn't even like the necklace she wears as it is considered an impediment in their complete union.

column2: ਬਾਲਕ ਅਚੇਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਕਰਤ ਲੀਲਾ ਅਨੇਕ; ਸੁਚਿਤ ਚਿੰਤਨ ਭਏ ਸਬੈ ਬਿਸਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as an innocent child plays many games in his childhood but forgets them all once he grows up.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਖਟ ਕਰਮ ਧਰਮ ਸ੍ਰਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਕਾਜ; ਗਿਆਨ ਭਾਨ ਉਦੈ ਉਡ ਕਰਮ ਉਡਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੦੫॥
column5: Similarly, the six forms of righteous deeds which are performed diligently for gaining knowledge, disappear like stars when the great knowledge of the Guru shines in its Sun like glory. All those deeds seem futile. Sagle karam dharam jug sodhe. Bin(u) nav

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਹਸ ਬੋਲਤ ਹੀ ਡਾਕਨਿ ਹਰੈ ਕਰੇਜੌ; ਬਾਲਕ ਤਾਹੀ ਲੌ ਧਾਵੈ ਜਾਨੈ ਗੋਦਿ ਲੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a malicious woman enamors a child with her sweet and gullible talk that attracts the child to her who thinks she would bestow her love upon him.

column2: ਰੋਵਤ ਸੁਤਹਿ ਜੈਸੇ ਅਉਖਧਿ ਪਿਆਵੈ ਮਾਤਾ; ਬਾਲਕੁ ਜਾਨਤ ਮੋਹਿ ਕਾਲਕੂਟਿ ਦੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a mother administer medicine to her suffering and crying son but the child feels she is serving him poison.

column2: ਹਰਨ ਭਰਨ ਗਤਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਜਾਨੀਐ ਨ; ਬਾਲਕ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਮਤਿ ਜਗਤ ਅਚੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: The intellect of the worldly beings is also like this child. They do not know the traits of God-like True Guru who is fully capable of destroying all the vices in them. In this regard, Bhai gurdas Ji says: “Avgun lai gun vikanai vachnai da sura” (Var 1).

column2: ਅਕਲ ਕਲਾ ਅਲਖ ਅਤਿ ਹੀ ਅਗਾਧ ਬੋਧ; ਆਪ ਹੀ ਜਾਨਤ ਆਪ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਹੈ॥੪੦੬॥
column5: The True Guru is perfect in all respects. He is beyond our perception. No one can fathom his vast knowledge. He alone knows His own capabilities. All that can be said is-He is Infinite, Infinite, Infinite. (406)

column2: ਦੈਤ ਸੁਤ ਭਗਤ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦ ਭਏ; ਦੇਵ ਸੁਤ ਜਗ ਮੈ ਸਨੀਚਰ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Bhagat Prehlad who made everyone in the city meditate on the name of the Lord, took birth in the house of evil-minded Harnakash. But Sanichar (Saturn) the son of Sun is believed to be an inauspicious and distressing constellation in the world.

column2: ਮਧੁ ਪੁਰ ਬਾਸੀ ਕੰਸ ਅਧਮ ਅਸੁਰ ਭਏ; ਲੰਕਾ ਬਾਸੀ ਸੇਵਕ ਭਭੀਖਨ ਪਛਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Of the six sacred cities, one is Mathura which was ruled by a vice-ridden demon-like king called Kansa. Also, Bhabhikhan a God-loving devotee was born in Lanka, the infamous city of Ravan.

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਗੰਭੀਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਬਿਖਿਆ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਈ; ਅਹਿ ਮਸਤਕਿ ਮਨਿ ਉਦੈ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: The deep ocean yielded the death-giving poison. It is also believed that the most poisonous snake has an invaluable jewel in its head.

column2: ਬਰਨ ਸਥਾਨ ਲਘੁ ਦੀਰਘ ਜਤਨ ਕਰੈ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਨੋਦ ਬਿਸਮ ਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥੪੦੭॥
column5: So, considering someone high or low, good or bad because of the place of his birth or family lineage is just a misconception. This is an indescribable and wondrous play of the Lord that no one can know. (407)

column2: ਚਿੰਤਾਮਨਿ ਚਿਤਵਤ ਚਿੰਤਾ ਚਿਤ ਤੇ ਚੁਰਾਈ; ਅਜੋਨੀ ਅਰਾਧੇ ਜੋਨਿ ਸੰਕਟ ਕਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Perpetual remembrance of the Lord the fulfiller all the desires and wants, removes all worries from the mind. Worshipping the Lord who is free of birth and death cycle, one is able to achieve emancipation from entering into life of various species.

column2: ਜਪਤ ਅਕਾਲ ਕਾਲ ਕੰਟਕ ਕਲੇਸ ਨਾਸੇ; ਨਿਰਭੈ ਭਜਨ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਭੈ ਦਲ ਭਜਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: By contemplating on that Timeless Supreme Lord, fear of death vanishes and one becomes fearless. Singing praises of the fearless Lord, all impressions of fear and suspicions are erased from the mind.

column2: ਸਿਮਰਤ ਨਾਥ ਨਿਰਵੈਰ ਬੈਰ ਭਾਉ ਤਿਆਗਿਓ; ਭਾਗਿਓ ਭੇਦੁ ਖੇਦੁ ਨਿਰਭੇਦ ਗੁਣ ਗਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Remembering repeatedly the name of the Lord who is sans animosity, all feelings of hate and enmity disappear. And those who sing His paeans with devoted mind, find themselves free of all dualities.

column2: ਅਕੁਲ ਅੰਚਲ ਗਹੇ ਕੁਲ ਨ ਬਿਚਾਰੈ ਕੋਊ; ਅਟਲ ਸਰਨਿ ਆਵਾ ਗਵਨ ਮਿਟਾਏ ਹੈ॥੪੦੮॥
column5: He who holds the apron of the casteless and classless Lord, is never noticed for his caste and family lineage. One is able to destroy the cycles of incarnation by coming to the refuge of stable and immovable Lord. (408)

column2: ਬਾਛੈ ਨ ਸ੍ਵਰਗ ਬਾਸ ਮਾਨੈ ਨ ਨਰਕ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ; ਆਸਾ ਨ ਕਰਤ ਚਿਤ ਹੋਨਹਾਰ ਹੁਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: An obedient disciple of the True Guru neither asks for heaven nor does he fear hell. He does not keep any longing or desire in his mind. Instead he believes that whatever God does is just right.

column2: ਸੰਪਤਿ ਨ ਹਰਖ. ਬਿਪਤ ਮੈ ਨ ਸੋਗ ਤਾਹਿ; ਸੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਬਿਹਸ ਨ ਰੋਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Acquisition of wealth does not make him happy. In the times of distress, he is never morose. Instead he treats distresses and comforts alike and does not lament or rejoice on them.

column2: ਜਨਮ ਜੀਵਨ ਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਭੇਦ ਖੇਦ; ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਕਾਲ ਬਾਲ ਬੁਧਿ ਅਵਿਲੋਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is not afraid of birth and death and has no desire of salvation. He is least affected by the worldly dualities and remain in a state of equipoise. He is aware of all the three periods of life and know all the happenings of the world. Yet he always look

column2: ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਅੰਜਨ ਕੈ ਚੀਨਤ ਨਿਰੰਜਨਹਿ; ਬਿਰਲੋ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਮੈ ਕੋਇ ਹੈ॥੪੦੯॥
column5: Who ever is blessed with the collyrium of knowledge of the True Guru, recognises the mammon-free Lord God. But such a person who is able to achieve that state is rare in the world. (409)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਮਿਠਾਈ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਛੁਪਾਇ ਜਤਨ ਕੈ; ਚੀਟੀ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਇ ਚੀਨਿ ਤਾਹਿ ਲਪਟਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as sweetmeats are hidden carefully and yet ants reach it with impunity and cling to them,

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਜਗਾਇ ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਦੁਰਾਇ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ; ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿ ਪਤੰਗ ਤਾ ਮੈ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a lit lamp is carefully hidden in the house, yet a moth is able to find it and merges in its flame,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਿਮਲ ਜਲ ਕਮਲ ਇਕਾਂਤ ਬਸੈ; ਮਧੁਕਰ ਮਧੁ ਅਚਵਨ ਤਹ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the lotus flower of fresh and clean water blooms in solitary place, but the black bee always reaches it to enjoy its elixir,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਿਹ ਘਟਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਤ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ; ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਤਿਹਿ ਦੁਆਰ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੧੦॥
column5: So does a devoted disciple of the True Guru whose heart is kindled with the Love of the Lord, the whole world beseeches and whines at his door. (410)

column2: ਬਾਜਤ ਨੀਸਾਨ ਸੁਨੀਅਤ ਚਹੂੰ ਓਰ ਜੈਸੇ; ਉਦਤ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ ਭਾਨ ਦੁਰੈ ਨ ਦੁਰਾਏ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as the beat of the drum is heard on all four sides (its sound cannot be hidden) and when the supreme celestial body-sun rises, its light cannot be concealed;

column2: ਦੀਪਕ ਸੈ ਦਾਵਾ ਭਏ ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਜਾਨੈ; ਘਟਿਕਾ ਮੈ ਸਿੰਧ ਜੈਸੇ ਛਿਪੈ ਨ ਛਪਾਏ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as the whole world knows that light emanates from a lamp, and the ocean cannot be contained in a small earthenware pitcher;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚਕ੍ਰਵੈ ਨ ਛਾਨੋ ਰਹਿਤ ਸਿੰਘਾਸਨ ਪੈ; ਦੇਸ ਮੈ ਦੁਹਾਈ ਫੇਰੇ ਮਿਟੇ ਨ ਮਿਟਾਏ ਸੈ॥
column5: Just as an emperor sitting on the throne of his mighty empire cannot remain hidden; he is known among the subjects of his kingdom and that glory and fame is hard to destroy;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੁ ਜਾਸੁ; ਗੁਪਤ ਨ ਰਹੈ ਮੋਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਿਤ ਉਪਜਾਏ ਸੈ॥੪੧੧॥
column5: Similarly, the Guru-oriented Sikh whose heart is enlightened by the love of the Lord and His meditation, cannot remain hidden. His silence gives him away. (411)

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਦੇਖਿ ਦੀਪਕ ਪਤੰਗ ਪਛਮ ਨੋ ਤਾਕੈ; ਜੀਵਨ ਜਨਮੁ ਕੁਲ ਲਾਛਨ ਲਗਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a moth sees a lit lamp and turns his face away from it, he defiles his life, birth and family.

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸੁਨਿ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਰਾਚੈ; ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਸੁਖ ਹੁਇ ਸਬਦ ਬੇਧੀ ਨ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Hearing the voice of musical instruments, if a deer ignores it and engrosses himself in some other thought, he may save his life but he can no more be known to belong to the family who love the music of Ghanda Herha, (an instrument to the sound of which d

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਜਲ ਸੈ ਨਿਕਸ ਮੀਨ ਸਰਜੀਵ ਰਹੈ; ਸਹੈ ਦੁਖ ਦੂਖਿਨ ਬਿਰਹੁ ਬਿਲਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a fish stays alive after coming out of water, she will have to bear the ignominy of stigmatising its clan, whimper and suffer pangs for having separated from its beloved water.

column2: ਸੇਵਾ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਤਜੈ. ਭਜੈ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਕਉ; ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਦਵੀ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥੪੧੨॥
column5: Similarly, if a devoted Sikh renounces True Guru's service, His teachings and contemplation of His name, is engrossed in worldly quandary, he then cannot attain the status of an obedient disciple of the True Guru in the holy congregation of Guru. (412)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਚੀਟੀ ਪਾਛੈ ਕੋਟਿ ਚੀਟੀ ਚਲੀ ਜਾਤਿ; ਇਕ ਟਕ ਪਗ ਡਗਮਗਿ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as millions of ants follow the path blazed by an ant, walk on it very attentively without faltering a step;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੂੰਜ ਪਾਂਤਿ ਭਲੀ ਭਾਂਤਿ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਸਹਜ ਮੈ; ਉਡਤ ਅਕਾਸਚਾਰੀ ਆਗੈ ਅਗਵਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as cranes fly in a disciplined formation very cautiously in peace and patience and all of them are led by one crane;

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਮਾਲ ਚਾਲ ਚਲਤ ਟਲਤ ਨਾਹਿ; ਜਤ੍ਰ ਤਤ੍ਰ ਅਗ੍ਰਭਾਗੀ ਰਮਤ ਤਤ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a herd of deers never stagger from their sharp march following their leader and all proceed so very attentively,

column2: ਚੀਟੀ ਖਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਪਾਛੇ ਲਾਗੇ ਜਾਹਿ; ਪ੍ਰਾਨੀ ਗੁਰ ਪੰਥ ਛਾਡਿ ਚਲਿਤ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥੪੧੩॥
column5: Ants, cranes and deers keep following their leader, but supreme leader of all the species who leaves the well defined path of the True Guru, is surely a fool and a highly ignorant person. (413)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਸੰਗਮ ਸੁਜਸੁ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਬਖਾਨੈ; ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸਜਨੀ ਸਗਲ ਬਿਗਸਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife describes her union with her husband to her friends who feel pleased hearing the details;

column2: ਸਿਮਰਿ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਿਸਮ ਹੁਇ; ਸੋਭਾ ਦੇਤ ਮੋਨਿ ਗਹੇ ਮਨਿ ਮੁਸਕਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: She fantasizes her union and goes into a state of ecstasy thinking about it. She expresses the beauty of the moment in her silence;

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਅਧਾਨ ਪ੍ਰਸੂਤ ਸਮੈ ਰੁਦਨ ਸੈ; ਗੁਰ ਜਨ ਮੁਦਿਤ ਹੁਇ ਤਾਹੀ ਲਪਟਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: On completion of her pregnancy and at the time of giving birth to the child, she cries in labour pain and her whimper pleases the elderly ladies of the house who express their love for her;

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਭਗਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸੁ ਜਾਸੁ; ਬੋਲਤ ਬੈਰਾਗ ਮੋਨਿ ਸਬਹੁ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੧੪॥
column5: Similarly, a devoted Guru-conscious slave of the True Guru, whose heart is kindled with love of the Lord by virtue of his loving meditation and contemplation on Lord's name, speaks in a state of renunciation from the world. Although he observes silence mo

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਾਛੀ ਫਲ ਹੇਤ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਰਖ ਰੋਪੈ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਰਹੈ ਬਿਰਖੈ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a gardener plants saplings of many trees for obtaining fruits, but one which does not bear any fruit becomes useless.

column2: ਸੰਤਤਿ ਨਿਮਿਤ ਨ੍ਰਿਪ ਅਨਿਕ ਬਿਵਾਹ ਕਰੈ; ਸੰਤਤਿ ਬਿਹੂਨ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਛਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a king marries many women for obtaining a heir of his kingdom, but the queen who does not bear him a child is not liked by anyone in the family.

column2: ਬਿਦਿਆ ਦਾਨ ਜਾਨ ਜੈਸੇ ਪਾਂਧਾ ਚਟਸਾਰ ਜੋਰੈ; ਬਿਦਿਆ ਹੀਨ ਦੀਨ ਖਲ ਨਾਮ ਉਪਰਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a teacher opens a school but the child who remains illiterate is called lazy and foolish.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸਾਖਾ ਸੰਗ੍ਰਹੈ ਸੁਗਿਆਨ ਨਿਮਿਤ; ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਧ੍ਰਿਗ ਜਨਮ ਕਉ ਲਾਜ ਹੈ॥੪੧੫॥
column5: Similarly, True Guru holds a congregation of his disciples in order to impart them with supreme form of knowledge (Naam). But he who remains bereft of Guru's teachings, is worthy of condemnation and is a blotch on the human birth. (415)

column2: ਸੁਰਸਰੀ ਸੁਰਸਤੀ ਜਮੁਨਾ ਗੋਦਾਵਰੀ; ਗਇਆ ਪ੍ਰਾਗ ਸੇਤੁ ਕੁਰਖੇਤ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Rivers like Ganges, Saraswati, Jamuna, Godavari and places of pilgrimage like Gaya, Prayagraj, Rameshwram, Kurukshetra and Mansarover lakes are located in India.

column2: ਕਾਂਸੀ ਕਾਂਤੀ ਦੁਆਰਾਵਤੀ. ਮਾਇਆ ਮਥੁਰਾ ਅਜੁਧਿਆ; ਗੋਮਤੀ ਆਵੰਤਕਾ ਕੇਦਾਰ ਹਿਮਧਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: So are the holy cities of Kashi, Kanti, Dwarka, Mayapuri, Mathura, Ayodhya, Avantika and river Gomti. The temple of Kedarnath in the snow clad hills is a sacred place.

column2: ਨਰਬਦਾ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਨ ਦੇਵ ਸਥਲ ਕਵਲਾਸ; ਨੀਲ ਮੰਦਰਾਚਲ ਸੁਮੇਰ ਗਿਰਵਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Then river like Narmada, temples of gods, tapovans, Kailash, the abode of Shiva, Neel mountains, Mandrachal and Sumer are places worth going on a pilgrimage to.

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਅਰਥ ਸਤ ਧਰਮ ਦਇਆ ਸੰਤੋਖ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਰਜ ਤੁਲ ਨ ਸਗਰ ਹੈ॥੪੧੬॥
column5: To seek the virtues of Truth, contentment, benevolence and righteousness, the holy places are idolized and worshipped. But all these are not equal to even the dust of the lotus feet of the True Guru. (Taking refuge of Satguru is supreme of all these place

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੁਆਰ ਕੰਨਿਆ ਮਿਲਿ ਖੇਲਤ ਅਨੇਕ ਸਖੀ; ਸਗਲ ਕੋ ਏਕੈ ਦਿਨ ਹੋਤ ਨ ਬਿਵਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as many virgin maids assemble and play with each other but all of them are not married off on the same day.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬੀਰ ਖੇਤ ਬਿਖੈ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ ਸੁਭਟ ਜੇਤੇ; ਸਬੈ ਨ ਮਰਤ ਤੇਤੇ ਸਸਤ੍ਰਨ ਸਨਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as many warriors go to the battlefield fully armed and protected with armour coat do not die in the battlefield.

column2: ਬਾਵਨ ਸਮੀਪ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਏਕੈ ਬੇਰ ਚੰਦਨ ਕਰਤ ਹੈ ਨ ਤਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as there are many trees and plants around a grove of sandalwood trees, but all are not blessed with the fragrance of Sandalwood at once.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਜਾਤੁ ਹੈ ਜਗਤੁ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਪਦ ਚਾਹਤ ਹੈ ਜਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥੪੧੭॥
column5: Similarly, the whole world may go to the refuge of the True Guru but he alone attains the status of living emancipated who 'is liked by Him. (That particular disciple who serves Guru with faith and devotion). (417)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਗੁਆਰ ਗਾਇਨ ਚਰਾਵਤ ਜਤਨ ਬਨ; ਖੇਤ ਨ ਪਰਤ ਸਬੈ ਚਰਤ ਅਘਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as cowherd grazer grazes his cows very attentively in the jungle and does not let them wander into some fields, and they graze to their satisfaction.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜਾ ਧਰਮ ਸਰੂਪ ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿ ਬਿਖੈ; ਤਾ ਕੇ ਦੇਸ ਪਰਜਾ ਬਸਤ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a king who is righteous and just, his subjects live in peace and prosperity.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਹੋਤ ਖੇਵਟ ਚੇਤੰਨਿ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਜਾ ਮੈ; ਲਾਗੈ ਨਿਰਬਿਘਨ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਪਾਰਿ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a sailor is very alert and conscious of his duties, that ship touches the yonder shore without any adverse happenings.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਉਨਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਜੋਤਿ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਕਰੈ ਸਿਖ ਸਮਝਾਇ ਕੈ॥੪੧੮॥
column5: Similarly, the True Guru who has merged with the Light divine of the Lord, like warp and weft of a cloth, alone can make a disciple living emancipated with His teachings. (418)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਾਉ ਘਾਇਲ ਕੋ ਜਤਨ ਕੈ ਨੀਕੋ ਹੋਤ; ਪੀਰ ਮਿਟਿ ਜਾਇ ਲੀਕ ਮਿਟਤ ਨ ਪੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: Just as the wound gets cured with medicine and the pain also vanishes, but the scar of the wound is never seen to vanish.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਫਾਟੋ ਅੰਬਰੋ ਸੀਆਇ ਪੁਨਿ ਓਢੀਅਤ; ਨਾਗੋ ਤਉ ਨ ਹੋਇ ਤਊ ਥੇਗਰੀ ਪਰੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a torn cloth stitched and worn does not bare the body but the seam of the stitch is visible and conspicuous.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਟੂਟੋ ਬਾਸਨੁ ਸਵਾਰ ਦੇਤ ਹੈ ਠਠੇਰੋ; ਗਿਰਤ ਨ ਪਾਨੀ ਪੈ ਗਠੀਲੋ ਭੇਖ ਭੇਖੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a broken utensil is repaired by coppersmith and even the water does not leak from it, but it is repaired form stays.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨਿ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਦੁਖ ਦੇਖਿ ਪੁਨਿ; ਸਰਨ ਗਹੈ ਪੁਨੀਤ ਪੈ ਕਲੰਕ ਲੇਖ ਲੇਖੀਐ॥੪੧੯॥
column5: Similarly, a disciple who has turned away from the holy feet of the True Guru comes back to the Guru's refuge when he feels the pain of his actions. Although he is freed of his sins and becomes pious, yet the .blemish of his apostasy remains. (419)

column2: ਦੇਖਿ ਦੇਖਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਦਰਸ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ; ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਨ ਜਾਨਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Seeing many colourful festivities with eyes, an ignorant person could not appreciate the glory of the glimpse of the True Guru. He did not even learn the importance of Naam Simran, having heard praises and slanders all the times.

column2: ਗਾਇ ਗਾਇ ਗੰਮਿਤਾ ਗੁਨ ਗਨ ਗੁਨਿਨਿਧਾਨ; ਹਸਿ ਹਸਿ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਕੌ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪੁ ਨ ਪਛਾਨਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Singing praises of worldly things and people day and night, he did not reach the ocean of virtues-the True Guru. He wasted his time in idle talks and laughs but did not recognise the wondrous love of the True Lord.

column2: ਰੋਇ ਰੋਇ ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਕੋ ਨ ਸੋਗੁ ਜਾਨਿਓ; ਮਨ ਗਹਿ ਗਹਿ ਮਨ ਮੁਘਦੁ ਨਾ ਮਾਨਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Wailing and crying for maya, he spent his life-time but never felt the pangs of separation of the True Guru. The mind remained engrossed in the worldly affairs but it was foolish enough not to take the refuge of the True Guru.

column2: ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਕੈ ਨ ਜਾਨਿ ਸਕਿਓ; ਜਨਮ ਜੀਵਨ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਬਿਮੁਖ ਬਿਹਾਨਿਓ ਹੈ॥੪੨੦॥
column5: Engrossed in the shallow prattles and ritualistic knowledge of Vedas and Shastras, the foolish being could not know the supreme knowledge of the True Guru. The birth and lifetime of such a person is worthy of condemnation that he has spent as a renegade f

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਮਨਿ ਕੋ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ ਵਾਰਉ; ਸਸੀਅਰ ਸੂਰ ਕੋਟਿ ਕੋਟਿਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: Shine of millions and millions of gems and pearls, light of countless Suns and Moons, are paltry and worthy of sacrifice over the obedient Sikh whose forehead is able to kiss the dust of the feet of the True Guru.

column2: ਕੋਟਿਨ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਭਾਗਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਛਬਿ; ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਹੈ ਸੁਜਸ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: The glory of millions of fortunate people and the glow of supreme honour is trivial before the beautiful sheen of the forehead that has acquired the dust of the feet of the True Guru.

column2: ਸਿਵ ਸਨਕਾਦਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾਦਿਕ ਮਨੋਰਥ ਕੈ; ਤੀਰਥ ਕੋਟਾਨਿ ਕੋਟਿ ਬਾਂਛਤ ਹੈ ਤਾਸ ਜੀ॥
column5: Shiv Ji, the four sons of Brahma (Sanak etc.), Brahma himself, that is the three prime gods of Hindu pantheon crave for the glorious dust of the True Guru's feet. Countless places of pilgrimage also long for this dust.

column2: ਮਸਤਕਿ ਦਰਸਨ ਸੋਭਾ ਕੋ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਬੋਧਿ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰੂ ਚਰਨ ਰਜ ਮਾਤ੍ਰ ਲਾਗੈ ਜਾਸ ਜੀ॥੪੨੧॥
column5: The forehead that acquires a small amount of dust of the lotus feet of the True Guru, the glory of his glimpse is beyond description. (421)

column2: ਸਵੈਯਾ॥ ਖਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਮੀਨ ਪਤੰਗ ਚਰਾਚਰ; ਜੋਨਿ ਅਨੇਕ ਬਿਖੈ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਆਇਓ॥
column5: Swaiye: A living being has wandered in many species of birds, animals, fish, insects, root and conscious beings.

column2: ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਪਾਇ ਰਸਾਤਲ ਭੂਤਲ; ਦੇਵਪੁਰੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਲਉ ਬਹੁ ਧਾਇਓ॥
column5: He wandered in the nether regions, earth and heaven in order to practice whatever sermons he had heard.

column2: ਜੋਗ ਹੂ ਭੋਗ ਦੁਖਾਦਿ ਸੁਖਾਦਿਕ; ਧਰਮ ਅਧਰਮ ਸੁਕਰਮ ਕਮਾਇਓ॥
column5: He kept performing good and bad deeds bearing the comforts and sufferings of various practices of Yoga.

column2: ਹਾਰਿ ਪਰਿਓ ਸਰਨਾਗਤਿ ਆਇ; ਗੁਰੂ ਮੁਖ ਦੇਖ ਗਰੂ ਸੁਖ ਪਾਇਓ॥੪੨੨॥
column5: He got tired going through these countless rigours of many births and then comes to the refuge of the True Guru. By adopting and accepting the teachings of the True Guru and beholding His glimpse, he is able to achieve the great spiritual comfort and peac

column2: ਕਬਿਤ॥ ਚਾਹਿ ਚਾਹਿ ਚੰਦ੍ਰ ਮੁਖ ਚਾਇ ਕੈ ਚਕੋਰ ਚਖਿ; ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕਿਰਨ ਅਚਵਤ ਨ ਅਘਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as Allectoris graeca (chakor) longs for the moon because of the eyes that keep seeing it and is never satiated drinking the nectar-like rays, so does a devoted Sikh of the Guru is never satiated with the glimpse of the True Guru.

column2: ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਅਨਹਦ ਸਬਦ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ; ਆਨੰਦੁ ਉਦੋਤ ਕਰਿ ਸਾਂਤਿ ਨ ਸਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a deer engrossed by hearing the melodious tune of the musical instrument called Ghanda Herha, but is never satiated hearing it. So is a devoted Sikh never satiated hearing the melody of the unstruck music of Naam Amrit.

column2: ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸਾਲ ਜਸੁ ਜੰਪਤ ਬਾਸੁਰ ਨਿਸ; ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the rain-bird is never tired of crying for the nectar like Swati drop day and night, similarly the tongue of a devoted and obedient disciple of Guru is never tired of repeatedly uttering the ambrosial Naam of the Lord.

column2: ਦੇਖਤ ਸੁਨਤ ਅਰੁ ਗਾਵਤ ਪਾਵਤ ਸੁਖ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਬਸ ਮਨ ਮਗਨ ਹਿਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੪੨੩॥
column5: Like Allectoris graeca, deer and rain-bird, the indescribable celestial happiness that he gets by the vision of the True Guru, hearing the melodious unstruck sound and singing the praises of the Lord Almighty, he remains in a state of ecstasy .

column2: ਸਲਿਲ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਜੈਸੇ ਮੀਨ ਕੀ ਨ ਘਟੈ ਰੁਚਿ; ਦੀਪਕ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਘਟੈ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਨ ਪਤੰਗ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as penchant for water for a fish never decreases and a moth's love for the flame of an oil lamp never wanes.

column2: ਕੁਸਮ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਜੈਸੋ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਨ ਮਧੁਪ ਕਉ; ਉਡਤ ਅਕਾਸ ਆਸ ਘਟੈ ਨ ਬਿਹੰਗ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a black bee is never satiated enjoying the fragrance of the flowers, a bird's desire to fly in sky never decreases.

column2: ਘਟਾ ਘਨਘੋਰ ਮੋਰ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਰਿਦੈ ਉਲਾਸ; ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸੁਨਿ ਰਤਿ ਘਟੈ ਨ ਕੁਰੰਗ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as hearing the thunder of the collected clouds gladens the heart of a peacock and the rain-bird, and love of a deer for hearing the sweet music of Chanda Herha does not diminish.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸਾਲ ਸੰਤ; ਘਟਤ ਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਕੀ॥੪੨੪॥
column5: So is the love of a Guru-conscious saint, the seeker of ambrosial nectar for his dear True Guru. The longing of love for his Guru that has permeated in every limb of his body and is flowing rapidly never decreases. (424)

column2: ਸਲਿਲ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਦੇਖੋ ਬੋਰਤ ਨ ਕਾਸਟਹਿ; ਲਾਜ ਗਹੈ ਕਹੈ ਅਪਨੋ ਹੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Look at water, the nature of it never drowns wood in it. It regards the wood as his own having brought it up by irrigating it and thus keeps the shame of this relationship.

column2: ਜੁਗਵਤ ਕਾਸਟ ਰਿਦੰਤਰਿ ਬੈਸੰਤਰਹਿ; ਬੈਸੰਤਰ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਲੈ ਕਾਸਟਿ ਪ੍ਰਜਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: Wood keeps fire in it latently but taking the wood in itself the fire burns it (wood) to ashes.

column2: ਅਗਰਹਿ ਜਲ ਬੋਰਿ ਕਾਢੈ ਬਾਢੈ ਮੋਲ ਤਾ ਕੋ; ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਦਗਧ ਕੈ ਅਧਿਕ ਅਉਟਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: The wood of Gularia Agalocha (Agar) resurfaces in water after sinking for sometime. This sinking increases the value of the wood. For burning it well in fire, it is boiled in water.

column2: ਤਊ ਤਾ ਕੋ ਰੁਧਿਰ ਚੁਇ ਚੋਆ ਹੋਇ ਸਲਿਲ ਮਿਲ; ਅਉਗਨਹਿ ਗੁਨ ਮਾਨੈ ਬਿਰਦੁ ਬੀਚਾਰਿਓ ਹੈ॥੪੨੫॥
column5: Then its essence mixes well in water that becomes sweet smelling. For extracting the essence of the wood, the water has to bear the heat of the fire. But for its calm and tolerant nature, water changes its demerits into merits and thus fulfils its duties

column2: ਸਲਿਲ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਵਨ ਗਵਨ ਗੁਨ; ਸੀਚੀਅਤ ਉਪਬਨ ਬਿਰਵਾ ਲਗਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as the nature of water is to flow downward, and that enables it to irrigate the plants and saplings planted in the garden,

column2: ਜਲਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਿਰਖਹਿ ਕਰਤ ਉਰਧ ਤਪ; ਸਾਖਾ ਨਏ ਸਫਲ ਹੁਇ ਝੁਕ ਰਹੈ ਆਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: On meeting with water, even the tree goes through the rigours of penance by standing erect and with new branches sprouting and fruit appearing, it bows downward, (its union with water makes it humble).

column2: ਪਾਹਨ ਹਨਤ ਫਲਦਾਈ. ਕਾਟੇ ਹੋਇ ਨਉਕਾ; ਲੋਸਟ ਕੈ ਛੇਦੈ ਭੇਦੇ ਬੰਧਨ ਬੰਧਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: Having acquired humility with its association with water, it yields fruit to even those who throw stones at it. When cut, a boat is made of its wood which takes people from one bank of the river to the other. The wood is first cut with steel and then nail

column2: ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਸੁਤ ਸਤ੍ਰੁ ਗਹਿ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰੈ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਦੋਖੀ ਤਾਰੈ ਸਮਝਾਇ ਕੈ॥੪੨੬॥
column5: The rapid flow of water brings the wood, its reared son along with its enemy (iron) and takes it across to the other bank. Like the humble and philanthropic nature of water, the True Guru does not deliberate on the vices of the slanderers of the Guru's Si

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਕਰਿ. ਭੈ ਭਵਨ; ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਚਾਇ ਕੈ ਚਈਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Guru-conscious persons imbibe the teachings of the Guru in their hearts. They maintain utmost devotion and love for the Lord in this fearsome world. They remain in a state of bliss by their faith in loving worship and live life enthusiastically.

column2: ਸੰਗਮ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ. ਸਹਜ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਸਾਧਿ; ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ ਰਸਿਕ ਰਸੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Enjoying the bliss of the union with God-like Guru and absorbed in a state of inactivity spiritually, they acquire the loving elixir of Naam from the True Guru and are ever engrossed in its practice.

column2: ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਟੇਕ ਏਕ ਅਉ ਅਨੇਕ ਲਿਵ; ਬਿਮਲ ਬੈਰਾਗ ਫਬਿ ਛਬਿ ਕੈ ਛਬੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: By virtue of refuge, knowledge received from God-like True Guru, their consciousness remains absorbed in the Omni permeated Lord. Due to supreme adornment of unblemished feelings of separation, they look glorious and graceful.

column2: ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਗਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਅਸਚਰਜਮੈ; ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਦੇਹ ਉਨਮਨ ਉਨਮੀਲੇ ਹੈ॥੪੨੭॥
column5: Their state is unique and astonishing. In this amazing condition, they are beyond the attractions of body relishments and remain in a blooming state of bliss. (427)

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਕਰਿ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਕਾਮਾਰਥੀ ਕਰਮ ਕੀਨੇ; ਪੂਰਨ ਮਨੋਰਥ ਭਇਓ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਾਮ ਕੋ॥
column5: So long as a human being performed deeds to fulfill his desires or with some aim in mind, neither his performed actions achieved anything nor any of his resolutions bore fruit.

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਕਰਿ ਆਸਾ ਆਸਵੰਤ ਹੁਇ ਆਸਰੋ ਗਹਿਓ; ਬਹਿਓ ਫਿਰਿਓ ਠੌਰ ਠੌਰ ਪਾਇਓ ਨ ਬਿਸ੍ਰਾਮ ਕੋ॥
column5: So long a human being remained dependent on others for fulfillment of his desires, he wandered from pillar to post without respite from anywhere.

column2: ਜਉ ਲਉ ਕਰਿ ਮਮਤਾ ਮਮਤ ਮੂੰਡ ਬੋਝ ਲੀਨੋ; ਦੀਨੋ ਡੰਡ ਖੰਡ ਖੰਡ ਖੇਦ ਠਾਮ ਠਾਮ ਕੋ॥
column5: So long a human being carried the load of I, my, me and yours under the influence of attachment with worldly goods and relations, he kept roving in distress from one place to the other.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਅਉ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਭਏ; ਨਿਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਖ ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਨਾਮ ਕੋ॥੪੨੮॥
column5: One can become unattached and free from all worldly allurements only by taking the refuge of the True Guru and practicing His sermon of Naam Simran that helps one achieve spiritual high, comfort of equipoise and humility. (428)

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਜ; ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੁਇ ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਲਪਟਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The mind of a devoted Sikh is ever entangled in the sweet smelling dust of the lotus feet of the Lord like a bumble bee. (He is ever engrossed in practicing meditation on Lord's name).

column2: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੁਇ; ਅਤਿ ਉਨਮਤਿ ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਬਿਸਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: He is ever longing to relish the Naam-elixir day and night. In its bliss and ecstasy, he ignores all other worldly awarenesses, allurements and knowledge.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਸਨੇਹ ਗੇਹ ਬਿਸਮ ਬਿਦੇਹ ਰੂਪ; ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤ ਬੂੰਦ ਗਤਿ ਸੀਪ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such a devoted Sikh's mind then lovingly resides in the holy feet of the Lord. He is free of all body desires. Like the Swati drop of rain falling on an oyster, he also is enclosed in the box of Lord's holy feet.

column2: ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਕਟਾਛ ਕਰਿ; ਮੁਕਤਾ ਮਹਾਂਤ ਹੁਇ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਠਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੪੨੯॥
column5: Engrossed in the refuge of ocean of peace-The True Guru, and by His grace, he too becomes an invaluable and an unique pearl like the pearl of the oyster. (429)

column2: ਰੋਮ ਰੋਮ ਕੋਟਿ ਮੁਖ ਮੁਖ ਰਸਨਾ ਅਨੰਤ; ਅਨਿਕ ਮਨੰਤਰ ਲਉ ਕਹਤ ਨ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: If every hair of the body is blessed with millions of mouths and each mouth has numerous tongues, even then the glorious state of the person who relishes Lord's name with them cannot be described over the eons.

column2: ਕੋਟਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਭਾਰ ਡਾਰ ਤੁਲਾਧਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਤੋਲੀਐ ਜਉ ਬਾਰਿ ਬਾਰਿ ਤੋਲ ਨ ਸਮਾਵਈ॥
column5: If we weigh the load of millions of universes with the spiritual bliss time and again, the great comfort and peace cannot be measured.

column2: ਚਤੁਰ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਅਉ ਸਾਗਰ ਸਮੂਹ ਸੁਖ; ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬੈਕੁੰਠ ਮੋਲ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: All the worldly treasures, seas full of pearls and numerous pleasures of heaven are virtually nothing compared to the glory and grandeur of reciting His name.

column2: ਸਮਝ ਨ ਪਰੈ ਕਰੈ ਕਉਨ ਗਉਨ ਭਉਨ ਮਨ; ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਨਾਵਈ॥੪੩੦॥
column5: The fortunate devout who is blessed with the consecration of Naam by the True Guru, how high a spiritual state can his mind get absorbed in? No one is capable of expressing and describing this condition. (430)

column2: ਲੋਚਨ ਪਤੰਗ ਦੀਪ ਦਰਸ ਦੇਖਨ ਗਏ; ਜੋਤੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਪੁਨਿ ਊਤਰ ਨ ਆਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The eyes of a moth who go for a glimpse of the flame of the lamp are never able to come back having engrossed themselves in its light. (So are the devoted disciples of the True Guru who are never able to return after a vision of Him).

column2: ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸੁਨਬੇ ਕਉ ਸ੍ਰਵਨ ਹਰਨ ਗਏ; ਸੁਨਿ ਧੁਨਿ ਥਕਤ ਭਏ ਨ ਬਹੁਰਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: The ears of a deer gone to hear the melody of Ghanda Herha (a music instrument) gets so engrossed that he is never able to return. (So are the ears of a Sikh gone to hear ambrosial utterances of his True Guru never want to leave Him)

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸਿ ਰਸਿਕ ਹੁਇ; ਮਨ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Embellished with the sweet smelling dust of the lotus feet of the True Guru, the mind of an obedient disciple gets engrossed like the black bee entranced by the sweet smell of the flower.

column2: ਰੂਪ ਗੁਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਪੂਰਨ ਪਰਮਪਦ; ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਰਸ ਭਰਮਿ ਭੁਲਾਨੇ ਹੈ॥੪੩੧॥
column5: By virtue of the loving merits of the Naam blessed by the radiant True Guru, a Sikh of the Guru attains supreme spiritual state and rejects all other worldly contemplations and awarenesses that put one in wandering of doubts. (431)

column2: ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਹੀ ਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਹਾਨਿ ਕੈ ਪਤੰਗ ਬਿਧਿ; ਪਾਛੈ ਕੈ ਅਨੂਪ ਰੂਪ ਦੀਪਕ ਦਿਖਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like a moth, an obedient human being of the Guru regards all other concentrations of mind as loss incurring proposition and then, like seeing the light of the lamp (by the moth), he sees the beautiful sight of the True Guru.

column2: ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਹੀ ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਬਿਸਰਜਿ ਕੈ; ਅਨਹਦ ਨਾਦ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਸੁਨਾਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a deer discards all other sounds in favour of the melody of Chanda Herha, a disciple of the Guru listens to the sound of unstruck music after obtaining and practicing on Guru's teachings and words.

column2: ਪ੍ਰਥਮ ਹੀ ਬਚਨ ਰਚਨ ਹਰਿ ਗੁੰਗ ਸਾਜਿ; ਪਾਛੈ ਕੈ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਰਸ ਅਪਿਓ ਪੀਆਏ ਹੈ॥
column5: Like a black bee, giving up its noisy stance and engulfing itself in the fragrance of the lotus-like feet of the Guru, he drinks deep the wondrous elixir of the Naam.

column2: ਪੇਖ ਸੁਨ ਅਚਵਤ ਹੀ ਭਏ ਬਿਸਮ ਅਤਿ; ਪਰਮਦਭੁਤ ਅਸਚਰਜ ਸਮਾਏ ਹੈ॥੪੩੨॥
column5: And thus a devoted Sikh of the Guru, seeing the vision of his Guru, hearing the sweet sound of Guru's words and relishing the Naam Amrit (elixir-like name of the Lord) reaches a high state of bliss and merges in the astonishing and supremely strange God.

column2: ਜਾਤਿ ਸਿਹਿਜਾਸਨ ਜਉ ਕਾਮਨੀ ਜਾਮਨੀ ਸਮੈ; ਗੁਰਜਨ ਸੁਜਨ ਕੀ ਬਾਤ ਨ ਸੁਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: When a wife proceeds to enjoy the union of her husband in his bed at night, no talk of any noble, elderly or holy person appeals to her.

column2: ਹਿਮਕਰ ਉਦਿਤ ਮੁਦਿਤ ਹੈ ਚਕੋਰ ਚਿਤਿ; ਇਕ ਟਕ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ ਸਮਾਰਤ ਨ ਗਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: As the moon rises, the ruddy sheldrake is immensely pleased and gazing at it with concentration of mind, is unaware of his own body even.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਧੁਕਰ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਰਸ ਲੁਭਿਤ ਹੈ; ਬਿਸਮ ਕਮਲ ਦਲ ਸੰਪਟ ਸਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a bumble bee is so engrossed in the sweet smelling nectar of the flower, that it gets trapped in the box-like lotus flower when the Sun sets.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਤਿ ਸਿਖ; ਦਰਸ ਪਰਸ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਮੁਸਕਾਤਿ ਹੈ॥੪੩੩॥
column5: Similarly a devoted slave disciple goes to the refuge of the holy feet of the True Guru; enjoying His sight and entranced in His love, he keeps smiling within while relishing the divine spectacle. (433)

column2: ਆਵਤ ਹੈ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਭੀਖ ਮਾਗਨਿ ਭਿਖਾਰੀ ਦੀਨ; ਦੇਖਤ ਅਧੀਨਹਿ ਨਿਰਾਸੋ ਨ ਬਿਡਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Whosoever is visited by a beggar for alms, impressed by his humility, the donor never turns him away disappointed.

column2: ਬੈਠਤ ਹੈ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਦੁਆਰ ਆਸਾ ਕੈ ਬਿਡਾਰ ਸ੍ਵਾਨ; ਅੰਤ ਕਰੁਨਾ ਕੈ ਤੋਰਿ ਟੂਕਿ ਤਾਹਿ ਡਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Whosoever has a dog coming to his door after discarding all other alternatives, the master of the house out of clemency serves him with a morsel of food.

column2: ਪਾਇਨ ਕੀ ਪਨਹੀ ਰਹਤ ਪਰਹਰੀ ਪਰੀ; ਤਾਹੂ ਕਾਹੂ ਕਾਜਿ ਉਠਿ ਚਲਤ ਸਮਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A shoe keeps lying unattended and uncared, but when its owner has to go out on some work, he too takes care of it then and uses it.

column2: ਛਾਡਿ ਅਹੰਕਾਰ ਛਾਰ ਹੋਇ ਗੁਰ ਮਾਰਗ ਮੈ; ਕਬਹੂ ਕੈ ਦਇਆ ਕੈ ਦਇਆਲ ਪਗਿ ਧਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥੪੩੪॥
column5: Similarly, he who ever discards his ego and pride and lives in the refuge of the True Guru in utter humility like the dust of His feet, the clement True Guru will definitely shower His benevolence one day and attach him with His feet (He blesses him with

column2: ਦ੍ਰੋਪਤੀ ਕੁਪੀਨ ਮਾਤ੍ਰ ਦਈ ਜਉ ਮੁਨੀਸਰਹਿ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਸਭਾ ਮਧਿ ਬਹਿਓ ਬਸਨ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Daropadi gave away a piece of cloth from her head-covering scarf to a sage Durbasha whose loin cloth had been washed away in the river. As a result, when efforts were made to strip her off in the court of Duryodhan, the cloth length coming off her body wo

column2: ਤਨਕ ਤੰਦੁਲ ਜਗਦੀਸਹਿ ਦਏ ਸੁਦਾਮਾ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਪਾਏ ਚਤੁਰ ਪਦਾਰਥ ਅਥਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: Sudama offered a handful of rice to Krishna Ji, with utmost love and in return, he attained the four aims of life as well as many other treasure-houses of His blessings.

column2: ਦੁਖਿਤ ਗਜਿੰਦ ਅਰਬਿੰਦ ਗਹਿ ਭੇਟ ਰਾਖੈ; ਤਾਂ ਕੈ ਕਾਜੈ ਚਕ੍ਰਪਾਨਿ ਆਨਿ ਗ੍ਰਸੇ ਗ੍ਰਾਹ ਜੀ॥
column5: A distressed elephant caught by an octopus, plucked a lotus flower in desperation and offered it to the Lord in humble supplication. He (elephant) was freed from the clutches of the octopus.

column2: ਕਹਾਂ ਕੋਊ ਕਰੈ ਕਛੂ ਹੋਤ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕੇ ਕੀਏ; ਜਾ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਮਾਨਿ ਲੇਹਿ ਸਭੈ ਸੁਖ ਤਾਹਿ ਜੀ॥੪੩੫॥
column5: What can one do with his own efforts ? Nothing tangible can be achieved by one's own efforts. All this is His blessing. One whose hard work and devotion is accepted by the Lord, gets all peace and comforts from Him. (435)

column2: ਸਰਵਨ ਸੇਵਾ ਕੀਨੀ ਮਾਤ ਪਿਤਾ ਕੀ ਬਿਸੇਖ; ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਗਾਈਅਤ ਜਸੁ ਜਗਤ ਮੈ ਤਾਹੂ ਕੋ॥
column5: Sarwan, the devoted son served his blind parents with love and dedication that has earned him fame and praise in the world.

column2: ਜਨ ਪ੍ਰਹਿਲਾਦਿ ਆਦਿ ਅੰਤ ਲਉ ਅਵਗਿਆ ਕੀਨੀ; ਤਾਤ ਘਾਤ ਕਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਪ੍ਰਨੁ ਵਾਹੂ ਕੋ॥
column5: What a strange play it is of the Lord that instead of serving his father, Bhagat Prahlad disobeyed his father's order that demanded of him not to meditate on the name of God (Ram). The Lord destroyed Harnakash (Prahlad's father) and protected Prahlad thus

column2: ਦੁਆਦਸ ਬਰਖ ਸੁਕ ਜਨਨੀ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਕਰੀ; ਸਿਧ ਭਏ ਤਤਖਿਨ ਜਨਮੁ ਹੈ ਜਾਹੂ ਕੋ॥
column5: It is said that sage Sukdev kept on causing pain to his mother by remaining in her womb for 12 years, but when born he was found to be an established and perfect sage, and all those born at that time turned out to be hermit with divine powers.

column2: ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬਿਸਮ ਜਾਨੀਐ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਕਛੁ; ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੁ ਆਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕੋ॥੪੩੬॥
column5: His mysterious play is beyond explanation and is astonishing. No one can know on whom will He be kind when and where and who will receive His blessings. (436)

column2: ਖਾਂਡ ਖਾਂਡ ਕਹੈ ਜਿਹਬਾ ਨ ਸ੍ਵਾਦ ਮੀਠੋ ਆਵੈ; ਅਗਨਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਕਹੈ ਸੀਤ ਨ ਬਿਨਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: No action but repeated utterances are futile. Repeatedly saying sugar, the tongue is unable to experience sweet taste, nor shivering with cold can stop by saying fire! fire!

column2: ਬੈਦ ਬੈਦ ਕਹੈ ਰੋਗ ਮਿਟਤ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਕੋ; ਦਰਬ ਦਰਬ ਕਹੈ ਕੋਊ ਦਰਬਹਿ ਨ ਬਿਲਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: No ailment can be cured by repeated utterance of doctor! doctor! nor can anyone enjoy the luxuries that money buys just by saying money! money!

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਚੰਦਨ ਕਹਤ ਪ੍ਰਗਟੈ ਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਬਾਸੁ; ਚੰਦੁ ਚੰਦੁ ਕਹੈ ਉਜਿਆਰੋ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as saying sandalwood! sandalwood, the fragrance of sandalwood cannot spread, nor can the radiance of the moon-light be experienced by repeatedly saying moon! moon! unless the moon rises.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੋਸਟਿ ਕਹਤ ਨ ਰਹਤ ਪਾਵੈ; ਕਰਨੀ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ ਭਾਨ ਉਦਤਿ ਅਕਾਸ ਹੈ॥੪੩੭॥
column5: Similarly, just listening to the holy sermons and discourses, no one can acquire the divine life-style and code of conduct. The most fundamental need is to practice the lessons in actual life. So by the practice of the Guru's blessed Naam Simran, the ligh

column2: ਹਸਤ ਹਸਤ ਪੂਛੈ ਹਸਿ ਹਸਿ ਕੈ ਹਸਾਇ; ਰੋਵਤ ਰੋਵਤ ਪੂਛੈ ਰੋਇ ਅਉ ਰੁਵਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: A laughing person merrily asks a happy and laughing person various things that can make them laugh. Similarly a crying person asks another crying person matters that causes crying.

column2: ਬੈਠੇ ਬੈਠੇ ਪੂਛੈ ਬੈਠਿ ਬੈਠਿ ਕੈ ਨਿਕਟ ਜਾਇ; ਚਾਲਤ ਚਲਤ ਪੂਛੈ ਦਹਦਿਸਿ ਧਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: A settled person will share with another settled person means of settling down. A person treading a path will ask another on the right path, things that would lead one to the right path.

column2: ਲੋਗ ਪੂਛੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ ਬੇਦ ਪੂਛੈ ਬੇਦ ਬਿਧਿ; ਜੋਗੀ ਭੋਗੀ ਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਜੁਗਾਇ ਕੈ॥
column5: A worldly person asks the other worldly persons various aspects of worldly affairs. One who studies Vedas would ask about Vedas from another who has the knowledge of Vedas.

column2: ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਕਾਹੂ ਨ ਮਿਟਾਇ ਸਾਕਿਓ; ਨਿਹਚਲ ਭਏ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਮਾਇ ਕੈ॥੪੩੮॥
column5: All the above do satiate the addiction of a person, but no one has been able to end the birth-death cycle of anyone by such prattles. Those who unite their attention in the holy feet of the Lord, only those obedient disciples of the Guru are able to end t

column2: ਪੂਛਤ ਪਥਿਕ ਤਿਹ ਮਾਰਗਿ ਨ ਧਾਰੈ ਪਗੁ; ਪ੍ਰੀਤਮ ਕੇ ਦੇਸ ਕੈਸੇ ਬਾਤਨ ਕੈ ਜਾਈਐ॥
column5: One asks a wayfarer of the beloved Lord's abode, the path to Him but does not tread even a step on it. Without launching oneself on that path, how can one reach the abode of the beloved Lord by mere prattles?

column2: ਪੂਛਤ ਹੈ ਬੈਦ ਖਾਤ ਅਉਖਧਿ ਨ ਸੰਜਮ ਸੈ; ਕੈਸੇ ਮਿਟੈ ਰੋਗ ਸੁਖ ਸਹਜਿ ਸਮਾਈਐ॥
column5: One asks the physician the True Guru, the medicine of curing the malady of ego, but does not consume the medicine with dedicated discipline and precautions. Then how can the ailment of ego be cured and spiritual peace acquired.

column2: ਪੂਛਤ ਸੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਕਰਮ ਹੈ ਦੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਕੈ; ਰਿਦੈ ਬਿਭਚਾਰ ਕਤ ਸਿਹਜਾ ਬੁਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: One asks from the dear and beloveds of the Lord husband the way of meeting Him, but all her actions and deeds are like of wretched and discarded women. Then how can such a seeker wife with deceitful heart ever be called to the nuptial bed of the husband L

column2: ਗਾਏ ਸੁਨੈ ਆਂਖੇ ਮੀਚੈ ਪਾਈਐ ਨ ਪਰਮਪਦੁ; ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸੁ ਗਹਿ ਜਉ ਲਉ ਨ ਕਮਾਈਐ॥੪੩੯॥
column5: Similarly without inhabiting the Lord in the heart, singing the praises, listening to His discourses and closing eyes for the beloved Lord cannot reach one to the higher spiritual state. Reaffirming of Guru's sermons in the heart and practicing them perpe

column2: ਖੋਜੀ ਖੋਜਿ ਦੇਖਿ ਚਲਿਓ ਜਾਇ ਪਹੁਚੇ ਠਿਕਾਨੇ; ਆਲਸ ਬਿਲੰਬ ਕੀਏ ਖੋਜ ਮਿਟ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a tracker proceeds along the footprints and reaches the desired place, but had he been lazy or complacent, these footprints trail would have been obliterated.

column2: ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸਮੈ ਰਮੈ ਭਰਤਾਰ ਬਰ ਨਾਰਿ ਸੋਈ; ਕਰੈ ਜਉ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਮਾਨੁ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਤ ਪ੍ਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a woman who moving to the bed of her husband at night is fortunate to enjoy the union with her husband is the prime wife of that man. But one who shows arrogance due to ignorance loses the opportunity of this union because of her laziness and comp

column2: ਬਰਖਤ ਮੇਘ ਜਲ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਪੀਏ; ਮੋਨ ਗਹੇ ਬਰਖਾ ਬਤੀਤੇ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a rain-bird may be able to quench his thirst when it is raining, but if he does not open his mouth and the rain stops, then he wails and cry.

column2: ਸਿਖ ਸੋਈ ਸੁਨਿ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਰਹਤ ਰਹੈ; ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਕੀਏ ਪਾਛੇ ਪਛੁਤਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੪੦॥
column5: Similarly, he alone is an obedient Sikh of the True Guru, who listens to His sermon and adopts it in his life immediately. (He commences practicing of Naam Simran immediately). Otherwise without inhabiting true love in the heart and demonstrating it outwa

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਛੁਰਾ ਬਿਛੁਰ ਪਰੈ ਆਨ ਗਾਇ ਥਨ; ਦੁਗਧ ਨ ਪਾਨ ਕਰੈ ਮਾਰਤ ਹੈ ਲਾਤ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a calf separated from his mother rushes to suck milk from another cow's teats, and he is denied sucking milk by the cow who kicks him away.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਹੰਸ ਆਨਸਰ ਜਾਤ; ਖਾਤ ਨ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਫਲ ਭੁਗਤ ਜੁ ਗਾਤ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a swan leaving Mansarover lake goes to some other lake cannot get his food of pearls to eat from there.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜ ਦੁਆਰ ਤਜਿ ਆਨ ਦੁਆਰ ਜਾਤ ਜਨ; ਹੋਤ ਮਾਨੁ ਭੰਗੁ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਬਾਤ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a guard on the door of the king leaves and serves on another's door, it hurts his pride and does not help his glory and grandeur anyway.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਕੀ ਸਰਨਿ ਜਾਹਿ; ਰਹਿਓ ਨ ਪਰਤ ਰਾਖਿ ਸਕਤ ਨ ਪਾਤਕੀ॥੪੪੧॥
column5: Similarly, if a devoted disciple of Guru leaves the refuge of his Guru and goes into the protection of other gods and goddesses, he cannot find his stay there worthwhile nor anyone would show any respect and regard towards him being a blemished sinner. (

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਨਘੋਰ ਮੋਰ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਸਨੇਹ ਗਤਿ; ਬਰਖਤ ਮੇਂਹ ਅਸਨੇਹ ਕੈ ਦਿਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a peacock's and rain-bird's love is confined to the thunder of the clouds and this love is visible only till the rain lasts. (Their love is not lasting.)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਕਮਲ ਜਲ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਦਿਸੰਤਰਿ ਹੁਇ; ਮਧੁਕਰ ਦਿਨਕਰ ਹੇਤ ਉਪਜਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a lotus flower closes at sunset but remains in water and the bumble bee keeps hovering over other flowers. But at sunrise when lotus flower opens up, its love for the lotus flower resurfaces. His love is not of permanent nature.

column2: ਦਾਦਰ ਨਿਰਾਦਰ ਹੁਇ ਜੀਅਤ ਪਵਨ ਭਖਿ; ਜਲ ਤਜਿ ਮਾਰਤ ਨ. ਪ੍ਰੇਮਹਿ ਲਜਾਵਈ॥
column5: A frog's love with water is very disrespecting. He comes out of water to breathe air. Out of water, it does not die. He thus shames his love for water.

column2: ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹੀ ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਹੈ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮੀਨ ਜਲ ਹੇਤ ਠਹਰਾਵਈ॥੪੪੨॥
column5: Similarly, a deceitful Sikh with demonstrative love is the follower of other gods and goddesses, whereas the love of a true and obedient Sikh for his True Guru is like fish and water. (He holds no love for anyone else other than the True Guru). (442)

column2: ਪੁਰਖ ਨਿਪੁੰਸਕ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਬਨਿਤਾ ਬਿਲਾਸ; ਬਾਂਝ ਕਹਾਂ ਜਾਨੈ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਤਤਿ ਸਨੇਹ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as an impotent person does not know what pleasure it is to share a union with a woman, and a barren woman cannot know the love and attachment of children.

column2: ਗਨਿਕਾ ਸੰਤਾਨ ਕੋ ਬਖਾਨ ਕਹਾ ਗੋਤਚਾਰ; ਨਾਹਿ ਉਪਚਾਰ ਕਛੁ ਕੁਸਟੀ ਕੀ ਦੇਹ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as the lineage of the children of a prostitute cannot be defined, and a leper cannot be cured anyway.

column2: ਆਂਧਰੋ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਨ ਦਸਨ ਛਬਿ; ਜਾਨਤ ਨ ਬਹਰੋ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਨ ਅਸਪ੍ਰੇਹ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as a blind person cannot know the beauty of the face and teeth of a woman and a deaf person cannot feel anyone's anger or happiness since he cannot hear.

column2: ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਜਵਾਸੋ ਨਹੀ ਚਾਹਤ ਹੈ ਮੇਹ ਕਉ॥੪੪੩॥
column5: Similarly, a devotee and follower of other gods and goddesses, cannot know the celestial bliss of service of true and perfect Guru. Just as camel-thorn (Alhagi maurorum) resents rain. (443)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਭੂਲਿ ਬਛੁਰਾ ਪਰਤ ਆਨ ਗਾਇ ਥਨ; ਬਹੁਰਿਓ ਮਿਲਤ ਮਾਤ ਬਾਤ ਨ ਸਮਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a calf goes to another cow for milk by mistake, and on coming back to his mother, she does not recall his mistake and feeds him.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਸਰ ਭ੍ਰਮਿ ਆਵੈ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਹੰਸ; ਦੇਤ ਮੁਕਤਾ ਅਮੋਲ ਦੋਖ ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a swan reaches Mansarover lake after wandering to various other lakes, Mansarover lake does not remind him his mistake and serves him with pearls.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨ੍ਰਿਪ ਸੇਵਕ ਜਉ ਆਨ ਦੁਆਰ ਹਾਰਿ ਆਵੈ; ਚਉਗਨੋ ਬਢਾਵੈ ਨ ਅਵਗਿਆ ਉਰਿਧਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a royal attendant, after wandering all over comes back to his master who does not recall him his departure and instead raises his status many time more.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਸਰਨਿ ਸਰਨਿ ਦਇਆਲ ਦੇਵ; ਸਿਖਨ ਕੋ ਭੂਲਿਬੋ ਨ ਰਿਦ ਮੈ ਨਿਹਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੪੪॥
column5: Similarly, the radiant and benevolent True Guru is the support of the destitute. He does not keep in mind the mistakes of those Sikhs who have separated themselves from the door of their Guru and are wandering on the door of gods and goddesses. (444)

column2: ਬਾਂਝ ਬਧੂ ਪੁਰਖੁ ਨਿਪੁੰਸਕ ਨ ਸੰਤਤਿ ਹੋਇ; ਸਲਿਲ ਬਿਲੋਏ ਕਤ ਮਾਖਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a barren woman and an impotent man cannot produce children, and churning of water cannot yield butter.

column2: ਫਨ ਗਹਿ ਦੁਗਧ ਪੀਆਏ ਨ ਮਿਟਤ ਬਿਖੁ; ਮੂਰੀ ਖਾਏ ਮੁਖ ਸੈ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਟੇ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a poison of a cobra cannot be destroyed by feeding him milk and one cannot get good smell from mouth after eating radish.

column2: ਮਾਨਸਰ ਪਰ ਬੈਠੇ ਬਾਇਸੁ ਉਦਾਸ ਬਾਸ; ਅਰਗਜਾ ਲੇਪੁ ਖਰ ਭਸਮ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a filth-eating crow on reaching lake Mansarover, becomes sad since he cannot get filth which he is so used to eating; and a donkey will roll in dust even if it is given a bath with sweet smelling scents.

column2: ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨ ਜਾਨੇ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਕਠਨ ਕੁਟੇਵ ਨ ਮਿਟਤ ਦੇਵ ਦਾਸ ਹੈ॥੪੪੫॥
column5: Similarly, the servant of other gods cannot realise the ecstasy of serving the True Guru, because the chronic and bad habits of the followers of god cannot perish. (445)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਗਗਨ ਘਟਾ ਘਮੰਡ ਬਿਲੋਕੀਅਤਿ; ਗਰਜਿ ਗਰਜਿ ਬਿਨੁ ਬਰਖਾ ਬਿਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as black clouds are often seen in the sky who make thundering sound but disperse without releasing a drop of rain.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਹਿਮਾਚਲਿ ਕਠੋਰ ਅਉ ਸੀਤਲ ਅਤਿ; ਸਕੀਐ ਨ ਖਾਇ ਖਾਏ ਤ੍ਰਿਖਾ ਨ ਮਿਟਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a snow clad mountain is very hard and cold; it yields no eatable nor can the thirst be quenched by eating the snow.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਓਸ ਪਰਤ ਕਰਤ ਹੈ ਸਜਲ ਦੇਹੀ; ਰਾਖੀਐ ਚਿਰੰਕਾਲ ਨ ਠਉਰ ਠਹਿਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as dew wets the body but it cannot be kept at a place for long. It cannot be stored.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਤ੍ਰਿਬਿਧੀ ਚਪਲ ਫਲ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਨਿਸ ਪ੍ਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੪੬॥
column5: So is the fruit of the service of gods who live life in the three traits of maya. Their reward is also influenced by the three traits of mammon. Only the service of the True Guru maintains the flow of the Naam-Bani elixir for ever. (446)

column2: ਬੈਸਨੋ ਅਨੰਨਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਨਿ ਸਾਲਗ੍ਰਾਮ ਸੇਵਾ; ਗੀਤਾ ਭਾਗਵਤ ਸ੍ਰੋਤਾ ਏਕਾਕੀ ਕਹਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: If someone is a worshipper of Vishnu, is brahmin by caste, worships (stone) and listens to the recitation of Geeta and Bhagwat in a secluded place;

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਧਰਮ ਦੇਵ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ਕਉ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਪੂਛਿ; ਕਰਤ ਗਵਨ ਸੋ ਮਹੂਰਤ ਸੋਧਾਵਹੀ॥
column5: Have the auspicious time and date worked out by learned brahmins before proceeding on religious places or visiting the temples of gods and goddesses located on banks of rivers;

column2: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਨਿਕਸਿ ਗਰਧਬ ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਸਗਨ ਕੈ; ਸੰਕਾ ਉਪਰਾਜਿ ਬਹੁਰਿ ਘਰਿ ਆਵਹੀ॥
column5: But when he sets out of the house and faces a dog or a donkey, he regards it inauspicious and a doubt arises in his mind forcing him to return home.

column2: ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਗਹਿ ਰਹਿ ਸਕਤ ਨ ਏਕਾ ਟੇਕ; ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਅਛਿਤ ਨ ਪਰੰਮ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਵਹੀ॥੪੪੭॥
column5: Despite belonging to a Guru like a faithful wife, if a person does not acknowledge the support of his Guru firmly and wanders at the door of one god or the other, he cannot reach the supreme state of Oneness with God having been caught in duality. (447)

column2: ਗੁਰ ਸਿਖ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲਾਪ ਕੋ ਪ੍ਰਤਾਪ ਐਸੋ; ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: The union of a Sikh with his Guru and becoming one with him is like a faithful wife who discards the desire of others and lives in the refuge of one husband.

column2: ਪੂਛਤ ਨ ਜੋਤਕ ਅਉ ਬੇਦ ਥਿਤਿ ਵਾਰ ਕਛੁ; ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਅਉ ਨਖਤ੍ਰ ਕੀ ਨ ਸੰਕਾ ਉਰਧਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Sikh who places his faith in the refuge of one True Guru, does not depend upon astrology or the command of Vedas, nor does he bring any doubt about the auspiciousness of a day/date or constellation of stars/planets to his mind.

column2: ਜਾਨਤ ਨ ਸਗਨ ਲਗਨ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਨੇਹੁ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Engrossed in the holy feet of Guru, the Sikh does not know anything about the good or bad omens or service of gods and goddesses. He has such inaccessible love with the True Guru, the manifestation of formless Lord, that by lodging the divine word of the

column2: ਸਿਖ ਸੰਤ ਬਾਲਕ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲਕ ਹੁਇ; ਜੀਵਨ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਗਤਿ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬੀਚਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥੪੪੮॥
column5: The father Guru protects and brings up the specially virtuous children. Such Sikhs are freed of all rites and rituals by the Guru during their life-time, and instills the ideology and thoughts of one Lord in their mind. (448)

column2: ਨਾਰਿ ਕੈ ਭਤਾਰ ਕੈ ਸਨੇਹ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਹੁਇ; ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਲੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife is considered faithful who lives life in the love of her husband. So does an obedient Sikh of the Guru take refuge of one Guru-God Lord.

column2: ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਅਉ ਸੰਬਾਦ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਹੁਇ; ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਨ ਕਾਨ ਸਿਖ ਦੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a husband enjoys the subject of mode of singing musical instruments and other conversation, so does a Sikh in the service of the Guru talks and listens nothing other than the sound of the divine words of the Guru.

column2: ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਸਰਬੰਗ ਹੇਰੇ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨ ਦਰਸਨ ਕੀਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a faithful wife admires the good looks, colour and beauty of all limbs of her husband, so does a devoted Sikh neither is the follower of any god nor proceeds to see any. Other than one True Guru, the form of True Master, he looks at no one else.

column2: ਸੁਜਨ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਗਉਨ ਕਰੈ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸਥਾਨ ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਬਿਨੁ ਮੀਨ ਹੈ॥੪੪੯॥
column5: Just as a faithful wife lives amongst the close relatives in her house and goes nowhere else; so does the Sikh of the Guru go nowhere else other than the court of the True Guru and assembly of His devoted and loving Sikhs. Places of other gods and goddess

column2: ਐਸੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਸੈ ਕੁਆਰ ਪਾਤ੍ਰ ਹੀ ਸੁਪਾਤ੍ਰ ਭਲੀ; ਆਸ ਪਿਆਸੀ ਮਾਤਾ ਪਿਤਾ ਏਕੈ ਕਾਹ ਦੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A virgin maid who is ever hopeful of achieving a place of superior authority in the house of a husband that her father will find for her one day is far better than a deceitful woman.

column2: ਐਸੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਸੈ ਦੀਨਤਾ ਕੈ ਦੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਭਲੀ; ਪਤਿਤ ਪਾਵਨ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪਾਇ ਲਾਇ ਲੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: A woman who has been disassociated with her husband by him and who regrets her actions by her humility, consequence of which her husband forgives her sins is far better than a deceitful woman.

column2: ਐਸੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਸੈ ਭਲੋ ਬਿਰਹਾ ਬਿਓਗ ਸੋਗ; ਲਗਨ ਸਗਨ ਸੋਧੇ ਸਰਧਾ ਸਹੇਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: That woman separated from her husband who bearing the pangs of separation is devotedly involved in finding out auspicious time and good omens for the reunion is better than a treacherous and deceitful woman.

column2: ਐਸੀ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਮਾਤ ਗਰਭ ਹੀ ਗਲੀ ਭਲੀ; ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਜਿਉ ਰਾਹੁ ਕੇਤੁ ਹੈ॥੪੫੦॥
column5: Such a woman of deceitful love should have perished in her mother's womb. Deceit filled love is full of such duality as the two demons Rahu and Ketu are who cause solar and lunar eclipse. (450)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਕੂਪ ਨਿਕਸਤ ਜਤਨ ਕੀਏ; ਸੀਚੀਅਤ ਖੇਤ ਏਕੈ ਪਹੁਚਤ ਨ ਆਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as water from a well can be drawn by different methods, such as bucket and rope, Persian wheel etc. and then it is directed to irrigate a field and it goes nowhere else.

column2: ਪਥਿਕ ਪਪੀਹਾ ਪਿਆਸੇ ਆਸ ਲਗਿ ਢਿਗ ਬੈਠਿ; ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਨ ਭਾਂਜਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਕਤ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: A traveller and a rain-bird may keep on sitting thirsty near a well but cannot quench their thirst without means to draw water from the well and therefore cannot assuage their thirst.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਸਕਲ ਦੇਵ ਟੇਵ ਸੈ ਟਰਤ ਨਾਹਿ; ਸੇਵਾ ਕੀਏ ਦੇਤ ਫਲ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਸਮਾਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: Likewise, all gods and goddesses can do something within their power. They can reward a devotee for his services only to that extent and that too of worldly desires.

column2: ਪੂਰਣ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਬਰਖਾ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਹਿਤਿ; ਬਰਖ ਹਰਖ ਦੇਤ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਕਉ॥੪੫੧॥
column5: But the complete and perfect God-like True Guru showers the spiritual pleasure-giving ambrosial nectar of Naam, the treasure-house of all the happiness and comforts. (The service of gods and goddesses is trivial in benefits whereas that of True Guru bless

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਉਲੂ ਦਿਨ ਸਮੈ ਕਾਹੂਐ ਨ ਦੇਖਿਓ ਭਾਵੈ; ਤੈਸੇ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮੈ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੈ॥
