column5: Just as sight of an owl during the day is not appreciated by any body, so is a follower of a god not liked by the disciple of the True Guru in their holy congregation.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਊਆ ਬਿਦਿਆਮਾਨ ਬੋਲਤ ਨ ਕਾਹੂ ਭਾਵੈ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਜਉ ਬੋਲੈ ਅਹੰਮੇਵ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a crow cawing is not appreciated by anyone, just as a devotee of a god is not appreciated in the holy assembly of god-like True Guru. (because he may be saying haughty traits of his deity)

column2: ਕਟਤ ਚਟਤ ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਬਿਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਜੈਸੇ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਸੁਹਾਇ ਨ ਕੁਟੇਵ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a dog licks when he is patted and bites when shouted and scolded at. (both acts are not good),

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਰਾਲ ਮਾਲ ਸੋਭਤ ਨ ਬਗੁ ਠਗੁ; ਕਾਢੀਐ ਪਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੈ॥੪੫੨॥
column5: Just as a heron does not fit in the group of swans and is turned out from there, so does a devotee of some god or goddess not fit into the holy assembly of God-worshipping saints. Such fake devotees should be turned out from these assemblies. (452)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਉਲੂ ਆਦਿਤ ਉਦੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਕਉ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੈ ਨ ਸੁਝੈ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ ਮੈ॥
column5: Just as an owl cannot know the greatness of sunlight, similarly a worshipper of other deities cannot have perception of True Guru's advice and company of the holy men.

column2: ਮਰਕਟ ਮਨਿ ਮਾਨਿਕ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨ ਸਬਦ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗ ਮੈ॥
column5: Just as a monkey does not know the value of pearls and diamonds, so can a follower of other deities not assess the importance of Guru's sermon.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਫਨਿੰਦ੍ਰ ਪੈ ਪਾਨ ਮਹਾਤਮੈ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਮਹਾਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਅੰਗ ਮੈ॥
column5: Just as a cobra cannot appreciate nectar-like milk, similarly a follower of other gods cannot understand significance of the blessings of the Guru's word and his consecrated gift of Karhah Parsad.

column2: ਬਿਨੁ ਹੰਸ ਬੰਸ ਬਗ ਠਗ ਨ ਸਕਤ ਟਿਕ; ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧ ਸੁਖ ਸਾਗਰ ਤਰੰਗ ਮੈ॥੪੫੩॥
column5: Just as an egret cannot fit in the flock of swans and has no knowledge of comforting waves of lake Mansarover. Similarly a worshipper (follower) of other gods cannot stay in the society of devout Sikhs blessed by the True Guru, nor can he understand the d

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਨਗਰ ਏਕ ਹੋਤ ਹੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਹਾਟੈ; ਗਾਹਕ ਅਸੰਖ ਆਵੈ ਬੇਚਨ ਅਰੁ ਲੈਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as a town has many shops which are visited by many customers who go there to buy or sell their merchandise.

column2: ਜਾ ਪੈ ਕਛੁ ਬੇਚੈ ਅਰੁ ਬਨਜੁ ਨ ਮਾਂਗੈ ਪਾਵੈ; ਆਨ ਪੈ ਬਿਸਾਹੈ ਜਾਇ ਦੇਖੈ ਸੁਖ ਨੈਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: When a customer who has sold something at a shop is unable to buy something from there since it is not available, he visits other shops. Finding his requirements there, he feels happy and relaxed.

column2: ਜਾ ਕੀ ਹਾਟ ਸਕਲ ਸਮਗ੍ਰੀ ਪਾਵੈ ਅਉ ਬਿਕਾਵੈ; ਬੇਚਤ ਬਿਸਾਹਤ ਚਾਹਤ ਚਿਤ ਚੈਨ ਕਉ॥
column5: A shopkeeper who keeps all types of commodities in his shop and which are sold frequently, a customer generally likes to sell or purchase from there. He feels happy and satisfied.

column2: ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਜਾਇ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਰੇ ਸਾਹਿ; ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਜਾ ਕੈ ਲੈਨ ਅਰੁ ਦੈਨ ਕਉ॥੪੫੪॥
column5: Similarly, if a follower of other god comes to the refuge of the perfect True Guru, he will find that his store-house is filled with all types of trading commodities (of loving worship). (454)

column2: ਬਨਜ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਬਿਖੈ ਰਤਨ ਪਾਰਖ ਹੋਇ; ਰਤਨ ਜਨਮ ਕੀ ਪਰੀਖਿਆ ਨਹੀ ਪਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the profession of trading, a man can assess and evaluate pearls and diamonds but has not been able to evaluate this precious human birth and his aim of coming to this world.

column2: ਲੇਖੈ ਚਿਤ੍ਰ ਗੁਪਤ ਸੇ ਲੇਖਕ ਲਿਖਾਰੀ ਭਏ; ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਕੀ ਅਸੰਕਾ ਨ ਮਿਟਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: One can be a good accountant and expert in keeping accounts but has not been able to erase the repeated cycle of his birth and death.

column2: ਬੀਰ ਬਿਦਿਆ ਮਹਾਬਲੀ ਭਏ ਹੈ ਧਨੁਖਧਾਰੀ; ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰਿ ਨ ਸਹਜਿ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈ ਹੈ॥
column5: In the profession of fighting in the battlefields, a man may become very brave, strong and powerful, acquire sound knowledge of archery, but has failed to overpower his internal enemies of ego and pride so as to acquire spiritual stability through the tea

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਕਲੀ ਕਾਲ; ਮਾਇਆ ਮੈ ਉਦਾਸੀ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਜਤਾਈ ਹੈ॥੪੫੫॥
column5: Living in the world of maya (mammon), the disciples of the Guru who have remained unsoiled of it have learnt that in this dark eons, the meditation on the name of God-like True Guru is supreme. (455)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਬਿਰਖ ਸਫਲ ਹੋਤ ਸਮੈ ਪਾਇ; ਸਰਬਦਾ ਫਲੰਤੇ ਸਦਾ ਫਲ ਸੁ ਸ੍ਵਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a tree bears fruit at a certain time of the year, but there are some trees which bear fruit all the time (like Kalap Variksh) and their fruit is very tasty too.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੂਪ ਜਲ ਨਿਕਸਤ ਹੈ ਜਤਨ ਕੀਏ; ਗੰਗਾ ਜਲ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as drawing water from the well demands some effort, but the flow of water in river Ganges is continuous and in plenty.

column2: ਮ੍ਰਿਤਕਾ ਅਗਨਿ ਤੂਲ ਤੇਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਦੀਪ ਦਿਪੈ; ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ ਸਸੀਅਰ ਬਿਸਮਾਦ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the combination of an earthen lamp, oil, cotton and fire results in a light-giving lamp that spreads its radiance in a limited place, but the radiance of the moon shines in the whole world and spreads strange happiness all around.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਕੀਏ ਫਲੁ ਦੇਤ ਜੇਤ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਨ ਸਾਸਨ ਜਮਾਦਿ ਹੈ॥੪੫੬॥
column5: Similarly, whatever quantum of devoted service one performs for a god, one receives reward accordingly. But a vision of the True teacher dispels the fear of angels of death beside blessing I one with many other goods. (All gods grant goods to their follow

column2: ਪੰਚ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਕੈ ਭਏ ਹੈ ਮਹਾਂਭਾਰਥ ਸੇ; ਪੰਚ ਮਾਰਿ ਕਾਹੂਐ ਨ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: During the times of Mahabharat, there had many warriors like the five Pandavas in the past but none ever endeavoured to end his duality by destroying the five vices residing within.

column2: ਗ੍ਰਿਹ ਤਜਿ ਨਵਿ ਨਾਥ ਸਿਧਿ ਜੋਗੀਸੁਰ ਹੁਇ ਨ; ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਅਤੀਤ ਨਿਜ ਆਸਨ ਮੈ ਤਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Renouncing home and family, many became the Masters, Sidhs and sages, but none had engrossed his mind in the higher spiritual state by keeping oneself free of the effect of the three traits of maya.

column2: ਬੇਦ ਪਾਠ ਪੜਿ ਪੜਿ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਪ੍ਰਬੋਧੈ ਜਗੁ; ਸਕੈ ਨ ਸਮੋਧ ਮਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਸਨਾ ਨ ਹਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: A learned person imparts knowledge to the world by studying the Vedas and other scriptures, but he could not bring around his own mind nor end his worldly desires.

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ; ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਲਿਵ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬੀਚਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥੪੫੭॥
column5: A devoted Sikh of the Guru who in the company of saintly persons, and serving the Lord-like True Guru has engrossed his mind in the divine word, is in reality the real scholar of the Lord. (457)

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਮ ਦੇਖਿ ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਹੁਇ; ਅਕਥ ਕਥਾ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਹਾਰਿ ਮੋਨਿਧਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: A pursuant disciple of the True Guru feels the presence of Lord Almighty in every living being and at all places, becomes impartial and instead of indulging in discussions of Lord's visible plays and performances, remains engrossed in Him.

column2: ਹੋਨਹਾਰ ਹੋਇ ਤਾਂ ਤੇ ਆਸਾ ਤੇ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਭਏ; ਕਾਰਨ ਕਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਜਾਨਿ ਹਉਮੈ ਮਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Whatever is happening, is happening in His will. Thus such a disciple remains unsullied of all his desire. Knowing the traits of the Almighty who is the cause and effect of everything, he loses his pride and ego in accordance with immortal saying of Gurba

column2: ਸੂਖਮ ਸਥੂਲ ਓਅੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ ਅਕਾਰ ਹੁਇ; ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਬੁਧਿ ਭਏ ਬ੍ਰਹਮਚਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: He accepts that all big or small forms have come out of One Lord. Adopting the divine wisdom, he becomes Godly in character.

column2: ਬਟ ਬੀਜ ਕੋ ਬਿਥਾਰ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਕੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਛਾਇਆ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨਿਵਾਰੀ ਹੈ॥੪੫੮॥
column5: Just as a well-spread banyan tree is born out of a seed, so is His form spreading around in the form of maya. An obedient Sikh of the Guru removes his duality by learning heavily on this one support. (He is never enamored by any god or goddess since he kn

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸਕਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਆਪਨੀ ਆਪਨੀ ਭਾਂਤਿ; ਚੰਦਨੁ ਚੰਦਨ ਕਰੈ ਸਰਬ ਤਮਾਲ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as all trees grow and spread according to the nature of their species and they cannot impose their influence on others but a sandalwood tree can make all other trees smell like itself.

column2: ਤਾਂਬਾ ਹੀ ਸੈ ਹੋਤ ਜੈਸੇ ਕੰਚਨ ਕਲੰਕੁ ਡਾਰੈ; ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਿ ਧਾਤੁ ਸਕਲ ਉਜਾਲ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as addition of some special chemical in copper. can convert it into gold, but all metals can become gold by the touch of a philosopher-stone.

column2: ਸਰਿਤਾ ਅਨੇਕ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਪ੍ਰਵਾਹ ਗਤਿ; ਸੁਰਸਰੀ ਸੰਗਮ ਸਮ ਜਲ ਸੁਢਾਲ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as the flow of many rivers is different in many ways, but their water becomes pure and sacred once they mingle with water of river Ganges.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਸਕਲ ਦੇਵ ਟੇਵ ਸੈ ਟਰਤ ਨਾਹਿ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਅਸਰਨਿ ਸਰਨਿ ਅਕਾਲ ਕਉ॥੪੫੯॥
column5: Similarly, none of the gods and goddesses change their basic character. (They can reward someone according to their nature). But like sandalwood, philosopher-stone and river Ganges, the True Guru takes all under his refuge and blessing them with Naam Amri

column2: ਗਿਰਗਟ ਕੈ ਰੰਗ ਕਮਲ ਸਮੇਹ ਬਹੁ; ਬਨੁ ਬਨੁ ਡੋਲੈ ਕਊਆ ਕਹਾਂ ਧਉ ਸਵਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: A chameleon who changes the colour of its body so very often looks very much like the form of a lotus flower. But this insect-eating chameleon cannot be holding the merits of a lotus flower. A dead flesh-eating crow that flies here and there cannot reach

column2: ਘਰ ਘਰ ਫਿਰਤ ਮੰਜਾਰ ਅਹਾਰ ਪਾਵੈ; ਬੇਸ੍ਵਾ ਬਿਸਨੀ ਅਨੇਕ ਸਤੀ ਨ ਸਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a male cat roams about various burrows and houses searching for food, and likewise a whore living life of several vices cannot reach out to a woman of truth, sincerity and virtues.

column2: ਸਰ ਸਰ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਨ ਮਿਲਤ ਮਰਾਲ ਮਾਲ; ਜੀਵ ਘਾਤ ਕਰਤ ਨ ਮੋਨੀ ਬਗੁ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as wandering from pond to pond, one cannot find a flock of swans who live in lake Mansarover and an egret who kills living beings for food cannot be contemplating.

column2: ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ; ਮਾਖੀ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਚੰਦਨ ਦੁਰਗੰਧਿ ਅਸਥਾਨ ਹੈ॥੪੬੦॥
column5: Similarly, without the service of perfect Guru, if someone becomes a follower of any other god/goddess, it is like a fly who giving up the fragrance of sandalwood goes and sits on foul smelling filth. (460)

column2: ਆਨ ਹਾਟ ਕੇ ਹਟੂਆ ਲੇਤ ਹੈ ਘਟਾਇ ਮੋਲ; ਦੇਤ ਹੈ ਚੜ੍ਹਾਇ ਡਹਕਤ ਜੋਈ ਆਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: When a shopkeeper or a trader approaches another but a clever shopkeeper, the later sells his merchandise at a profit and manipulates to buy other's goods at a lesser price.

column2: ਤਿਨ ਸੈ ਬਨਜ ਕੀਏ ਬਿੜਤਾ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਕੋਊ; ਟੋਟਾ ਕੋ ਬਨਜ ਪੇਖਿ ਪੇਖਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Dealing with such deceitful shopkeepers cannot be profitable. Every trader repents at conducting a deal at a loss.

column2: ਕਾਠ ਕੀ ਹਾਂਡੀ ਜੈਸੇ ਚਢੈ ਏਕੈ ਬਾਰਿ ਕੋਊ; ਕਪਟ ਬਿਉਹਾਰ ਕੀਏ ਆਪਹਿ ਲਖਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a wooden pot can be used for cooking only once, similarly he who indulges in cheating in business exposes his self through his deceitful dealings.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਾਹ ਗੁਨ ਬੇਚ ਅਵਗੁਨ ਲੇਤ; ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸੁਜਸ ਜਗਤ ਉਠਿ ਧਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥੪੬੧॥
column5: Contrary to the dishonest and deceitful trading, the True Guru is the truthful trader of true commodity. He sells the commodity of Lord's name to the Sikhs who come to trade with Him. In the bargain, He takes away from them all the sins and vices that the

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਮਸਰਿ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਨਾਸਤਿ; ਪ੍ਰਤਿਮਾ ਅਨੇਕ ਹੋਇ ਕੈਸੇ ਬਨਿ ਆਵਈ॥
column5: When the perfect Lord manifests Himself totally in all and there is none like Him, then how can his myriad forms be made and installed in the temples?

column2: ਘਟਿ ਘਟਿ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੁ ਦੇਖੈ ਸੁਨੈ ਬੋਲੈ; ਪ੍ਰਤਿਮਾ ਮੈ ਕਾਹੇ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਟੁ ਹੁਇ ਦਿਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: When He Himself pervades in all, He Himself listens, speaks and sees, then why is He not seen speaking, listening and seeing in the idols of the temples?

column2: ਘਰ ਘਾਰ ਘਰਨਿ ਅਨੇਕ ਏਕ ਰੂਪ ਹੁਤੇ; ਪ੍ਰਤਿਮਾ ਸਕਲ ਦੇਵਸਥਲ ਹੁਇ ਨ ਸੁਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Every house has utensils of many form but made from the same material. Like that material, the light effulgent of the Lord exists in all. But why is that radiance not seen in its full grandeur in the idols installed in various temples?

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਸੋਈ; ਏਕ ਜੋਤਿ ਮੂਰਤਿ ਜੁਗਲ ਹੁਇ ਪੁਜਾਵਈ॥੪੬੨॥
column5: True Guru is the embodiment of the complete and perfect Lord, the light is one that exists both in Absolute and Transcendental form. The same Effulgent Lord is getting Himself worshipped in the form of True Guru. (462)

column2: ਮਾਨਸਰ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਆਨ ਸਰ ਜਾਇ ਬੈਠੇ ਹੰਸੁ; ਖਾਇ ਜਲ ਜੰਤ ਹੰਸ ਬੰਸਹਿ ਲਜਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a swan leaves lake Mansarover and resides in a pond, starts eating living beings from the pond like a heron, he will shame the species of swans.

column2: ਸਲਿਲ ਬਿਛੋਹ ਭਏ ਜੀਅਤ ਜਉ ਰਹੈ ਮੀਨ; ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹ ਕੈ ਸਨੇਹੀ ਨ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a fish survives outside water, then its love for water will be considered false and it would not be called as beloved of water.

column2: ਬਿਨੁ ਘਨ ਬੂੰਦ ਜਉ ਅਨਤ ਜਲ ਪਾਨ ਕਰੈ; ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਸੰਤਾਨ ਬਿਖੈ ਲਾਛਨ ਲਗਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a rain-bird satiates its thirst with a drop of water other than the swati drop, he would stigmatise his family.

column2: ਚਰਨਕਮਲ ਅਲਿ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਮੋਖ ਹੁਇ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ ਮੁਕਤਿ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥੪੬੩॥
column5: A devoted disciple of the True Guru preaches the teachings of the True Guru and achieves emancipation. But a disciple who gives up his love for the True Guru and bows before other gods, self.-made saints and sages and worships them; his love with Guru is

column2: ਜਉ ਕੋਊ ਮਵਾਸ ਸਾਧਿ ਭੂਮੀਆ ਮਿਲਾਵੈ ਆਨਿ; ਤਾ ਪਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਨ ਹੋਤ ਨਿਰਖ ਨਰਿੰਦ ਜੀ॥
column5: If a brave warrior defeats a rebel landlord and brings him into the protection of the king, the king rewards him out of happiness and glory is bestowed upon him.

column2: ਜਉ ਕੋਊ ਨ੍ਰਿਪਤਿ ਭ੍ਰਿਤਿ ਭਾਗਿ ਭੂਮੀਆ ਪੈ ਜਾਇ; ਧਾਇ ਮਾਰੇ ਭੂਮੀਆ ਸਹਿਤ ਹੀ ਰਜਿੰਦ ਜੀ॥
column5: But if an employee of the king absconds the king and joins the rebel landlord, the king launches a campaign against him and kills both the rebel landlord as well as the disloyal servant.

column2: ਆਨ ਕੋ ਸੇਵਕ ਰਾਜ ਦੁਆਰ ਜਾਇ ਸੋਭਾ ਪਾਵੈ; ਸੇਵਕ ਨਰੇਸ਼ ਆਨ ਦੁਆਰ ਜਾਤ ਨਿੰਦ ਜੀ॥
column5: If someone's employee takes refuge of the king, he earns praise there. But if a servant of the king goes to someone, he earns slander from all around.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਆਨ ਅਨਤ ਸਰਨਿ ਗੁਰ; ਆਨ ਨ ਸਮਰਥ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਬਿੰਦ ਜੀ॥੪੬੪॥
column5: Similarly, if a devotee of some god/goddess comes to the True Guru as a devoted disciple, the True Guru bless him with His refuge, initiates him in the meditation of His name. But no god or goddess is capable of granting refuge to any devoted Sikh of the

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਉਪਬਨ ਆਂਬ ਸੇਂਬਲ ਹੈ ਊਚ ਨੀਚ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਸਫਲ ਪ੍ਰਗਟ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as there are mango and silk cotton trees in the same garden, but a mango tree is more respected because of the fruits that it yields, whereas the Silk cotton tree being without fruits is considered inferior.

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸਮੀਪ ਜੈਸੇ ਬਾਂਸ ਅਉ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਗੰਧ ਨਿਰਗੰਧ ਸਿਵ ਸਕਤਿ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as in a jungle, there are sandalwood and bamboo trees. Since bamboo remains devoid of fragrance is known as egoistic and proud, whereas others absorb sandalwood's fragrance and are considered peace and comfort-giving trees.

column2: ਸੀਪ ਸੰਖ ਦੋਊ ਜੈਸੇ ਰਹਿਤ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਸੰਤਤਿ ਨ ਸਮਤ ਬਿਧਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as an oyster and conch shell are found in the same sea but oyster accepting ambrosial drop of rain water yields a pearl whereas a conch shell remains useless. Thus both cannot be graded equal.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕਨ ਭੇਦ; ਅਹੰਬੁਧਿ ਨਿਮ੍ਰਤਾ ਅਮਾਨ ਜਗ ਮਾਨੀਐ॥੪੬੫॥
column5: Similarly there is a difference between the devotees of the True Guru-the blesser of truth, and gods and goddesses. The followers of gods are proud of their intellect whereas the disciples of True Guru are considered humble and non-arrogant by the world.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਪਰ ਪੁਰਖੈ ਨ ਦੇਖਿਓ ਚਾਹੈ; ਪੂਰਨ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਕੈ ਪਤਿ ਹੀ ਕੈ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a faithful wife does not like to look at another man and being sincere and faithful always supports her husband in her mind.

column2: ਸਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਨ ਚਾਹੈ ਕਾਹੂ; ਆਸ ਘਨ ਬੂੰਦ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਗੁਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a rain-bird does not want water from a lake river or sea, but keeps on wailing for Swati drop from the clouds.

column2: ਦਿਨਕਰ ਓਰ ਭੋਰ ਚਾਹਤ ਨਹੀ ਚਕੋਰ; ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਹਿਮਕਰ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a Ruddy sheldrake does not like to look at the Sun even when the Sun is rising because the moon is his beloved in all respects.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਰਹਿਤ ਪੈ; ਸਹਜ ਸੁਭਾਵ ਨ ਅਵਗਿਆ ਅਭਿਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥੪੬੬॥
column5: So is a devoted disciple of the True Guru who does not worship any other god or goddess except the dearer than his life-True Guru. But, by remaining in a state of tranquility, he neither disrespects anyone nor shows the arrogance of his supremacy. (466)

column2: ਦੋਇ ਦਰਪਨ ਦੇਖੈ ਏਕ ਸੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਰੂਪ; ਦੋਇ ਨਾਵ ਪਾਵ ਧਰੈ ਪਹੁਚੈ ਨ ਪਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as looking into two or more mirrors placed side by side show more than one image; and placing feet in two boats does not enable one to sail across the river.

column2: ਦੋਇ ਦਿਸਾ ਗਹੇ ਗਹਾਏ ਸੈ ਹਾਥ ਪਾਉ ਟੂਟੈ; ਦੁਰਾਹੇ ਦੁਚਿਤ ਹੋਇ ਭੂਲਿ ਪਗੁ ਧਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the arms or legs are put to risk of breaking when pulled from both sides at the same time; one often errs in selection of the right path at the cross-road.

column2: ਦੋਇ ਭੂਪ ਤਾ ਕੋ ਗਾਉ ਪਰਜਾ ਨ ਸੁਖੀ ਹੋਤ; ਦੋਇ ਪੁਰਖਨ ਕੀ ਨ ਕੁਲਾਬਧੂ ਨਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a city if ruled by two kings cannot provide peace and comfort to the subjects, nor can a woman wedded to two men can be sincere and loyal or faithful to either family.

column2: ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਹੋਇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਟੇਵ ਗਹੈ; ਸਹੈ ਜਮ ਡੰਡੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗ ਜੀਵਨੁ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੬੭॥
column5: Similarly, if a devout Sikh of Guru worships other gods and goddesses to assuage his addiction, what to speak of his liberation, he even bears the punishment of the angels of death. His life is condemned by the world. (467)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਿਰਖ ਮੂਲ ਸੀਚੀਐ ਸਲਿਲ ਤਾ ਤੇ; ਸਾਖਾ ਸਾਖਾ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਕਰਿ ਹਰਿਓ ਹੋਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as by watering the roots and trunk of a tree, all its leaves and branches become green.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਸਤਿ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ; ਸਕਲ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸੁਪ੍ਰਸੰਨਿ ਧੰਨਿ ਸੋਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a faithful, truthful, virtuous wife remains attentive in the service of her husband, the whole family praises her, adores her very happily.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੁਖ ਦੁਆਰ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਭੋਜਨ ਕੈ; ਅੰਗ ਅੰਗ ਤੁਸਟਿ ਪੁਸਟਿ ਅਵਿਲੋਇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the mouth eats sweetmeats and all the limbs of the body feels satiated and strong.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਜਾਂਹਿ ਤਾਂਹਿ; ਸੁਰ ਨਰ ਬਰੰ ਬ੍ਰੂਹ ਕੋਟਿ ਮਧੇ ਕੋਇ ਹੈ॥੪੬੮॥
column5: Similarly, the obedient disciple of the Guru who instead of other gods and goddesses is ever keen to obey the command of his Guru, everyone and all the gods praise him and call him blessed. But such an obedient and loyal disciple of the True Guru is very

column2: ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਖਾਤ ਗਾਤਿ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਹੋਤ; ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਖਾਤ ਗਾਤ ਹੋਤ ਉਪਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as consuming raw mercury produces several ailments in the body but when treated with certain chemicals and purified, can cure many diseases.

column2: ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਪਰਸਤ ਕੰਚਨਹਿ ਸੋਖ ਲੇਤ; ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਪਰਸ ਤਾਂਬੋ ਕਨਿਕ ਧਾਰਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as gold placed in raw mercury reacts to lose its identity but when the same chemically reacted mercury mixes with copper becomes gold.

column2: ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਅਗਹੁ ਨ ਹਾਥਨ ਕੈ ਗਹਿਓ ਜਾਇ; ਸੋਈ ਪਾਰੋ ਗੁਟਕਾ ਹੁਇ ਸਿਧ ਨਮਸਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The mercury that is so unstable and restless that it cannot be held with hands but the same becomes respectable for yogis and sidhs when converted chemically into small tablets.

column2: ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮ ਪਾਇ ਜੈਸੀਐ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲੈ; ਤੈਸੀ ਪਾਵੈ ਪਦਵੀ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤ ਅਧਿਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੬੯॥
column5: Similarly whatever company a person keeps during his life, he attains that capability and status in the world. If he enjoys the congregation of true devotees of the True Guru he achieves salvation by virtue of Guru's teachings. But despite being a discipl

column2: ਕੂਆ ਕੋ ਮੇਡਕ ਨਿਧਿ ਜਾਨੈ ਕਹਾ ਸਾਗਰ ਕੀ; ਸ੍ਵਾਂਤਿ ਬੂੰਦ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਸੰਖ ਜੀਅ ਜਾਨਈ॥
column5: Just as a frog living in well cannot know the greatness and extent of the ocean, and the hollow conch shell cannot appreciate the importance of the ambrosial drop of rain water that turns into a pearl when it falls on an oyster.

column2: ਦਿਨਕਰਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੋ ਉਦੋਤ ਕਹਾ ਜਾਨੈ ਉਲੂ; ਸੇਂਬਲ ਸੈ ਕਹਾ ਖਾਇ ਸੂਆ ਹਿਤ ਠਾਨਈ॥
column5: Just as an owl cannot know the light of the Sun or a parrot cannot eat the insipid fruits of a silk cotton tree nor can he love them.

column2: ਬਾਇਸ ਨ ਜਾਨਤ ਮਰਾਲ ਮਾਲ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਕੋ; ਮਰਕਟ ਮਾਨਕ ਹੀਰਾ ਨ ਪਹਿਚਾਨਈ॥
column5: Just as a crow cannot know the importance of the company of swans nor can a monkey appreciate the value of gems and diamonds.

column2: ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਗੂੰਗੇ ਬਹਰੇ ਨ ਕਹਿ ਸੁਨ ਮਨ ਮਾਨਈ॥੪੭੦॥
column5: Similarly, a worshipper of other gods cannot understand the significance of serving True Guru. He is like a deaf and dumb person 'whose mind is not at all receptive to the sermons of the True Guru and therefore cannot act upon them. (470)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਾਮ ਤੀਖਨ ਤਪਤਿ ਅਤਿ ਬਿਖਮ; ਬੈਸੰਤਰਿ ਬਿਹੂਨ ਸਿਧ ਕਰਤਿ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਾਸ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as the Sun may be very harsh and hot but one is unable to cook food without fire.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਸ ਓਸ ਕੈ ਸਜਲ ਹੋਤ ਮੇਰ ਤਿਨ; ਬਿਨੁ ਜਲ ਪਾਨ ਨ ਨਿਵਾਰਤ ਪਿਆਸ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as dew drenches mountains and hay at night but without drinking water, that dew cannot satiate anyone's thirst.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਗ੍ਰੀਖਮ ਰੁਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਗਟੈ ਪ੍ਰਸੇਦ ਅੰਗ; ਮਿਟਤ ਨ ਫੂਕੇ. ਬਿਨੁ ਪਵਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਕਉ॥
column5: Just as body perspires during summer season that cannot be dried by blowing at it. Fanning alone dries it and gives comfort.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਵਾ ਗੌਨ ਨ ਮਿਟਤ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਨਿਜ ਪਦ ਕੇ ਨਿਵਾਸ ਕਉ॥੪੭੧॥
column5: Similarly, serving gods cannot emancipate one from repeated births and deaths. One can achieve the higher spiritual state by becoming an obedient disciple of the True Guru. (471)

column2: ਆਂਬ ਕੀ ਸਧਰ ਕਤ ਮਿਟਤ ਆਂਬਲੀ ਖਾਏ; ਪਿਤਾ ਕੋ ਪਿਆਰੁ ਨ ਪਰੋਸੀ ਪਹਿ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: How can the desire of eating ripe mango be satiated by eating raw mango? One cannot receive father-like love from one's neighbour.

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਕੀ ਨਿਧਿ ਕਤ ਪਾਈਅਤ ਪੋਖਰ ਸੈ; ਦਿਨਕਰਿ ਸਰਿ ਦੀਪ ਜੋਤਿ ਨ ਪੁਜਾਈਐ॥
column5: How can one find the wealth of oceans from small ponds? nor can the light of a beacon reach the brightness of the Sun.

column2: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਬਰਖਾ ਸਮਾਨ ਪੁਜਸਿ ਨ ਕੂਪ ਜਲ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਬਾਸ ਨ ਪਲਾਸ ਮਹਿਕਾਈਐ॥
column5: The water of well cannot reach the water that comes down from the clouds in the form of rain nor can Butea frondosa tree spread fragrance like sandalwood.

column2: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਦਇਆਲ ਕੀ ਦਇਆ ਨ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਮੈ; ਜਉ ਖੰਡ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਉਦੈ ਅਸਤ ਲਉ ਧਾਈਐ॥੪੭੨॥
column5: Similarly, no god or goddess can have the amount of kindness that the clement True Guru showers on His Sikhs. One may wander in realms and regions from East to West in search of it. (472)

column2: ਗਿਰਤ ਅਕਾਸ ਤੇ ਪਰਤ ਪ੍ਰਿਥੀ ਪਰ ਜਉ; ਗਹੈ ਆਸਰੋ ਪਵਨ ਕਵਨਹਿ ਕਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a person falling from the sky try to take support of the air, and that support is futile.

column2: ਜਰਤ ਬੈਸੰਤਰ ਜਉ ਧਾਇ ਧਾਇ ਧੂਮ ਗਹੈ; ਨਿਕਸਿਓ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਖਲ ਬੁਧਿ ਉਪਰਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a person ablaze in the fire tries to escape from its wrath by catching smoke, he cannot escape from the fire. On the contrary it only shows his foolishness.

column2: ਸਾਗਰ ਅਪਾਰ ਧਾਰ ਬੂਡਤ ਜਉ ਫੇਨ ਗਹੈ; ਅਨਿਥਾ ਬੀਚਾਰ ਪਾਰ ਜੈਬੇ ਕਉ ਨ ਸਾਜ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a person drowning in the rapid waves of the sea try to save himself catching the surf of the water, such a thought is totally foolish since the surf is not a means of crossing the sea.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਵਾ ਗਵਨ ਦੁਖਤ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਨਿ ਨ ਮੋਖ ਪਦੁ ਰਾਜ ਹੈ॥੪੭੩॥
column5: Similarly, the cycle of birth and death cannot end by worshipping or serving any god or goddess. Without taking the refuge of the perfect True Guru, no one can achieve salvation. (473)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰੂਪ ਰੰਗ ਬਿਧਿ ਪੂਛੈ ਅੰਧੁ ਅੰਧ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਆਪ ਹੀ ਨ ਦੇਖੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਕੈਸੇ ਕੈ ਦਿਖਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a blind person asks another blind person about the features and beauty of a person, how can he tell him, when he cannot see anything?

column2: ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਪੂਛੇ ਬਹਰੋ ਜਉ ਬਹਰਾ ਪੈ; ਸਮਝੈ ਨ ਆਪਿ ਤਾਹਿ ਕੈਸੇ ਸਮਝਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a deaf wishes to know about the tune and rhythm of a song from another person who is also deaf, then what can one who himself is deaf explain to the other deaf?

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਗੁੰਗ ਗੁੰਗ ਪਹਿ ਬਚਨ ਬਿਬੇਕ ਪੂਛੈ ਚਾਹੈ; ਬੋਲਿ ਨ ਸਕਤ ਕੈਸੇ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਨਾਵਈ॥
column5: If a dumb wants to learn something from another dumb, what can anyone who himself is unable to speak, explain to the other dumb?

column2: ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਖੋਜੈ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਅਨਿਥਾ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਮਤਿ ਆਨ ਪੈ ਨ ਪਾਵਈ॥੪੭੪॥
column5: Similarly it is foolishness to seek spiritual knowledge from other gods and goddesses leaving the True Guru who is perfect manifestation of the Lord. No one else can impart this wisdom or knowledge. (474)

column2: ਅੰਬਰ ਬੇਚਨ ਜਾਇ ਦੇਸ ਦਿਗੰਬਰਨ ਕੇ; ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤ ਨ ਹੋਇ ਲਾਭ ਸਹਸੋ ਹੈ ਮੂਲਿ ਕੋ॥
column5: If a cloth merchant visits a place where everyone lives naked, he is not going to benefit from it. He may lose his principal goods.

column2: ਰਤਨ ਪਰੀਖਿਆ ਸੀਖਿਆ ਚਾਹੈ ਜਉ ਆਂਧਨ ਪੈ; ਰੰਕਨ ਪੈ ਰਾਜ ਮਾਂਗੈ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਭੂਲਿ ਕੋ॥
column5: If a person wishes to learn the science of evaluating gems from a blind person or asks for the kingdom from paupers, that would be his foolishness and mistake.

column2: ਗੁੰਗਾ ਪੈ ਪੜ੍ਹਨ ਜਾਇ ਜੋਤਕ ਬੈਦਕ ਬਿਦਿਆ; ਬਹਿਰਾ ਪੈ ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਅਨਿਥਾ ਅਭੂਲਿ ਕੋ॥
column5: If someone wishes to learn astrology or acquire knowledge of Vedas from a dumb person, or wishes to know about music from a deaf person, this would be an utter foolish effort.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਦੋਖ ਮੇਟਿ ਮੋਖ ਚਾਹੈ; ਬਿਨੁ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੁਖ ਸਹੈ ਜਮ ਸੂਲਿ ਕੋ॥੪੭੫॥
column5: Similarly, if anyone tries to get rid of his sins by serving and worshipping other gods and goddesses,. and thus achieve salvation, this would be an act of foolishness. Without obtaining initiation of True-name from True Guru, he will only bear the pricks

column2: ਬੀਜ ਬੋਇ ਕਾਲਰ ਮੈ ਨਿਪਜੈ ਨ ਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ; ਮੂਲ ਖੋਇ ਰੋਵੈ ਪੁਨਿ ਰਾਜ ਡੰਡੁ ਲਾਗਈ॥
column5: Just as a seed sown in saline and barren land yields not even a leaf, one loses the capital (seed) and cries at the waste beside forced to pay revenue.

column2: ਸਲਿਲ ਬਿਲੋਇ ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਕਸਤ ਨਾਹਿ ਘ੍ਰਿਤਿ; ਮਟੁਕੀ ਮਥਨੀਆ ਹੂ ਫੋਰਿ ਤੋਰਿ ਭਾਗਈ॥
column5: Just as churning of water does not yield butter but in the process, one may break the churner and the earthenware.

column2: ਭੂਤਨ ਪੈ ਪੂਤ ਮਾਂਗੈ ਹੋਤ ਨ ਸਪੂਤੀ ਕੋਊ; ਜੀਅ ਕੋ ਪਰਤ ਸੰਸੋ. ਤਿਆਗੈ ਹੂ. ਨ ਤਿਆਗਈ॥
column5: Just as a barren woman under the influence of witchcraft and black magic seeks blessings of a son from ghosts and witches, she can't bear a son but instead fears losing her own life. She endeavours to free herself from their spell but they (ghosts and wit

column2: ਬਿਨ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ. ਆਨ ਸੇਵ ਦੁਖਦਾਇਕ ਹੈ; ਲੋਕ ਪਰਲੋਕ ਸੋਕਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਅਨੁਰਾਗਈ॥੪੭੬॥
column5: Without acquiring the teachings and wisdom from True Guru, service of other gods and goddesses give distress only. Loving them puts one in sufferings both in this world and the world hereafter. (476)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਰਾਜ ਤਨੁ ਜੰਬੁਕ ਅਧੀਨ ਹੋਤ; ਖਗਪਤਿ ਸੁਤ ਜਾਇ ਜੁਹਾਰਤ ਕਾਗ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a cub becomes subservient to a jackal or a Garud (Ardea argala) bows to a crow.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਹ ਕੇਤ ਬਸ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਨ ਮੈ. ਸੁਰਤਰੁ ਸੋਭ ਨ ਅਰਕ ਬਨਿ; ਰਵਿ ਸਸਿ ਲਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as Sun and Moon resides in the houses of Rahu and Ketu (the two unfriendly mythological demons), or Kalapbrichh the all giving tree of heavens does not fit into the jungle of Calotropis procera.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਾਮਧੇਨ ਸੁਤ ਸੂਕਰੀ ਸਥਨ ਪਾਨ; ਐਰਾਪਤਿ ਸੁਤ ਗਰਧਬ ਅਗ੍ਰਭਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a calf of ever milk-yielding cow (Kamdhenu) sucks the teats of a she pig, or the young one of Airawat the elephant of god Inder keeps bowing before a donkey.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਸੁਤ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਜਨਮੁ ਜਿਉ ਬੰਸ ਮੈ ਬਜਾਗਿ ਹੈ॥੪੭੭॥
column5: Similarly, if son of a Guru's Sikh starts worshipping gods and goddesses, his human birth would be a failure just as a son of two fathers would be in a respectable family. (477)

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਤੂੰਬਰੀ ਨਾ ਬੂਡੈ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਪਰਵਾਹ ਬਿਖੈ; ਬਿਖਮੈ ਤਊ ਨ ਤਜਤ ਹੈ ਮਨ ਤੇ॥
column5: If a colocynth (Tumna) does not sink in fast flowing river and it does not shed its bitterness even in sweet cold water, then what good it is?

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਲਪਟੈ ਪਾਖਾਨ ਪਾਵਕ ਨ ਜਰੈ ਸੂਤ੍ਰ; ਜਲ ਮੈ ਲੈ ਬੋਰਤਿ ਰਿਦੈ ਕਠੋਰਪਨ ਤੇ॥
column5: If the flame of fire cannot burn the stone, and if due to its rigid nature sinks everything along with it, then what good it is ?

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਗੁਡੀ ਉਡੀ ਦੇਖੀਅਤ ਹੈ ਅਕਾਸਚਾਰੀ; ਬਰਖਤ ਮੇਂਹ ਬਾਚੀਐ ਨ ਬਾਲਕਨ ਤੇ॥
column5: A kite is seen flying in the sky like a bird, but when it starts raining it cannot be saved and retrieved by the children flying it.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਰਿਧਿ ਸਿਧਿ ਭਾਉ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਤ੍ਰਿਗੁਨ ਖੇਲ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਨਾਹਿ ਕ੍ਰਿਤਘਨ ਤੇ॥੪੭੮॥
column5: Similarly, acquiring miraculous powers like walking on the water, immunity of burning or floating in the sky is indulging in duality and is the influence of the three trait mammon (maya). (Acquiring these cannot free one from internal bitterness, nor can

column2: ਕਉਡਾ ਪੈਸਾ ਰੁਪਈਆ ਸੁਨਈਆ ਕੋ ਬਨਜੁ ਕਰੈ; ਰਤਨ ਪਾਰਖੁ ਹੋਇ ਜਉਹਰੀ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as one starts dealing in shells in the beginning, then in money, gold coins and then becomes evaluator of diamonds and precious stones. He is then called a jeweller.

column2: ਜਉਹਰੀ ਕਹਾਇ ਪੁਨਿ ਕਉਡਾ ਕੋ ਬਨਜੁ ਕਰੈ; ਪੰਚ ਪਰਵਾਨ ਮੈ ਪਤਿਸਟਾ ਘਟਾਵਈ॥
column5: But after becoming famous as a jeweller, one starts dealing in shells, he loses his respect among the elite people.

column2: ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਕੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਹੁਇ; ਲੋਕ ਪਰਲੋਕ ਬਿਖੈ ਊਚ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Similarly, if a follower of some god comes into the service of True Guru, he acquires high status in this and the world beyond.

column2: ਛਾਡਿ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ. ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਹੁਇ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਜਨਮੁ ਕਪੂਤ ਹੁਇ ਹਸਾਵਈ॥੪੭੯॥
column5: But if someone leaves the service of the True Guru, and becomes a follower of some other god, then he wastes away his human life and he is laughed at by others being known as a bad son. (479)

column2: ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਕੈ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਕਰੈ ਜਉ ਨਾਰਿ; ਤਾਹਿ ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਚਾਹਤ ਭਤਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: If a wife discharges her duties faithfully and loyally and is devoted to her husband, such a wife is loved dearly by her husband.

column2: ਅਭਰਨ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਚਾਰ ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ; ਸਕਲ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਹੀ ਮੈ ਤਾ ਕੋ ਜੈ ਜੈਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such a lady is blessed with the opportunity of adoring herself and meeting with her husband. Being virtuous she is praised and appreciated by the whole family.

column2: ਸਹਜ ਅਨੰਦ ਸੁਖ ਮੰਗਲ ਸੁਹਾਗ ਭਾਗ; ਸੁੰਦਰ ਮੰਦਰ ਛਬਿ ਸੋਭਤ ਸੁਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: She acquires the comforts of married life gently and gradually. Because of the beauty of her high merits she adores the beautiful mansions with her presence.

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਕਉ ਰਾਖਤ ਗ੍ਰਿਸਤ ਮੈ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਭਾਉ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਨਿਵਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੮੦॥
column5: Similarly, the Sikhs of the Guru who love the True Guru from the core of their hearts, are kept conscious by the True Guru even when they are spending house-holders life. True Guru removes the duality of their devotion and worship of gods and goddesses. (

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤ ਮੈ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ; ਤਾਹੀ ਤੇ ਗ੍ਰਿਹੇਸੁਰ ਹੁਇ ਨਾਇਕਾ ਕਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a loyal and faithful wife is ever conscious of fulfilling her wifely obligations, and that makes her the prime person of the family.

column2: ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਧਨ ਧਾਮ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪੁਜਾਵੈ; ਸੋਭਿਤ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਚਾਰ ਸਿਹਜਾ ਸਮਾਵਈ॥
column5: Her husband fulfils all her needs of bedding, clothing, food, wealth, house and other property, and she in returns embellishes herself to enjoy oneness with her husband on the nuptial bed,

column2: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਸਿਖਨ ਕਉ ਰਾਖਤ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤ ਮੈ; ਸੰਪਤਾ ਸਮੂਹ ਸੁਖ ਲੁਡੇ ਤੇ ਲਡਾਵਈ॥
column5: Similarly, the True Guru keeps His devoted and obedient Sikhs lovingly in their householders life. With the blessings of ambrosial name of the Lord, he reaches the spiritual peace in their family life.

column2: ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਧਨ ਧਾਮ ਕਾਮਨਾ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਭਾਉ ਦੁਤੀਆ ਮਿਟਾਵਈ॥੪੮੧॥
column5: In the desire of the sacred name, the True Guru blesses His Sikhs with food, bedding, clothing, mansions and other worldly assets. He removes all their duality of serving and following other gods and goddesses. (481)

column2: ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਗਿਆਨ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਹੈ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਕਉ; ਮਨ ਬਚ ਕ੍ਰਮ ਸ੍ਵਾਮੀ ਸੇਵਾ ਅਧਿਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: The teachings of folk traditions and Vedas lay down that a faithful and loyal wife has the sole right to serve her husband devotedly in words and actions.

column2: ਨਾਮ ਇਸਨਾਨ ਦਾਨ ਸੰਜਮ ਨ ਜਾਪ ਤਾਪ; ਤੀਰਥ ਬਰਤ ਪੂਜਾ ਨੇਮ ਨਾ ਤਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Such a loyal, devoted and faithful wife does not even look at all the futile rites and rituals like; meditations on various names, bathing at places of pilgrimage on specific days, charity, self-imposed discipline, penances, visit to holy places, fasting

column2: ਹੋਮ ਜਗ ਭੋਗ ਨਈਬੇਦ ਨਹੀ ਦੇਵੀ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ; ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਨ ਸੰਬਾਦ ਆਨ ਦੁਆਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: For her, sacrificial fire, yoga, offerings, arid other rituals connected with worship of gods and goddesses are meaningless. She is not interested in any modes of singing, musical instruments, rationale and illogic or going to any other door.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਮੈ ਏਕ ਟੇਕ ਹੀ ਪ੍ਰਧਾਨ; ਆਨ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਸਿਮਰਨ ਬਿਬਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੮੨॥
column5: Similarly, like a faithful wife, the devoted Sikhs of the True Guru, must consider and adopt the refuge of the Guru as their primary means (of happiness and peace). For them, meditating on other incantations or focusing their mind on other teachings and d

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਤਿਬ੍ਰਤਾ ਕਉ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਘਰਿ ਵਾਸ ਨ੍ਹਾਤ; ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਧਨ ਧਾਮ ਲੋਕਾਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as living in her house, bathing, eating and sleeping etc. and discharging her worldly duties according to the social customs and traditions are all sacred for a faithful and loyal wife.

column2: ਤਾਤ ਮਾਤ ਭ੍ਰਾਤ ਸੁਤ ਸੁਜਨ ਕੁਟੰਬ ਸਖਾ; ਸੇਵਾ ਗੁਰਜਨ ਸੁਖ ਅਭਰਨ ਸ਼ਿੰਗਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: It is her natural duty to embellish herself with ornaments for the happiness of her husband beside serving and respecting parents, brothers, sisters, sons, other elders in the family, friends and other social contacts.

column2: ਕਿਰਤਿ ਬਿਰਤਿ ਪਰਸੂਤ ਮਲ ਮੂਤ੍ਰਧਾਰੀ; ਸਕਲ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਜੋਈ ਬਿਬਿਧਿ ਅਚਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Attending to the household chores, bearing children, bringing them up, keeping them clean and neat is all sacred for a faithful and loyal wife.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖਨ ਕਉ ਲੇਪੁ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਸਤ ਮੈ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਧ੍ਰਿਗ ਜਨਮੁ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਹੈ॥੪੮੩॥
column5: Similarly, the disciples of the Guru are never blemished while leading a householder's life. Like loyal and faithful wife, they consider indulgence of worship of any other god over the True Guru as a condemnable act in the world. (483)

column2: ਆਦਿਤ ਅਉ ਸੋਮ ਭੌਮ ਬੁਧ ਹੂੰ ਬ੍ਰਹਸਪਤਿ; ਸੁਕਰ ਸਨੀਚਰ ਸਾਤੋ ਬਾਰ ਬਾਂਟ ਲੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Starting from Sunday, all seven days of the week are overtaken by the gods like, Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Saturn respectively.

column2: ਥਿਤਿ ਪਛ ਮਾਸ ਰੁਤਿ ਲੋਗਨ ਮੈ ਲੋਗਾਚਾਰ; ਏਕ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਕੋ ਨ ਕੋਊ ਦਿਨ ਦੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: For fulfillment of all rites and rituals related to god-land, the society has further divided the time into bright and dark period. (waxing and wanning of moon) twelve months and six seasons. But not a day has been set aside for the remembrance and in the

column2: ਜਨਮ ਅਸਟਮੀ ਰਾਮ ਨਉਮੀ ਏਕਾਦਸੀ ਭਈ; ਦੁਆਦਸੀ ਚਤੁਰਦਸੀ ਜਨਮੁ ਏ ਕੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: God is free of births but Janam Ashtami, Ram Naumi and Ekadashi are the birth days of Lord Krishna, Lord Rama, and god Haribasar. Duadasi is the day of Vaman god, while Chaudasi is the day of Narsinh. These days have been fixed as birthdays of these gods.

column2: ਪਰਜਾ ਉਪਾਰਜਨ ਕੋ ਨ ਕੋਊ ਪਾਵੈ ਦਿਨ; ਅਜੋਨੀ ਜਨਮ ਦਿਨੁ ਕਹੌ ਕੈਸੇ ਚੀਨੇ ਹੈ॥੪੮੪॥
column5: No one can tell the day of the creation of this cosmos. Then how can one know the birthday of such Lord who is Ajuni (beyond birth) ? Thus worship of gods who are born and who die is futile. Worship of eternal Lord is only purposeful. (484)

column2: ਜਾ ਕੋ ਨਾਮੁ ਹੈ ਅਜੋਨੀ ਕੈਸੇ ਕੈ ਜਨਮੁ ਲੈ; ਕਹਾ ਜਾਨ ਬ੍ਰਤ ਜਨਮਾਸਟਮੀ ਕੋ ਕੀਨੋ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord whose name is Ajoni (who is never born), how could He have taken birth. And on what reason has the foolish people fixed the Janam Ashtami (the birthday of Krishan Ji) as a day of fasting?

column2: ਜਾ ਕੋ ਜਗਜੀਵਨ ਅਕਾਲ ਅਬਿਨਾਸੀ ਨਾਮੁ; ਕੈਸੇ ਕੈ ਬਧਿਕ ਮਾਰਿਓ ਅਪਜਸੁ ਲੀਨੋ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord whose name is Akal (beyond times), Eternal and who is the life-support of the whole world, how could a hunter had killed Him in the form of Krishan and earned infamy?

column2: ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਮੋਖ ਪਦੁ ਜਾ ਕੇ ਨਾਮਿ; ਗੋਪੀਨਾਥ ਕੈਸੇ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਰਹ ਦੁਖ ਦੀਨੋ ਹੈ॥
column5: The Lord whose name makes a person do good, whose name frees a person from all vices, who is emancipator, how could He be the Master of milkmaids in the form of Krishan and make them suffer in His separation?

column2: ਪਾਹਨ ਕੀ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਮਾ ਕੇ ਅੰਧ ਕੰਧ ਹੈ ਪੂਜਾਰੀ; ਅੰਤਰਿ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਮਤਿ ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਰ ਹੀਨੋ ਹੈ॥੪੮੫॥
column5: Those who are bereft of the initiation of True Guru, support ignorant mind in them. Such ignorants and blind people create the idols of the Life-giving, Imperishable, Timeless and Blemishless Lord reducing Him into gods and then become their followers and

column2: ਸੂਰਜ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ. ਨਾਸ ਉਡਗਨ ਅਗਨਿਤ ਜਿਉ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਕੇ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as with Sunrise, the stars disappear; similarly a Sikh feels unconcerned about the worship and service of gods and goddesses due to the knowledge acquired from the True Guru and practicing and focusing of the mind on his words.

column2: ਹਾਟ ਬਾਟ ਘਾਟ ਠਾਟੁ ਘਟੈ ਘਟੈ ਨਿਸ ਦਿਨੁ; ਤੈਸੇ ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਭੇਦ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as the charm of shops, pathways, roads and quays reduce with time, so do the doubts and ignorance created by the worldly knowledge, rationale and illogic of Vedas diminish with the appearance of the True Guru's knowledge.

column2: ਚੋਰ ਜਾਰ ਅਉ ਜੂਆਰ ਮੋਹ ਦ੍ਰੋਹ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ; ਪ੍ਰਾਤ ਸਮੈ ਸੋਭਾ ਨਾਮ ਦਾਨ ਇਸਨਾਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: The activities of thieves, evil persons and gamblers thrive in the darkness of the night but on daybreak the unique influence of bathing and meditation as drilled by the True Guru in His disciples become conspicuous.

column2: ਆਨ ਸਰ ਮੇਡਕੁ ਸਿਵਾਲ ਘੋਘਾ; ਮਾਨਸਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਬ੍ਰਹਮ ਗੁਰ ਸਰਬ ਨਿਧਾਨ ਕੈ॥੪੮੬॥
column5: Worshippers of other gods and goddesses can only be the dross of the tri-trait maya or the frogs of some pond and even useless shells in the sand. But in the Mansarover-like congregation, all treasures and invaluable commodities providing Naam, blessed by

column2: ਨਿਸ ਦਿਨ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਜਿਉ ਅੰਤਰੁ ਬਖਾਨੀਅਤ; ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Serving and worshipping of gods and goddesses viz-a-viz the True Guru is like the difference between night and day.

column2: ਨਿਸ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਬਹੁ ਤਾਰਿਕਾ ਚਮਤਕਾਰ; ਦਿਨ ਦਿਨਕਰ ਏਕੰਕਾਰ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: In the darkness of night (ignorance), there is much radiance of stars (gods) but with the appearance of True Guru's radiance of knowledge (with the rise of the Sun during the day) God, the Only One becomes conspicuous and obvious.

column2: ਨਿਸ ਅੰਧਿਆਰੀ ਮੈ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ ਹੈ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਹੇਤੁ; ਪ੍ਰਾਤ ਸਮੈ ਨੇਹੁ ਨਿਰੰਕਾਰੀ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: The vice and evil-doers are enamored by the evil and vicious acts, but by the knowledge of the True Guru, the devoted Sikhs contemplate on the Lord's name at ambrosial hour by becoming one with Him.

column2: ਰੈਨ ਸੈਨ ਸਮੈ ਠਗ ਚੋਰ ਜਾਰ ਹੋਇ ਅਨੀਤ; ਰਾਜਨੀਤਿ ਰੀਤਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਬਾਸੁਰ ਬਖਾਨੀਐ॥੪੮੭॥
column5: At night when the time for sleep comes, the evil designs of treacherous, deceitful and vicious people prevail. But with the daybreak at ambrosial hour (radiance of knowledge by the True Guru) the righteousness and justice of the Lord prevails. (The divine

column2: ਨਿਸ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਹੁਇ ਅਧਰਮ ਕਰਮ ਹੇਤੁ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਬਾਸੁਰ ਸੁ ਧਰਮ ਕਰਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: A base wisdom is full of ignorance. It encourages sin and evil deeds. The wisdom given by the True Guru is like the brightness of the day that pronounces righteous deeds.

column2: ਦਿਨਕਰਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੋ ਉਦੋਤ ਸਭ ਕਿਛੁ ਸੂਝੈ; ਨਿਸ ਅੰਧਿਆਰੀ ਭੂਲੇ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਭਰਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: With the emergence of Sun-like teachings of the True Guru, all that would stand in good stead becomes conspicuous. But consider any idol worship as dark night where one keeps wandering in doubts and suspicions by going wayward from the True path.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਦਿਬਿ ਦੇਹ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਹੁਇ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਹੁਇ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਚਰਮ ਹੈ॥
column5: By the virtues of Naam obtained from the True Guru an obedient Sikh becomes capable of seeing all that is not visible openly or conspicuously. Whereas the followers of gods and goddesses remain manifested with evil or sinning vision.

column2: ਸੰਸਾਰੀ ਸੰਸਾਰੀ ਸੰਗਿ ਅੰਧ ਅੰਧ ਕੰਧ ਲਾਗੈ; ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੰਧ ਪਰਮਾਰਥ ਮਰਮੁ ਹੈ॥੪੮੮॥
column5: The worldly people's association with gods and goddesses for acquiring worldly pleasures from them, is just like a blind person is holding on to the shoulder of a blind person in search of the right path. But those Sikhs who are united with the True Guru

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਮਿਲਿ ਬਹੁ ਬਰਨ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਬਨ ਚੰਦਨ ਕਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as water produces vegetation of various colours and forms, but the fragrance of Sandalwood makes all other vegetation around it smelling like itself (Just as water brings variety in vegetation, so is the association with gods and goddesses who make o

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਅਗਨਿ ਅਗਨਿ ਧਾਤੁ ਜੋਈ ਸੋਈ ਦੇਖੀਅਤਿ; ਪਾਰਸ ਪਰਸਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਕੰਚਨ ਧਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a metal shines like fire when placed in it, but in reality it is no different than what it is. But with a touch of philosopher stone, the same metal becomes gold and shines like it.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਮਿਟਤ ਨਹੀ ਕੁਟੇਵ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵ ਭੈਜਲ ਤਰਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Similarly the service of other gods and goddesses cannot destroy the dross of ego of many births. But successful service of the reffulgent True Guru sails one across the worldly ocean.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸੁਖ ਫਲ ਮਹਾਤਮ ਅਗਾਧ ਬੋਧ; ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨੇਤਿ ਨਮੋ ਨਮੋ ਉਚਰਤ ਹੈ॥੪੮੯॥
column5: Significance and the ecstasy of True Guru blessed Naam Simran is beyond explanation. That is why all supplicate and salute Him saying repeatedly-Not this, Not this and Not even this. (489)

column2: ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਬਿਬਿਚਾਰ ਕਰੈ ਗਨਿਕਾ ਪੈ; ਤਾਹਿ ਲੋਗ ਬੇਦ ਅਰੁ ਗਿਆਨ ਕੀ ਨ ਕਾਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A whore openly commits vice with other men. She has no regard and respect for the morality or code of conduct as laid down in social and religious books.

column2: ਕੁਲਾ ਬਧੂ ਛਾਡਿ ਭਰਤਾਰ ਆਨ ਦੁਆਰ ਜਾਇ; ਲਾਛਨੁ ਲਗਾਵੈ ਕੁਲ ਅੰਕੁਸ ਨ ਮਾਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: But if a lady of respectable family goes to another man tarnishes the image of her family.

column2: ਕਪਟ ਸਨੇਹੀ ਬਗ ਧਿਆਨ ਆਨ ਸਰ ਫਿਰੈ; ਮਾਨਸਰ ਛਾਡੈ ਹੰਸ ਬੰਸ ਮੈ ਅਗਿਆਨਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: An egret with false love in his heart wanders from one pond to the other. But if someone belonging to the family of swans (Sikh of the Guru) leaves the Mansarover lake like congregation of the True Guru, that ignorant person is a big fool.

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ; ਪਰ ਤਨ ਧਨ ਲੇਪ ਨਿਰਲੇਪ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥੪੯੦॥
column5: An obedient Sikh of the True Guru engrosses his mind in the consecrated words of wisdom of the True Guru, keeps himself unsullied by the evils of other's wealth and other's body. But one separated from the True Guru and worshipper of gods and goddesses, r

column2: ਪਾਨ ਕਪੂਰ ਲਉਂਗ ਚਰ ਕਾਗੈ ਆਗੈ ਰਾਖੈ; ਬਿਸਟਾ ਬਿਗੰਧ ਖਾਤ ਅਧਿਕ ਸਿਯਾਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: If aromatic substances like betel leaf, camphor, clove etc. are placed before a crow, even then by his notion of being wise, he will eat filth and bad smelling things.

column2: ਬਾਰ ਬਾਰ ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਜਉ ਪੈ ਗੰਗਾ ਇਸਨਾਨੁ ਕਰੈ; ਟਰੈ ਨ ਕੁਟੇਵ. ਦੇਵ ਹੋਤ ਨ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: If a dog bathes in river Ganges many a time, even then he can't get over his bad habit of eating the left overs. Because of this foolishness, he cannot be of divine disposition.

column2: ਸਾਪਹਿ ਪੈ ਪਾਨ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਮਹਾਂ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਕੈ; ਉਗਲਤ ਕਾਲਕੂਟ ਹਉਮੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨ ਕੈ॥
column5: If a snake is served very sweetened milk, even then intoxicated with pride, he will spill out venom.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਨਸਰ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਰਾਲ ਸਭਾ; ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕ ਤਕਤ ਬਗ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ॥੪੯੧॥
column5: Similarly, the Mansarover lake like congregation is an assembly of Sikhs of the Guru who pick pearls from there. But if this assembly is also visited by a follower of gods and goddesses, he would be looking around at others, their wealth with evil eyes an

column2: ਚਕਈ ਚਕੋਰ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਸਸਿ ਭਾਨ ਧਿਆਨ; ਜਾਹੀ ਜਾਹੀ ਰੰਗ ਰਚਿਓ ਤਾਹੀ ਤਾਹੀ ਚਾਹੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: The attention of a Ruddy sheldrake and of Allectoris graeca is always towards Sun and Moon respectively. One loves only that in whom one's mind gets engrossed.

column2: ਮੀਨ ਅਉ ਪਤੰਗ ਜਲ ਪਾਵਕ ਪ੍ਰਸੰਗਿ ਹੇਤ; ਟਾਰੀ ਨ ਟਰਤ ਟੇਵ ਓਰ ਨਿਰਬਾਹੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: In the context of love, fish loves water while a moth is mad over the flame of fire. Their habit of loving cannot be stopped and they live through their love till their last breath.

column2: ਮਾਨਸਰ ਆਨ ਸਰ ਹੰਸ ਬਗ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਰੀਤਿ; ਉਤਮ ਅਉ ਨੀਚ ਨ ਸਮਾਨ ਸਮਤਾ ਹੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: In the context of love, a swan is associated with Mansarover while an egret is found in ponds and puddles. There cannot be any equality in the love of high and low.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਦੇਵ ਆਨ ਦੇਵ ਸੇਵਕਨ ਭੇਦ; ਸਮਸਰ ਨ ਹੋਤ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਹੈ ਜੀ॥੪੯੨॥
column5: Similarly, there is much difference in the love of the Sikhs of the Guru and followers of gods and goddesses. True Guru is like ocean full of divine virtues whereas gods and goddesses are like rivers and brooks. Ocean and streams can never be alike. (492

column2: ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਪੇਖੈ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬੁ ਚਕਈ ਜਿਉ ਨਿਸਿ; ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਆਪਾ ਆਪ ਚੀਨ ਪਹਿਚਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as Ruddy sheldrake looks amorously at her shadow during moonlit nights believing it to be her beloved, so does a Sikh of the Guru recognises the existence of his dear Lord within him and engrosses himself in it.

column2: ਬੈਰ ਭਾਇ ਪੇਖਿ ਪਰਛਾਈ ਕੂਪੰਤਰਿ ਪਰੈ; ਸਿੰਘੁ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਲਾਗਿ ਦੁਬਿਧਾ ਕੈ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a lion sees his own shadow in the well and under the influence of his jealous feelings, considers it another lion and pounces upon it; similarly a Manmukh separated from his Guru due to his base wisdom is seen entangled in doubts.

column2: ਗਊ ਸੁਤ ਅਨੇਕ ਏਕ ਸੰਗ ਹਿਲਿ ਮਿਲਿ ਰਹੈ; ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਆਨ ਦੇਖਤ ਬਿਰੁਧ ਜੁਧ ਠਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as several calves of cow live together in harmony, so do the obedient sons (Sikhs) of the Guru live in love and brotherhood with each other. But a dog cannot stand another dog and fights with him. (So do the self-willed persons are ever ready to pick

column2: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਚੰਦਨ ਅਉ ਬਾਂਸ ਬਿਧਿ; ਬਰਨ ਕੋ ਦੋਖੀ ਬਿਕਾਰੀ ਉਪਕਾਰੀ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥੪੯੩॥
column5: The behaviour of Guru-conscious and self-conscious persons is like sandalwood and bamboo. Evil persons fight with others and destroy themselves as bamboos set themselves on fire. On the contrary, virtuous people are seen doing good to their companions. (

column2: ਜਉ ਕੋਊ ਬੁਲਾਵੈ ਕਹਿ ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਸਰਪ ਕੈ; ਸੁਨਤ ਰਿਜਾਇ ਧਾਇ ਗਾਰਿ ਮਾਰਿ ਦੀਜੀਐ॥
column5: If a forgetful person is addressed by somebody as dog, animal or snake, he comes into rage and pounces on him as if he is going to kill him (Such a person is worst than these three species) because-

column2: ਸ੍ਵਾਨ ਸ੍ਵਾਮਿ ਕਾਮ ਲਾਗਿ ਜਾਮਨੀ ਜਾਗਤ ਰਹੈ; ਨਾਦਹਿ ਸੁਨਾਇ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਹਾਨਿ ਕੀਜੀਐ॥
column5: A dog remains watchful of his master throughout the night and serves him, and a deer goes to the extent of losing his life when it hears the musical sound of Ghanda Herha.

column2: ਧੁਨ ਮੰਤ੍ਰ ਪੜੈ ਸਰਪ ਅਰਪ ਦੇਤ ਤਨ ਮਨ; ਦੰਤ ਹੰਤ ਹੋਤ ਗੋਤ ਲਾਜਿ ਗਹਿ ਲੀਜੀਐ॥
column5: Bewitched by the sound of snake charmer's flute and incantation of Garud, a snake surrenders himself to the charmer. The charmer breaks his fangs and invoking him with the name of his family, catches him.

column2: ਮੋਹ ਨ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਵ ਸਬਦ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਹੀਨਿ; ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਬਿਨੁ ਧ੍ਰਿਗੁ ਜਗਿ ਜੀਜੀਐ॥੪੯੪॥
column5: He who has turned himself away from the True Guru cannot have dog-like love for his Master Lord. They are even bereft of enchantment of the music (unlike deer) and without the consecration of the True Guru's incantations, their living life in the world is

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਘਰਿ ਲਾਗੈ ਆਗਿ ਜਾਗਿ ਕੂਆ ਖੋਦਿਓ ਚਾਹੈ; ਕਾਰਜੁ ਨ ਸਿਧਿ ਹੋਇ ਰੋਇ ਪਛੁਤਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as while sleeping, someone's house catches fire and he wakes up and starts digging well, he cannot succeed in putting off the fire. Instead, he then repents and cries.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਸੰਗ੍ਰਾਮ ਸਮੈ ਸੀਖਿਓ ਚਾਹੈ ਬੀਰ ਬਿਦਿਆ; ਅਨਿਥਾ ਉਦਮ ਜੈਤ ਪਦਵੀ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as someone wants to learn the art of warfare when the battle is in progress, it is a futile effort. Victory cannot be achieved.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਨਿਸਿ ਸੋਵਤ ਸੰਘਾਤੀ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਤਿ; ਪਾਛੇ ਭੋਰ ਭਏ ਭਾਰ ਬਾਂਧ ਚਲੇ ਕਤ ਜਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as a traveller goes to sleep at night and all his companions proceed further leaving him behind, then where will he go with all his luggage when the day breaks?

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਧੰਧ ਅੰਧ ਅਵਧਿ ਬਿਹਾਇ ਜਾਇ; ਅੰਤਕਾਲ ਕੈਸੇ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ॥੪੯੫॥
column5: Similarly, an ignorant man entangled in worldly love and attachments, spends his life accumulating wealth. How can he engross his mind in the name of the Lord when he is on his last breaths? (495)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਚਪਲ ਜਲ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਨ ਦੇਖੀਅਤਿ; ਪੂਰਨੁ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਬਿੰਬ ਰਵਿ ਸਸਿ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as one cannot see full image of Sun or Moon in unstable and wavy water.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਮਲੀਨ ਦਰਪਨ ਮੈ ਨ ਦੇਖੀਅਤਿ; ਨਿਰਮਲ ਬਦਨ ਸਰੂਪ ਉਰਬਸਿ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as one cannot see the complete beauty of the face of Urvashi the divine fairy in a dirty mirror.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਦੀਪ ਨ ਸਮੀਪ ਕੋ ਬਿਲੋਕੀਅਤ; ਭਵਨ ਭਇਆਨ ਅੰਧਕਾਰ ਤ੍ਰਾਸ ਤਸ ਕੋ॥
column5: Just as without the light of a lamp, one cannot even see a thing lying close by. A house in darkness looks scary and frightening beside fear of intrusion of thieves.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਾਇਆ ਧਰਮ ਅਧਮ ਅਛਾਦਿਓ ਮਨੁ; ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਸੁਖ ਨਾਨ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸ ਕੋ॥੪੯੬॥
column5: So is the mind entangled in the darkness of mammon (maya). An ignorant mind cannot enjoy the unique bliss of the contemplation of True Guru and meditation on Lord's name. (496)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਸਮੈ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਸਫਲ ਸਪਤ੍ਰ ਪੁਨਿ; ਏਕ ਸਮੈ ਫੂਲ ਫਲ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਗਿਰ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a tree is full of fruits and leaves at one time and then at another time, all leaves, fruits etc. fall off.

column2: ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਲਿਲ ਜੈਸੇ ਕਬਹੂੰ ਸਮਾਨ ਬਹੈ; ਕਬਹੂੰ ਅਥਾਹ ਅਤਿ ਪ੍ਰਬਲ ਦਿਖਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a stream flows calmly at a place but at another place it is rapid and noisy.

column2: ਏਕ ਸਮੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਹੀਰਾ ਹੋਤ ਜੀਰਨਾਂਬਰ ਮੈ; ਏਕ ਸਮੈ ਕੰਚਨ ਜੜੇ ਜਗਮਗਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a diamond is wrapped in a (silk) rag at one time. but at another time, the same diamond is inlaid in gold and shines with its grandeur.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਰਾਜ ਕੁਮਾਰ ਜੋਗੀਸੁਰ ਹੈ; ਮਾਇਆਧਾਰੀ ਭਾਰੀ ਜੋਗ ਜੁਗਤ ਜੁਗਾਤ ਹੈ॥੪੯੭॥
column5: Similarly, an obedient Sikh of the Guru is a prince at one time and a supreme ascetic at another time. Even when he is wealthy, he still remains absorbed in the methods of realisation of the Lord. (497)

column2: ਅਸਨ ਬਸਨ ਸੰਗ ਲੀਨੇ ਅਉ ਬਚਨ ਕੀਨੇ; ਜਨਮ ਲੈ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰ ਅਰਾਧਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: This human being brings his food and clothing from the Lord when he takes birth and he promises Him that he will keep company of noble souls and meditate on His name.

column2: ਈਹਾਂ ਆਏ ਦਾਤਾ ਬਿਸਰਾਏ ਦਾਸੀ ਲਪਟਾਏ; ਪੰਚ ਦੂਤ ਭੂਤ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਭ੍ਰਮਤ ਅਸਾਧਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: But once he comes to this world, he forsakes the all-giving God and gets enamored with His maid-servant-maya.. He then wanders in the dragon net of the five demons like lust, anger etc. There is no remedy for his escape.

column2: ਸਾਚੁ ਮਰਨੋ ਬਿਸਾਰ. ਜੀਵਨ ਮਿਥਿਆ ਸੰਸਾਰ; ਸਮਝੈ ਨ ਜੀਤ ਹਾਰ ਸੁਪਨ ਸਮਾਧਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: A human being forgets this truth that the world is false and death is real. He does not understand what is beneficial to him and what causes him loss. Engrossing oneself in the worldly goods is sure defeat, while living life in the contemplation of the Tr

column2: ਅਉਸਰ ਹੁਇ ਹੈ ਬਿਤੀਤ ਲੀਜੀਏ ਜਨਮੁ ਜੀਤਿ; ਕੀਜੀਐ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਅਗਮ ਅਗਾਧਿ ਹੈ॥੪੯੮॥
column5: Therefore, 0 fellow being! the time of this life is passing by. You must win the game of life. Enjoin the holy gathering of saintly souls and develop your love for the infinite Lord. (498)

column2: ਸਫਲ ਜਨੰਮੁ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਲਿਵ; ਸਫਲ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸ ਅਲੋਈਐ॥
column5: Human life is successful if spent in the refuge of the True Guru remembering the Supreme Being. Vision of the eyes is purposeful if it has the desire to see Him.

column2: ਸਫਲ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਨਤ ਨਿਤ; ਜਿਹਬਾ ਸਫਲ ਗੁਨਨਿਧਿ ਗੁਨ ਗੋਈਐ॥
column5: Their hearing power is fruitful who hear that creative sound of the True Guru all the time. That tongue is blessed if it keeps on uttering the virtues of the Lord.

column2: ਸਫਲ ਹਸਤ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਪੂਜਾ ਪ੍ਰਨਾਮ; ਸਫਲ ਚਰਨ ਪਰਦਛਨਾ ਕੋ ਪੋਈਐ॥
column5: Hands are blessed if they serve the True Guru and keep on praying to Him at His feet. Those feet are blessed that keep moving circumambulating the True Guru.

column2: ਸੰਗਮ ਸਫਲ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸਹਜ ਘਰਿ ਹਿਰਦਾ ਸਫਲ ਗੁਰਮਤਿ ਕੈ ਸਮੋਈਐ॥੪੯੯॥
column5: Union with saintly congregation is blessed if it brings in a state of equipoise. The mind is blessed only when it imbibes the teachings of the True Guru. (499)

column2: ਕਤ ਪੁਨਿ ਮਾਨਸ ਜਨਮੁ ਕਤ ਸਾਧਸੰਗ; ਨਿਸਦਿਨ ਕੀਰਤਨ ਸਮੈ ਚਲਿ ਜਾਈਐ॥
column5: After wandering through eighty-four lakh species, we are blessed with this human birth. If we miss this opportunity, when will we get it again and when will we enjoy the company of the saintly persons? Therefore, we should attend the holy congregation day

column2: ਕਤ ਪੁਨਿ ਦ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਦਰਸ ਹੁਇ ਪਰਸਪਰ; ਭਾਵਨੀ ਭਗਤਿ ਭਾਇ ਸੇਵਾ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: When will I have face to face glimpse of the True Guru and receive His grace? Therefore I should engross my mind in the loving worship and devotion of the Lord.

column2: ਕਤ ਪੁਨਿ ਰਾਗ ਨਾਦ ਬਾਦ ਸੰਗੀਤ ਰੀਤਿ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਧੁਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਪੁਨਿ ਗਾਈਐ॥
column5: When will I get the opportunity of listening to the divine compositions of the True Guru in the accompaniment of musical instruments and sung in the classical mode of singing? Therefore I must find all possible occasions to hear and sing the praises of th

column2: ਕਤ ਪੁਨਿ ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਤਾਸ ਲੇਖ ਮਸੁਵਾਨੀ; ਸ੍ਰੀ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਲਿਖਿ ਨਿਜ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਈਐ॥੫੦੦॥
column5: When will I get a chance to write the name of the Lord on the paper-like mind with the conscious-like ink? Therefore I should write the True Guru blessed word on the paper-like heart and reach the self-realisation (through constant meditation). (500)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਪਲਾਸ ਪਤ੍ਰ ਨਾਗਬੇਲ ਮੇਲ ਭਏ; ਪਹੁਚਤ ਕਰਿ ਨਰਪਤਿ ਜਗ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a meritless leaf of Butea frondosa when enjoins a betel leaf can reach the hands of the king and this is known the world over.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਕੁਚੀਲ ਨੀਲ ਬਰਨੁ ਬਰਨ ਬਿਖੈ; ਹੀਰ ਚੀਰ ਸੰਗਿ ਨਿਰਦੋਖ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as blue colour is regarded dirty amongst all the colours, but a dress of that colour when enjoins a diamond is considered flawless and unadulterated.

column2: ਸਾਲਗ੍ਰਾਮ ਸੇਵਾ ਸਮੈ ਮਹਾ ਅਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਸੰਖ; ਪਰਮਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਜਗ ਭੋਗ ਬਿਖੈ ਆਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a conch is high by impious, being the skeleton of a sea worm, but its sounding at the time of worship of idols, distribution of consecrated offerings and holding of yoga is considered supreme and sacred.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਮ ਕਾਗ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਰਾਲ ਮਾਲ; ਮਾਰ ਨ ਉਠਾਵਤ ਗਾਵਤ ਗੁਰਬਾਨੀਐ॥੫੦੧॥
column5: Similarly, a congregation of saintly persons in the presence of True Guru is an assembly of swans where I, person of crow- like temperament indulging in singing of Gurbani not turned out and away. (501)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਲ ਮਧਿ ਮੀਨ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਪੁਨਿ; ਜਲ ਬਿਨੁ ਤਲਫ ਤਲਫ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a fish does not understand the importance of water while swimming in it but she realises its importance when separated from it and dies yearning for are-union.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਨ ਬਸਤ ਮਹਾਤਮੈ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਪੁਨਿ; ਪਰ ਬਸ ਭਏ ਖਗ ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a deer and a bird living in a jungle do not realize its importance but realises its significance when caught and put in a cage by the hunter and wail for going back to the jungle.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਸੰਗਮ ਕੈ ਸੁਖਹਿ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਆ; ਬਿਛੁਰਤ ਬਿਰਹ ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾ ਕੈ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife does not appreciate the importance of staying with her husband when together but comes to her senses when separated from her husband. She wails and cries due to pangs of separation from him.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਆਤਮਾ ਅਚੇਤ; ਅੰਤਰੁ ਪਰਤ ਸਿਮਰਤ ਪਛੁਤਾਤ ਹੈ॥੫੦੨॥
column5: Similarly, a seeker living in the refuge of the True Guru remains oblivious of the greatness of Guru. But when separated from Him, repents and laments. (502)

column2: ਭਗਤ ਵਛਲ ਸੁਨਿ ਹੋਤ ਨਿਰਾਸ ਰਿਦੈ; ਪਤਿਤ ਪਾਵਨ ਸੁਨਿ ਆਸਾ ਉਰਧਾਰਿ ਹੌਂ॥
column5: O Lord, when I hear that you are beloved of those who worship you all the time, I, who is bereft of your worship become sad and disappointed. But on hearing that you forgive sinners and make them pious, a ray of hope kindles in my heart.

column2: ਅੰਤਰਜਾਮੀ ਸੁਨਿ ਕੰਪਤ ਹੌ ਅੰਤਰਗਤਿ; ਦੀਨ ਕੋ ਦਇਆਲ ਸੁਨਿ ਭੈ ਭ੍ਰਮ ਟਾਰਿ ਹੌਂ॥
column5: I, the evil-doer, when hear that you are knower of innate feelings and thoughts of everyone, I tremble within. But hearing that you are clement on poor and destitutes, I shed all my fears.

column2: ਜਲਧਰ ਸੰਗਮ ਕੈ ਅਫਲ ਸੇਂਬਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ; ਚੰਦਨ ਸੁਗੰਧ ਸਨਬੰਧ ਮਲਗਾਰ ਹੌਂ॥
column5: Just as a silk cotton tree (Bombax heptaphylum) is well spread and high, it does not bear any flower or fruit even during rainy season, but when brought closer to sandalwood tree becomes equally fragrant. So does an egoistic person coming into contact wit

column2: ਅਪਨੀ ਕਰਨੀ ਕਰਿ ਨਰਕ ਹੂੰ ਨ ਪਾਵਉ ਠਉਰ; ਤੁਮਰੇ ਬਿਰਦ ਕਰਿ ਆਸਰੋ ਸਮ੍ਹਾਰ ਹੌਂ॥੫੦੩॥
column5: Because of my ill deeds, I cannot find a place even in hell. But I am leaning and depending upon your character of merciful, benevolent, clement, and corrector of the evil-doers. (503)

column2: ਜਉ ਹਮ ਅਧਮ ਕਰਮ ਕੈ ਪਤਿਤ ਭਏ; ਪਤਿਤ ਪਾਵਨ ਪ੍ਰਭ ਨਾਮ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: If we have fallen from your favour due to our evil and unrighteous deeds, then 0 Lord! You have made known that You bless the sinners with Your grace and make them good and pious.

column2: ਜਉ ਭਏ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਅਰੁ ਦੀਨ ਪਰਚੀਨ ਲਗਿ; ਦੀਨ ਦੁਖ ਭੰਜਨ ਬਿਰਦੁ ਬਿਰਦਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: If we are suffering due to our ill deeds and sins of the previous births, then 0 Lord! You have made it conspicuous that You dispel the sufferings of the poor and destitudes.

column2: ਜਉ ਗ੍ਰਸੇ ਅਰਕ ਸੁਤ ਨਰਕ ਨਿਵਾਸੀ ਭਏ; ਨਰਕ ਨਿਵਾਰਨ ਜਗਤ ਜਸੁ ਗਾਇਓ ਹੈ॥
column5: If we are in the grip of the angels of death and become deserving of a life in hell because of our bad and evil deeds, then 0 Lord ! The whole world is singing Your paeans that You are the liberator of all from the vagaries of hell.

column2: ਗੁਨ ਕੀਏ ਗੁਨ ਸਭ ਕੋਊ ਕਰੈ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਨਿਧਾਨ; ਅਵਗੁਨ ਕੀਏ ਗੁਨ ਤੋਹੀ ਬਨਿ ਆਇਓ ਹੈ॥੫੦੪॥
column5: O store house of clemency! One. who does good to others begets good in returns. But doing good to low and evil doers like us behoves only You. (You alone can bless and forgive the sins and evil deeds of all). (504)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਅਰੋਗ ਭੋਗ ਭੋਗਵੈ ਨਾਨਾ ਪ੍ਰਕਾਰ; ਬ੍ਰਿਥਾਵੰਤ ਖਾਨਿ ਪਾਨਿ ਰਿਦੈ ਨ ਹਿਤਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a healthy person eats many types of dishes and eatables but a sick man does not like eating any of them.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਹਖੀ ਸਹਨਸੀਲ ਕੈ ਧੀਰਜ ਧੁਜਾ; ਅਜਿਆ ਮੈ ਤਨਕ ਕਲੇਜੋ ਨ ਸਮਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a buffalo, because of its tolerance is known to have great patience but a goat on the other hand does not have even a fraction of that patience.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਜਉਹਰੀ ਬਿਸਾਹੈ ਵੇਚੇ ਹੀਰਾ ਮਾਨਕਾਦਿ; ਰੰਕ ਪੈ ਨ ਰਾਖਿਓ ਪਰੈ ਜੋਗ ਨ ਜੁਗਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a jeweller trades in diamonds and precious stones but no valuable diamond can be kept with a pauper since he does not have the ability to keep such expensive item.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਪਰਚੇ ਪਵਿਤ੍ਰ ਹੈ ਪੂਜਾ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ; ਅਪਚੁ ਅਪਰਚੇ ਦੁਸਹਿ ਦੁਖ ਪਾਵਈ॥੫੦੫॥
column5: Similarly, a devotee who remains engaged in the service and remembrance of the Lord, eating the offerings and consecrated food for him is justified. But he who is far removed from obeying the command of the Guru cannot consume the worship offerings. Consu

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਖੁ ਤਨਕ ਹੀ ਖਾਤ ਮਰਿ ਜਾਤਿ ਤਾਤ; ਗਾਤਿ ਮੁਰਝਾਤ ਪ੍ਰਤਿਪਾਲੀ ਬਰਖਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as by taking a very small quantity of poison, one dies instantly, destroying the body that had been reared and sustained over many years.

column2: ਮਹਿਖੀ ਦੁਹਾਇ ਦੂਧ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਭਾਂਜਨ ਭਰਿ; ਪਰਤਿ ਕਾਂਜੀ ਕੀ ਬੂੰਦ ਬਾਦਿ ਨ ਰਖਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a can of milk of buffalo contaminated with a drop of citric acid becomes useless and not worth keeping.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੋਟਿ ਭਾਰਿ ਤੂਲਿ ਰੰਚਕ ਚਿਨਗ ਪਰੇ; ਹੋਤ ਭਸਮਾਤ ਛਿਨ ਮੈ ਅਕਰਖਨ ਕੀ॥
column5: Just as a spark of fire can burn millions of bales of cotton in a short time.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਤਨ ਪਰ ਧਨ ਦੂਖਨਾ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਕੀਏ; ਹਰੈ ਨਿਧਿ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਸਹਜ ਹਰਖਨ ਕੀ॥੫੦੬॥
column5: Similarly, the vices and sins that one acquires by associating oneself with other's wealth and beauty, one loses the much valuable commodity of happiness, good deeds and peace. (506)

column2: ਚੰਦਨ ਸਮੀਪ ਬਸਿ ਬਾਂਸ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ; ਆਨ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ ਦੂਰਹੁ ਭਏ ਬਾਸਨ ਕੈ ਬੋਹੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Even while living in the close proximity of sandalwood, a bamboo has not appreciated its characteristic of spreading its fragrance whereas other trees become equally fragrant despite their distance from it.

column2: ਦਾਦਰ ਸਰੋਵਰ ਮੈ ਜਾਨੀ ਨ ਕਮਲ ਗਤਿ; ਮਧੁਕਰ ਮਨ ਮਕਰੰਦ ਕੈ ਬਿਮੋਹੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: Staying in a pond, a frog has never appreciated the characteristics of a lotus flower whereas a bumble bee is eternally attracted to its sweet smell even while staying far away from it.

column2: ਤੀਰਥ ਬਸਤ ਬਗੁ ਮਰਮੁ ਨ ਜਾਨਿਓ ਕਛੁ; ਸਰਧਾ ਕੈ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ਹੇਤ ਜਾਤ੍ਰੀ ਜਨ ਸੋਹੇ ਹੈ॥
column5: A heron staying at holy places do not realise the spiritual importance of these places of pilgrimage whereas devoted travellers earn a good name for themselves on their return from there.

column2: ਨਿਕਟ ਬਸਤ ਮਮ ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਹੀਨ; ਦੁਰੰਤਰਿ ਸਿਖਿ ਉਰ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਲੈ ਪੋਹੇ ਹੈ॥੫੦੭॥
column5: Similarly, like bamboo, frog and heron, I am devoid of practicing Guru's teachings despite the fact that I live near my Guru. On the contrary Sikhs residing far off acquire Guru's wisdom and lodge it in their heart to practice on. (507)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਦਾਰਾ ਕੋ ਦਰਸੁ ਦ੍ਰਿਗ ਦੇਖਿਓ ਚਾਹੈ; ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਦਰਸਨ ਦੇਖਤ ਹੈ ਨ ਚਾਹ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a forgetful person does not desire a glimpse of his Guru with the same intensity that he uses his eyes to ogle at other women.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਸੁਨੈ ਸਾਵਧਾਨ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਕੈ; ਤੈਸੋ ਗੁਰਸਬਦ ਸੁਨੈ ਨ ਉਤਸਾਹ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a worldly man listens to the slander of other persons very attentively, he does not listen to the divine words of Guru with same fondness.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਦਰਬ ਹਰਨ ਕਉ ਚਰਨ ਧਾਵੈ; ਤੈਸੇ ਕੀਰਤਨ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਨ ਉਮਾਹ ਕੈ॥
column5: Just as a person greedy of wealth walks a distance to cheat another person of his hard earned money, he does not show the same enthusiasm of going to the divine congregation to listen to the adulations of Almighty.

column2: ਉਲੂ ਕਾਗ ਨਾਗਿ ਧਿਆਨ ਖਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਕਉ ਨ ਜਾਨੈ; ਊਚ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਵੈ ਨਹੀ. ਨੀਚ ਪਦੁ ਗਾਹਕੈ॥੫੦੮॥
column5: Like an owl, I do not know the value of radiance of True Guru, like a crow am not aware of the sweet smelling traits of the True Guru nor do I know the relishment of the elixir like Naam just as a she snake is unaware of the elixir like milk. Thus I canno

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰੈਨਿ ਸਮੈ ਸਬ ਲੋਗ ਮੈ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਭੋਗ; ਚਕਈ ਬਿਓਗ ਸੋਗ ਭਾਗ ਹੀਨ ਜਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as everyone enjoys the company of their dear ones at night, but a ruddy sheldrake is considered unfortunate having been separated from its beloved.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਦਿਨਕਰਿ ਕੈ ਉਦੋਤਿ ਜੋਤਿ ਜਗਮਗ; ਉਲੂ ਅੰਧ ਕੰਧ ਪਰਚੀਨ ਉਨਮਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Just as sunrise brightens up the place but an owl is seen hidden in dark allays and walls.

column2: ਸਰਵਰ ਸਰਿਤਾ ਸਮੁੰਦਰ ਜਲ ਪੂਰਨ ਹੈ; ਤ੍ਰਿਖਾਵੰਤ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਰਹਤ ਬਕਬਾਨੀਐ॥
column5: Ponds, streams and oceans are seen to be full to the brim with water, but longing for rain, a rain-bird remains thirsty and keeps wailing and crying for that Swati drop.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਮਿਲਿ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਿ ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ਤਰਿਓ; ਮੋਹਿ ਅਪਰਾਧੀ ਅਪਰਾਧਨ ਬਿਹਾਨੀਐ॥੫੦੯॥
column5: Similarly by associating themselves with the congregation of the True Guru, the whole world is sailing across the worldly ocean but I, the sinner is spending all his life in evil deeds and vices. (509)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਫਲ ਫੂਲਹਿ ਲੈ ਜਾਇ ਬਨਰਾਇ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਕਰੈ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੁ ਕਹੋ ਕੈਸੇ ਬਨਿ ਆਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone takes a handful of fruits and flowers to present it to the king of the jungle where fruits and flowers abound, and then feels proud of his present, how can he be liked?

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਸਮੁੰਦ੍ਰਹਿ ਦਿਖਾਵੈ ਜਾਇ; ਬਾਰ ਬਾਰ ਹੀ ਸਰਾਹੈ ਸੋਭਾ ਤਉ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone takes a handful of pearls to the treasure house of pearls-ocean, and praises his pearls again and again, he does not earn any appreciation.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਨੀ ਕੰਚਨ ਸੁਮੇਰ ਸਨਮੁਖ ਰਾਖਿ; ਮਨ ਮੈ ਗਰਬੁ ਕਰੈ ਬਾਵਰੋ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone presents a small piece of gold nugget to Sumer mountain (the home of gold) and feels proud of his gold, he would be called a fool.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਠਾਨਿ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਦੈ ਰੀਝਾਇਓ ਚਾਹੈ; ਪ੍ਰਾਨਪਤਿ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੈਸੇ ਕੈ ਰੀਝਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥੫੧੦॥
column5: Similarly if someone talks of knowledge and contemplations and pretends to surrender himself with a view to please and entice the True Guru, he cannot succeed in his nefarious designs of pleasing the True Guru the master of all life. (510)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚੋਆ ਚੰਦਨ ਅਉ ਧਾਨ ਪਾਨ ਬੇਚਨ ਕਉ; ਪੂਰਬ ਦਿਸਾ ਲੈ ਜਾਇ ਕੈਸੇ ਬਨਿ ਆਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone takes the products grown in the East like rice, betel, sandalwood to sell there, he cannot gain anything in their trading.

column2: ਪਛਮ ਦਿਸਾ ਦਾਖ ਦਾਰਮ ਲੈ ਜਾਇ ਜੈਸੇ; ਮ੍ਰਿਗ ਮਦ ਕੇਸਰ ਲੈ ਉਤਰਹਿ ਧਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone takes the products grown in the West like grapes and pomegranates, and those commodities grown in the North like saffron and musk to the West and North respectively, what gain does he get out of such trading?

column2: ਦਖਨ ਦਿਸਾ ਲੈ ਜਾਇ ਲਾਇਚੀ ਲਵੰਗ ਲਾਦਿ; ਬਾਦਿ ਆਸਾ ਉਦਮ ਹੈ ਬਿੜਤੋ ਨ ਪਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as someone takes commodities like cardamom and clove to South where these are grown, all his efforts of earning any profit would be futile.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਨਨਿਧਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਾਗਰ ਕੈ ਬਿਦਿਮਾਨ; ਗਿਆਨ ਗੁਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਟਿ ਕੈ ਬਾਵਰੋ ਕਹਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥੫੧੧॥
column5: Similarly if someone tries to display his traits and knowledge before the True Guru who Himself is ocean of knowledge and divine traits, such a person will be called a fool. (511)

column2: ਚਲਨੀ ਮੈ ਜੈਸੇ ਦੇਖੀਅਤ ਹੈ ਅਨੇਕ ਛਿਦ੍ਰ; ਕਰੈ ਕਰਵਾ ਕੀ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕੈਸੇ ਬਨਿ ਆਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a sieve has so many holes and if it slanders an earthen pot, then how can it be respected.

column2: ਬਿਰਖ ਬਥੂਰ ਭਰਪੂਰ ਬਹੁ ਸੂਰਨ ਸੈ; ਕਮਲੈ ਕਟੀਲੋ ਕਹੈ ਕਾਹੂ ਨ ਸੁਖਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as an acacia tree which is full of thorns call a lotus flower thorny, this allegation will not be appreciated by anyone.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਉਪਹਾਸੁ ਕਰੈ ਬਾਇਸੁ ਮਰਾਲ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ; ਛਾਡਿ ਮੁਕਤਾਹਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਗੰਧ ਲਿਵ ਲਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as leaving pearls, a filth-eating crow cracks a joke on swan, the eater of pearls of lake Mansarover, this is nothing but his dirtiness.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹਉ ਮਹਾ ਅਪ੍ਰਾਧੀ ਅਪਰਾਧ ਭਰਿਓ; ਸਕਲ ਸੰਸਾਰ ਕੋ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਮੋਹਿ ਭਾਵੈ ਜੀ॥੫੧੨॥
column5: Similarly sin-filled I, am a big sinner. The sin of slandering the whole world pleases me. (512)

column2: ਆਪਦਾ ਅਧੀਨ ਜੈਸੇ ਦੁਖਿਤ ਦੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਕੋ; ਸਹਜਿ ਸੁਹਾਗ ਨ ਸੁਹਾਗਨਿ ਕੋ ਭਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a distressed, divorced woman cannot bear to see or tolerate the loving and happy union of another woman with her husband,

column2: ਬਿਰਹਨੀ ਬਿਰਹ ਬਿਓਗ ਮੈ ਸੰਜੋਗਨਿ ਕੋ; ਸੁੰਦਰ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਅਧਿਕਾਰੁ ਨ ਸੁਹਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a woman separated from her husband and bearing the pangs of separation cannot tolerate the embellishments of another woman who is united with her husband,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਨ ਮਾਂਝਿ ਬਾਂਝਿ ਰੋਗ ਸੋਗ ਸੰਸੋ ਸ੍ਰਮ; ਸਉਤ ਕੋ ਸੁਤਹਿ ਪੇਖਿ ਮਹਾਂ ਦੁਖ ਪਾਵਈ॥
column5: Just as a distressed and fatigued woman suffering from her inability to bear a child feels much distressed on seeing the son of her co-wife,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਤਨ ਧਨ ਦੂਖਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਦੋਖ ਮਮ; ਸਾਧਨ ਕੋ ਸੁਕ੍ਰਿਤ ਨ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਹਿਤਾਵਈ॥੫੧੩॥
column5: Similarly I am infested with three chronic ailments-namely other's women, other's wealth and slander. And that is why praise of the devoted and loving Sikhs of the True Guru does not please me. (513)

column2: ਜਲ ਸੈ ਨਿਕਾਸਿ ਮੀਨੁ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਪਟੰਬਰ ਮੈ; ਬਿਨੁ ਜਲ ਤਲਫ ਤਜਤ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਪ੍ਰਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: A fish removed from water, though kept in silk cloth yet dies having been separated from her beloved water.

column2: ਬਨ ਸੈ ਪਕਰਿ ਪੰਛੀ ਪਿੰਜਰੀ ਮੈ ਰਾਖੀਐ ਤਉ; ਬਿਨੁ ਬਨ ਮਨ ਉਨਮਨੋ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a bird is caught from the jungle and put in a beautiful cage with very delicious food, his mind is seen to be restless without the freedom of the jungle.

column2: ਭਾਮਨੀ ਭਤਾਰਿ ਬਿਛੁਰਤ ਅਤਿ ਛੀਨ ਦੀਨ; ਬਿਲਖ ਬਦਨ ਤਾਹਿ ਭਵਨ ਭਇਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a pretty woman becomes weak and grieved on separation from her husband. Her face looks perplexed and confused and she feels scared of her own home.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਬਿਛੁਰਤ ਸਾਧਸੰਗਤਿ ਸੈ; ਜੀਵਨ ਜਤਨ ਬਿਨੁ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਨ ਆਨ ਹੈ॥੫੧੪॥
column5: Similarly separated from the saintly congregation of the True Guru, a Sikh of the Guru wails, tosses and turns, feels miserable and perplexed. Without the company of saintly souls of the True Guru, he has no other aim in life. (514)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਟੂਟੇ ਨਾਗਬੇਲ ਸੈ ਬਿਦੇਸ ਜਾਤਿ; ਸਲਿਲ ਸੰਜੋਗ ਚਿਰੰਕਾਲ ਜੁਗਵਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as betel leaves plucked from the creeper are sent to distant places and if kept in damp cloth remains useful for long,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕੂੰਜ ਬਚਰਾ ਤਿਆਗਿ ਦਿਸੰਤਰਿ ਜਾਤਿ; ਸਿਮਰਤ ਚਿਤਿ ਨਿਰਬਿਘਨ ਰਹਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a crane deposits her young ones and fly out to distant land but always remember them in her mind as a result of which they remain alive and grow,

column2: ਗੰਗੋਦਿਕ ਜੈਸੇ ਭਰਿ ਭਾਂਜਨ ਲੈ ਜਾਤਿ ਜਾਤ੍ਰੀ; ਸੁਜਸੁ ਅਧਾਰ ਨਿਰਮਲ ਨਿਬਹਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as travellers carry water of river Ganges in their container, and being of superior disposition it stays good for long,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਸਿਖ; ਸਬਦੁ ਸੰਗਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਧਿਆਨ ਕੈ ਜੀਅਤ ਹੈ॥੫੧੫॥
column5: Similarly if a Sikh of the True Guru somehow get separated from his Guru, he remains invigorated by virtue of holy congregation, meditation on His name and contemplating and focusing his mind in the holy feet of his True Guru. (515)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਪਵਨ ਕਵਨ ਗੁਨ ਚੰਦਨ ਸੈ; ਬਿਨੁ ਮਲਿਆਗਰ ਪਵਨ ਕਤ ਬਾਸਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a sandalwood tree cannot impart its fragrance to others without breeze and without the air of malay mountain, how can the atmosphere become fragranced,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੈਦ ਅਵਖਦ ਗੁਨ ਗੋਪਿ ਹੋਤ; ਅਵਖਦ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੈਦ ਰੋਗਹਿ ਨ ਗ੍ਰਾਸ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a physician know the merit of every herb or medicine and without medicine, no physician can cure an ailing person,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਨੁ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਨ ਪਾਰਿ ਪਰੈ ਖੇਵਟ ਸੈ; ਖੇਵਟ ਬਿਹੂੰਨ ਕਤ ਬੋਹਿਥ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸੁ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as no one can cross the ocean without sailor nor can it be crossed without a ship,

column2: ਤੈਸੋ ਗੁਰ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨੁ ਗੰਮ ਨ ਪਰਮਪਦੁ; ਬਿਨੁ ਗੁਰ ਨਾਮ ਨਿਹਕਾਮ ਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਹੈ॥੫੧੬॥
column5: Similarly without the boon of Lord's name given by the True Guru, God cannot be realised. And without Naam the liberator from the worldly desires and blessed by the True Guru, no one can acquire spiritual effulgence. (516)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਕਾਚੋ ਪਾਰੋ ਖਾਤ ਉਪਜੈ ਬਿਕਾਰ ਗਾਤਿ; ਰੋਮ ਰੋਮ ਕੈ ਪਿਰਾਤਿ ਮਹਾ ਦੁਖ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as consuming raw mercury causes such a disorder in the body that causes pain in every limb and one feels discomfort.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਲਸਨ ਖਾਏ ਮੋਨਿ ਕੈ ਸਭਾ ਮੈ ਬੈਠੇ; ਪ੍ਰਗਟੈ ਦੁਰਗੰਧ ਨਾਹਿ ਦੁਰਤਿ ਦੁਰਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as after eating garlic one may remain silent in an assembly, even then its foul odour cannot be hidden.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮਿਸਟਾਨ ਪਾਨਿ ਸੰਗਮ ਕੈ ਮਾਖੀ ਲੀਲੇ; ਹੋਤ ਉਕਲੇਦ ਖੇਦੁ ਸੰਕਟ ਸਹਾਈਐ॥
column5: Just as a person may swallow a fly while eating sweetmeat, he vomits immediately. He bears much suffering and distress.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਅਪਰਚੇ ਪਿੰਡ ਸਿਖਨ ਕੀ ਭਿਖਿਆ ਖਾਏ; ਅੰਤ ਕਾਲ ਭਾਰੀ ਹੋਇ ਜਮਲੋਕ ਜਾਈਐ॥੫੧੭॥
column5: Similarly an ignorant person consumes the offerings made by the devotees of the True Guru. He suffers much at the time of his death. He has to face the wrath of the angels of death. (517)

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਮੇਘ ਬਰਖਤ ਹਰਖਤਿ ਹੈ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਾਨ; ਬਿਲਖ ਬਦਨ ਲੋਧਾ ਲੋਨ ਗਰਿ ਜਾਤਿ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a farmer is delighted to see the rainfall but the face of a weaver becomes ashen and he feels restless and miserable.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਪਰਫੁਲਿਤ ਹੁਇ ਸਕਲ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਸੁਕਤ ਜਵਾਸੋ ਆਕ ਮੂਲ ਮੁਰਝਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as all the vegetation turns green with the fall of rain but the plant of camel thorn (Alhagi maurorum) withers while akk (Calotropis procera) dries out right from its roots.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਖੇਤ ਸਰਵਰ ਪੂਰਨ ਕਿਰਖ ਜਲ; ਊਚ ਥਲ ਕਾਲਰ ਨ ਜਲ ਠਹਿਰਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as ponds and fields are filled with water when it rains, but no water can accumulate on mounds and saline land.

column2: ਗੁਰ ਉਪਦੇਸ ਪਰਵੇਸ ਗੁਰਸਿਖ ਰਿਦੈ; ਸਾਕਤ ਸਕਤਿ ਮਨਿ ਸੁਨਿ ਸਕੁਚਾਤ ਹੈ॥੫੧੮॥
column5: Similarly, the sermon of the True Guru permeates in the mind of a Sikh of the Guru, that always keeps him in a state of bloom and happiness. But a self-oriented person who is in the grip of the worldly attractions is ever engrossed in mammon (maya). Thus

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਰਾਜਾ ਰਵਤ ਅਨੇਕ ਰਵਨੀ ਸਹੇਤ; ਸਕਲ ਸਪੂਤੀ ਏਕ ਬਾਂਝ ਨ ਸੰਤਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a king loves many queens who all bear him a son, but there may be one who is barren, who cannot bear any issue.

column2: ਸੀਚਤ ਸਲਿਲ ਜੈਸੇ ਸਫਲ ਸਕਲ ਦ੍ਰੁਮ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਸੇਂਬਲ ਸਲਿਲ ਨਿਰਬਾਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as irrigating the trees help them bear fruits but the cotton silk tree remains fruitless. It does not accept the influence of water.

column2: ਦਾਦਰ ਕਮਲ ਜੈਸੇ ਏਕ ਸਰਵਰ ਬਿਖੈ; ਉਤਮ ਅਉ ਨੀਚ ਕੀਚ ਦਿਨਕਰਿ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a frog and a lotus flower live in one pond but lotus is supreme since it faces Sun and frog is low since it remains engrossed in mud.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਹੈ ਸਕਲ ਜਗੁ; ਚੰਦਨ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ ਬਾਂਸ ਉਨਮਾਨ ਹੈ॥੫੧੯॥
column5: Similarly the whole world comes into the refuge of the True Guru. Devoted Sikhs of the True Guru who expel sandalwood-like fragrance obtain the elixir-like Naam from Him and become fragrant too. But a bamboo-like arrogant, knotty and self wise person rema

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਛੁਰਾ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਮਾਤ ਮਿਲਬੇ ਕਉ; ਬੰਧਨ ਕੈ ਬਸਿ ਕਛੁ ਬਸੁ ਨ ਬਸਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a calf writhes and wriggles to meet his mother but tethered with rope makes him helpless.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਿਗਾਰੀ ਚਾਹੈ ਭਵਨ ਗਵਨ ਕੀਓ; ਪਰ ਬਸਿ ਪਰੇ ਚਿਤਵਤ ਹੀ ਬਿਹਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a person caught in forced or unpaid labour wants to go home and spends time planning while remaining in other's control.

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਬਿਰਹਨੀ ਪ੍ਰਿਅ ਸੰਗਮ ਸਨੇਹੁ ਚਾਹੈ; ਲਾਜ ਕੁਲ ਅੰਕੁਸ ਕੈ ਦੁਰਬਲ ਗਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a wife separated from her husband wants love and union but cannot do so for fear of family shame and thus loses her physical attraction.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਗੁਰ ਚਰਨ ਸਰਨਿ ਸੁਖ ਚਾਹੈ ਸਿਖੁ; ਆਗਿਆ ਬਧ ਰਹਿਤ ਬਿਦੇਸ ਅਕੁਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥੫੨੦॥
column5: Similarly a true disciple wants to enjoy the pleasures of the refuge of True Guru but bound by his command he wanders around dejectedly in another place. (520)

column2: ਪਰ ਧਨ ਪਰ ਤਨ ਪਰ ਅਪਵਾਦ ਬਾਦ; ਬਲ ਛਲ ਬੰਚ ਪਰਪੰਚ ਹੀ ਕਮਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who keeps his interest in other person's wife, wealth and who indulges in other's slander, trickery and cheating,

column2: ਮਿਤ੍ਰ ਗੁਰ ਸ੍ਵਾਮਿ ਦ੍ਰੋਹ ਕਾਮ ਕ੍ਰੋਧ ਲੋਭ ਮੋਹ; ਗੋਬਧ ਬਧੂ ਬਿਸ੍ਵਾਸ ਬੰਸ ਬਿਪ੍ਰ ਘਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: He who betrays a friend, Guru and master, who is caught in the vices of lust, anger, greed and attachment, who kills cow, woman, cheats, betrays his family and murders Brahmin,

column2: ਰੋਗ ਸੋਗ ਹੁਇ ਬਿਓਗ ਅਪਦਾ ਦਰਿਦ੍ਰ ਛਿਦ੍ਰ; ਜਨਮ ਮਰਨ ਜਮ ਲੋਕ ਬਿਲਲਾਤ ਹੈ॥
column5: Who is suffering due to various ailments and distresses, who is troubled, lazy and vice-ridden who is caught in the cycle of birth and death and is in the stranglehold of the angels of death,

column2: ਕ੍ਰਿਤਘਨ ਬਿਸਿਖ ਬਿਖਿਆਦੀ. ਕੋਟਿ ਦੋਖੀ ਦੀਨ; ਅਧਮ ਅਸੰਖ ਮਮ ਰੋਮ ਨ ਪੁਜਾਤ ਹੈ॥੫੨੧॥
column5: Who is ungrateful, venomous and user of arrow-like sharp words, who is miserable due to countless sins, vices or imperfections; such countless evil-doers cannot match even a hair of my sins. I am many time more evil than them. (521)

column2: ਬੇਸ੍ਵਾ ਕੇ ਸਿੰਗਾਰ ਬਿਬਿਚਾਰ ਕੋ ਨ ਪਾਰ ਪਾਈਐ; ਬਿਨੁ ਭਰਤਾਰ ਕਾ ਕੀ ਨਾਰਿ ਕੈ ਬੁਲਾਈਐ॥
column5: The embellishments of a whore and her relationships with many men cannot be fathomed. Without a husband, whose wife can she be known as?

column2: ਬਗੁ ਸੇਤ ਜੀਵ ਘਾਤ ਕਰਿ ਖਾਤ ਕੇਤੇ ਕੋ; ਮੋਨਿ ਗਹਿ ਧਿਆਨ ਧਰੇ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਨ ਪਾਈਐ॥
column5: A heron is white like a swan but it kills many living beings to appease his appetite. To do this evil act, he stands in perfect silence, but in doing so, he does not achieve the know-how of yog.

column2: ਭਾਂਡ ਕੀ ਭੰਡਾਈ ਬੁਰਵਾਈ ਨ ਕਹਤ ਆਵੈ; ਅਤਿ ਹੀ ਢਿਠਾਈ ਸੁਕਚਤ ਨ ਲਜਾਈਐ॥
column5: One cannot explain the shamelessness of the actions and words used by a mimic. He does not shy away from using bad words out of sheer obstinacy.

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਪਰ ਤਨ ਧਨ ਦੂਖਨ ਤ੍ਰਿਦੋਖ ਮਮ; ਅਧਮ ਅਨੇਕ ਏਕ ਰੋਮ ਨ ਪੁਜਾਈਐ॥੫੨੨॥
column5: Similarly, like these low character people, I am low too. I am a chronic patient of the three diseases, that is looking at others wealth, woman and slandering others. Numerous sinners cannot match even a hair of my sinful life. I am lowest of all the low

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਚੋਰ ਚਾਹੀਐ ਚੜਾਇਓ ਸੂਰੀ ਚਉਬਟਾ ਮੈ; ਚੁਹਟੀ ਲਗਾਇ ਛਾਡੀਐ ਤਉ ਕਹਾਂ ਮਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as a thief should be crucified on a cross, but if he is just pinched and let off, it is no punishment for him,

column2: ਖੋਟਸਾਰੀਓ ਨਿਕਾਰਿਓ ਚਾਹੀਐ ਨਗਰ ਹੂੰ ਸੈ; ਤਾਂ ਕੀ ਓਰ ਮੋਰ ਮੁਖ ਬੈਠੇ ਕਹਾਂ ਆਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as manufacturer of counterfeit coins should be exiled. But if we just turn our face away from him, it is no punishment for him,

column2: ਮਹਾਂ ਬਜਰ ਭਾਰੁ ਡਾਰਿਓ ਚਾਹੀਐ ਜਉ ਹਾਥੀ ਪਰ; ਤਾਂਹਿ ਸਿਰ ਛਾਰਿ ਕੇ ਉਡਾਏ ਕਹਾਂ ਭਾਰ ਹੈ॥
column5: As an elephant may be loaded with heavy weight but if just a bit of dust is sprinkled on him, it is no burden for him,

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਹੀ ਪਤਿਤ ਪਤਿ ਕੋਟਿ ਨ ਪਾਸੰਗ ਭਰਿ; ਮੋਹਿ ਜਮ ਡੰਡ ਅਉ ਨਰਕ ਉਪਕਾਰ ਹੈ॥੫੨੩॥
column5: Similarly millions of sins are not even counterweight of my sins. But to punish me with abode in hell and entrusting me to the angels of death is showing mercy on me. (523)

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਚੋਰੁ ਚੋਰੀ ਕੈ ਬਤਾਵੈ ਹੰਸ ਮਾਨਸਰ; ਛੂਟਿ ਕੈ ਨ ਜਾਇ ਘਰਿ. ਸੂਰੀ ਚਾੜਿ ਮਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: If a thief steals and yet declare himself pious like swans of lake Mansarover, he is not forgiven but is crucified and killed.

column2: ਬਾਟ ਮਾਰ ਬਟਵਾਰੋ ਬਗੁ ਮੀਨ ਜਉ ਬਤਾਵੈ; ਤਤਖਿਨ ਤਾਤਕਾਲ ਮੂੰਡ ਕਾਟਿ ਡਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: If a wayside dacoit declares himself kind and good doer of the wayside travellers just as a heron feels towards the fish and frogs in a pond, his claim cannot be accepted and he should be beheaded there and then.

column2: ਜਉ ਪੈ ਪਰ ਦਾਰਾ ਭਜਿ ਮ੍ਰਿਗਨ ਬਤਾਵੈ ਬਿਟੁ; ਕਾਨ ਨਾਕ ਖੰਡ ਡੰਡ ਨਗਰ ਨਿਕਾਰੀਐ॥
column5: Just as a lecherous person declares himself chaste and celibate like the deers of a jungle after committing adultery with some other woman, he is not let off on his statement. Instead his nose and ears are chopped off and he is expelled from the city.

column2: ਚੋਰੀ ਬਟਵਾਰੀ ਪਰ ਨਾਰੀ ਕੈ ਤ੍ਰਿਦੋਖ ਮਮ; ਨਰਕ ਅਰਕ ਸੁਤ ਡੰਡ ਦੇਤ ਹਾਰੀਐ॥੫੨੪॥
column5: A thief, dacoit and a lecherous man is punished so severely for one crime they commit. But I am a sufferer of all these three ailments like tuberculosis. So punishing me for all these sins, the angels of death will get tired. (524)

column2: ਜਾਤ ਹੈ ਜਗਤ੍ਰ ਜੈਸੇ ਤੀਰਥ ਜਾਤ੍ਰਾ ਨਮਿਤ; ਮਾਝ ਹੀ ਬਸਤ ਬਗ ਮਹਿਮਾ ਨ ਜਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as the whole world go to places of pilgrimage, but the egret living there has not appreciated the greatness of these places,

column2: ਪੂਰਨ ਪ੍ਰਗਾਸ ਭਾਸਕਰਿ ਜਗਮਗ ਜੋਤਿ; ਉਲੂ ਅੰਧ ਕੰਧ ਬੁਰੀ ਕਰਨੀ ਕਮਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as bright light spreads all around when the Sun rises, but an owl has committed so many ill deeds that he remains hidden in dark caves and burrows,

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਤਉ ਬਸੰਤ ਸਮੈ ਸਫਲ ਬਨਾਸਪਤੀ; ਨਿਹਫਲ ਸੇਂਬਲ ਬਡਾਈ ਉਰ ਆਨੀ ਹੈ॥
column5: Just as all vegetation bears flowers and fruits during spring season but a cotton silk tree who has brought in him the praise of being big and mighty, remains bereft of flowers and fruits.

column2: ਮੋਹਿ ਗੁਰ ਸਾਗਰ ਮੈ ਚਾਖਿਓ ਨਹੀ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ ਰਸੁ; ਤ੍ਰਿਖਾਵੰਤ ਚਾਤ੍ਰਿਕ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਬਕਬਾਨੀ ਹੈ॥੫੨੫॥
column5: Despite living near the vast ocean like True Guru, I, the unfortunate one, had not tasted the divine elixir obtained by His loving worship. I have only been making noise of my thirst like a rain-bird. I have only indulged in empty arguments and contemplat

column2: ਜੈਸੇ ਗਜਰਾਜ ਗਾਜਿ. ਮਾਰਤ ਮਨੁਖ ਸਿਰਿ ਡਾਰਤ ਹੈ ਛਾਰ; ਤਾਹਿ ਕਹਤ ਅਰੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a giant elephant trumpets, kill people and throws dust on himself, he is known to be healthy (Those who are intoxicated in their arrogance, cruel or who kick up dust are good according to the world).

column2: ਸੂਆ ਜਿਉ ਪਿੰਜਰ ਮੈ ਕਹਿਤ ਬਨਾਇ ਬਾਤੈ; ਪੇਖ ਸੁਨ ਕਹੈ ਤਾਹਿ ਰਾਜ ਗ੍ਰਿਹਿ ਜੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Just as a parrot in a cage listens to others conversation and copies them. Those who listen and see him, opine that he is very wise and knowledgeable. He is fit to live in king's palace. (To the world, he who talks much is a wise person).

column2: ਤੈਸੇ ਸੁਖ ਸੰਪਤਿ ਮਾਇਆ ਮਦੋਨ ਪਾਪ ਕਰੈ; ਤਾਹਿ ਕਹੈ ਸੁਖੀਆ ਰਮਤ ਰਸ ਭੋਗ ਜੀ॥
column5: Similarly a person enjoys and engrosses himself in countless materialistic pleasures and commits sins. People call him happy and comfortable. (In the eyes of the world, material things are means of happiness and comfort).

column2: ਜਤੀ ਸਤੀ ਅਉ ਸੰਤੋਖੀ ਸਾਧਨ ਕੀ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਕਰੈ; ਉਲਟੋ ਈ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਹੈ ਅਗਿਆਨ ਲੋਗ ਜੀ॥੫੨੬॥
column5: The ignorant world's perception is contrary (to the truth of Guru's words). The world slanders those who are disciplined, truthful, contented and supreme. (526)

column2: ਸਵੈਯਾ॥ ਜਉ ਗਰਬੈ ਬਹੁ ਬੂੰਦ ਚਿਤੰਤਰਿ; ਸਨਮੁਖ ਸਿੰਧ ਸੋਭ ਨਹੀਂ ਪਾਵੈ॥
column5: If a drop of water feels proud of its greatness in its mind, it does not earn a good name or praise before the vast ocean.

column2: ਜਉ ਬਹੁ ਉਡੈ ਖਗ ਧਾਰਿ ਮਹਾਂਬਲ; ਪੇਖਿ ਅਕਾਸ ਰਿਦੈ ਸੁਕਚਾਵੈ॥
column5: If a bird flies high and far putting much effort, he is sure to feel ashamed of its effort seeing the infinitely vast expanse of the sky.

column2: ਜਿਉ ਬ੍ਰਹਮੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਚੰਡ ਬਿਲੋਕਤ; ਗੂਲਰ ਜੰਤ ਉਡੰਤ ਲਜਾਵੈ॥
column5: Just as the fruit of a kind of fig tree (cotton boll in full bloom) sees the vast expense of the Universe after coming out of the fruit, he feels shy of his insignificant existence.

column2: ਤੂੰ ਕਰਤਾ ਹਮ ਕੀਏ ਤਿਹਾਰੇ ਜੀ; ਤੋ ਪਹਿ ਬੋਲਨ ਕਿਉ ਬਨਿ ਆਵੈ॥੫੨੭॥
